properties.Title: Lagash-Umma dispute in ancient Sumeria over water and irrigation leads to a century of conflict
properties.Description: The dispute over water and irrigated lands in the Gu'edena (the "edge of paradise") region, between the Sumerian city-states of Umma and Lagash, lasts for a century in the first known water conflict.

properties.Title: Tigris River dammed in ancient dispute in Babylon
properties.Description: A grandson of Hammurabi, Abish or Abi-Eshuh, dams the Tigris to prevent the retreat of rebels led by Iluma-Ilum, who declares the independence of Babylon. This failed attempt marks the decline of the Sumerians who had reached their apex under Hammurabi.

properties.Title: Kishon River floods and contributes to the defeat of Sisera in the biblical battle between the Israelites and the Canaanites
properties.Description: The Old Testament gives an account of the defeat of Sisera and his "nine hundred chariots of iron' by the unmounted army of Barak on the fabled Plains of Esdraelon in the Battle of Mount Tabor between the Israelites and the Canaanites. In this account, God sends heavy rainfall in the mountains, and the Kishon River overflows the plain and immobilizes or destroys Sisera's forces ("...the earth trem

properties.Title: Assyrian king Sargon II uses water as a weapon and target during multiple campaigns
properties.Description: In several military campaigns, King Sargon II of Assyria and his opponents both use water as a weapon and a target, damaging wells, diverting and destroying irrigation canals, and flooding lands for offensive and defensive purposes.

properties.Title: Sennacherib razes Babylon and diverts an irrigation canal to wash over the ruins
properties.Description: In quelling rebellious Assyrians in 695 BCE, Sennacherib razes Babylon and diverts one of the principal irrigation canals so that its waters wash over the ruins.

properties.Title: Hezekiah stops springs in advance of Assyrian invasion led by Sennacherib
properties.Description: When King Hezekiah of Judah sees that Sennacherib of Assyria is coming in war, he has springs and a brook outside Jerusalem stopped to keep water from the Assyrians. ("So there was gathered much people together, who stopped all the fountains, and the brook that ran through the midst of the land, saying, Why should the kings of Assyria come, and find much water" 2 Chronicles 32:1"4).

properties.Title: Assyrian king cuts off water of enemy in siege of Tyre
properties.Description: Esarhaddon, an Assyrian, refers to an earlier period when gods, angered by insolent mortals, created destructive floods. According to inscriptions recorded during his reign, Esarhaddon besieges Tyre, cutting off food and water.

properties.Title: Assyrian king dries up enemy's wells in a war against Elam
properties.Description: Ashurbanipal's inscriptions also refer to a siege against Tyre, although scholars attribute it to Esarhaddon. In campaigns against both Arabia and Elam in 645 BCE, Ashurbanipal, son of Esarhaddon, dries up wells to deprive Elamite troops. He also guards wells from Arabian fugitives in an earlier Arabian war. On his return from victorious battle against Elam, Ashurbanipal floods the city of Sapibel

properties.Title: Khosr River diverted by Babylonians to cause a flood to support an attack on Nineveh
properties.Description: A coalition of Egyptian, Median (Persian), and Babylonian forces attacks and destroys Nineveh, the capital of Assyria. Nebuchadnezzar's father, Nabopolassar, leads the Babylonians. The converging armies divert the Khosr River to create a flood, which allows them to elevate their siege engines on rafts.

properties.Title: Nebuchadnezzar uses Euphrates River as defense of Babylon
properties.Description: Nebuchadnezzar builds immense walls around Babylon, using the Euphrates and canals as defensive moats surrounding the inner castle.

properties.Title: Assyrians poison wells of enemies
properties.Description: Assyrians poison the wells of their enemies with rye ergot.

properties.Title: Hellebore is used to poison water supplies during the Cirraean War, at the beginning of the 6th century BCE
properties.Description: Athenian legislator Solon reportedly has roots of Helleborus thrown into a small river or aqueduct leading from the Pleistrus River to Cirrha during a siege of this city. The enemy forces become violently ill and are defeated as a result. Some accounts have Solon building a dam across the Plesitus River cutting off the city's water supply. Such practices are widespread.

properties.Title: Forces under Nebuchadnezzar breach an aqueduct to end siege of the ancient city of Tyre
properties.Description: Nebuchadnezzar breaches the aqueduct that supplies the city of Tyre in order to end a long siege.

properties.Title: Cyrus diverts the Diyalah River during his war against Nabonidus in Babylon
properties.Description: On his way from Sardis to defeat Nabonidus at Babylon, Cyrus faces a powerful tributary of the Tigris, probably the Diyalah. According to Herodotus' account, the river drowns his royal white horse and presents a formidable obstacle to his march. Cyrus, angered by the "insolence" of the river, halts his army and orders them to cut 360 canals to divert the river's flow. Other historians argue that C

properties.Title: Cyrus diverts the Euphrates in his attack on Babylon
properties.Description: According to Herodotus, Cyrus invades Babylon by diverting the Euphrates above the city and marching troops along the dry riverbed. This popular account describes a midnight attack that coincided with a Babylonian feast.

properties.Title: The city of Jinyang is flooded by the army of Zhi Yao in central China
properties.Description: In 454 BCE, during a conflict between the Jin and Zhao clans in central China, Zhi Yao of Jin builds a dam to divert the Fen River to inundate the city of Jinyang (now Taiyuan).

properties.Title: Spartans poison cisterns of Piraeus during the Peloponnesian War
properties.Description: During the second year of the Peloponnesian War, a plague breaks out in Athens. The Spartans are accused of poisoning the cisterns of the Piraeus, the source of most of Athens' water.

properties.Title: Water is used as a weapon in the battle of Delium, 424 BC
properties.Description: In the battle of Delium, the Athenians are at war with the Theban Confederacy. It is a custom at that time not to damage sacred areas, such as the waters at the Delium temple. In this conflict, however, the Athenians foul the temple waters and destroy local vineyards and agricultural fields for a short-term military advantage.

properties.Title: Alexander tears down Persian dams and defensive weirs along the Tigris River
properties.Description: Returning from the razing of Persepolis, Alexander proceeds to India. After the Indian campaigns, he heads back to Babylon via the Persian Gulf and the Tigris, where he tears down defensive weirs that the Persians had constructed along the river. The Greek historian Arrian describes Alexander's disdain for the Persians' attempt to block navigation, which he saw as "unbecoming to men who are victor

properties.Title: Alexander the Great conquers Cyropolis by infiltrating the city through a dried up waterway
properties.Description: Alexander the Great subdues the city of Cyropolis in 329 BC by laying siege to the city and sneaking through a dried-up watercourse that went under the town's wall, opening the city gates and admitting his invading army.

properties.Title: Chinese Qin general Bai Qi floods the city of Yicheng, reportedly drowning hundreds of thousands
properties.Description: In 278 BCE, Qin general Bai Qi attacks the capital of the state of Chu, flooding the city (now Yicheng) from the Yangtze River, and reportedly drowning hundreds of thousands of people.

properties.Title: During the Chinese wars of unification an army redirects the waters of the Yellow River killing over 100,000 people
properties.Description: In 225 BC, a 600,000-strong Qin army led by Wang Ben during Qin's wars of unification laid seige to Daliang (present day Kaifeng, Henan, China). Wang Ben ordered his troops to redirect the waters from the Yellow River and the Hong Canal to flood Daliang and after three months of laboring, succeeded in their plan. Daliang was heavily flooded and over 100,000 people died, including civilians.

properties.Title: Parthian king Arsaces III destroys a water-supply system to slow the attacking army of Seleucid Antiochus
properties.Description: Around 210 BCE, Arsaces III, one of the Parthian kings, destroys qanats (underground channels) in order to make it difficult for the attacking army of Seleucid Antiochus to advance toward the Parthian capital, Hecatompylos.

properties.Title: Wei River dams in China are intentionally breached in 204 BC
properties.Description: An intentional rupturing of the Wei River dams leads to the victory of  Han Dynasty forces.

properties.Title: Thousands are drowned when Chinese general traps and then releases water during battles in 204 BCE
properties.Description: In 204 BCE, after the collapse of the Qin Dynasty and during extensive battles before the establishment of the Han Dynasty, Han general Han Xin lures an opposing Chu army into the Wei River and releases water held back by a dam, trapping and drowning thousands.

properties.Title: The Achaeans use water as a weapon in the siege of Phana
properties.Description: Pausanias speaks of an Etolian town named Phana (perhaps the Paeanium of Polybius) besieged by the Achaeans, but without success. After an unsuccessful consultation with the Oracle of Delphi,  the Achaeans accidentally discover the spring from which the besieged town derives its only supply of water. This being cut off, the town is forced to surrender.

properties.Title: Water is used as a weapon in 101 BCE China in the siege of Fergana (Yuan)
properties.Description: Fergana (Yuan), the capital of China, surrenders after the enemy cuts off the city's water supplies. "In the city of the king of Yuan there were no wells, and the people had to obtain water from a river outside the city, whereupon experts in hydraulics were sent to divert the course of the river, thus depriving the city of water, besides effecting an opening though which the city might be laid ope

properties.Title: Caesar constructs ditches as part of his blocade in the Siege of Alesia
properties.Description: Caesar constructs water-filled ditches as blockade during Siege of Alesia in Gaul, site of modern-day Alise-Sainte-Reine in Côte-d'Or near Dijon, France.

properties.Title: Caesar attacks water supplies during the siege of Uxellodunum
properties.Description: Caesar attacks water supplies during siege of Uxellodunum by undermining one of the local springs and placing attackers near the other. Shortage of water leads to the surrender of the Gauls.

properties.Title: Massilia (modern-day Marseille) uses water to defend against Roman siege by Julius Caesar
properties.Description: During the first year of the Great Roman Civil War, Julius Caesar's troops lay siege to the walled city of Massilia (modern-day Marseille, France) using siege towers, battering rams, and by digging "mines" or tunnels to undermine the city walls. Massilians defend their city with "dogged determination;" tactics included directing water through pipes to wash down on attackers, which the Romans count
properties.Sources: Illustrated History of the Roman Empire n.d.; Caesar 1906

properties.Title: Alexandria’s water supply disrupted during siege against Julius Caesar
properties.Description: In 48 BCE, Julius Caesar is besieged in Alexandria. General Ganimedes, chief of operations against Caesar, blocks the town’s water supply, causing panic in Caesar’s army. “By encouragement and reasoning Caesar allayed his men’s alarm, declaring that sweet water could be found in wells and trenches… whereupon a great quantity of sweet water was discovered.”

properties.Title: Jewish protestors killed by Roman troops in protest over stream diversion
properties.Description: Roman Procurator Pontius Pilate uses sacred money to divert a stream to Jerusalem, Israel. The Jews are angered at the diversion and tens of thousands gather to protest. Pilate's soldiers mingle among the crowd and with daggers hidden in their garments, attack the protesters. "A great number" are slain and wounded.

properties.Title: The Chinese warlord Cao Cao floods the city of Xiapi at the end of the Han Dynasty in 199, forcing its surrender
properties.Description: In 199 AD at the end of the Han Dynasty, the warlord Cao Cao besieges the city of Xiapi (modern Pizhou) in eastern China, rerouting the rivers Yi and Si, and flooding the city, forcing its ultimate surrender.

properties.Title: Goths cut aqueducts in their siege of Rome
properties.Description: In the 6th century AD, as the Roman Empire begins to decline, the Goths besiege Rome and cut almost all of the aqueducts leading into the city. In 537 AD this siege is successful. The only aqueduct that continues to function is the Aqua Virgo, which runs almost entirely underground.

properties.Title: To win a battle forces in Korea drown thousands of Chinese soldiers by building a dam and then releasing the water
properties.Description: The Goguryeo–Sui War launched by the Sui dynasty of China in the seventh century includes an attack by 300,000 troops against Pyongyang, the capital of Goguryeo. During the attack in 612, Goguryeo General Eulji Mundeok cuts off the flow of water in the Salsu river with a dam. When Sui troops reached the river, Mundeok opened the dam, flooding and killing thousands of enemy soldiers.

properties.Title: The Tang Dynasty general Li Shimin builds and breaches a levee to flood the army of Liu Heit in China
properties.Description: In 622 AD, between the end of the Sui Dynasty and the start of the Tang Dynasty, the Tang general Li Shimin builds and then breaches a levee to defeat the rebel lord Liu Heit.

properties.Title: The water Siege at the Battle of Karbala was a pivotal event in Islamic history, with the use of the Euphrates River as a weapon
properties.Description: In a pivotal event in Islamic history known as the Siege at the Battle of Karbala, water is used as a weapon by the Umayyad caliph Yazid's army against Imam Hussein bin Ali, grandson of the Prophet Muhammad. During the siege, water from the Euphrates River is cut off from Hussein's forces.

properties.Title: The Chinese governor of Kaifeng in 1128 breaches dikes along the Yellow River in an effort to stop an advancing army
properties.Description: During the Chinese Jurchen “kingdom” (c. 1115 to 1234 AD) the governor of Kaifeng breaches the dikes along the Yellow River in a final, but futile effort to halt the advance of the Jurchen forces.
properties.Sources: Chen et al. 2012; Storozum et al. 2018

properties.Title: In 1155 AD, in Tortona, Italy, Emperor Barbarossa poisons wells with human bodies
properties.Description: In 1155 AD, in Tortona, Italy, 12th-century Holy Roman Emperor Frederick Barbarossa poisons wells with human bodies.

properties.Title: Saladin cuts off Crusaders' water
properties.Description: Saladin is able to defeat the Crusaders at the Horns of Hattin in 1187 by denying them access to water. In some reports, Saladin fills all the wells along the way with sand and destroys the villages of the Maronite Christians who would supply the Christian army with water.

properties.Title: Victims of a Viking conflict are tossed into a drinking water well and the water supply destroyed
properties.Description: In the year 1197, King Sverre Sigurdsson and Birkebeiner mercenaries are attacked and defeated by his rivals, the Baglers, in Sverresborg castle. A Viking legend tells that the Baglers destroy the buildings there as well as the freshwater supply of the castle, by disposing of victims in the well and filling it with stones. Recent archeological evidence confirms this legend.

properties.Title: Mongols open dikes to flood the opposing Abbasid army as a prelude to attacking Baghdad
properties.Description: During the siege of Baghdad in 1258, Mongol forces break the levees of the Dujayl Canal and flood the camp of the opposing Abbasid army, drowning many and opening the way for the final attack on Baghdad.

properties.Title: Water-supply canals and reservoirs are destroyed in Mughal, India in local protests over social and political conditions
properties.Description: Historian M. Athar Ali writes that in Mughal India, in the 1260s, the water canals and reservoirs feeding Delhi were intentionally dried up "because the channels feeding it were dammed up by 'dishonest men,'" referring to local protests over social and political conditions.

properties.Title: A flooding attempt by the Republic of Florence backfires
properties.Description: Florentine architect Filippo Brunelleschi develops a project to use the waters of the Serchio River to flood Lucca. During the attempt to change the course of the water, Lucca builds its own weir and instead of flooding the town, the water is diverted back into the fields flooding the Florentine camp.

properties.Title: Vlad the Impaler poisons wells in his battle with the Ottoman Empire in 1462
properties.Description: Prince Vlad III of Wallachia (Vlad the Impaler) poisoned wells in his battle with the Ottoman Empire and Sultan Mehmed II in 1462.

properties.Title: A siege during the Flemish revolt against Maximilian of Austria ends when a farmer diverts water from a moat
properties.Description: A siege against the Flemish succeeds when water in a moat is diverted away during the revolt by Flemish cities against Austrian rule.

properties.Title: Machiavelli and Leonardo da Vinci develop a plan to cut Pisa's water
properties.Description: Leonardo da Vinci and Machiavelli plan to divert Arno River away from Pisa during conflict between Pisa and Florence (Italy).

properties.Title: Dutch flood land to repel Spaniards during the eighty years war, in what became known as the Dutch Water Line strategy
properties.Description: In 1573 at the beginning of the eighty years war against Spain, the Dutch flood the land to break the siege of Spanish troops on the town Alkmaar. The same defense is used to protect Leiden in 1574. This strategy becomes known as the Dutch Water Line and is used frequently for defense in later years.

properties.Title: Kenshin defeats Nobunaga forces in Japan by opening floodgates on the Tedori River
properties.Description: During the Battle of Tedorigawa in 1577 in Japan’s Kaga Province between the forces of Oda Nobunaga and Uesugi Kensin, Kensin orders floodgates on the Tedori River to be opened, killing Oda clan troops and repulsing their attack.

properties.Title: Seawalls intentionally breached to relieve towns besieged by the Spanish during the Dutch war of independence against Spain
properties.Description: In order to relieve Antwerp, Campen, and Terneuzen during the Dutch war against the Spanish, seawalls are destroyed at Saeftinghe in February 1584, flooding more than 25,000 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area.

properties.Title: The Dutch flood more than 7,500 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during the Eighty Years War with the Spanish Empire
properties.Description: The Dutch flood more than 7,500 hectares near Axel in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during the Eighty Years War with the Spanish Empire.

properties.Title: The Dutch flood more than 5,500 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during a conflict with the Portuguese
properties.Description: The Dutch flood more than 5,500 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during a conflict with the Portuguese.

properties.Title: Spain attempts to re-route Rhine River to damage the Dutch economy
properties.Description: The Spanish Habsburgs attempt to prevent ship traffic on the River Rhine from reaching the Dutch Republic in order to damage the Dutch economy. Plans are also made to divert water from the Rhine to lands under Spanish control, to dry up downstream cities in Holland. The first stage is a canal between the Rhine and Meuse, between the cities of Rheinberg and Venlo. Plans for a later stage call for a

properties.Title: 300,000 deaths when Yellow River dikes are breached in 1642
properties.Description: In 1642, in a battle between the incoming Manchu and the forces of the Ming dynasty, the Huang He River (Yellow River) dikes are breached by both sides, creating a catastrophe for Kaifeng in which 300,000 of its 378,000 citizens die.

properties.Title: Dutch flood land to repel French attack on the Netherlands
properties.Description: Louis XIV starts the third of the Dutch Wars in 1672, in which the French overrun the Netherlands. In defense, the Dutch open their dikes and flood the country, creating a watery barrier that is virtually impenetrable.

properties.Title: The Dutch flood around 3,500 hectares during a conflict with England, France, and two German electors
properties.Description: The Dutch flood around 3,500 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen and Flanders area during a conflict with England, France, and two German electors.

properties.Title: The Dutch flood around 775 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during the War of the Spanish Succession
properties.Description: The Dutch flood around 775 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during the War of the Spanish Succession.

properties.Title: The Dutch flood more than 2,200 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during the War of Austrian Succession
properties.Description: The Dutch flood more than 2,200 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during the War of Austrian Succession.

properties.Title: Fire set in 1748 New York water dispute
properties.Description: Ferry house on Brooklyn shore of East River burns down. New Yorkers accuse Brooklynites of having set the fire as revenge for unfair East River water rights.
properties.Sources: Museum of the City of New York (MCNY) n.d.

properties.Title: British attack the water system of New York during the Revolutionary War
properties.Description: British and Hessians attack the water-works system of New York: "...the enemy wantonly destroyed the New York water works' during the War for Independence."

properties.Title: The Dutch flood around than 1,300 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during the Anglo-Dutch wars
properties.Description: The Dutch flood around than 1,300 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during the Anglo-Dutch wars.

properties.Title: The Dutch flood around than 9,500 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during a conflict with the French
properties.Description: The Dutch flood around than 9,500 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during a conflict with the French.

properties.Title: Napoleon floods the plains around Mantua, Italy, to enhance the spread of malaria
properties.Description: Napoleon floods the plains around Mantua, Italy, to enhance the spread of malaria.

properties.Title: Wells polluted during the slave rebellion in Saint-Domingue, Hispaniola
properties.Description: A leader of the slave rebellion on the island of Hispaniola in the Caribbean, Toussaint L'Oueverture, says "The only resources we have are destruction and fire. Annihilate everything and burn everything. Block the roads, pollute the wells with corpses and dead horses."

properties.Title: Napoleon attempts to re-route Rhine River to harm Dutch
properties.Description: Napoleon orders the construction of a canal between Neuss and Venlo, to connect the Rhine and Meuse rivers, to divert trade from the Batavia Republic to the Southern Netherlands, then under French control. Three-quarters of the canal are completed, but work stops because of lack of funds.

properties.Title: The French flood lands in the Netherlands to try to stop a British attack on Napolean's forces
properties.Description: During the effort of Britain to attack France's naval capacity in their fight against Napolean, they attempted to seize control of  Walcheren and the port of Flushing in the Scheldt estuary in the Netherlands. The attack on Flushing was a success, though the French attempted to impeded the besiegers by cutting the dykes and flooding the land occupied by them. The flooding of the land led to a mass

properties.Title: Russians destroy local water infrastructure to hinder the invasion by French Emperor Napoleon
properties.Description: Troops defending Russia against Napoleon’s invasion destroy or pollute existing water-supply infrastructure in order to deny its use by the French in 1812.

properties.Title: A reservoir destroyed in Canada over health fears
properties.Description: A reservoir in Ops Township, Upper Canada (now Ontario) is destroyed by neighbors who consider it a hazard to health.

properties.Title: A mob destroys reservoir in Ohio over health fears
properties.Description: A reservoir in Mercer County, Ohio is destroyed by a mob that considered it a health hazard.

properties.Title: New Hampshire residents attack dam in dispute over water availability
properties.Description: Attack on a New Hampshire dam that impounds water for factories downstream by local residents unhappy over its effect on water levels.

properties.Title: Mobs destroy canals in Indiana over health fears
properties.Description: Repeated destruction of the banks and reservoirs of the Wabash and Erie Canal in southern Indiana by mobs regarding it as a health hazard.

properties.Title: Water wells are poisoned during the US Civil War
properties.Description: General William T. Sherman's memoirs contain an account of Confederate soldiers poisoning ponds by dumping the carcasses of dead animals into them. Other accounts suggest this tactic was used by both sides.

properties.Title: US Civil War Confederate forces use dams to cut off Union troops
properties.Description: During the US Civil War, Confederate forces near Yorktown use dams to flood the Warwick River and cut off Union troops. "The enemy is pushed behind a branch of the Warwick River in which they control the depths of water by dams. McClellan did not intend to pass that stream at that time, or at that point where the skirmish took place. But the troops, finding the stream fordable went over (under who

properties.Title: Levees are cut by Union forces in Vicksburg, Virginia during the US Civil War
properties.Description: General Ulysses S. Grant, during the Civil War campaign against Vicksburg, cut levees in the battle against the Confederates.

properties.Title: Water violence in New Mexico over water rights
properties.Description: Recurrent friction and eventual violent conflict over water rights in the vicinity of Tularosa, New Mexico involving villagers, ranchers, and farmers.

properties.Title: Cattlemen fight Texan landowners for access to water and pasture
properties.Description: Drought in Texas in the early 1880s lead to growing disputes among ranch and land owners and cattlemen looking for access to grass and water for their stock. Cattlemen begin cutting fences to open access to water and pasture in what became known as the "Texas Fence Cutters Range War." At least three men are killed in clashes between fence cutters and ranchmen during this period, until agreements a

properties.Title: Mob dynamites Ohio reservoir
properties.Description: Dynamiting of a canal reservoir in Paulding County, Ohio by a mob regarding it as a health hazard. State Militia called out to restore order.

properties.Title: Canal lock destroyed in Canada
properties.Description: Partly successful attempt to destroy a lock on the Welland Canal in Ontario, Canada either by Fenians protesting English Policy in Ireland or by agents of Buffalo, NY grain handlers unhappy at the diversion of trade through the canal.

properties.Title: Many are killed during the Johnson County Water War between competing cattle association over access to water resources and land
properties.Description: The Johnson County Water War (or the War on Powder River) leads to many deaths among competing cattle interests. The conflict is fueled by pressure over water and land during severe droughts and hard winters in Wyoming and on the Great Plains.

properties.Title: France and Britain battle over control of the headwaters of the Nile River
properties.Description: Military conflict nearly ensues between Britain and France in 1898 when a French expedition attempts to gain control of the headwaters of the White Nile. While the parties ultimately negotiate a settlement of the dispute, the incident is since characterized as having "dramatized Egypt's vulnerable dependence on the Nile, and fixed the attitude of Egyptian policy-makers ever since."

properties.Title: A deathly riot arises over the cost of water in Port of Spain, Trinidad
properties.Description: In 1903, the government in Port-of-Spain, Trinidad passes an ordinance increasing the price of water following the construction of national waterworks infrastructure. Public meetings are held to protest the increase, culminating in a riot at the seat of government, the Red House. Protestors set the building on fire and during the riot police kill 16 people and injure 42 others.

properties.Title: German colonial troops poison desert water wells
properties.Description: German colonial troops brutally suppress a rebellion by the Herero people of German South-West Africa (modern-day Namibia). General Lothar von Trotha pledges to exterminate the Herero people in what has become known as the Herero and Namaqua Genocide. The Germans drive ethnic Herero and Namaqua populations into the Namib desert leading to massive death from dehydration and starvation; they also re

properties.Title: Five sticks of dynamite are found at the town well in Stratford, Iowa, USA but the bomb fails to explode
properties.Description: Five sticks of dynamite are found at the town well in Stratford, Iowa. The bomb fails to explode because of a faulty fuse.

properties.Title: Violence over fishing and water access kills one in Tennessee
properties.Description: Violence, including a murder, directed against agents of a land company that claims title to Reelfoot Lake in northwestern Tennessee who attempt to levy charges for fish taken and threaten to drain the lake for agriculture.

properties.Title: Two powerful bombs are placed at the aqueduct that carries half of Glasgow, Scotland's water supply but they fail to go off
properties.Description: Two powerful bombs are placed at the aqueduct that carries half of Glasgow, Scotland's water supply but they fail to go off. A note stating the destruction of the aqueduct was a protest against the treatment of Emmeline Pankhurst, a leader of the British suffragette movement.

properties.Title: Belgium floods land in an attempt to stop the German invasion in the First World War
properties.Description: During the early days of World War I in 1914 when the Germans invade Belgium, the French high command plan to flood land to obstruct the Germans. The plan is postponed because the Belgian army is preparing to flood the area between the Yser and its tributary canals, which happens in late October when the Belgians open the sluices at Nieuwpoort raising the water level until an impassable area is cr

properties.Title: German troops poison wells in Southwest Africa (now Namibia)
properties.Description: Union of South African troops capture Windhoek, capital of German Southwest Africa in May 1915. Retreating German troops poison wells - "a violation of the Hague convention."

properties.Title: During WWI, Germans devastate 30-km strip in 'Operation Alberich,' destroying water utilities to hinder Allied advance
properties.Description: Before the scheduled withdrawal of German forces in World War I from the Siegfried Line, the Germans devastate a 30-km-deep strip of land in ‘Operation Alberich’ to deprive advancing Allied troops of basic facilities, including water utilities and water infrastructure.

properties.Title: Two explosions destroy sections of the Los Angeles Aqueduct, California, USA
properties.Description: Two explosions destroy sections of the California aqueduct, in a protest against the diversion of water from the Owens Valley to Los Angeles.

properties.Title: Farmers bomb a canal to reduce diversions from a river in Idaho
properties.Description: Farmers angered by what they saw as excessive diversions from Mackay Reservoir on the Big Lost River in Idaho tried to blow up the dam. Reports say that when the farmers arrived at the dam wall, the operator said they didn’t have enough dynamite to do damage. He suggested targeting a specific diversion canal instead, and the explosion sent water back to the river’s natural channel.

properties.Title: The concrete wall of the Alabama Gates of the Los Angeles Aqueduct is bombed, California, USA
properties.Description: The concrete wall of the Alabama Gates of the Los Angeles Aqueduct is bombed in protest of the taking of water from the Owens Valley, California.

properties.Title: Yangtze River dikes in China are breached during battles between the National Revolutionary Army and a warlord
properties.Description: In 1926, the National Revolutionary Army of the Kuomintang (KMT) launches a military campaign to reunify China after the 1911 revolution. During battles, forces under the warlord Wu Peifu breach several Yangtze River dikes, temporarily slowing the advancing KMT army.

properties.Title: A city well feeding the Los Angeles Aqueduct, California, USA is bombed
properties.Description: A well feeding the Los Angeles Aqueduct to bring water from the Owens Valley to Los Angeles, California is bombed.

properties.Title: A ten-foot hole is blasted in Los Angeles Aqueduct, California, USA
properties.Description: A ten-foot hole is blasted in the Los Angeles Aqueduct near the Alabama Gates, in the ongoing violence protesting diversion of water from the Owens Valley to Los Angeles, California.

properties.Title: A major section of the Los Angeles Aqueduct is destroyed by bombing, California, USA
properties.Description: A large section (the "No Name Siphon") of the Los Angeles Aqueduct is destroyed by bombing near Little Lake, California.

properties.Title: The water intake to a power plant in Big Pine, California, USA is bombed
properties.Description: The water intake to the city power plant in Big Pine, California is bombed.

properties.Title: Los Angeles Aqueduct is bombed at Cottonwood Creek near Lone Pine, California, USA
properties.Description: The concrete wall of the Los Angeles Aqueduct at Cottonwood Creek below Lone Pine, California is bombed.

properties.Title: Los Angeles Aqueduct is bombed again, near Tuttle Creek, Lone Pine, California, USA
properties.Description: The concrete wall of the Los Angeles Aqueduct at Tuttle Creek below Lone Pine, California is bombed, blowing a sixteen-foot hole in the aqueduct.

properties.Title: Bomb explodes in an attempt to block the Los Angeles Aqueduct with a landslide, California, USA
properties.Description: Bomb explodes in the Alabama Hills in an attempt to block the Los Angeles Aqueduct with a landslide.

properties.Title: Los Angeles Aqueduct and control gates are bombed in the Alabama Hills and separately, near Independence, California, USA
properties.Description: Two sections of aqueduct wall are damaged in separate bombings of the Los Angeles Aqueduct near the Alabama Hills and Independence, California. Control gates are also destroyed.

properties.Title: A section of the Los Angeles Aqueduct is bombed in Kern County, California, USA
properties.Description: A ten-foot section of the Los Angeles Aqueduct is bombed in Kern County, California where the pipeline forms a siphon.

properties.Title: Arizona mobilizes troops to protest Parker Dam and creates the "Arizona Navy."
properties.Description: Arizona calls out the National Guard and militia units to the border with California to protest the construction of Parker Dam and diversions from the Colorado River, briefly creating the "Arizona Navy"; the dispute ultimately is settled in court.

properties.Title: Dams are attacked during the Spanish Civil War
properties.Description: During the Spanish Civil War, two concrete gravity dams, at Burguillo and Ordunte, are attacked by the Nationalist army, with a 2.5-ton charge placed in an inspection gallery at Ordunte. Some limited damage occurs.

properties.Title: China destroys flood-control levees along the Yellow River to defend from Japan, with a very high death toll
properties.Description: Chiang Kai-shek orders the destruction of flood-control dikes of the Huayuankou, Henan section of the Huang He (Yellow) River, in order to flood areas threatened by the Japanese army. West of Kaifeng, dikes are destroyed with dynamite, spilling water across the flat plain. Even though the flood destroys part of the invading army and mires its equipment in mud, Wuhan, the headquarters of the Nation

properties.Title: Dutch flood valley to defend from Germany during World War II
properties.Description: During the mobilization of the Dutch at the beginning of World War II, 1939-40, the Dutch attempt to flood the Gelderse Vallei with the New Dutch Water Defense Line, which had been completed in 1885. During the German invasion in May 1940, large areas are inundated.
properties.Sources: Information and Documentation Center for the Geography of the Netherlands (IDG) 1996

properties.Title: Japan's Unit 731 poisons wells in China
properties.Description: Japanese chemical and biological weapons activities reportedly include tests by 'Unit 731' against military and civilian targets in China by lacing water wells and reservoirs with typhoid and other pathogens.

properties.Title: 1939 Finns poison wells to combat invading Soviet troops
properties.Description: In the 1939-40 Russo-Finnish Winter War, Finns render wells unusable by putting animal carcasses or feces in them in order to combat invading Soviet forces.

properties.Title: Finns use canal water against Soviets
properties.Description: Manipulation of the waters of the Saimaa Canal (Finland) by partisan Finns in order to flood surrounding land and hinder Soviet troop movements during the Soviet-Finnish conflict.

properties.Title: The Soviet Union's hydroelectric dams are attacked during World War II
properties.Description: World War II inflicts enormous harm to hydroelectricity systems in the Soviet Union. Over two-thirds of the hydroelectric power stations are lost.

properties.Title: The Dnipro Dam is targeted during World War II by both Soviets and Germans
properties.Description: The strategically-important Dnipro hydropower plant in Ukraine is targeted by both Soviet and German troops during WWII. On August 18, 1941, the dam and power plant are dynamited by Soviet troops retreating in front of advancing German forces. The facility is bombed again in 1943 by retreating German troops.
properties.Sources: Pagan 2005; New York Times, The 1941; Makarov 2005; Axis History Forum 2004

properties.Title: Soviets create flood to slow German troops
properties.Description: In November 1941, Soviet troops flood the area to the south of the Istra Reservoir near Moscow in an effort to slow the German advance. Just a few weeks later, German troops use the same tactic to create a water barrier to halt advances by the Soviet 16th Army.

properties.Title: The Dutch flood around than 5,800 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during World War II
properties.Description: The Dutch flood around than 5,800 hectares in the Zeeuws-Vlaanderen area during World War II.

properties.Title: The water supply for Singapore is cut by the Japanese during the battle for Singapore in 1942
properties.Description: During the battle for Singapore in February 1942, the Japanese bombed the civilian water supply and cut off Singapore's water from Malaysia.

properties.Title: German dams on the Möhne, Sorpe, and Eder Rivers, Germany are destroyed by Allies
properties.Description: British Royal Air Force bomb dams on the Möhne, Sorpe, and Eder Rivers, Germany (May 16 and 17). Möhne Dam breech kills 1,200, destroys all downstream dams for 50 km. The flood that occurs after breaking the Eder dam reaches a peak discharge of 8,500 meters per second, which is nine times higher than the highest flood observed. Many houses and bridges are destroyed, and 68 are killed.

properties.Title: Ennepe Dam bombed in WWII
properties.Description: As part of the British "Dambusters" campaign in 1943 in World War II, the Ennepe Dam in the Ruhr Valley, Germany, is damaged.

properties.Title: Germans flood Ill River Valley
properties.Description: Germans flood the Ill River Valley during the Battle of the Bulge (winter 1944-45) creating a lake 16 kilometers long, 3-6 kilometers wide, and 1-2 meters deep, greatly delaying the American Army's advance toward the Rhine.

properties.Title: Germany floods Pontine Marshes
properties.Description: The German army floods the Pontine Marches, on the coast of Italy southeast of Rome, by stopping pumps and opening dikes, in order to disrupt Allied forces who had established a beachhead at Anzio, Italy. Allied forces are surrounded by German artillery and pinned down for months by heavy shelling. The purpose of the flooding is to bring mosquitoes and malaria, "deliberately introduced as an act o
properties.Sources: US Army Corps of Engineers 1953; Evans 2009

properties.Title: Germans use Italian rivers against Allies
properties.Description: German forces use waters from the Isoletta Dam (Liri River, Italy) in January and February to successfully destroy British assault forces crossing the Garigliano River (downstream of Liri River). The German Army then dam the Rapido River, flooding a valley occupied by the American Army.

properties.Title: The Nazis flood part of the Netherlands as a defense act during World War II
properties.Description: The Nazis flood more than 29,000 hectares in the islands of Schouwen-Duiveland and Tholen, St. Philipsland during World War II.

properties.Title: Soviets attack Finn-controlled dam near Leningrad
properties.Description: In June, the Soviet air force attacked the Svir River Dam near Leningrad, then under the control of the Finnish military.

properties.Title: US targets Japanese water supplies in bombardment of Saipan
properties.Description: The US bombardment of the Japanese-occupied island of Saipan in June 1944 targets water-supply points, resulting in severe shortages.

properties.Title: Germans flood land to hinder the invasion of Normandy
properties.Description: Germans flood land around Utah Beach and the area around the Douve River during the invasion of Normandy, France, in June 1944, hindering the advances of the US 82nd and 101st Airborne Divisions.

properties.Title: Germans flood Ay River
properties.Description: Germans flood the Ay River in France in July 1944, creating a lake two meters deep and several kilometers wide, slowing an advance on Saint-Lô, a German communications center in Normandy.

properties.Title: October 1944: Allies breach dikes in Netherlands, flooding island and forcing German retreat; depicted in The Forgotten Battle
properties.Description: During the Battle of Walcheren Causeway in the Netherlands in October 1944, the Allies breach dikes protecting Walcheren Island, where German batteries blocked access to the port of Antwerp. These actions flood the central part of the island, forcing German defenders onto high ground. The Germans are ultimately forced to retreat. A film, The Forgotten Battle, commemorates these events.

properties.Title: During World War II Greek saboteurs attack water facilities, industries, and transportation systems
properties.Description: During World War II Greek saboteurs attack water facilities, industries, and transportation systems.

properties.Title: US bombing raid on Bangkok in World War II cuts off water and power to the city
properties.Description: A World War II B-29 raid on Bangkok destroys two key power plants leaving the city without power and water. This was the last major attack conducted against targets in Thailand prior to the Japanese surrender in August 1945.

properties.Title: Germans pollute reservoir in Bohemia
properties.Description: In one of the few verified German tactical uses of biological warfare, German forces pollute a large reservoir in northwestern Bohemia, Czechoslovakia with sewage in May 1945. See also the entry for the Pontine Marshes in 1944.

properties.Title: Water and food supplies cut off during Arab siege of Jerusalem
properties.Description: Water and food supplies are cut off by Arab forces during their siege of Jerusalem from December 1, 1947 to July 10, 1948. Shortages cause Israelis to begin rationing water on May 12, limiting each person to 2 gallons per day (8 L), of which four pints (2 L) are for drinking.
properties.Sources: Collins and LaPierre 1972; Joseph 1960; Wolf 1995; Wolf 1997

properties.Title: In April 1948, India cuts the flow of Indus River irrigation water to Pakistan and the city of Lahore
properties.Description: In April 1948, India stops the flow of Indus River water to irrigation canals on its side, cutting off water to important agricultural areas in Pakistan and to the city of Lahore.

properties.Title: Zionist forces poison the water supply of the city of Acre and Gaza with infectious disease and bacteria
properties.Description: In May 1948, Zionist forces poison the water supply of the city of Acre (now a part of present day Israel) with typhoid bacteria, leading to a typhoid epidemic; another attempt is made to poison wells in Gaza with typhoid and dysentery.

properties.Title: Native Africans resist removal from land due to impending filling of Kariba Dam in the Zambezi watershed
properties.Description: The African people living in the Zambezi River Valley protest and resist removal from their home land, even as British colonizers fill the newly built Kariba Dam and threaten to prosecute demonstrators and burn homes and crops. During at least one protest that turned violent, eight Tonga people are shot and killed.

properties.Title: Israel and Syria fight over Yarmouk River
properties.Description: Jordan makes public its plans to irrigate the Jordan Valley by tapping the Yarmouk River; Israel responds by commencing drainage of the Huleh swamps located in the demilitarized zone between Israel and Syria; border skirmishes ensue between Israel and Syria.

properties.Title: North Korea floods Pukhan Valley
properties.Description: North Korea releases flood waves from the Hwachon Dam damaging floating bridges operated by UN troops in the Pukhan Valley. The US Navy then sends planes to destroy the spillway crest gates.

properties.Title: US Bombers Destroy Korea's Hwacheon Dam
properties.Description: The Hwacheon Dam in Korea, completed in 1944, becomes both a target and a tool of opposing forces during the Korean War. In 1951, North Korea opens the dam to flood downstream areas and slow advancing UN forces. In response, the US Navy sends aircraft to bomb the dam, the last known American use of aerial torpedoes in war.

properties.Title: Burmese government forces recapture Gyobyu Waterworks, previously occupied by rebel forces
properties.Description: Burma government forces recapture the Gyobyu Waterworks in 1951, which had been occupied by rebel forces for three years.

properties.Title: Syrian forces fire on Israeli workers draining Huleh swamps
properties.Description: Syrian forces open fire on Israeli workers engaged in draining the Huleh Swamp, in northeastern Israel in 1951, wounding one. The Syrian Government argues that the reclamation work violates the Israel-Syrian armistice agreement.
properties.Sources: Jewish Telegraphic Agency 1951; U.S. Department of State 1951

properties.Title: A water pipeline is cut and the repair party attacked in Malaya during the extended communist insurgency
properties.Description: During the communist insurgency in Malaya between 1948 and 1960, civilian water infrastructure was attacked. In 1952 in Tanjong Malim a water pipeline is cut and the party repairing it was ambushed.

properties.Title: UN Command air forces conduct a series of massive bombings of 13 North Korean hydroelectric facilities
properties.Description: In June 1952, UN Command forces bomb a series of hydroelectric dams in North Korea on the Yalu River, including the Sui-ho dam, Kyosen dam, Fusen dam and Choisen dam. The attacks temporarily knock out 90% of North Korea's power-production capacity. Follow up attacks continue through September 1952.

properties.Title: Israel and Syria clash over Sea of Galilee and the construction of the Israeli National Water Carrier
properties.Description: Israel begins construction of its National Water Carrier to transfer water from the north of the Sea of Galilee out of the Jordan basin to the Negev Desert for irrigation. Syrian military actions along the border and international disapproval lead Israel to move its intake to the Sea of Galilee.
properties.Sources: Naff and Matson 1984; Samson and Charrier 1997

properties.Title: North Korean dam at Sui-ho attacked again by UN Command air forces during the Korean War
properties.Description: A new bombing attack on Sui-ho dam generators is conducted by UN Command air units in February 1953.

properties.Title: Massive additional attacks on North Korean dams are conducted during the Korean War
properties.Description: During May, 1953, the UN Command air units again bomb Sui-ho dam generators, and also bombs Toksan, Chasan, Kuwonga, Namsi, and Taechon dams. The ensuing floods destroy thousands of homes and threaten several million North Koreans with starvation.

properties.Title: Additional attacks on Sui-ho dam conducted during the Korean War
properties.Description: A final attack of the Korean War on the Sui-ho hydroelectric facility by UN forces is conducted on June 7, 1953.

properties.Title: Syria clashes with Israeli forces in dispute over Israeli water systems, April 1957
properties.Description: In April 1957, Syria clashes with Israel in Lake Huleh area in dispute over completion of Israeli water and drainage works.

properties.Title: Syria kills Israeli engineer working on Huleh swamp water diversions
properties.Description: Syria kills an Israeli engineer from across the Jordan River while working in the Huleh Swamp area on water diversions.

properties.Title: Syria clashes with Israeli forces in July/August 1957 dispute over Israeli water systems
properties.Description: In July and August 1957, Syria clashes with Israel in Lake Huleh area in dispute over completion of Israeli water and drainage works

properties.Title: Two guards of the Israeli water company, Mekorot, are killed
properties.Description: Two guards of the Israeli Mekorot water company are killed in Kibbutz Beit Govrin in August 1957.

properties.Title: Egypt and Sudan clash over Nile
properties.Description: Egypt sends an unsuccessful military expedition into disputed territory amidst pending negotiations over the Nile waters, Sudanese general elections, and an Egyptian vote on Sudan-Egypt unification; Nile Water Treaty signed when pro-Egyptian government elected in Sudan.

properties.Title: India and Pakistan fight over Surma River control and land disputes
properties.Description: In March 1958 violent clashes begin between India and Pakistan on the East Pakistan-Assam border in a conflict over ownership of fertile land along the Surma River, and a dispute over claims over control of the river. A demand from Assam that Pakistan abandon claims over any portion of the Surma were considered by Pakistan to be military provocation.

properties.Title: Syria clashes with Israel over Huleh canal project
properties.Description: Over a series of days in late March 1958, Syria clashes with Israel in Lake Huleh area over Israel's work to dig a canal to drain water from the marshes.

properties.Title: India and Pakistan fight over Surma River control and land disputes in a second round of clashes in May 1958
properties.Description: In May 1958 clashes reoccur between India and Pakistan on the East Pakistan-Assam border in a conflict over the Surma River, and a dispute over claims over control of the river.

properties.Title: A third set of clashes between India and Pakistan over Surma River control occurs in August 1958
properties.Description: In August 1958 a third round of clashes occur between India and Pakistan on the East Pakistan-Assam border in a conflict over ownership of fertile land along the Surma River, and a dispute over claims over control of the river.

properties.Title: Israel and Syria clash over water diversions from Huleh swamp area
properties.Description: In November and December 1958, clashes restart between Israel and Syria over irrigation, farmland, and water diversions in the Huleh Swamp area, with intervention by UN peacekeepers.

properties.Title: US bombs irrigation systems in Vietnam
properties.Description: Irrigation water supply systems in North Vietnam are bombed during the Vietnam War. An estimated 661 sections of dikes damaged or destroyed.

properties.Title: Violent dispute over access to irrigation water in the Al-Chibayish marshes of Iraq during drought
properties.Description: Disputes in the Al-Chibayish Marshes, part of Dhi Qar and Basrah governorates, escalate to armed fighting between two clans in the 1960s. One report describes a bloody dispute between the two clans over the allocation of irrigation water for rice farms during droughts.

properties.Title: Brazil and Paraguay clash over Paraná River
properties.Description: Negotiations between Brazil and Paraguay over the development of the Paraná River are interrupted by a unilateral show of military force by Brazil in 1962, which invades the area and claims control over the Guadalajara Falls site. Military forces are withdrawn in 1967 following an agreement for a joint commission to examine development in the region.

properties.Title: Israel destroys irrigation ditches in demilitarized zone on Syrian border
properties.Description: Israel destroys irrigation ditches in the lower Tarfiq in the demilitarized zone. Syria complains.

properties.Title: Kaptai Dam displaces Jumma people in Eastern Pakistan
properties.Description: In 1957, Eastern Pakistan (present-day Bangladesh) begins building the Kaptai Hydroelectric Dam on the Karnaphuli River in the Chittagong Hills Tract in the southeast. Completed in 1962, the dam flooded 655 square kilometers of land and displaced 100,000 people, most of them Jumma, or ethnic minorities in a country ruled by a Bengali majority. The displaced Jumma are offered little land or compens

properties.Title: Ethiopia and Somali nomads fight for desert water
properties.Description: Creation of boundaries in 1948 leaves Somali nomads under Ethiopian rule; border skirmishes occur over disputed territory in Ogaden desert where critical water and oil resources are located; cease-fire is negotiated only after several hundred are killed.

properties.Title: A bomb is used to attack a Welsh dam to protest the flooding of a village
properties.Description: In February 1963, a bomb is used by the Mudiad Amddiffyn Cymru (Movement for the Defence of Wales) to attack an electricity transformer at a dam about to flood the village of Capel Celyn, Wales.

properties.Title: Israel drops bombs in a dispute over the source of the Dan River
properties.Description: Headwaters of the Dan River on the Jordan River are bombed at Tell El-Qadi in a dispute about sovereignty over the source of the Dan.

properties.Title: Cuba cuts off water to US at Guantanamo
properties.Description: On February 6, 1964, the Cuban government cuts off the normal water supply to the US Naval Base at Guantanamo Bay, in retaliation for the US seizure of four Cuban fishing boats days earlier near Florida.

properties.Title: British defend South African dam against possible saboteurs
properties.Description: President Kenneth Kaunda of Zambia calls on British government to send troops to Kariba Dam to protect it from possible saboteurs from Rhodesian government.

properties.Title: Feud over the Buffalo National River turns violent between environmentalists and local populations
properties.Description: Feuding between two groups over the fate of the Buffalo National River in Arkansas and a potential dam project turn violent when a canoe race on Memorial Day, 1965 is disrupted by barbed wire strung across the river and gun shots near the canoes. Small injuries caused by the barbed wire to canoeists are reported.

properties.Title: Israel attacks over an Arab plan to divert headwaters of the Jordan River away from Israel
properties.Description: Fire is exchanged over "all-Arab" plan to divert the Jordan River headwaters (Hasbani and Banias) and presumably preempt Israeli National Water Carrier; Syria halts construction of its diversion in July 1966.

properties.Title: Palestinians attack Israeli water pumps for the National Water Carrier
properties.Description: First attack ever by the Palestinian National Liberation Movement Al-Fatah is on the diversion pumps for the Israeli National Water Carrier. Attack fails.

properties.Title: Brazil and Paraguay military have a confrontation over the Paraná River
properties.Description: On June 17, 1965 Brazil sends a military detachment to occupy land along the Paraná River in order to make a claim on disputed borders between Brazil and Paraguay. This presence of Brazil’s military is considered a major "act of aggresion" and a violation of their territorial sovereignty.

properties.Title: In a continuation of the dispute between Brazil and Paraguay over the Paraná River, a Brazilian military detachment makes arrest
properties.Description: Paraguay sends a military and diplomatic group to contested land on the border with Brazil. A Brazilian military detachment arrests them in a confrontation. This dispute precedes the construction of the major Itaipu Dam on the Paraná River.

properties.Title: Violent protest in Paraguay over Brazilian occupation of land along the Paraná River and control of the river
properties.Description: In a growing dispute over the control of the Paraná River between Paraguay and Brazil, Paraguayan protestors burn a Brazilian flag near the Brazilian embassy, and throw Molotov cocktails into Brazilian-owned businesses chanting ‘Paraguay sí. Bandeirantes no. Fuera los mamelucos’ (‘Paraguay yes, invaders no:out with the bastards’). Police disperse the crowd violently and arrest 15.

properties.Title: US attempts to flood land to slow Vietnamese military efforts
properties.Description: The US military, in 'Operation Popeye,' uses silver iodide for cloud seeding over Indochina (Vietnam), in an attempt to extend the monsoon season and stop the flow of materiel along Ho Chi Minh trail. "Continuous rainfall was intended to slow down the truck traffic and was relatively successful."

properties.Title: Israel attacks Jordan water works on the Banias
properties.Description: Israel destroys the Arab diversion works on the Jordan River headwaters. During Arab-Israeli War Israel occupies Golan Heights, with Banias tributary to the Jordan; Israel occupies West Bank.
properties.Sources: Gleick 1993; Wolf 1995; Wolf 1997; Wallenstein and Swain 1997

properties.Title: Palestinians attack civilian targets in Israel, destroying water pumps and irrigation equipment
properties.Description: In the spring of 1967, Palestinian groups attack civilian targets in Israel, including destroying water pumps and irrigation equipment in the north of the country.

properties.Title: Kuwait and Iraq fight over border dispute, destroying water wells
properties.Description: Kuwait and Iraq move troops to their frontier and recall their ambassadors after Iraqi border guards cross the frontier, destroy a tent camp of workers drilling water wells, and briefly occupy the area, according to reports from both countries.

properties.Title: Youth International Party threatens to spike Chicago's water supply with LSD ahead of Democratic National Convention
properties.Description: The Youth International Party in the United States threatens to spike Lake Michigan with the hallucinogenic LSD. Lake Michigan serves as the city of Chicago's drinking water supply. The threat is targeted at the Democratic National Convention of 1968. No known poisonings occur.
properties.Sources: Mohtadi and Murshid 2006; CBC News Online 2004

properties.Title: Palestinian group attempts to pollute Israeli water with attack on an oil pipeline
properties.Description: The Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP) places an explosive device on the Trans-Arabian Pipeline in Jordan in an attempt to pollute water that provides drinking water and water for fisheries to Israel in the Hutch Valley. While the explosive device did cause significant damage to the pipeline, reports do not indicate that water supplies were affected.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; Heradstveit 2017

properties.Title: There are multiple attacks on Belfast's water supply system, Northern Ireland
properties.Description: Four separate explosions occur in April, 1969, damaging water-supply infrastructure in Northern Ireland. The first explosion occurs at a water installation serving Belfast at Dunadry, County Antrim. The second occurs at Silent Valley reservoir in County Down and temporarily interrupts water supply for Belfast. The third occurs a few days later at a water pipeline between Lugh Neagh and Belfast. Th

properties.Title: Israel attacks East Ghor Canal, June 1969
properties.Description: On June 5, 1969, Israel attacks the East Ghor Canal on the eastern bank of the Jordan River in reprisal for the shelling of Israeli border settlement and in an effort to pressure Jordan to prevent Palestinian attacks in Israel from Jordanian territory.

properties.Title: China and Soviet Union fight over navigation and control of the Amur River as part of disputes over shared waterways
properties.Description: An armed conflict between China and the Soviet Union breaks out over navigation and control of the Amur River in July 1969, part of an ongoing series of disputes over shared waterways.

properties.Title: Israel attacks East Ghor Canal, August 1969
properties.Description: On August 10, 1969 Israel again attacks the East Ghor Canal on the eastern bank of the Jordan River in reprisal for the shelling of Israeli border settlement and to pressure Jordan to stop Palestinian attacks in Israel from Jordanian territory.

properties.Title: Egyptian commandos destroy water pipeline in Sinai desert north of Ismalia
properties.Description: Egypt claims to carry out four sabotage forays against Israeli positions in the Sinai Desert. Among other objectives, the commandos said they destroyed observation posts and blew up a water pipeline 10 miles north of Ismalia.

properties.Title: Alleged plot to contaminate US water supply
properties.Description: The Weathermen, a group opposed to American imperialism and the Vietnam War, allegedly attempts to obtain biological agents to contaminate the water supply systems of US urban centers.
properties.Sources: Kupperman and Trent 1979; Eitzen and Takafuji 1997; Purver 1995

properties.Title: Ku Klux Klan poisons livestock water supply owned by black Muslim group in Alabama, US
properties.Description: Members of the Ku Klux Klan poison the water supply used for livestock on a farm owned by black Muslims in Ashville, Alabama. 30 cows are killed and at least nine others are sickened.

properties.Title: Neo-Nazis drop bombs in Maryland sewage plant
properties.Description: A group of teenagers associated with a neo-Nazi group attempt to spark racial conflict by dropping bombs into a sewage pumping station in Maryland, USA, an event they hope will be blamed on African Americans. Damage is caused to the pumping station.
properties.Sources: Baltimore Sun 1970; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 197010020004

properties.Title: Pipeline bombed during a labor dispute in California
properties.Description: During a labor dispute, a pipeline carrying brine to the American Potash Plant in Trona, CA, is bombed, causing destruction of the pipeline and temporarily disrupting the town water supply.
properties.Sources: Committee on Government Operations United States Senate (CGOUSS) 1970; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Respon

properties.Title: Violent conflicts over use of China's Zhang River
properties.Description: Conflicts over excessive water withdrawals and subsequent water shortages from China's Zhang River have been worsening for over three decades between villages in Shenxian and Linzhou counties. In the 1970s, militias from competing villages fight over withdrawals. (See also entries for 1976, 1991, 1992, and 1999.)

properties.Title: Water-cooling unit is bombed at a Louisiana country club
properties.Description: A water cooling unit is bombed at a country club in Louisiana, USA. Suspects are thought to be black nationalists seeking revenge. No casualties are reported.
properties.Sources: Committee on Government Operations United States Senate (CGOUSS) 1970; New York Times, The 1970; Washington Post 1970; National

properties.Title: Israel attacks East Ghor Canal, January 1970
properties.Description: On January 1, 1970, Israel again attacks the East Ghor Canal to pressure Jordan to prevent Palestinian attacks in Israel from Jordanian territory.

properties.Title: Britain expels Soviet agents reportedly planning to poison London's water supply
properties.Description: Britain expels 105 Soviet agents in 1971 after learning that plans existed for those agents to sabotage or disrupt London's water supply system by poisoning.

properties.Title: US bombs water works in Vietnam
properties.Description: United States bombs dikes in the Red River delta, rivers, and canals during massive bombing campaign.

properties.Title: Attempt to poison Chicago's waters
properties.Description: Two members of the right-wing "Order of the Rising Sun" are arrested in Chicago with 30-40 kg of typhoid cultures with which they allegedly planned to poison the water supply in Chicago, St. Louis, and other cities. Experts say the plan is unlikely to cause health problems due to water chlorination.

properties.Title: New York water threatened
properties.Description: Reported threat to contaminate water supply of New York City with nerve gas.

properties.Title: German water supply threatened
properties.Description: A German biologist threatens to contaminate water supplies with bacilli of anthrax and botulinum toxin unless he is paid $8.5 million.
properties.Sources: Jenkins and Rubin 1978; Kupperman and Trent 1979

properties.Title: Iraq threatens Syria dam
properties.Description: Iraq threatens to bomb the Euphrates dam (also known as al-Thawra or Tabqa dam) in Syria and masses troops along the border, alleging that the dam had reduced the flow of Euphrates River water to Iraq.

properties.Title: Multiple attacks on water infrastructure in Puerto Rico
properties.Description: Striking workers are thought to be the cause of multiple attacks on various water infrastructure run by the Puerto Rico Aqueduct and Sewers Authority, a government-owned water agency. One attack destroys water pipes connected to two major refineries, while another leaves a large portion of the City of San Juan without running water for an entire day. The attacks are attributed to a group known as
properties.Sources: New York Times, The 1974; Hewitt 2005; Sater 1981; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (ST

properties.Title: Iraq, Syria mobilize troops over drought tensions
properties.Description: As upstream dams are filled during a low-flow year on the Euphrates, Iraqis claim that flow reaching its territory is "intolerable" and asks the Arab League to intervene. Syrians claim they are receiving less than half the river's normal flow and pull out of an Arab League technical committee formed to mediate the conflict. In May Syria closes its airspace to Iraqi flights and both Syrian and Iraq

properties.Title: South Africa takes over Angolan dam
properties.Description: South African troops move into Angola to occupy and defend the Ruacana hydropower complex, including the Gove Dam on the Kunene River. Goal is to take possession of and defend the water resources of southwestern Africa and Namibia.

properties.Title: Locks and control devices on a Department of Water and Power control gate on a river in California are sabotaged in May 1975
properties.Description: Locks and control devices on a Department of Water and Power control gate on the Owens River, near Bishop, California are sabotaged during a dispute over groundwater pumping.

properties.Title: Powerline insulators to Department of Water and Power water pumps on a river in California are shot out in September 1975
properties.Description: Insulators on a power line to the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power water pumps on the Owens River, California are shot out in an ongoing dispute over groundwater pumping by the city of Los Angeles.

properties.Title: Bacteriological agent used to poison groundwater and surface water in Mozambique
properties.Description: An unidentified bacteriological agent is found in groundwater and surface water sources in Mozambique. Sources report that the Rhodesian government had deposited the agent to target the black population in the country.

properties.Title: Gates diverting water to the Los Angeles Aqueduct from the Owens Valley are dynamited
properties.Description: Gates diverting water to the Los Angeles Aqueduct from the Owens Valley are dynamited, leading to 100 million gallons of water flows returning to Owens Lake.

properties.Title: Militia chief shot in China's Zhang River conflict
properties.Description: A local militia chief is shot to death in a clash over the damming of Zhang River. Conflicts over excessive water withdrawals and subsequent water shortages from China's Zhang River have been worsening for over three decades. (See also entries for 1970, 1991, 1992, 1999.)

properties.Title: Los Angeles, California, USA aqueduct gates are opened by a blow torch to fill Diaz Lake for recreational use
properties.Description: Los Angeles aqueduct gates are opened by unknown persons using a blow torch in order to fill Diaz Lake for recreational use.

properties.Title: Explosions damage water system at British military base
properties.Description: Four separate explosions occur at several British military bases, one damaging the water system at Akrotiri (Santorini), an airbase near Limassol, in southern Cyprus, Greece.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: North Carolina reservoir poisoned
properties.Description: Contamination of a North Carolina reservoir with unknown materials. According to Clark: "Safety caps and valves were removed, and poison chemicals were sent into the reservoir....Water had to be brought in."

properties.Title: Kariba dam on the Zambezi river is site of battle between Rhodesian and Zambian forces
properties.Description: Rhodesian and Zambian forces fight a 10?hour battle across the Zambezi River and a power station at the Kariba dam is attacked.

properties.Title: A water tank is poisoned in protest of airport construction in Japan
properties.Description: A water tank at a water purification plant is contaminated with oil and agricultural chemicals. The attack on the water supplies is in protest of the construction of an airport in Hokusol/Narita, Japan.

properties.Title: Bombing of water plant in Puerto Rico cuts off water for 30,000
properties.Description: Puerto Rican nationalists are suspected in the bombing of a substation owned by Water Resource Authority. This incident leaves approximately 30,000 people in San Juan without electricity. This bombing is linked to a larger group of violent attacks on the state-owned Water Resources Authority.
properties.Sources: Sater 1981; Hewitt 2005; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 197801190007

properties.Title: Deaths of protestors and damage to construction of the Jonglei canal in Sudan
properties.Description: Demonstrations in Juba, Sudan in 1978 opposing the construction of the Jonglei Canal lead to the deaths of two students. In 1984, construction of the canal is suspended following a series of attacks on the construction site.

properties.Title: Machine gun used to attack the U.S. Embassy with goal of reducing cost of water and other necessary resources
properties.Description: The Popular Liberation Forces (PLF) uses a machine gun to attack the U.S. Embassy in El Salvador. This attack was stated by PLF to be part of their war against U.S. imperialism. Demands included the reduction in the cost of water, as well the cost of housing, fertilizer, rents, agricultural land, and electric power, as well as for the release of a number of prisoners assumed to be held for politic

properties.Title: While battling insurgents, the Ethiopian military destroys basic civilian water wells and water sources
properties.Description: During the so-called "Secret Wars" in southeast Ethiopia, water wells are destroyed and poisoned by Ethiopian troops in an attempt to halt insurgents. The Ethiopian military is thought to have been advised in applying these tactics by Soviet and Cuban officials.

properties.Title: Equipment providing power to Los Angeles Department of Water and Power pumps on a river in California, USA is damaged
properties.Description: Electrical insulators are shot and damaged at a Southern California Edison site providing power to Los Angeles Department of Water and Power water pumps on the Owens River, California.

properties.Title: Several different facilities owned by the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power are burned down, USA
properties.Description: A shack and platform at the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power well between Big Pine and Independence is burned down along with a house, barns, and shop at DWR land in Lone Pine, California.

properties.Title: Attempted extortion of a casino in Lake Tahoe, Nevada, USA with threat to poison water
properties.Description: Attempted extortion of a casino in Lake Tahoe, Nevada with threat to poison water.
properties.Sources: New Jersey Office of Homeland Security and Preparedness 2009

properties.Title: Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, US water mains are deliberately contaminated with weed killer
properties.Description: Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, United States water mains are deliberately contaminated with weed killer.
properties.Sources: New Jersey Office of Homeland Security and Preparedness 2009

properties.Title: Government troops demolish water catchments in Somalia
properties.Description: Between 1980 and 1982, as part of President Barre's "scorched earth" policy in Somalia, government troops demolish water-points and berkads (catchments) in many of the towns and villages of Mudug and neighboring Nugal.

properties.Title: Military encounters between Cameroon and Niger over the recession of Lake Chad
properties.Description: Starting in the 1980s, rapid recession of Lake Chad draws Nigerian fishermen farther into Cameroonian territory, leading to several military encounters. By the 1990s, more than thirty Lake Chad villages founded by Nigerians are counted in Cameroon. In 2002, the border dispute goes to the International Court of Justice, which rules in Cameroon's favor.

properties.Title: Mozambique targets Cahora Bassa Dam in fight for independence
properties.Description: Regular destruction of power lines from Cahora Bassa Dam during fight for independence in the region. Dam targeted by RENAMO (the Mozambican National Resistance).

properties.Title: Soviet forces in Afghanistan destroy crops, irrigation sysems, and wells
properties.Description: In July 1980 Soviet forces destroyed dozens of villages south of Kabul during a two-week operation that included destruction of crops, irrigation systems, livestock, and wells.

properties.Title: Thai soldiers capture two Vietnamese trying to poison the water supply in a Kampuchean relocation camp in Thailand
properties.Description: In June 1981, Thai soldiers capture two Vietnamese trying to poison the water supply in a Kampuchean relocation camp in Thailand.

properties.Title: Iran targets Iraq hydroelectric dam
properties.Description: Iran claims to have bombed a hydroelectric facility in Kurdistan, thereby blacking out large portions of Iraq, during the Iran-Iraq War.

properties.Title: Namibia targets Angola pipeline
properties.Description: Water infrastructure, including dams and the major Kunene-Cuvelai pipeline, is targeted during the conflicts in Namibia and Angola in the 1980s.

properties.Title: A Thai citizen dies from poison placed in drinking water by Vietnamese troops near the Kampuchean border
properties.Description: In March 1981, a Thai citizen dies from poison placed in drinking water by Vietnamese troops near the Kampuchean border; others are sickened.

properties.Title: Los Angeles Department of Water and Power in California, USA has equipment destroyed by arson
properties.Description: Los Angeles Department of Water and Power equipment, a frontloader, is destroyed by arson.

properties.Title: Bomb attack launched on Severn Trent Water Authority in protest over water taken from Welsh reservoirs
properties.Description: A bombing at the Birmingham headquarters of the Severn Trent Water Authority is claimed by the Workers Army of the Welsh Republic, as part of a demand the water agency pay more for water taken from Welsh reservoirs.

properties.Title: Guatemalan forces more than 400 indigenous community members resisting forced migration from a new dam
properties.Description: In Rio Negro, Guatemala, between February 1982 and 1983, approximately 440 Maya Achi indigenous people, including hundreds of women and children, are killed by the Guatemalan Army and Civil Self-Defense Patrols in an effort to remove the community from an area slated to be flooded by the Chixoy dam. There are reports that the victims were tourtured and raped, as well. These killing became known as
properties.Sources: Levy 2000; Comisión para el Esclarecimiento Histórico 1999; Wikipedia contributors 2025c

properties.Title: Israel cuts off Beirut water
properties.Description: Israel cuts off the water supply of Beirut during siege.

properties.Title: Plan to poison Los Angeles water supply
properties.Description: Los Angeles police and the FBI arrest a man who was preparing to poison the city's water supply with a biological agent.

properties.Title: Terrorists destroy US military barracks in Lebanon
properties.Description: An explosives-laden truck disguised as a water-delivery vehicle destroys a barracks in a US military compound, killing more than 300 people. The attack is blamed on Hezbollah with the support of the Iranian government.

properties.Title: Terrorists plot to poison Israel water
properties.Description: The Israeli government reports that it had uncovered a plot by Israeli Arabs to poison the water in Galilee with "an unidentified powder."

properties.Title: An attempt was made to poison the water supply in Louisiana, United States with cyanide
properties.Description: An attempt was made to poison the water supply in Louisiana, United States with cyanide.
properties.Sources: New Jersey Office of Homeland Security and Preparedness 2009

properties.Title: Water truck bombed in front of U.S. Embassy in Amman, Jordan
