properties.Description: A water truck is destroyed when a bomb placed underneath it explodes. The truck was outside the U.S. Embassy in Amman, Jordan. Two people are injured.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; US Secretary of State 1986

properties.Title: Destruction of construction equipment at Atatürk Dam
properties.Description: During construction of the Atatürk Dam, the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK) destroys construction equipment and nearly 1,100 vehicles, severely delaying the project.

properties.Title: Oregon water supply poisoned
properties.Description: Members of the Rajneesh religious cult contaminate a city water supply tank in The Dalles, Oregon, using Salmonella. A community outbreak of over 750 cases occurred in a county that normally reports fewer than five cases per year.

properties.Title: A Los Angeles Department of Water and Power boat explodes under suspicious circumstances in California, USA
properties.Description: A Los Angeles Department of Water and Power boat on Mono Lake explodes under suspicious circumstances in continued violence over water diversions from the Owens Valley.

properties.Title: Anonymous note threatens to contaminate New York City reservoirs
properties.Description: An anonymous letter writer threatens to contaminate New York City's water reservoirs with plutonium unless charges against a man accused of vigilantism for shooting four African American men in the New York subway are dropped. Testing detects minute levels of plutonium in the water but scientists cannot determine whether the threat to contaminate the New York City water supply was actually carried

properties.Title: Cult plans to poison US waters
properties.Description: Law enforcement authorities discover that a small survivalist group in the Ozark Mountains of Arkansas known as The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord (CSA) acquired a drum containing 30 gallons of potassium cyanide, with the apparent intent to poison water supplies in New York, Chicago, and Washington, D.C. CSA members believe that such attacks would make the Messiah return more quickly

properties.Title: A plan to blow up the Atatürk Dam in Turkey is thwarted
properties.Description: Turkish police discover Syrian-backed terrorists designing a plan to blow up the Atatürk Dam.

properties.Title: Lesotho coup supported by South Africa, partly to advance water agreement
properties.Description: South Africa supports a bloodless coup by Lesotho's defense forces. Immediately afterward, the two countries agree to share water from the Highlands of Lesotho, following 30 years of unsuccessful negotiations. There is disagreement over the degree to which water was a motivating factor for either party.
properties.Sources: Inventory of Conflict and the Environment 2000b; Mohamed 2001

properties.Title: South Koreans uneasy over threat posed by North Korean dam plan
properties.Description: North Korea's announcement of its plans to build the Kumgansan hydroelectric dam on a tributary of the Han River upstream of Seoul raises concerns in South Korea that the dam could be used as a tool for ecological destruction or war.

properties.Title: 19 police recruits in the Philippines die from poisoned water and candy from an unknown source
properties.Description: Nineteen Filipino police recruits die after drinking and eating water and candy from an unknown person.

properties.Title: At least 12 police die in the Philippines after drinking poisoned water during a running race
properties.Description: At least 12 and up to 19 runners die after drinking poisoned bottled water during a race. The runners were members of the Philippine Constabulary.

properties.Title: Lack of water from drought contributes to onset of the Arab-Fur War.
properties.Description: The Arab-Fur War (1987-1989) between the Arab and Fur ethnic groups in Libya, is triggered in part by severe drought that strains farmer-herder relationships. Libyan leader Gaddafi sends drought relief supplies and weaponry to opposing sides.

properties.Title: A water tank is bombed in Pakistan, injuring 15
properties.Description: A bomb placed under a water tank explodes, injuring 15 people in Peshawar, Pakistan.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Cubans attack the Calueque Dam in Angola, occupied by South African forces, during the Angolan civil war
properties.Description: Cuban and Angolan forces launch an attack on Calueque Dam, occupied by South African forces, via land and then air. Considerable damage inflicted on dam wall; the power supply from the dam is cut. Water pipeline to Ovamboland is cut and destroyed.

properties.Title: A bomb is discovered near a water pumping station in Israel
properties.Description: A large amount of dynamite is discovered near a water pumping station near Moshav Ramon, Israel prior to detonation. The detonation would have likely caused flooding and damage to agricultural land.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Alleged attempt to infect the water supply to a refugee camp with biological agents in Namibia
properties.Description: The Civilian Cooperation Bureau, a covert South African unit, is accused of an attempt to infect the water supply of a refugee camp in Dabora, Namibia, with cholera and yellow fever. The attempt is thought to fail due to the high chlorine content in the water.

properties.Title: Water and other ecological threats used as justification for attack on cattle company
properties.Description: A fire is set in a cattle company building outside of Sacramento, California, causing hundreds of thousands of dollars in damage. An unidentified caller claiming to be part of Earth First! states he set the fire "because we are opposed to the livestock industry because it causes irreparable damage to the environment by putting chemicals in food and water and destroying natural habitat for wildlife

properties.Title: South Africa cuts off black community's water
properties.Description: Pro-apartheid council cuts off water to the Wesselton township of 50,000 blacks following protests over miserable sanitation and living conditions.

properties.Title: Violence over water competition kills 300 along Uzbekistan border
properties.Description: The Fergana Valley, shared by Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, and Uzbekistan, is especially vulnerable to violent eruptions over water and ethnicity. In 1990 an outbreak of violence in the Kyrgyz town of Osh on the border with Uzbekistan claims over 300 lives. The violence is provoked by competition for water, limited arable land, and ethnic tensions.

properties.Title: Attacks on energy systems in Iraq leaves cities without water
properties.Description: During the Gulf War, targeted attacks on transformers and turbines at water-treatment plants leave whole cities, such as Basra, without water or wastewater treatment. And due to embargoes, parts needed to fix the plants are not available. It is estimated that at least 25 percent of water-treatment plants in Iraq do not have backup power supplies and are inoperable after electrical grids are damage

properties.Title: Flows of water from Turkey's Atatürk Dam are threatened and used as a weapon of war
properties.Description: The flow of the Euphrates is interrupted for a month as Turkey finishes construction of the Atatürk Dam, part of the Grand Anatolia Project. Syria and Iraq protest that Turkey now has a weapon of war. In mid-1990 Turkish president Turgut Özal threatens to restrict water flow to Syria to force it to withdraw support for Kurdish rebels operating in southern Turkey.

properties.Title: Police report nine people are sickened by the intentional contamination of the water supply of a private apartment building in S
properties.Description: At least nine people in an apartment building in Edinburgh, Scotland report coming down with a diarrheal disease caused by Giardia lamblia. Investigators of the incident believe that the water supply to the apartment had been deliberately contaminated with fecal matter containing Giardia.

properties.Title: 18 killed in Bengaluru, India after court order telling Karnataka to release water to Tamil Nadu on the Cauvery River
properties.Description: At least 18 people are killed in Bengaluru, India after a court order telling Karnataka to release water from the Cauvery River to Tamil Nadu sparked riots.

properties.Title: British Columbia water supply threatened
properties.Description: A threat is made via an anonymous letter to contaminate the water supply of the city of Kelowna, British Columbia, with "biological contaminates." The motive is apparently "associated with the Gulf War." The security of the water supply is increased in response and no group is identified as the perpetrator.

properties.Title: Chinese villages exchange mortar fire over water diversion
properties.Description: In December 1991, Huanglongkou village and Qianyu village exchange mortar fire over the construction of new water diversion facilities. Conflicts over excessive water withdrawals and subsequent water shortages from China's Zhang River have been worsening for over three decades. (See also entries for 1970, 1976, 1992, and 1999.)

properties.Title: During Persian Gulf War, allied forces attack and destroy urban water and sanitation facilities in Basra, Iraq
properties.Description: During the Persian Gulf War, Allied forces target water and sanitation facilities in Iraq's second-largest city, Basra.
properties.Sources: Vulliamy 1991; Middle East Watch (MEW) 1991a; Middle East Watch (MEW) 1991b

properties.Title: Iraq destroys Kuwait desalination plants
properties.Description: During the Gulf War, Iraq destroys much of Kuwait's desalination capacity during retreat.

properties.Title: UN considers cutting river flow to Iraq
properties.Description: Discussions are held at the United Nations about using the Atatürk Dam in Turkey to cut off flows of the Euphrates to Iraq.

properties.Title: US destroys Iraq water systems during the 1991 Persian Gulf War
properties.Description: During the Persian Gulf War, Allied Coalition forces damage Baghdad's modern water supply and sanitation system "intentionally and unintentionally." Four of seven major pumping stations were destroyed, as were 31 municipal water and sewerage facilities in Baghdad "resulting in sewage pouring into the Tigris. Water purification plants were incapacitated throughout Iraq" (Arbuthnot 2000). Following

properties.Title: US sanctions against Iraq target water systems
properties.Description: United States deliberately pursues policies that prevent repair and operation of Iraq's water systems through sanctions and withholding contracts.

properties.Title: Violence erupts between Karnataka and Tamil Nadu over use of India's Cauvery River
properties.Description: Violence erupts when Karnataka, India rejects an Interim Order handed down by the Kaveri Waters Tribunal, set up by the Indian Supreme Court. The Tribunal was established in 1990 to settle two decades of dispute between Karnataka and Tamil Nadu over irrigation rights to the Kaveri River.
properties.Sources: Gleick 1993; Butts 1997; Inventory of Conflict and the Environment (ICE) 2000a

properties.Title: Russians attack Moldovan hydroelectric dam
properties.Description: In June, hostilities between Moldova and Russia in a short but intense conflict include a rocket-artillery attack on the hydroelectric turbines at the Dubasari power station on the Nistru (or Dniester) River.

properties.Title: Serbs cut off water and power to Bosnian cities
properties.Description: The Serbian siege of Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina, includes a cutoff of all electrical power and the water feeding the city from the surrounding mountains. The lack of power cuts the two main pumping stations inside the city despite pledges from Serbian nationalist leaders to United Nations officials that they would not use their control of Sarajevo's utilities as a weapon. Bosnian Serbs take

properties.Title: Violent raids target water infrastructure between Uzbekistan and Turkmenistan
properties.Description: The Tyuyamuyun reservoir in the delta of the Amu Darya is a point of contention between Uzbekistan and Turkmenistan. Violence occurs in 1992 over the re-direction of drainage waters and raids by both sides to cut off pipes and irrigation canals. Today, the Tyuyamuyun remains one of several disputed areas in continuing water dispute with Uzbekistan.

properties.Title: Water is used as a weapon in the dispute between Armenia and Azerbaijan
properties.Description: In a dispute over the Karabakh region, Armenia takes control of the Sarsang Reservoir in 1992. Subsequently, Azerbaijan claims Armenia is releasing water from Sarsang Reservoir to damage Azerbaijan territories with artificial floods and cutting off water for agricultural irrigation.

properties.Title: Water supply to a Turkish air force base is poisoned
properties.Description: Potassium cyanide is discovered in lethal quantities in the water supply of a Turkish air force base. No one is poisoned prior to the discovery. The attack is attributed to the Kurdish Workers' Party.

properties.Title: Hungary and Czechoslovakia dispute over Danube
properties.Description: Hungary abrogates a 1977 treaty with Czechoslovakia concerning construction of the Gabčíkovo-Nagymaros project based on environmental concerns. Slovakia continues construction unilaterally, completes the dam, and diverts the Danube into a canal inside the Slovakian republic. Massive public protest and movement of military to the border ensue; issue taken to the International Court of Justice.

properties.Title: Canal on China's Zhang River bombed
properties.Description: In August 1992, bombs are set off along a Zhang River distribution canal collapsing part of the canal and causing flooding and economic losses in a continuation of three decades of violence with confrontations, mortar attacks, and bombings. Conflicts over excessive water withdrawals and subsequent water shortages from China's Zhang River have been worsening. (See also entries for 1970, 1976, 1991,

properties.Title: A major dam on the Cetina River, Yugoslavia is attacked in the Croatian War
properties.Description: The 65-meter high Peru?a Dam on the Cetina River was Yugoslavia's second-largest hydroelectric facility before the country's breakup with the Croatian War beginning in 1991. On January 28, 1993, Serbian/Yugoslav army forces detonate explosives at the dam in an attempt to wipe out Croatian villages and the port city of Omi'. A successful Croatian counter-attack allows military engineers to reach th

properties.Title: Iran threatens West's water
properties.Description: A report suggests that proposals were made at a meeting of fundamentalist groups in Tehran, under the auspices of the Iranian Foreign Ministry, to poison water supplies of major cities in the West "as a possible response to Western offensives against Islamic organizations and states."

properties.Title: Iraq uses water to silence opposition
properties.Description: To quell opposition to his government, Saddam Hussein reportedly poisons and drains the water supplies of southern Shiite Muslims, the Marsh Arabs, or Ma'dan. The marshes of southern Iraq are intentionally targeted. The European Parliament and UN Human Rights Commission deplore use of water as weapon in region.

properties.Title: Indonesian security forces kill four villagers protesting the construction of Nipah Dam, Madura Island, Indonesia
properties.Description: Indonesian security forces fire on villagers protesting the construction of Nipah Dam in Sampang Regency, in Madura Island of Indonesia's East Java province, September 25, 1993, killing four.

properties.Title: Violence occurs over land use of a water catchment area in Kenya
properties.Description: In October 1993, violence erupts in the Narok district of Kenya's Rift Valley province. Maasai warriors attack immigrant Kikuyu settlers and massacre at least 17 of them, after Kikuyi settlers allegedly mutilated Maasai cattle. 16 more Kikuyus are killed in other parts of Narok and the rest of the Kikuyu population is forced out of the area and into refugee camps. Environmental concerns played a c

properties.Title: Moldova threatens Russian army's water
properties.Description: Reported threat by Moldovian General Nikolay Matveyev to contaminate the water supply of the Russian 14th Army in Tiraspol, Moldova with mercury.

properties.Title: Ecuador, Peru fight over Cenepa River
properties.Description: Armed skirmishes arise between Ecuador and Peru in part because of a border disagreement over the control of the headwaters of Cenepa River.

properties.Title: Orma and Degoodi clans wage war over scarce resources in Kenya
properties.Description: The Orma and Degoodi clans in Kenya wage war over grazing land and water due to a severe drought, with 10 fatalities.

properties.Title: Clash between police and students protesting the lack of water on campus in Nigeria, injuring three
properties.Description: Three students are seriously wounded in a clash with police at the University of Ibadan in Nigeria. Police are preventing students from protesting the lack of water and electricity on campus.

properties.Title: Malaysia threatens to cut Singapore's water
properties.Description: Malaysia supplies about half of Singapore's water and in 1997 threatened to cut off that supply in retribution for criticisms by Singapore of policy in Malaysia.

properties.Title: Protest against water prices in Mauritania
properties.Description: The Mauritania government breaks up protests of water and electricity prices.

properties.Title: Protests against plans to privatize water and sanitation services in South Africa
properties.Description: Congress of South African Trade Unions, South African Municipal Workers Union and the South African Communist Party marched on the Nelspruit council to protest against plans to privatize water and sanitation services.

properties.Title: Uzbekistan cuts downstream flow to Kazakhstan, prompting a riot by farmers
properties.Description: Uzbekistan raises regional tensions by cutting off 70 percent of flow downstream, threatening 100,000 hectares and prompting a riot by Kazakh farmers.

properties.Title: Uzbekistani deploy troops to guard reservoirs along border with Kyrgyzstan
properties.Description: Continued serious water tensions between Kyrgyzstan and Uzbekistan lead to the deployment of 130,000 Uzbekistani troops on the Kyrgyz border to guard reservoirs straddling the two countries. Uzbekistan accuses Kyrgyzstan of releasing too much water from the Toktogul reservoir. Kyrgyzstan, through media leak, hints that in case the reservoir would be blown up, the resulting flood would sweep away U

properties.Title: Waterworks are sabotaged by armed groups in Mozambique
properties.Description: In Mozambique, unidentified armed groups sabotage waterworks and burn houses of civilians.

properties.Title: Bangladesh Nationalist Party strikes spark violence; demand to scrap Ganges River water-sharing treaty with India
properties.Description: General strikes called by Bangladesh's main opposition party, the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP), lead to sporadic violence with several injured on both sides, barricaded roads and rail tracks, damage to railway facilities, and stones thrown at trains. Among the key concerns are recent decisions concerning India, including an agreement on a landmark 30-year water-sharing treaty on the Ganges R

properties.Title: Abkhazia cuts energy supplies to Georgia from the Inguri dam
properties.Description: Energy supplies to Georgia from the Inguri hydroelectric plant are cut by Abkhazia (a disputed republic at the intersection of Eastern Europe and Western Asia) in retaliation for Georgia rerouting telephone lines from Abkhazia through Georgia. The dam and power plant are situated at the shared border with the breakaway Republic of Abkhazia. Negotiations between Georgia authorities and Abkhazia als

properties.Title: Angolan rebels, government fight at dam
properties.Description: In September 1998, fierce fighting between UNITA and Angolan government forces broke out at Gove Dam on the Kunene River for control of the installation.

properties.Title: Attack on water system in Algeria
properties.Description: A bomb placed by a group suspected to be affiliated with an extremist Islamic insurgency detonates near the Sidi Khaled water tower, Algeria, damaging a two-meter section of pipe. Four other similar attacks occur in Hassi R'mel, Ouled, Tolba, and Arzew.
properties.Sources: A.B. 1998; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 199802200004; RAND Databa

properties.Title: Clashes between rival tribes over water in Darfur, Sudan
properties.Description: Clashes between rival tribes (Rezaigat and Massalit) over water and grazing rights leave more than 100 people dead and about 45 villages gutted in West Darfur, Sudan.

properties.Title: Congo rebels attack dam
properties.Description: Attacks on Inga Dam during efforts to topple President Kabila. Disruption of electricity supplies from Inga Dam and water supplies to Kinshasa, Congo.

properties.Title: Four people are hospitalized after drinking poisoned water in Guinea
properties.Description: Four people are hospitalized after drinking water poisoned with acid and sold at the 28 September Stadium in Guinea, where the President held a final campaign rally.

properties.Title: Guerillas threaten Tajikistan dam
properties.Description: Tajik guerrilla commander Makhmud Khudoberdyev threatens to blow up a dam on the Kairakum channel if his political demands are not met.

properties.Title: Major Puerto Rican aqueduct project is attacked
properties.Description: A group called the Boricua Popular Army (Macheteros) claims responsibility for the bombing of a major aqueduct construction project in Puerto Rico, stating the attack is the initiation of an effort to defend Puerto Rico's natural resources from the government. The explosion causes significant damage to the project.
properties.Sources: US Department of Justice 1998; Associated Press (AP) 1999a; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terr

properties.Title: Militant attacks on water supplies leave one dead and many villages in the Abkhazia region of Georgia without water
properties.Description: Over the course of a few days, militant groups in the Abkhazia region of Georgia attack and damage numerous water-supply structures, leaving at least twelve villages without water. In addition, one person who is thought to be responsible for the placement of an explosive device on a water tower is killed when the device detonates.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Oil pipeline dynamited in Colombia, contaminating drinking water source for 200 families
properties.Description: The Caño Limón – Coveñas oil pipeline owned by Colombia Petroleum Enterprise is dynamited, causing oil to spill into the La Gritane stream, which supplies drinking water to approximately 200 families in rural Colombia .
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: War between Eritrea and Ethiopia includes destruction of water systems
properties.Description: During the war with Ethiopia in 1998, water-supply systems in the mountainous region of Debub and Gash Barka in Eritrea are destroyed.
properties.Sources: International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) 2009

properties.Title: Water line attack leaves 11 communities without water in the Negev area of Israel
properties.Description: Eleven communities in the Negev area of Israel are left without water after a main water line is attacked by an unknown group.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water pipeline connecting a marshland with an oil facility attacked in Colombia
properties.Description: A water pipeline that delivers water from San Silvestre marshland to the Ecopetrol Industrial Complex is attacked. The Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) is presumed responsible.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water plants destroyed in Ethiopia-Eritrea war
properties.Description: Water pumping plants and pipelines in the border town of Adi Kuala are destroyed during the civil war between Eritrea and Ethiopia.
properties.Sources: International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) 2003

properties.Title: Water supply to Kukes, Albania and surrounding villages is shut off after an unnamed chemical agent is discovered
properties.Description: A chemical agent is discovered in the main waterline supplying the city of Kukes, Albania. The water supply is shut-off, impacting Kukes and the surrounding villages.

properties.Title: Wells contaminated during Kosovo war
properties.Description: Contamination of water supplies/wells by Serbs disposing of bodies of Kosovar Albanians in local wells. Other reports of Yugoslav federal forces poisoning wells with carcasses and hazardous materials.

properties.Title: Yemeni pipeline destroyed in attempt to pressure government to build water and power systems
properties.Description: Yemeni tribesmen, thought to be from the Jahm tribe, attack and destroy a pipeline in the Marib province in order to pressure the national government to build water distribution systems and power plants in their region.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: The Taliban Pashtun kill Hazaras in Afghanistan, throwing bodies down wells to poison the water supply
properties.Description: In 1998, Taliban Pashtun troops capture the Afghanistan city of Mazar-e-Sharif. Investigators say thousands of Hazaras are killed and hundreds of bodies are thrown down water wells to poison the water supply.

properties.Title: Members of a white supremacist group, The New Order, are arrested in Illinois with plans to poison water supplies with cyanide
properties.Description: A white supremacist group called The New Order plans to poison water supplies with cyanide, rob banks, and conduct murders and bombings in New York and Montgomery, Alabama. Members are arrested in sothern Illinois.

properties.Title: Attacks on water supplies during the Kosovo War leave tens of thousands without water
properties.Description: During the Kosovo War, several attacks over the course of two days in early 1998 leave tens of thousands of Albanians without water. The first attack on February 21, 1998 destroys a main waterline for the town of Kukës, Albania and 15 other villages. The second attack happens on February 22, 18 km away at Kolesjan village.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; UN Environment Programme and UN Center for Human Settlements 1999

properties.Title: South African military attack the Katse and Mohale dam sites in Lesotho during political instability there
properties.Description: On September 22, 1998, South African National Defense Forces attempt to secure the Katse and Mohale dam sites in the Lesotho highlands during a military intervention over the disputed 1998 elections and subsequent political instability. South Africa is heavily dependent on water and power from the Lesotho Highlands Water Project. Acting South African State President described the three objectives

properties.Title: Puerto Ricans protest US Navy's water use
properties.Description: Protesters blocked water intake to Roosevelt Roads Navy Base in opposition to US military presence and Navy's use of the Blanco River, following chronic water shortages in neighboring towns.

properties.Title: Serbs cut off water to Pristina
properties.Description: Serbian engineers shut down water system in Pristina prior to occupation by NATO.

properties.Title: Tajikistan releases water from Kairakum reservoir to cut water for cotton farmers in Kazakhstan; Kyrgyzstan cuts water to Kazakh
properties.Description: In summer 1999, Tajikistan releases 700 million cubic meters of water from the Kairakum reservoir without warning its downstream neighbors. Among other effects, this cuts water to cotton crops in southern Kazakhstan, causing significant losses to farmers. At the same time, Kyrgyzstan cuts flows to southern Kazakhstan in retaliation for Kazakhstan's failure to supply coal under barter agreements.

properties.Title: Villagers killed in Yemen water clash
properties.Description: Yemen sends 700 soldiers to quell fighting that claims six lives and injured 60 others in clashes between two villages fighting over a local spring near Taiz. The village of Al-Marzuh believes it is entitled to exclusive use of a spring because it is located on their land; the neighboring village of Quradah believes their right to the water was affirmed in a 50-year-old court verdict.

properties.Title: Water cooler spiked with chemical agent sickens seven law students
properties.Description: A water cooler spiked with potassium cyanide sickens seven law school students in Springfield, Massachusetts. Swastika-like graffiti around the water cooler draws speculation about the attacker.

properties.Title: Water for Luanda, Angola is cut off during conflict
properties.Description: In Angola, the National Union for the Total Independence of Angola attacks and occupies Catete, leaving Luanda without water. Additionally eight civilians and two FAA are killed and many others are captured.

properties.Title: Water riots in Gujarat, India over control and access to water
properties.Description: Three people die and 20 are injured in December 1999 in Falla, Gujarat, India when the police open fire on 300 people protesting the state government's decision to reserve water from the nearby Kankavati Dam for Jamnagar, a neighboring town. The dam had become the only source of water for about 60 villages near Falla, and shortages and overdraft of groundwater contribute to a water crisis.
properties.Sources: Financial Times Global Water Report (FTGWR) 2000; Centre for Science and the Environment 2000

properties.Title: Water tank in Istanbul, Turkey is attacked
properties.Description: A water tank is damaged from a petrol bomb thrown by unknown assailants in Istanbul, Turkey.
properties.Sources: Anatolia 1999; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 199909280001; RAND Da

properties.Title: Water-supply pipeline is attacked in France
properties.Description: Explosives are placed along a water-supply pipeline by the Comité Régional d'Action Viticole in France. No injuries to people are reported.
properties.Sources: France-Inter Radio Network 1999; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 199

properties.Title: Yugoslavia refuses to clear river debris
properties.Description: Yugoslavia refuses to clear war debris on Danube (downed bridges) unless financial aid for reconstruction is provided; European countries on Danube fear flooding due to winter ice dams will result. Diplomats decry environmental blackmail.

properties.Title: 100 bodies found in Angolan wells
properties.Description: One hundred bodies are found in four drinking water wells in central Angola.

properties.Title: African nations fight over Zambezi island and access to water
properties.Description: Namibia and Botswana engage in a territorial dispute over Sedudu Island, or Kasikili Island, in the Zambezi River, or Chobe River, related to the location of the border and access to water. The International Court of Justice rules in favor of Botswana in 1999.

properties.Title: Bomb discovered at South African reservoir
properties.Description: A home-made bomb is discovered at a water reservoir at Wallmansthal near Pretoria. It is thought to have been meant to sabotage water supplies to farmers.

properties.Title: Bomb explodes on Cavallo off the coast of Corsica, cutting off water supply
properties.Description: A bomb explodes on the island of Cavallo, off the coast of Corsica, causing a disruption to the local water supply. The attack was likely by the National Liberation Front of Corsica (FLNC), which seeks independence from France.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Chinese farmers fight over water
properties.Description: Around the Chinese New Year, farmers from Hebei and Henan Provinces fight over limited water resources. Heavy weapons, including mortars and bombs, are used and nearly 100 villagers are injured.

properties.Title: Clashes between two villages in Mauritania over the control of a water hole
properties.Description: Clashes break out between the village of Djigueni and Missira in Mauritania over the control of a water hole for cattle. Clashes over water rights are common during the dry season.

properties.Title: Fighting breaks out over the use of a watering hole in Mali and two deaths are reported
properties.Description: Malian herdsmen in Missira-Samoura refuse to allow a horseman from Mauritania to use a watering hole. The Mauritanian horseman rides off and returns to the village with other men. Fighting breaks out and results in two people being killed.

properties.Title: Indonesian militia dump bodies in wells in East Timor
properties.Description: Militia opposing East Timor independence kills pro-independence supporters and throws bodies in water well.

properties.Title: Injuries at protest in Bangladesh
properties.Description: Fifty people are hurt during strikes called to protest power and water shortages. Protests are led by former Prime Minister Begum Khaleda Zia over decrease in public services and deterioration of law and order.

properties.Title: Major water pipeline in Zambia is bombed, cutting supplies to three million people
properties.Description: A water pipeline is bombed along the Great North Road in Lusaka, Zambia, cutting off water three million people. A total of sixteen bombs are reported to have exploded throughout the city of Lusaka, with damage targeted at water pipes, power lines, and the Angolan Embassy. At least one person is killed and two are injured. No one claims responsibility for the bombs, however, it is speculated that
properties.Sources: Agence France-Presse (AFP) 1999; Associated Press (AP) 1999b; Mulenga 1999; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and R

properties.Title: Multiple attacks on French utilities, including water, in one day
properties.Description: Reports find at least three attacks on utilities in the Hérault area of France, including one that damaged a water-supply pipeline.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: NATO strikes water facilities in Yugoslavia
properties.Description: NATO targets utilities and shuts down water supplies in Belgrade. NATO bombs bridges on Danube, disrupting navigation.

properties.Title: NATO targets dam in Kosovo
properties.Description: Belgrade reports that NATO planes had targeted a hydroelectric plant during the Kosovo campaign.

properties.Title: NATO targets water plants
properties.Description: Water processing plants are targeted, among other structures, in an air attack on Yugoslavia during the Kosovo War.

properties.Title: Petrochemical and fertilizer factors attacked by NATO, leading to contamination of the Danube River
properties.Description: NATO attacks petrochemical and fertilizer factories in Pancevo, Serbia, claiming they are "dual use," producing chemicals and parts for both military and civilian uses. The attack leads to the extensive release of ammonia, ethylene dichloride, and other chemicals into the Danube River and contaminates the city's water supply.

properties.Title: Chinese man killed for speaking out against displacement of communities by the Three Gorges Dam
properties.Description: In March 1999, Lei Weidong is killed for his opposition to the Three Gorges Dam in China. Before his death, Mr. Weidong traveled to Beijing to protest the dam that would displace hundreds of thousands of people.

properties.Title: Lethal clashes between Malian herders and Mauritania clans over access to a watering hole
properties.Description: Thirteen people died in communal clashes in June 1999 along the border between Mauritania and Mali. Conflict started when herdsmen in Missira-Samoura village in west Mali refused to allow a Mauritania horseman to use a watering hole. The horseman returned with some of his clansmen, attacking the village on June 20, causing 13 deaths.

properties.Title: A man is arrested for attempted poisoning of community water supply in Turkey
properties.Description: A man is arrested for attempted poisoning of the water supply for the village of Kurusaray, Turkey.

properties.Title: Aqueduct in Colombia is attacked
properties.Description: A bombing attempt occurs at an aqueduct in Colombia. The bomb does not detonate. The National Liberation Army is suspected in the attempt.
properties.Sources: Agence France-Presse (AFP) 2000; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200

properties.Title: Australian hacker causes sewage spills
properties.Description: In Queensland, Australia, police arrest a man for using a computer and radio transmitter to take control of the Maroochy Shire wastewater system and release sewage into parks, rivers, and property.

properties.Title: Central Asian nations cut off water to neighbors in ongoing dispute
properties.Description: Kyrgyzstan cuts off water to Kazakhstan until coal is delivered; Uzbekistan cuts off water to Kazakhstan for non-payment of debt.

properties.Title: Clashes between rioters and police over water shortages in Sudan leads to one death and many injuries
properties.Description: Sudanese rioters who are protesting against the failure of schools to reopen as well as water and electricity shortages clash with police who intervene to quell the riot. At least one student is killed and many are injured.

properties.Title: Ethiopian killed in water fight
properties.Description: One man is stabbed to death during fight over clean water during famine in Ethiopia.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers are accused of intentionally releasing sewage to contaminate Palestinian agricultural fields in the West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli settlers from Efrat are blamed for deliberately releasing sewer water into Palestinian agricultural fields in the West Bank.

properties.Title: Kenyans battle monkeys for drought relief
properties.Description: A clash between villagers and thirsty monkeys leaves eight apes dead and ten villagers wounded. The duel starts after water tankers bring water to a drought-stricken area and monkeys desperate for water attack the villagers.

properties.Title: Luxury home burned to protest impact on watershed
properties.Description: The Earth Liberation Front (ELF) claims responsibility for setting a luxury home under construction on fire. Their written statement says they targeted the home because of its location in the Lake Monroe watershed, a drinking water supply for the town of Bloomington, Indiana that is in jeopardy from urban sprawl.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; Eco-Action 2003

properties.Title: Moroccan army destroys wells
properties.Description: Units of the Moroccan army destroy the wells situated in the municipalities of Guelta and Boujdour. Citizens usually use these wells to provide water for their sheep in the summer.

properties.Title: Regional drought heightens tensions in Ethiopia and Kenya
properties.Description: The Ethiopian government says that regional drought is worsening conflicts at watering points and over access to pasture. The tensions are heightened by the presence of the Oromo Liberation Front (OLF), an Ethiopian armed opposition group said to be operating from Kenyan territory near the common border.

properties.Title: Riots over water privatization in Cochabamba, Bolivia
properties.Description: Massive protests, riots, and violence result from efforts to privatize the water system of Cochabamba, Bolivia.

properties.Title: Settlers attack a village in Palestine, injuring civilians and cutting water supply
properties.Description: In a village in Palestine, three Palestinians are injured by gunshots from Israeli settlers. The same settlers also cut the water supply to the village by damaging the pipes.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Students riot to protest water shortages in Kenya
properties.Description: Students riot to protest water shortages in Kenya.

properties.Title: Terrorist drill gets out of hand in California
properties.Description: A drill simulating a terrorist attack on the Nacimiento Dam in Monterey County, California got out of hand when two radio stations reported it as a real attack.

properties.Title: Violent conflict in Afghanistan over access to water
properties.Description: Violent conflicts break out over water resources in the villages Burna Legan and Taina Legan, Afghanistan and in other parts of the region, as drought depleted local resources.

properties.Title: Water company is bombed to demand release of prisoners
properties.Description: A water company office in Ustaritz, France is damaged by a bomb thought to be placed in support of a group called the Basque Fatherland and Freedom (aka ETA). The attackers leave writing on the wall in the building demanding the release of ETA prisoners.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water storage facility is destroyed
properties.Description: Eritrea accuses Ethiopia of bombing the Red Sea port at Assab, saying several bombs are dropped on Assab, destroying a water storage facility on the outskirts of the town.

properties.Title: Water-supply tank for a condominium in Singapore deliberately contaminated
properties.Description: A water-supply tank is deliberately poisoned with kerosene and turpentine. Residents of the condominium complex in Singapore discover the contamination from the odor of their water.

properties.Title: Riots in northern China over use and allocation of water from Baiyang Lake
properties.Description: Civil unrest erupts over use and allocation of water from Baiyang Lake, in northern China. Several people die in riots by villagers in July 2000 in Shandong after officials cut off water supplies.

properties.Title: French workers pollute river over labor dispute
properties.Description: In July, workers at the Cellatex chemical plant in northern France dump 5,000 liters of sulfuric acid into a tributary of the Meuse River after they are denied workers' benefits. A French analyst points out that this is the first time "the environment and public health were made hostage in order to exert pressure, an unheard-of situation until now."

properties.Title: Six deaths reported when officials in Guangdong, China blow up a water channel to prevent cross-border diversion of water
properties.Description: In August 2000, six die when officials in the southern province of Guangdong blow up a water channel to prevent a neighboring county from diverting water.

properties.Title: Residents riot over cut of water supplies in South Africa
properties.Description: Residents riot when officials in South Africa cut of water supplies to 1000 homes who are defunct on their payment. Police move in and disperse the crowd with rubber bullets.

properties.Title: Taliban in Afhanistan cuts off vital water supply to Iran
properties.Description: During a 1999 drought, Taliban authorities cut off the flow of the Helmand River between Afghanistan and Sistan-Baluchestan, Iran, which relies on the Helmand as its only source of irrigation. The Iranian government allegedly responds by entering Afghanistan and building a series of diversion canals to restore flows. A 1973 water agreement between the two countries is never ratified and both Iran

properties.Title: Twenty people are killed over access to fresh water
properties.Description: Twenty people are killed in additional clashes between Orma and Pokomo ethnic groups over access to fresh water in Kenya.

properties.Title: Two leaders of a water utility labor union assassinated
properties.Description: The president of the Codazzi Waterworks Labor Union, Aldo Mejia Martinez, is shot and killed by five unidentified people. Six months later, his successor, Engeniano Sánchez Díaz, is also murdered. The murders may be linked to a larger trend of homicides in Colombia of trade-union leaders and activists.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; Trade Union Confederation 2001

properties.Title: US bombs Afghan hydroelectric facility at Kajaki Dam
properties.Description: American forces bomb the hydroelectric facility at Kajaki Dam in Helmand province of Afghanistan, cutting off electricity for the city of Kandahar. The dam itself is not targeted.

properties.Title: A water tank car is destroyed by a landmine in Russia
properties.Description: A water tank car is destroyed by a landmine in Grozny, Chechnya, Russia. A teenage girl is wounded in the explosion.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200108140011

properties.Title: Bombing disrupts Lebanese water supplies
properties.Description: Water supplies to more than 20 towns are disrupted after a bomb explodes near the town of Baalbek in Lebanon.
properties.Sources: FBIS 2001; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200108270004

properties.Title: Chinese protestors block canal in protest over uncontrolled water pollution
properties.Description: In an act to protest the destruction of fisheries from uncontrolled water pollution, fishermen in northern Jiaxing City, Zhejiang Province, dam a large industrial wastewater canal for 23 days. The wastewater discharges into the neighboring Shengze Town, Jiangsu Province, killing fish and threatening public health.

properties.Title: Clashes between ethnic groups over access to water
properties.Description: Clashes occur between Pokomo and Wordei ethnic groups over access to a water fresh source in Kenya.

properties.Title: Deadly fight between Kenyan herders and farmers
properties.Description: At least 130 people are killed in a string of clashes between Pokomo farmers and Orma, semi-nomadic cattle herders over access to land and river water.

properties.Title: Kyrgyzstan charges downstream countries for water provoking a natural gas cut off from Uzbekistan
properties.Description: In June 2001, the Kyrgyz parliament adopts a law classifying water as a commodity and announces that downstream countries would be charged for water. In response, Uzbekistan cuts off all deliveries of natural gas to Kyrgyzstan and accuses Kyrgyzstan of failing to honor an agreement to provide Uzbekistan with water in return for oil and gas.

properties.Title: Nepali soldiers stop rebels from targeting drinking water supplies
properties.Description: According to the Nepal Defense Ministry, soldiers repulse attempts by rebels to blow up the sources of Kathmandu's drinking water supplies at Shivapuri and Sundarijal. No details of these incidents are given.

properties.Title: Palestinians destroy water supply to Israeli settlements
properties.Description: Palestinians destroy water-supply pipelines to the West Bank settlement of Yitzhar and to Kibbutz Kissufim. The Agbat Jabar refugee camp near Jericho disconnects from its water supply after Palestinians loot and damage local water pumps. Palestinians accuse Israel of destroying a water cistern, blocking water-tanker deliveries, and attacking materials for a wastewater treatment project.
properties.Sources: Israel Line 2001a; Israel Line 2001b; Environment News Service 2001

properties.Title: Protests over water shortages in Algeria
properties.Description: Angry protests take place in various communities of Ain Berda province, Algeria over gas, water and electricity shortages. Youths build barricades and block roads during these demonstrations.

properties.Title: Unrest over water shortages in Pakistan
properties.Description: Civil unrest over severe water shortages caused by the long-term drought. Protests began in March and April and continued into summer with riots, four bombs in Karachi (June 13), one death, 12 injuries, 30 arrests. Ethnic conflicts contribute as some groups "accuse the government of favoring the populous Punjab province [over Sindh province] in water distribution."

properties.Title: Water cut off in Macedonian conflict
properties.Description: Water flow to Kumanovo (population 100,000), Macedonia cut off for 12 days in conflict between ethnic Albanians and Macedonian forces. Valves of Glaznja and Lipkovo Lakes damaged.
properties.Sources: Agence France-Presse (AFP) 2001; Macedonia Information Agency 2001; Guardian, The 2001

properties.Title: Local community leader against the Babilonia hydro project murdered in his home in Honduras
properties.Description: A local community leader in La Venta, Honduras, is murdered in his home by six guards employed by a dam company, Energisa S.A. Carlow Roberto Flores had worked to oppose the Babilonia Hydroelectric Project in Sierra de Agalta National Park.

properties.Title: An inter-ethnic conflict between Macedonia and Albania includes a fight over control of the major water-supply reservoir
properties.Description: Macedonians and native Albanians in the border village of Radusa, Macedonia, fight to control the water reservoir that supplies water to Skopje, the nation's capital.

properties.Title: An explosive device is detonated at Lhokseumawe Reservoir in Indonesia
properties.Description: A homemade bomb explodes at Lhokseumawe Reservoir in Aceh Province, Indonesia, with minor damage to the facility. The attack is attributed to the Free Aceh Movement.

properties.Title: Philippine water poisoned
properties.Description: The militant Islamist separatist group Abu Sayyaf threatens to poison the water supply in Isabela, a mainly Christian town on Basilan Island in the country's south. In October, residents in six nearby villages report water contamination from what smells like gasoline. Local officials respond by closing pipelines and bringing in drinking water by truck.
properties.Sources: World Environment News 2001; Fenton Helm and Dutter 2001

properties.Title: An explosive device is detonated at the Panauti hydropower plant in Khopasi, Nepal
properties.Description: Suspected Nepal communist party members detonate an explosive devise at the Panauti hydropower plant, causing around $500,000 in damages.

properties.Title: Terrorist targets water systems in the United States
properties.Description: Papers seized during the arrest of a Lebanese Imam at a mosque in Seattle include "instructions on poisoning water sources" from a London-based al Qaeda recruiter. The FBI issue a bulletin to computer security experts around the country indicating that al-Qaeda terrorists may have been studying American dams and water-supply systems in preparation for new attacks. "U.S. law enforcement and intelli

properties.Title: Violent clashes triggered over a hydroelectric project in Ecuador
properties.Description: A small community, San Pablo de Amali, in Bolivar province, Ecuador, resists a hydroelectric project on the local Dulcepamba River. The resistance by the community is generally peaceful, but some community members are arrested for acts of sabotage and destruction. In support of the developer, Hidrotambo S.A., the national government sends troops to the community on several instances, resulting in
properties.Sources: Carson 2019, Staberock and Lahidji 2016; Environmental Justice Atlas 2019; Observatorio del Cambio Rural n.d.; Calvopiña 2016

properties.Title: Warehouse of drinking water project destroyed
properties.Description: A rebel group, called the Communist Party of Nepal-Maoists, destroys a warehouse of the Bijauri Drinking Water Project in Nepal, including 30,000 meters of polythene pipes.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water company offices in Chile bombed in response to high tariffs for water
properties.Description: Three explosions occur at the offices of a water company, Aquas Andinas of Chile. The explosions are attributed to a group called Pueblo Reagrupado. Pueblo Reagrupado is thought to have placed the bombs in response to high tariffs for water.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water pipe destroyed by explosion in Serbia
properties.Description: A water pipe that had been planned for a new water-supply system in Bujanovac, Serbia is destroyed by an explosion. An Albanian group is thought be responsible for the bomb.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water reservoir sustains minor damages from bombing in Colombia
properties.Description: The chief engineer of a water company reports minor damage from a bomb placed near a reservoir in Bogota by the  Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water shortages cause riots in Algeria
properties.Description: Power outages and water shortages cause multiple days of rioting in Rouached, Algeria. 11 people are injured. Riot police intervene.

properties.Title: Youths destroy local water, electric, and telecommunication facilities in Cote d'lvoire
properties.Description: On October 10, youths claiming to be members of the Group of Patriots for Peace (GPP) destroy facilities of the Ivoirian Water Distribution Company, the Ivoirian Electricity Company, and Cote d'Ivoire Telecommunications. Demonstrators say they were protesting the "free" supply of water, electricity, and telephone in rebel-controlled areas.

properties.Title: Priest in Honduras murdered for speaking out against the Babilonia hydro project
properties.Description: A priest, Fredy Cornelia Benitez, is murdered in 2002 in Honduras for speaking out against the Babilonia Hydroelectric project.

properties.Title: A man is arrested after threatening to dump mercury into local drinking water supply in Salem, Massachusetts, United States
properties.Description: A man is arrested after threatening to dump mercury into local drinking water supply in Salem, Massachusetts, United States.
properties.Sources: New Jersey Office of Homeland Security and Preparedness 2009

properties.Title: "Unknown agent" released into water-treatment plant in Colombia
properties.Description: Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) releases an "unknown agent," presumably thought to be poisonous, in a water-treatment plant. No injuries are reported.
properties.Sources: Bogota Cambio 2002a; Bogota Cambio 2002b; Xinhua General News Service 2002; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and R

properties.Title: Algerians riot over water shortages
properties.Description: Algerians protesting against water shortages riot in the eastern coastal town of El Arrouch, Algeria, setting fire to buildings and vehicles.

properties.Title: Bomb explodes in water-utility office building in Corsica, France
properties.Description: A powerful bomb explodes in a water-utility office building on the Island of Corsica, France. The building is also graffitied with the nationalist slogan "Freedom for Patriots."
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Bomb found in water pump station defused in Colombia
properties.Description: A bomb is found in a water-supply pump station along a canal, however it is defused prior to causing any damage to the system. The Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) is blamed for the attempted attack.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Botswana authorities destroy wells of Kalahari Bushmen
properties.Description: Botswana's president Festus Mogae sends troops to the Kalahari Desert to destroy wells and empty water sources of indigenous Khoisan (also known as Bushmen), ostensibly in an effort to remove them from their ancestral lands and assimilate them into modern society. Critics blame the government of taking away water rights in favor of mining interests and labeled the government's actions a "siege." B

properties.Title: Colombian rebels bomb dam
properties.Description: Colombian rebels in January damage a gate valve in the dam that supplies most of Bogota's drinking water. Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) detonate an explosive device planted on a German-made gate valve located inside a tunnel in the Chingaza Dam.

properties.Title: Colorado eco-terrorists threaten water supply
properties.Description: Earth Liberation Front threatens the water supply for the town of Winter Park. Previously, this group claimed responsibility for the destruction of a ski lodge in Vail, Colorado that threatened lynx habitat.

properties.Title: Deadly clash over water sharing in Kashmir, India
properties.Description: Two people are killed and 25 others injured in Kashmir, India, when police fire at a group of villagers clashing over water sharing. The incident took place in Garend village in a dispute over water from an irrigation stream.

properties.Title: Drinking water in Libornia, Colombia is poisoned
properties.Description: Just one month after the poisoning of a water supply in another small town in Colombia, the water supply for the  town of Libornia is poisoned as well.

properties.Title: Drinking water poisoned in Pitalito, Colombia
properties.Description: The Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) is accused of poisoning the town of Pitalito's water supply with heavy metals.

properties.Title: Far right-wing nationalists in South Africa plot to kill millions of black South Africans by poisoning water
properties.Description: A major plot to kill millions of black South Africans living in the townships near Johannesburg is discovered by the South African National Intelligence Agency. The attackers are a group of far right-wing white South Africans. Their plans consist of contaminating water supplies by pouring an agricultural chemical into reservoirs that supply four townships.

properties.Title: Four wells found with human remains in Cote d'lvoire
properties.Description: Conflict between the government of Laurent Gbagbo and opposing rebel forces leads to extensive violence and regional conflict. In late September 2002, there are reports of several mass graves discovered in the Bangolo. In Zeregbo and Bahably, four water wells are found with human remains. Early reports indicate that western rebel groups who captured the area killed the persons in the mass graves a

properties.Title: Gas line set on fire in a water treatment plant
properties.Description: A gas pipeline in a water treatment plant is set on fire by "People's Militias" in Dosquebradas, Colombia. One death and two injuries are reported in association with the event.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Italy foils terror plot targeting water supplies
properties.Description: Italian police arrest four Moroccans allegedly planning to contaminate the water-supply system in Rome with a cyanide-based chemical, targeting buildings that included the United States embassy. Ties to Al-Queda are suggested.

properties.Title: Multiple attacks on water towers in Pakistan
properties.Description: Two water towers are blown up in Jango Ghari, Pakistan, a tribal area, where several other similar attacks have occurred. The power systems atop the towers are destroyed in the blast as well.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Nepal rebels blow up powerhouse
properties.Description: The Khumbuwan Liberation Front (KLF) blows up a 250 kilowatt hydroelectric powerhouse in Nepal's Bhojpur District, cutting off power to Bhojpur and surrounding areas. The damages take 6 months to repair and cost 10 million Rs (US $120,000). During 2002, Maoist rebels destroy more than seven micro-hydro projects, a water-supply intake, and supply pipelines to Khalanga in western Nepal.
properties.Sources: Kathmandu Post, The 2002; Financial Times Global Water Report (FTGWR) 2002

properties.Title: Plot to poison water and food supplies reported by Russian special forces
properties.Description: A plot by Chechen rebels to poison water and food supplies in Groznyy, Russia, is reported by Russian special forces.

properties.Title: Protesters clash with police over water shortages
properties.Description: Clashes between protesters and police are reported in the east and south of Algeria, as well as in different neighborhoods of the capital, over water shortages. Streets are blocked and public buildings are burned.

properties.Title: Indian military protecting reservoir attacked by terrorists
properties.Description: A camp of the Assam Rifles of the Indian military at Yairipok in Thoubal district with a mission to protect a water reservoir is attacked by suspected terrorists who fired grenades and other explosives on January 27. Later, a spokesman for the United National Liberation Front (UNLF) claims that the attack was carried out by its armed wing, the Manipur People's Army, which has been fighting for wha

properties.Title: Inter-clan fighting of the Mudug region in Somalia occurs over resource scarcity
properties.Description: About 40 people are killed and 60 wounded in inter-clan fighting in the Mudug region of central Somalia. The violence is sparked by revenge killings over the deaths of eight fisherman, but a local elder, Muhammad Salad Du'ale, attributes the ongoing violence to the scarcity of water and grazing.

properties.Title: Rival Ethiopian and Somali ethnic groups clash over access to water
properties.Description: Rival ethnic groups, the Afar (Ethiopia) and Issas (Somali), clash over scarce water in mid-2002. The scarcity is due to severe drought pushing Issas into Afar territory for use of their watering holes. The UN Emergencies Unit for Ethiopia says that "immediate political solutions are essential to negotiate conflict resolution between Afar and Issa communities over water and grazing land."

properties.Title: Outspoken defender of the environment and settler rights, and opponent of the Belo Monte dam project, is assassinated in Brazil
properties.Description: In August 2002, Gilzan Teixeira Lima, a leader of a fight against the construction of the Belo Monte Dam project on the Xingu River in Brazil, is assassinated. He had received death threats for his defense of the environment and settler rights.

properties.Title: Violence over Cauvery River in India
properties.Description: Continuing violence over the allocation of the Kaveri River between Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. Riots, property destruction, more than 30 injuries, arrests through September and October.

properties.Title: Bolivian irrigators and residents clash over water rights, with destruction of water delivery infrastructure
properties.Description: Tarata, Bolivia experiences violent confrontations between irrigators from the region of Arbieto and residents of the nearby town of Tarata. The dispute centers on the allocation of water among these users and new water infrastructure that took water from the Laka Laka dam. In October, irrigators destroy approximately two kilometers of water supply pipeline. In response, residents destroy part of
properties.Sources: Bustamante et al 2004, Moriarty Butterworth and van Koopen 2004

properties.Title: Man poisons water reservoir in Henan Province, China, sickening over 60 to boost water purifier sales
properties.Description: A man who sells water purifiers poisons a reservoir in Ruyang County, Henan Province, China, sickening at least 64 people. The man claims that he wanted to improve sales of his product.

properties.Title: Al Qaeda threatens US water
properties.Description: Al-Qaeda threatens US water systems via call to a London-based, Saudi-owned magazine. The spokesman told the magazine that "al Qaeda [does not rule out] using sarin gas and poisoning drinking water in U.S. and Western cities."
properties.Sources: Associated Press (AP) 2003a; Waterman 2003; Washington Times, The 2003

properties.Title: Armed cadres kill Bangladesh Nationalist Party workers over resource dispute
properties.Description: Suspected armed cadres of the Purbo Banglar Communist Party (PBCP) kill six Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) workers at Naidighee village in Naogaon, on April 25. According to police sources, the incident appears to be a continuation of a long-standing dispute over nine government water reservoirs and khas (farming) land in the village.

properties.Title: Bomb explosion breaks water main, cutting water supply for all of Baghdad, Iraq
properties.Description: Insurgents bomb a main water pipeline in Baghdad, Iraq, cutting all water supply and flooding neighborhoods. City engineers say this is the first strike against Baghdad's water system during the Iraq War, which began in March 2003. The bombing occurs around seven in the morning, when a blue Volkswagen Passat stops on an overpass near the Nidaa mosque and an explosive is fired at the six-foot-wide
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) 2003; Tierney and Worth 2003

properties.Title: Bomb found in water-supply facility in Ireland
properties.Description: A bomb is defused at a water supply facility in Keady, Ireland. The facility supplies about 10,000 homes with water. A group called the Continuity Irish Republican Army (CIRA) is suspected to be responsible for placing the bomb.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Bombs found at US bottling plant
properties.Description: Four incendiary devices are found in the pumping station of a Michigan water-bottling plant. The Earth Liberation Front (ELF) claims responsibility, accusing Ice Mountain Water Company of "stealing" water for profit. Ice Mountain is a subsidiary of Nestle Waters.

properties.Title: Colombian water plant bombed
properties.Description: A bomb blast at the Cali Drinking Water Treatment Plant kills 3 workers May 8th in Colombia. The workers were members of a trade union involved in intense negotiations over privatization of the water system.

properties.Title: Darfur war triggered in part by disputes over control of water resources
properties.Description: The Darfur War is triggered in part by conflicts over the control of water and other natural resources.

properties.Title: Group bombs a rural water supply office and other offices in India
properties.Description: A rural water supply office building is blown up along with other offices, in Parkal, India.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Caño Limón – Coveñas pipeline attacked, spilling oil into stream and polluting water for 4,700 families; 972 attacks since 1985
properties.Description: A controversial pipeline is attacked once again, causing oil to spill into a local stream, polluting the water supply used by 4700 families. The Caño Limón – Coveñas pipeline has come under attack hundreds of times in an attempt by the local population to disrupt and halt natural resource extraction by multinational companies. One source reports that since the pipeline was built in 1985 it has bee
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; SourceWatch 2018

properties.Title: Iraqi agents attempts to poison water at US base in Jordan
properties.Description: Jordanian authorities arrest Iraqi agents in connection with a botched plot to poison the water supply that serves American troops in the eastern Jordanian desert near the border with Iraq. The scheme involved poisoning a water tank that supplies American soldiers at a military base in Khao, which lies in an arid region of the eastern frontier near the industrial town of Zarqa.

properties.Title: Iraqi water treatment plant damaged
properties.Description: The Abu Nawas pumping station on the Tigris River, which supplies water to Sadr City and other treatment plants in Baghdad, is severely damaged during the 2003 conflict.
properties.Sources: International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) 2009

properties.Title: Israeli warplanes attack water systems in Gaza
properties.Description: Israeli warplanes hit water supply wells, pipelines, and wastewater treatment plants servicing hundreds of thousands of Palestinians in the Gaza region. Palestinian officials state that the attacks intentionally targeted the infrastructure, amounting to a war crime under the Geneva Protocol.

properties.Title: Residents block highways in Algeria over lack of drinking water
properties.Description: A major cold wave and lack of electricity, gas, and drinking water has led residents of Ain Mlila, Algeria to block major highways until their demands are met.

properties.Title: Riots over lack of water services in Algeria
properties.Description: Rioters in Algeria armed with knives and molotov cocktails set fire to government buildings in protest over lack of electrical and water services.

properties.Title: Sa'ad and Dir clans fight over water and grazing in Somalia
properties.Description: Two days of heavy fighting in Mudug region of Somalia leave at least 43 people dead and over 90 injured. The fighting breaks out between the Sa'ad, Habar Gedir subclan and the Dir, and is concentrated in and around the villages of Towfiq and Awle, some 200 km east of Galkayo. As with the 2002 events, the clashes are triggered by revenge killings but the ensuing escalation of violence is also attri

properties.Title: Sudan targets water in civil war
properties.Description: The ongoing civil war in the Sudan has included violence against water resources. In 2003, villagers from around Tina said that bombings had destroyed water wells. In Khasan Basao they alleged that water wells were poisoned. In 2004, wells in Darfur were intentionally contaminated as part of a strategy of harassment against displaced populations.

properties.Title: Water bottles are intentionally contaminated in 20 different Italian cities, sickening more than 50 people
properties.Description: Around 50 people are sickened by water contaminated with either bleach acetone or ammonia in more than 20 different cities from across Italy. Authorities blame a "saboteur" or "copy-cat saboteurs" for these poisonings.

properties.Title: Water systems damaged in US-Iraq war
properties.Description: During the US-led invasion of Iraq, water systems are reportedly damaged or destroyed by different parties, and major dams are military objectives of the US forces. Damage directly attributable to the war includes vast segments of the water distribution system and the Baghdad water system, damaged by a missile.

properties.Title: Women block water access to a naval base in Nigeria
properties.Description: A group of women in canoes protest safeguards on oil standards by blocking water access to a naval base in the Niger Delta, Nigeria.

properties.Title: A package with ricin and a note are found threatening to poison a local water supply in South Carolina, United States
properties.Description: A package with ricin and a note are found threatening to poison a local water supply in Greenville, South Carolina, United States.
properties.Sources: New Jersey Office of Homeland Security and Preparedness 2009

properties.Title: Grenades are fired at a water reservoir plant in Kidapawan, Philippines destroying a piepline and disrupting water supplies
properties.Description: Two grenades destroy a water pipeline at a reservoir in Kidapawan, Philippines, disrupting water supply to approximately 100,000 residents. The Moro Islamic Liberation Front is accused of the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200303190005; U.S Department of Home

properties.Title: Rockets are fired at Kajaki Dam, Helmand Province, Afghanistan
properties.Description: The governor of Helmand Province, Afghanistan reports that three rockets are fired at Kajaki Dam. 60 suspected Taliban are arrested for the attack.

properties.Title: The Sudan government bombs wells in Furawiya, Sudan killing animals and destroying a well
properties.Description: The Sudan government bombs wells in Furawiya, Sudan killing hundreds of animals and destroying a well.

properties.Title: Rebels destroy offices of local water utility in Sudan
properties.Description: Rebels destroy several houses and the offices of the local police and water utility in Sudan.

properties.Title: Riots in prison over water shortage, Cote d'Ivoire
properties.Description: Riots occur at the main prison in Abidjan, Cote d'Ivoire over a water shortage.

properties.Title: Riots over water shortages in Algeria
properties.Description: Riots break out in Algeria due to unemployment, water shortages, and resentment of the government. Protesters attack town halls and other administrative buildings.

properties.Title: Security staff of a public water system shot at while working
properties.Description: Bullets are fired at the security staff of a public water pumping station in Kosovska Mitrovica, Kosovo. Authorities blame Albanian extremists for the attack.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Several government agencies related to water and other services attacked
properties.Description: Over two months in 2004 in Nepal, the Communist Party of Nepal-Maoists bomb several government offices, including the Water Induced Calamities Control Sub-Division in Nadipur, Pokhara and the Groundwater Irrigation Project in Dang District.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Students protest over water shortages
properties.Description: Students protest over water shortages.

properties.Title: Students riot over power cuts and water shortages in the Democratic Republic of the Congo
properties.Description: Students riot over power cuts and water shortages in the Democratic Republic of the Congo

properties.Title: Terrorists bomb water pipe in India
properties.Description: Twelve Indian security forces are killed by an IED planted in an underground water pipe during "counter-insurgency operation in Khanabal area in Anantnag district.'

properties.Title: US halts water projects in Gaza
properties.Description: The United States halts two water development projects as punishment to the Palestinian Authority for their failure to find those responsible for a deadly attack on a US diplomatic convoy in October 2003.

properties.Title: US Pentagon report raises possibility that Taiwan could target China's Three Gorges Dam as deterrence threat
properties.Description: A 2004 Pentagon report on China's military capacity raises the concept of Taipei adopting military systems capable of being used as a tool for deterring Chinese military coercion by "presenting credible threats to China's urban population or high-value targets, such as the Three Gorges Dam." China promptly denounces "a U.S. suggestion' that Taiwan's military target the Three Gorges Dam, leading th
properties.Sources: China Daily 2004a; US Department of Defense 2004

properties.Title: Violence over water in Ethiopia
properties.Description: At least 250 people killed and many more injured in clashes over water wells and pastoral lands. Villagers call it the "War of the Well" and describe "well warlords, well widows, and well warriors." A three-year drought has led to extensive violence over limited water resources, worsened by the lack of effective government and central planning.
properties.Sources: British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) 2004; Associated Press (AP) 2005; Wax 2006

properties.Title: Violent protests in South Africa
properties.Description: Poor delivery of water and sanitation services in Phumelela Township leads to several months of protests. No one is killed during the protests, but a few people are seriously injured, and municipal property is damaged.

properties.Title: The Sudan government and militias attack water systems in Furawiya, Southern Sudan
properties.Description: The Sudanese government and Janjaweed militia poison a well in Furawiya, destroy the sole pump for a communal well in Terbeba, and toss dead bodies down a well in Bendisi, southern Sudan.

properties.Title: Terrorists attempt to recruit workers at a US water utility to poison drinking water
properties.Description: The FBI and Homeland Security warn that terrorists are attempting to recruit workers at a US water utility to poison drinking water.
properties.Sources: New Jersey Office of Homeland Security and Preparedness 2009

properties.Title: Chinese engineers working on a dam are kidnapped, one is killed
properties.Description: Two Chinese engineers working on the Gomal Zam dam and a canal in Waziristan, Pakistan, are kidnapped by Pakistani militants seeking the release of six Pakistani comrades. During a rescue attempt, one of the two engineers is shot and killed, while the other is brought to safety.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; China Daily 2004b

properties.Title: Driver killed while transporting water in Iraq
properties.Description: A driver of a truck transporting mineral water in Mosul, Iraq is killed when his truck is set on fire.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; Reuters 2004c

properties.Title: Four people employed by the water and sewage department kidnapped in Kirkuk, Iraq
properties.Description: Three engineers and one contractor, employed by the city water and sewage board, are kidnapped in Kirkuk, Iraq. No reports could be found detailing the outcome of the kidnapping.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Mexican farmers shot in duel over spring
properties.Description: Two Mexican farmers argue for years over water rights to a small spring used to irrigate a small corn plot near the town of Pihuamo. In March, these farmers shoot each other dead.

properties.Title: Militia attack village over land and water disputes
properties.Description: Boran militia attack Gabra village near the Ethiopian border. The attack is linked to long-running disputes over land and water.

properties.Title: Militias smash water wells in Sudan
properties.Description: Janjaweed militias smash water wells and burn both food and seeds in the Jebel Marra area of Sudan. They also allegedly throw dead bodies into some open wells.

properties.Title: Missionaries working on a water project attacked in Mosul, Iraq
properties.Description: Four Christian missionaries from the U.S. are killed and one other seriously wounded when they are attacked while working on a water project in Mosul, Iraq.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; Renaud and Johnson 2004

properties.Title: Pakistan targets terrorists' water
properties.Description: In military action aimed at Islamic terrorists, including Al Qaeda and the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan, homes, schools, and water wells are damaged and destroyed in Pakistan.
