properties.Title: Police kill dam protestor in China
properties.Description: Tens of thousands of farmers stage a sit-in against the construction of the Pubugou dam on the Dadu River in Sichuan Province, China. Riot police are deployed to quell the unrest and one person is killed. Witnesses also report the deaths of a number of residents.

properties.Title: Police kill water protestors in India
properties.Description: Four people are killed in October and more than 30 injured in November in ongoing protests by farmers over allocations of water from the Indira Ghandi Irrigation Canal in Sri Ganganagar district, which borders Pakistan. Authorities impose curfews on the towns of Gharsana, Raola and Anoopgarh.

properties.Title: Protesters blockade a road over installation of pre-paid water meters in South Africa
properties.Description: Members of the Soweto Electricity Crisis Committee blockade a road in their city in protest of the installation of pre-paid water meters in their homes. Police use stun-guns to disperse them.

properties.Title: Armed men attempt to gain access to the Zelenchuck Dam, Russia and are repelled, with one attacker injured
properties.Description: A group of three armed men attempted to gain access to the water intake area of the Zelenchuck Dam in the Karachay-Cherkessia Republic of Russia. Guards at the dam successfully repel the attack, wounding one attacker and arresting the others.

properties.Title: Murusade and Duduble subclans clash over water well in Somalia
properties.Description: Murusade subclan and Duduble subclan clash over a water well, with 23 fatalities. Separately, a dispute between the Dir and Marehan clans over control of water and grazing lands in Heraale leads to a death in October.
properties.Sources: Puntland Development Research Centre (PDRC) 2014; The Somali Dir Clan's History: Codka Beesha Direed 2012; Salehyan et al 2014

properties.Title: Government shuts off electricity and water supplies in northern Cote d'lvoire
properties.Description: On November 4, the army carries out "Operation Dignity" and mounts surprise attacks on Bouake, stronghold of opposition forces and the largest city in the north, shattering an 18-month ceasefire. The Government cuts off electricity and water supplies to the north the day before the attacks. They remain off for about a week.

properties.Title: Riots over water shortage kill prisoners in Côte d'Ivoire
properties.Description: There are two incidents in early November at Abidjan's main jail, La Maison d'arrêt et de correction d'Abidjan (MACA), Côte d'Ivoire in which prisoners are killed. The first incident is sparked by a lengthy water shortage; prisoners reportedly had not had water for 5 days except for small rations of drinking water. At least 7 prisoners die and 30 are injured in the ensuing riots. U.N. human rights

properties.Title: A bomb explodes at the construction site for Dumarao Dam, Philippines
properties.Description: Suspected communists detonate a bomb at the construction site of the Dumarao Dam, Capiz province, Philippines.

properties.Title: A bomb explodes at the Selaghat Dam, Nepal cutting electricity to the area
properties.Description: A powerful bomb explodes at the powerhouse of the Selaghat Dam, Bajura District, Nepal. No group claims responsibility.

properties.Title: Rocket lands near an Israeli national water company facility
properties.Description: A Qassam rocket lands near a facility belonging to the Israeli national water company, Mekorot, in the western Negev desert. There are no injuries or damages resulting from the strike.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200512120004

properties.Title: Six employees of a water utility found murdered inside the water treatment plant in Baghdad
properties.Description: Six employees of a water utility in Baghdad are found murdered inside a water treatment plant. The actual date of their deaths is not known.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Three Iraqis killed while driving water to an army base
properties.Description: A group of three Iraqi men are shot and killed while transporting water to a military base near Taji, Iraq.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water pipeline attacked with bomb in Iraq
properties.Description: A bomb placed near a water pipeline explodes in Iraq, causing it to break. That same day, two nearby oil pipelines are also attacked.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water pipeline targeted in attack in Baghdad
properties.Description: A water pipeline is damaged by a targeted explosion, disrupting water services to approximately two million people in Baghdad, Iraq.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; New Humanitarian, The 2005

properties.Title: A lawyer who advised people affected by the construction of a dam in Mexico is killed
properties.Description: A Mexican lawyer, Jaime Bracamonte Arias, who advised people affected by the construction of El Cajon Dam is killed in 2005 after years of receiving death threats.

properties.Title: Over years of opposition to the La Parota dam, three local organizers are killed in Mexico
properties.Description: Between 2005 and 2007 three local organizers in Guerrero, Mexico, Tomas Cruz Zamora, Eduardo Maya Manrique, and Benito Cruz Jacinto, are killed for their opposition to La Parota Dam project.

properties.Title: A mortar round hits a water facility in Iraq
properties.Description: A mortar round hits a water facility in Baghdad, Iraq, resulting in a disruption of water supply to residents serviced by the facility. No one claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200507010006

properties.Title: Bomb destroys power source for local water system in Albania
properties.Description: A bomb explodes in the Margegaj village of Tropojë District in Albania, destroying a power source for the local water system. Several villages are left without water.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Bomb placed at a water station in Iraq explodes prematurely and kills both attackers
properties.Description: A bomb at a water station in Iraq explodes prematurely, killing the two men placing the bomb.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Car bomb near water services director kills one
properties.Description: A car bomb placed near the Kirkuk city water services director detonates, killing one person and injuring at least 17 others in Kirkuk, Iraq.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Communist party in Nepal destroys a drinking water project
properties.Description: The Communist Party of Nepal-Maoists destroys a drinking water project in the District of Arghakhanchi, Nepal, disrupting water service to thousands of people.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Gunman take out electric lines, disrupting water service in Baghdad
properties.Description: Electric lines that provide power for water-supply services in Baghdad, Iraq are shot down by a gunman, causing a disruption to water services. These same power lines had been recently fixed after suffering from a similar attack in the months prior.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Gunmen attack civilians working on a water project in Iraq
properties.Description: Gunmen attack civilians working on a water project in Al-Faris, Iraq, killing 18 and wounding 22. No group claims responsibility for the shooting.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200507260001

properties.Title: Marehan and Garre clans clash over land and water rights in Somalia
properties.Description: The Marehan and Garre clans clash over land and water rights in Somalia, with 34 fatalities.

properties.Title: Power station damaged, taking down some of Baghdad's water distribution system
properties.Description: A power station in Tarmiyah, Iraq is damaged by a fire, disabling a portion of Baghdad's water-distribution system.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Over 90 dead in Kenya water fight
properties.Description: Police are sent to the northwestern part of Kenya to control a major violent dispute between Kikuyu and Maasai groups over water. More than 20 people are killed in fighting in January. By July, the death toll exceeds 90, principally in the rural center of Turbi. The tensions arise over grazing and water. Maasai herdsmen accuse a local Kikuyu politician of diverting a river to irrigate his farm, de

properties.Title: A bomb prematurely explodes at Mirani Dam in southern Pakistan, killing one attacker and injuring the other
properties.Description: Two militants attempt to detonate an explosive device at the Merani Dam Project offices in southern Pakistan, near Iran. The explosive device detonates prematurely, killing one attacker and injuring the other.

properties.Title: Assailants detonate a bomb at the Haditha Dam on the Euphrates River in Iraq
properties.Description: Assailants detonate a timed IED outside the central transformer room of the Haditha Dam on the Euphrates River in Iraq, damaging the dam. No group claims responsibility for the attack.

properties.Title: Indian border guards and Bangladesh military clash over contruction of infrastructure on the Mahananda River
properties.Description: Indian Border Security Force (BSF) and Bangladesh Rifles (BDR) exchange fire over the construction of embankments on the Mahananda River. Indian officials argue the construction of a concrete embankment by Bangladesh will increase erosion on the Indian side and violate a 1975 agreement on border and river management between the two countries.

properties.Title: A rocket-propelled grenade is fired at the Haditha Dam
properties.Description: Insurgents fire a rocket-propelled grenade at Haditha Dam, Iraq. The grenade strikes power lines before detonating on the dam.

properties.Title: Suspected Taliban militants lay explosives in an apparent attempt to cause failure of the Kajaki Dam in Afghanistan
properties.Description: Twenty suspected Taliban militants access the Kajaki Dam and began to lay explosives in an apparent attempt to cause a dam failure. NATO and Afghan troops arrest the militants prior to detonation of explosives.

properties.Title: Rocket attack damages a Mekorot water station, Israel
properties.Description: Palestinian Islamic Jihad (PIJ) claims responsibility for a rocket attack, which is aimed at Ashkelon, Israel, but lands in an industrial area just south of Ashkelon, damaging a Mekorot water station.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200602030011

properties.Title: Sudanese militia attacks villagers concerned about the impacts of a dam, killing three
properties.Description: Militia of the Merowe Dam Militia Implementation Unit in the Sudan attack a gathering of villagers concerned about the community impacts of the dam at a school in Amri village, killing three farmers and injuring more than 50 others.

properties.Title: Suspected terrorists kill a water tanker driver in Pakistan
properties.Description: Suspected terrorists shoot and kill a water tanker driver and his assistant in Dherdoni who supply water to security forces near the Afghan border in North Waziristan, Pakistan. No group claims responsibility for the attack, but the Taliban is blamed.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200604100009

properties.Title: Taliban attack workers repairing the Kajaki Dam, Afghanistan
properties.Description: Soldiers, police, and workers at Afghanistan’s Kajaki Dam are attacked by Taliban insurgents in an effort to disrupt assistance programs and prevent people from cooperating with the government and foreign forces.

properties.Title: Two bombs explode at a water collection point in India
properties.Description: Two bombs detonate at a water collection point near a village in India. No one is injured from the explosions.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Two water truck drivers killed in Iraq
properties.Description: The drivers of a water truck are killed in Baghdad, Iraq by a gunman while driving for work.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Unsuccessful targeting of water pipeline In Baghdad, Iraq
properties.Description: A water pipeline in Baghdad is targeted by an explosive device placed nearby. However, the explosion does not damage the pipeline.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Violent water clashes in Somali
properties.Description: At least 12 people die and over 20 are wounded in clashes over competition for water and pasture in the Somali border region.

properties.Title: Water and gas supplies disrupted by explosion in Pakistan
properties.Description: Water and gas pipelines in Pir Koh, Pakistan are destroyed, disrupting the delivery of these supplies to nearby communities. The Baloch Liberation Army (BLA) takes responsibility for the explosion.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water clashes kill 40 in Kenya and Ethiopia
properties.Description: At least 40 people die in Kenya and Ethiopia in continuing clashes over water, livestock, and grazing land. Fighting occurs in the southern Ethiopia in the region of Oromo and the northern Kenya Marsabit district.

properties.Title: Water pipelines destroyed by rocket attacks and explosives in Pakistan
properties.Description: Several attacks with rockets and other explosives on pipelines that supply water to a gas plant in Dera Bugti, Pakistan, disrupt gas production.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; Dawn 2006

properties.Title: Water tank drivers killed in Pakistan
properties.Description: Two people driving a water tank in Pakistan are shot and killed. They had delivered water to security forces along the Afghan border.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water utility systems destroyed during conflict in Congo
properties.Description: During the long-running conflict in the Congo, many water-utility systems are attacked and destroyed. By 2006, the World Bank estimates that at least one-third of water utility installations have been looted and completely destroyed.

properties.Title: Planted computer virus used to control chlorine output found in a water-treatment system in Pennsylvania, United States
properties.Description: An unknown individual plants a computer virus in a water-treatment system in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, United States to control chlorine output.
properties.Sources: New Jersey Office of Homeland Security and Preparedness 2009

properties.Title: 200 people attack home of Water Resource Minister in India
properties.Description: A group of around 200 people attack the home residence of Kamlesh Kumar Singh, the Jharkhand Water Resources Minister, in Kamgarpur, Palamu District, India. The minister is not home during the attack.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: An explosion damages a water pipeline supplying water to parts of Sri Lanka's capital, Colombo and surrounding suburbs
properties.Description: An explosion damages a water pipeline that supplies water to parts of Sri Lanka's capital, Colombo, and surrounding suburbs. Tens of thousands of homes are without potable water as a consequence.  No group  claims responsibility but the act of sabotage occurs shortly after the Sri Lankan government reinvokes anti-terrorism legislation.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200610120005

properties.Title: Armed clashes in Yemen over water well
properties.Description: Local media report a struggle between Hajja and Amran tribes over a well located between the two governorates in Yemen. According to news reports, armed clashes between the two sides force many families to leave their homes and migrate. News reports confirm that authorities had arrested 20 people in an attempt to stop the fighting.

properties.Title: Bomb attached to police vehicle carrying water supplies injures three in Sri Lanka
properties.Description: Three police officers are injured after a bomb explodes from underneath a water truck in Vavuniya District, Sri Lanka.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Bomb explodes near water pump station in Pakistan
properties.Description: A bomb explodes near a water pump station in Quetta, Pakistan. No one is injured in the explosion.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Dam protester executed in China
properties.Description: Chinese authorities execute a man who took part in protests against the Pubugou dam in Sichuan province in 2004 (see China 2004 entry). Chen Tao is convicted of killing a policeman, but is executed before legal appeals are completed.

properties.Title: Director of water project killed
properties.Description: The director of the Baqubah water project, Husayn Ali Atiyah, is killed by gunmen. The attack is considered part of a larger effort to undermine the Iraqi reconstruction process.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Drinking water supply for thousands destroyed in Nepal
properties.Description: Several drinking water supply reservoirs and pipelines that provide water for more than 20,000 people are destroyed by explosives in the Narayan and Jajarkot areas of Nepal. The Communist Party of Nepal-Maoists is suspected to be responsible for the attack.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Employee of Iraq's Ministry of Water Resources killed
properties.Description: An employee of the Ministry of Water Resources in Iraq is killed by a gunman.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Explosive device targeting a water main in Iraq detonates early, killing two, wounding one
properties.Description: An explosive device targeting a water main in Kirkuk, Iraq, detonates prematurely, killing two and injuring one of the attackers.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Gas and water pipeline blown up by insurgents in Pakistan
properties.Description: A gas line and water pipeline in the Pir Koh - Dera Bugti District, Pakistan, is blown up by insurgents, resulting in the suspension of water and gas supply to adjacent areas.

properties.Title: Government water official killed in Iraq
properties.Description: A government official working with the water authority in Kirkuk, Iraq is killed.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Gunmen attack the drinking water treatment plant to the U.S. Ayn al-Asad base in Iraq
properties.Description: Gunmen blow up the pipes of a drinking water treatment plant running to the U.S. Ayn al-Asad base in Iraq. Clashes rage between the gunmen and members of the plant's security, and are followed by explosions that lead to the destruction of the plant's water pumps and power generators. No group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200604100025

properties.Title: Lebanon, Israel target water infrastructure in attacks
properties.Description: Hezbollah rockets damage a wastewater plant in Israel. Israeli counter-attacks damage water systems throughout southern Lebanon, including tanks, pipes, pumping stations, and facilities along the Litani River.

properties.Title: Militants wound guards at a water-supply company in Algeria
properties.Description: Militants from the Salafist Group for Call and Combat attack a guard station at a water-supply company in Thenia, near Boumerdès, Algeria. Two guards are wounded in the attack.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Sri Lankan rebels cut water supplies
properties.Description: A major battle between the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam (Tamil Tigers, LTTE) and the Sri Lankan Armed Forces takes place in July and August 2006 around the watershed of the Mavil Aru (Mother River) in the Kallar area of Trincomalee district. The Mavil Aru, a tributary of the Mahaweli River, supplies water to 20,000 villagers in the eastern Sri Lanka. The sluice-gates of the waterway are captur
properties.Sources: British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) 2006b; British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) 2006c; Gutierrez 2006

properties.Title: British soldiers are injured by mines at Kajaki Dam, Afghanistan
properties.Description: Five British soldiers are injured by land mines at Afghanistan's Kajaki Dam on the Helmand River. The mines were left over from the Soviet occupation of Afghanistan.

properties.Title: A forced displacement campaign by the Ethiopian military includes destruction of water wells and other sources
properties.Description: During a violent, forced displacement effort by the Ethiopian military, water wells and other water supply sources are destroyed. The Ethiopian military is seeking to move people out of the country side and into larger towns and cities in order to continue their counterinsurgency fight against the Ogaden National Liberation Front. Numerous sources report executions of those who refuse to cooperate

properties.Title: A review of long-term violence in Guatemala identifies more than 60 environmental activists killed
properties.Description: Research by a professor at the University of Regina, Canada, finds that between 2007 and 2017 at least 61 Guatemalan activists were murdered for their work protecting rivers, lands, and the people who depend on them for their livelihoods.

properties.Title: Attack on a water filtering station leaves at least 33 dead in Diyala, Iraq
properties.Description: An attack on a water-filtering station in the Al-Khalis District of Diyala Governorate in Iraq is thwarted by the Iraqi military and other forces, leaving at least 33 of the attackers dead. The number of deaths on the Iraqi side is not reported.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Australian man murdered in fight over water restrictions
properties.Description: A 36-year-old Australian is charged with murder after killing a man during a fight over water restrictions in Sydney. A number of incidents have been reported following ten years of drought and water restrictions, leading scholars to suggest a "link between persistent urban water restrictions and civil unrest."

properties.Title: Bomb placed on water meter kills four police officers in Colombia, while another under a car injures ten
properties.Description: A bomb placed on a water meter in Cielo Gonzalez Villa, Neiva, Colombia detonates, killing four police officers. On the same day, another bomb placed under a car detonates in front of a building occupied by the local mayor, injuring ten people. The Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) is suspected of the crimes.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018: Kraul 2007

properties.Title: Conflicts in Burkina Faso
properties.Description: Declining rainfall has led to growing fights between animal herders and farmers with competing needs in Burkina Faso. In August, 2007 people are forced to flee their homes by fighting in Zounweogo province.
properties.Sources: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (UNOCHA) 2007

properties.Title: Director-general of water projects killed by gunmen in Iraq
properties.Description: The director-general of water projects in Badush, Iraq is shot and killed by gunmen.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Injuries at protest in Orissa, India
properties.Description: Thousands of farmers breach security and storm the area of Hirakud dam to protest allocation of water to industry. Minor injuries are reported during the conflict between the farmers and police.

properties.Title: Israel's sanctions against Gaza cause water shortages
properties.Description: Israel's sanctions against Gaza cause water shortages and a growing public health risk. In particular, restrictions on fuel, spare parts, and maintenance equipment threaten the functioning of Gaza's already limited water and sanitation system.

properties.Title: Mortar fired at water-treatment plant in Iraq
properties.Description: A mortar is fired at a water treatment plant in Baghdad, Iraq. No casualties are reported.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Protestors block main road in response to water diversion in Egypt
properties.Description: In 2007 and 2008, shortages of drinking water provoke demonstrations in towns and villages in the Nile Delta: participants speak of a "Revolution of the Thirsty." Protestors block the main coastal road after the regional water company diverted water from farming and fishing towns to affluent resort communities. Riot police are sent to quell the disturbances.

properties.Title: Soldiers threaten to throw woman into a well and drown her if she does not comply
properties.Description: A 20-year-old woman reports that prior to being beaten by military forces, she was threatened with drowning in a well if she does not comply. This report is associated with reports soldiers guarding water holes and of other human rights abuses by the Ethiopian military from their campaign to defeat the Ogaden National Liberation Front.

properties.Title: Tampering of bottled water in Toronto, Canada
properties.Description: A Toronto, Canada man previously accused of attempted murder and illegal possession of explosives is charged with eight more counts of attempted murder after allegedly tampering with bottled water, which he injected with an unspecified liquid.

properties.Title: Tigris River polluted after bomb strikes oil pipeline in Iraq
properties.Description: The Tigris River is polluted with oil after a bomb strikes a pipeline near Baiji, Iraq. The river supplies drinking water for nearby communities.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Two people wounded by bomb at local watering hole in Algeria
properties.Description: Two people are wounded when a bomb explodes at a local watering hole in Jijel, Algeria.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Protests against the Kajbar Dam in Sudan turn violent
properties.Description: Villagers of ethnic minority Nubians set to loose their homeland in the Sudan stage protests against Kajbar Dam; four villagers are killed by government militia.

properties.Title: Water distribution facility warehouse burnt down in Thailand
properties.Description: A warehouse of a water distribution facility is burnt down in Yala Province, Thailand. Islamic insurgents seeking independence from Thailand are the suspected arsonists.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Water pipeline and gas pipeline damaged by explosion in Pakistan
properties.Description: Water supply is disrupted when a water pipeline and nearby gas pipeline are blown up in Pakistan.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Workers constructing water pipe attacked; two killed in Iraq
properties.Description: A bomb placed along a road kills two workers and wounds five others as they lay water pipes in Tal Afar, Iraq.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Workers fixing a water pipe killed in Iraq
properties.Description: Two workers fixing a water pipe in Baghdad, Iraq, are killed in an event that is reported as either a drive-by shooting or a bomb explosion. The pipeline was being fixed from an attack a month earlier.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Someone sabotages a Sacramento River water canal by installing unauthorized software and damaging a computer
properties.Description: Someone sabotages a water canal that diverts water from the Sacramento River, California, by installing unauthorized software and damaging a computer.
properties.Sources: New Jersey Office of Homeland Security and Preparedness 2009

properties.Title: The Kajaki Dam is the subject of fighting between coalition forces and Taliban insurgents
properties.Description: In February 2007, the Kajaki Dam is the focus of fighting between coalition forces and Taliban insurgents, as part of Operation Kryptonite. The intention of the operation was to clear the area around the Kajakai Dam of Taliban fighters so the power generation station could be reopened. The Taliban destroy or dismantle parts of the dam.

properties.Title: A geologist working at the Hlaingbwe Dam, Karen Province, Myanmar is killed by a land mine
properties.Description: A geologist from the Electricity Generating Authority of Thailand working at the controversial Hlaingbwe Dam on the Salween River in Myanmar is killed by a land mine near the project site.

properties.Title: Taliban fighters from Pakistan attack a major Afghanistan dam
properties.Description: At least 700 Taliban fighters cross from Pakistan into Afghanistan to attack Kajaki Dam, a major source of electricity, according to reports from a provincial governor.

properties.Title: A former employee of a California canal system is charged with damaging a computer used to divert water to an irrigation canal
properties.Description: In the United States, a former employee of a California canal system is charged with installing unauthorized software and intentionally damaging a computer used to divert water from the Sacramento River to an irrigation canal.

properties.Title: Suspected Islamist separatists detonate an explosive at the Waeng Hydro Station in Thailand
properties.Description: Suspected Islamist separatists detonate an explosive at the Waeng Hydro Station in Thailand. The explosion damages the control station, but causes no injuries.

properties.Title: Attackers fire at the construction site of the Hlaignbwe Dam in Myanmar, killing a Thai worker
properties.Description: Unknown attackers fire mortars or artillery shells at the construction site of the Hlaignbwe Dam on the Salween River in Myanmar, killing a Thai worker. Attackers are suspected to be from the Karen National Union group.

properties.Title: Riots break out in Somaliland when water drilling rig is removed
properties.Description: Riots break out after a water drilling rig that had been drilling water wells for one community in the capital is transferred by government authorities to the western part of the country. Five people died and fifteen are wounded in the demonstration.

properties.Title: Security forces open fire on water privatization protesters in Mali
properties.Description: One person is killed and five injured when security forces open fire on protests over an attempt to privatize water services in Lere, northwestern Mali.

properties.Title: Taliban threatens water supply dam in Pakistan
properties.Description: In October, the Taliban threatens to blow up Warsak Dam, the main water supply for Peshawar, Pakistan during a government offensive in the region.

properties.Title: Unknown gunmen kidnap four Sudanese government drivers of the state water corporation
properties.Description: Unknown gunmen kidnap four Sudanese government drivers of the state water corporation in Um Tajok, Darfur and steal a drilling rig. It is not known if the kidnapped persons and drilling rig are returned.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200803200009

properties.Title: Unknown militants in Afghanistan attack an aid agency's water tanker
properties.Description: Unknown militants fire mortars at an aid agency's water tanker, then kill the driver and steal his tanker in an undisclosed location in eastern Afghanistan. No property damage is reported and no one claims responsibility for the incident.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200805200002

properties.Title: Violent protests in Nigeria over the price of water
properties.Description: A protest over the price of water in Nyanya, Abuja, Nigeria results in violence, including the beating of water vendors.

properties.Title: Water aid workers kidnapped in Somalia
properties.Description: Five aid workers, employed by the charity Water for Life, are kidnapped at gunpoint in Somalia. This is one of the first instances of Somali natives being kidnapped, as well as one of the first instances of a woman being kidnapped.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018; New Humanitarian, The 2008

properties.Title: Water delivery pipelines destroyed in protest over water transfers in Yemen
properties.Description: Pipelines are destroyed in Yemen by locals protesting the extraction of water in Abyan for piping to Aden.

properties.Title: A community organizer in Ecuador working to stop the Baba Dam is killed
properties.Description: In Ecuador, community organizers against the Baba Dam receive death threats. One of the threats materializes when one of the organizers is found dead at the dam site.

properties.Title: A local community in Peru takes control of dam outlet to prevent loss of water supply
properties.Description: Over 100 villagers and farmers from the local community near Lake Paron in Peru use force to remove employees and take over the outlet controls of the lake after the lake had been drained by nearly 50% by the operator of the controls, a subsidiary of Duke Energy International, for hydropower operations.

properties.Title: Assailants attempt to damage a water pump outside of a refugee camp in Darfur
properties.Description: At the Hassa Hissa camp near Zalinjay, Gharb Darfur, Sudan, ten assailants attempt to damage a water pump outside of an internally displaced person (IDP) camp. The armed men open fire on the pump and set fire to the pump and five generators. The assailants fight with occupants of the camp, wounding one. No group claims responsibility, although it is widely believed the Janjaweed Militia is respons
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200812010034

properties.Title: Assailants set fire to two water tanker trucks in Muqur, Badghis, Afghanistan
properties.Description: Assailants set fire to two water tanker trucks in Muqur, Badghis, Afghanistan, killing one driver, wounding two others and destroying the two trucks. No group claims responsibility, although the Taliban is widely believed to be responsible.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200806110020

properties.Title: Bomb detonates in front of drinking water office in Nepal
properties.Description: Unidentified persons detonate a bomb in front of the drinking water office in Tripureshwor, Nepal. No casualties or damage are reported, and no group claims responsibility.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200804050020

properties.Title: China cracks down on Tibet with implications for wide regional water control and management
properties.Description: China launches a political crackdown in Tibet. At least some observers have noted the importance of Tibet for the water resources of China, though the political complications between Tibet and China extend far beyond water. As noted: "Tibet is referred to in some circles as the "world's water tower' ; the Tibetan plateau is home to vast reserves of glaciated water, the sources of 10 of the largest

properties.Title: Confrontations occur along border of Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan over water resources
properties.Description: Disputes over unclear borders and poor communications lead to a series of confrontations over water resources in a border area of Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan. The confrontations with Kyrgyz border guards occur when villagers from Isfara Tajikistan cross into the southern Batken district of Kyrgyzstan to remove a dam blocking an irrigation canal preventing water from reaching the Tajik village of Hoj

properties.Title: Driver of a water tanker truck killed in Afghanistan
properties.Description: A driver of a water tanker truck owned by an aid agency is killed while driving. Local officials place blame on Taliban militants.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Explosive device detonates destroying a tube well in Pakistan
properties.Description: At Kachhi canal project in Lund Nullah near Sui, Balochistan Province, Pakistan, unknown assailants detonate an explosive device destroying a tube well. No casualties or motive are reported. No group claims responsibility.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200804190021

properties.Title: Four killed in Kenya over dispute over boreholes
properties.Description: Fighting over boreholes in arid northern Kenya kills at least four people as competition for resources grows in the drought-hit region between the Murulle and Garre clans in El Wak, Mandera District.

properties.Title: Iraqi water station destroyed
properties.Description: A water station under construction is destroyed in an attack by an unidentified gunman. Explosives are also placed on the road near the project, injuring one person. The station was going to provide potable water to four villages that were at a distance from the Tigris River, east of Dalouiya.

properties.Title: Militants blow up a section of a water pipeline in Iraq
properties.Description: Militants blow up a section of a water pipeline in Baghdad, Iraq. The explosion tears an 18-inch hole in the pipe that carries drinking water to Baghdad's Adhamiyah, Rusafa, and Karrada districts. No casualties are reported and no group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200810300021

properties.Title: Multiple civilians killed by remotely-triggered bombs targeting water trucks in Ethiopia
properties.Description: At least two water trucks being used to deliver supplies to the Ethiopian army are targeted by remotely-triggered bombs. At least four civilians are killed and another eight are injured.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Tipaimukh Dam, India is attacked by Hmar militants
properties.Description: Hmar People’s Convention-Democracy (HPC-D) militants attack and damage dam equipment installed at the Tipaimukh Multi-Purpose Hydel Project in the state of Manipur, India. The HPC-D said in a statement that the ongoing drilling work at the site is against the land, rights, and dignity of the Hmar people.
properties.Sources: Nabi Dar and Alam 2022; U.S Department of Homeland Security 2012

properties.Title: Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan threaten to contaminate local water resources
properties.Description: The Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) threatens to contaminate local water resources in a letter faxed to the Directorate of Military Lands and Cantonments in Rawalpindi, Pakistan. The letter stated that the TTP had procured 200 liters of a poisonous material that would be used to contaminate water sources and reservoirs unless the Army stopped military operations in South Waziristan.

properties.Title: Village attacked in Ethiopian water supply conflict
properties.Description: Ethiopian Somalis attack a Borana community in the Oromia region over ownership of a new borehole being drilled on the disputed border between them. Three people from the Oromia village of Kafa are killed and seven injured, and the entire community driven from their homes. The drilling rig is destroyed, as well.

properties.Title: Water trucks contaminated with cyanide in Afghanistan
properties.Description: Water trucks supplying water to U.S. military forces in Afghanistan are contaminated with cyanide.

properties.Title: A bomb placed at water station kills six, including five children, in Khost City, Afghanistan
properties.Description: A remote control bomb placed at a water distribution station detonates, killing six, including five children, in Khost City, Afghanistan.

properties.Title: An improvised explosive device attached to a water tanker explodes in Iraq
properties.Description: An improvised explosive device attached to a water tanker in the al-Mahawii district of the Babel province explodes, wounding the driver. The blast causes severe damage to the tanker. No one claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200912110015

properties.Title: Assailants detonate an improvised explosive device targeting a civilian water truck
properties.Description: On Sunday morning, in Marawara, Konar, Afghanistan, assailants detonate an improvised explosive device targeting a civilian water truck, wounding four civilians and damaging one truck. No group claims responsibility.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200903070013

properties.Title: Attack on electrical grid in Pakistan cuts off water for multiple water wells
properties.Description: Insecurity in northwestern Pakistan leads to a rocket attack on on the electrical grid cutting off 36 bore-holes that supplied water to Mingora town, Pakistan. Citizens resort to collecting water from contaminated surface-water sources, resulting in an outbreak of diarrhoeal disease.

properties.Title: Bomb damages water treatment plant, leaving community without water in Colombia
properties.Description: A bomb planted at a water treatment plant explodes damaging the plant and wounding two police officers in Villavicencio, Colombia. The town is left without water supply.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Brazilian landowners fight with indigenous tribe over land and water
properties.Description: A dispute over water and land consider sacred by the indigenous Guarani-Kaiowa tribe begins with nearby landowners attempting to gain control over disputed land. The dispute continues until at least 2012 and leads to several deaths.

properties.Title: During kidnapping by Taliban in Afghanistan, water tank destroyed
properties.Description: A group of four employees of a construction company in Kunar province, Afghanistan are kidnapped by the Taliban. The kidnappers also destroy a water tank during the attack.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Energy and Water Department attacked by suicide bombers, killing six and injuring dozens in Afghanistan
properties.Description: Six civilians die and approximately 30 others are injured when two suicide bombers attack the building of the Energy and Water Department in Khost City, Afghanistan.
properties.Sources: RAND Database of Worldwide Terrorism Incidents 2018

properties.Title: Homemade bomb is planted inside the gates of the Metro Water District, Philippines
properties.Description: On Wednesday, ordnance experts defuse a homemade bomb planted on a bench inside the gates of the Metro Water District, a government-run building at Cotabato, Philippines. No damage or casualties occur. No group claims of responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 200904290018

properties.Title: Hydroelectric construction company hires armed private security to quell local opposition and violence in Guatemala
properties.Description: In the Q'anjob'al and Chug Territory of northern Huehuetenango, Guatemala, three proposed hydroelectric projects supported by the national government incite strong local opposition. The hydroelectric construction company hires armed private security personnel to quell confrontations and acts of vandalism and violence over three years.

properties.Title: Man killed in Mumbai, India protest over water cuts
properties.Description: Police clash with hundreds of Mumbai, India residents protesting water cuts. One man is killed and a dozen others injured. Mumbai authorities are forced to ration supplies after the worst monsoon season in decades.

properties.Title: North Korea dam release kills six in South Korea
properties.Description: Without previous warning, North Korea releases 40 million cubic meters of water from the Hwanggag dam, causing a flash flood on the Imjin River. In South Korea, at least 6 fisherman and campers are drowned. North Korea claims that the water had to be urgently released and promises to warn the South of future releases. South Korea fears that North Korea could use the water of the dam as a weapon du

properties.Title: One dead in South Yemen protest over water cuts
properties.Description: At least one Yemeni is killed and three wounded in clashes with police in Aden in southern Yemen over the cutting off of water.

properties.Title: Police use live ammunition against demonstrators protesting water shortages in Somaliland
properties.Description: Police use live ammunition against demonstrators in the southern part of Hargeisa. Residents of Ahmed Dhagah district in Hargeisa, mostly women and children, take to the streets protesting severe water shortages.

properties.Title: Drought and water inequities spark killings India
properties.Description: A family in Madhya Pradesh state in India is killed by a mob for illegally drawing water from a municipal pipe. Others run to collect water for themselves before the pipe runs out. Drought and inequality in water distribution lead to increasing conflict in the region. Indian media report more than 50 violent clashes in the state capital, Bhopal during May alone. Since January, 12 people are killed

properties.Title: Environmental activist in a region flooded by Tucurui Dam killed in Brazil
properties.Description: After a region of Brazil was flooded by the Tucurui Dam, Raimundo Nonato do Carmo helped to lead the fight for environmental protection of the flooded and destroyed land.  He was shot in a drive-by attack and killed in 2009.

properties.Title: Local anti-dam activist in Colombia killed in his home
properties.Description: A community leader involved in anti-dam protests that threatened the Magdalena River in Colombia, Honorio Llorente Merendez, is shot and killed in his home in Puente Sogamoso.

properties.Title: Suspected Maoists set fire to the power station at the Balimela Dam, India
properties.Description: Suspected Maoists set fire to the power station at the Balimela Dam, India, damaging equipment and water intake tunnels. The attackers say the attack is in retaliation for the failure to provide electricity and communications utilities to local villages.

properties.Title: Severe flooding strengthens militant groups in Pakistan
properties.Description: Militant groups in Pakistan take advantage of severe flooding to reorganize and gain political power. Military resources in the country have been diverted to humanitarian relief efforts.

properties.Title: The Myitsone Dam, Myanmar is attacked by locals opposed to dam projects, killing at least one
properties.Description: Myanmar’s Myitsone Dam, in Kachin State, is the site of major local opposition to dam projects. In 2010, 10 bombs explode around the dam site, killing at least one Chinese worker. Construction is suspended by the Myanmar government in 2011 in response to a widespread opposition campaign.

properties.Title: Two unknown gunmen riding a motorbike fire upon the driver of a water tanker
properties.Description: On an evening in January, two unknown gunmen riding a motorbike fire upon the driver of a water tanker in Hariana Colony, Karachi, Pakistan. The driver, Noor Shah, is killed and the tanker rams into a moving auto-rickshaw with four people on board. Eight-year-old Unair dies and his younger brother is wounded. Both attackers escape. No group claims responsibility.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201001310008

properties.Title: Unidentified militants detonate an improvised explosive device targeting a water tanker
properties.Description: On Thursday, in the town of al-Qaim in Iraq's Anbar province, a driver and two civilians are wounded and a water tanker truck is damaged when unidentified militants detonate an improvised explosive device targeting a water tanker. No group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201005060012

properties.Title: Violent confrontations occur in the West Bank over Israeli actions limiting water supply to new West Bank development
properties.Description: The construction and completion of a billion-dollar new middle-class Palestinian city of Rawabi in the West Bank is delayed for years while the Israeli government withholds access to basic water supplies. The delay is due, in part, to a larger dispute over the actions of the Israeli-Palestinian Joint Water Committee. In March 2015, a limited supply of water to Rawabi is finally approved, sufficien

properties.Title: Violent water protest in India
properties.Description: A protest about water shortages in the National Capital Territory of Delhi in India leads to violence. Erratic water supply and cutoffs in the Kondli area of Mayur Vihar in East Delhi causes a violent protest and several injuries.

properties.Title: Water disputes between Iran and Afghanistan escalate
properties.Description: Disputes over water between Iran and Afghanistan are escalating. One Afghan newspaper, Weesa, suggests that Iran blocked the transport of fuel oil to Afghanistan in 2010 as a means to put more pressure on the country over water. An Iranian editorial calls for bolder action by the Foreign Ministry and states that any aid to Afghanistan should be linked to "Iran's rights to water."
properties.Sources: Aman 2013; Etedaalier 2011; Muhammadi 2011; Radio Free Europe 2011

properties.Title: An improvised explosive device detonates at a potable water plant in Iraq
properties.Description: In the Abu Ghurayb neighborhood of Baghdad city, Baghdad province Iraq, two government employees are killed and another is wounded when an improvised explosive device detonates at a potable water plant. The amount of damage from the attack is unknown. No group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201012020006

properties.Title: Army troops in Colombia locate and defuse three bombs to be used against a town's aqueduct
properties.Description: Army troops locate and defuse three bombs suspected to be planted by the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia's (FARC) 6th Front in Cajibio, Cauca, Colombia. According to authorities, the explosives were going to be used against the town's aqueduct. No casualties or damages are reported and no group claims responsibility for the attempted bombing attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201001280009

properties.Title: Bomb in water truck kills three in Afghanistan
properties.Description: A remote-controlled bomb hidden in a water truck kills three people, including two children, in the eastern Afghan province of Khost, which borders Pakistan.

properties.Title: Fighting in Pakistan over irrigation water
properties.Description: More than 100 are dead and scores injured following two weeks of tribal fighting in Parachinar in the Kurram region of Pakistan, near the Afghanistan border. The conflict over irrigation water began as the Shalozan Tangi tribe cut off supplies to the Shalozan tribe. Some report that al Qaeda may be involved; others claim sectarian violence is to blame as one group is Sunni Muslim and the other Shi

properties.Title: Guatemalan mine protestors shot
properties.Description: Two unidentified gunmen on a motorbike shoot an activist protesting the impacts of mining on water quality and local water rights in Guatemala.

properties.Title: 2010-2022: Violent dispute over corporate mine in Honduras national park leads to 100+ deaths and many arrests
properties.Description: From 2010 to 2022 in Honduras, a major violent dispute ensues over the development of a mine controlled by the Dinant Corporation in Carlos Escaleras National Park. The dispute leads to more than one hundred deaths, several attacks on community activists and environmentalists, and many arrests. The mine threatens land and water resources.

properties.Title: Injuries and a death are reported during protests over water contamination from gold mining near Lake Conococha in Peru
properties.Description: Communities concerned with water contamination of Lake Conococha, both a water source and cultural icon, are met with violence from local police forces as they protest gold mining in the region. At least one person is reported dead and four others injured from the interaction.

properties.Title: Militants blow up a water supply tube-well
properties.Description: In Lakaro area of Mohmand, Federally Administered Tribal Areas, Pakistan, Tehrik-e-Taliban (TTP) militants blow up a water supply tube-well. TTP claims responsibility for the attack saying the tube-well is used for provision of drinking water to Security Forces.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201011040001

properties.Title: Militants blow up pump houses belonging to the Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL) and to the railways
properties.Description: On Monday, in the Karrampada area of West Singbhum, Jharkhand, India, suspected Communist Party of India - Maoist (CPI-Maoist) militants blow up two pump houses, one belonging to the Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL) and the other to the railways. Water supply is affected in the area as a result of the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201004190016

properties.Title: More than 30 armed men detonate an improvised explosive device at the electric transformer of a pump house in the Philippines
properties.Description: At night in Victorias City, Negros Occidental, Philippines, more than 30 armed men detonate an improvised explosive device at the electric transformer of Malago Pump House owned by the Victorias Milling Company sugar refinery before setting it on fire. The damage is initially placed at  P400,000 ($9,246 USD). No casualties are reported. New Peoples Army militants claim responsibility.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201010050009

properties.Title: Pakistan irrigation dispute kills 116
properties.Description: A water dispute in Pakistan's tribal region leads to 116 deaths. In early September, the Mangal tribe cut off the supply of irrigation water to lands used by the neighboring Tori tribe, leading to fighting.

properties.Title: Protestors over land and water killed and injured in India
properties.Description: At least three deaths and dozens of injuries are reported during protests over land and water given away for a power plant in Sompeta in Srikakulam district in Andhra Pradesh, India.

properties.Title: Pakistani police fire on protesters who are demanding share of royalties from a new dam project, killing two
properties.Description: While a new dam is under construction in Pakistan, protests erupt in Chilas where people are demanding their share of royalties from the dam. Two protestors are killed, others are injured, and property of the state is damaged and destroyed.

properties.Title: A series of attacks on hydropower facilities in Myanmar are reported, including on the Thawt Yin Kha Dam
properties.Description: Grenade attacks on the construction site of the Thawt Yin Kha Dam in Myanmar injure at least four. The attacks are claimed by the Karen National Liberation Army with the intent of destroying the project.

properties.Title: Local police find remains of Molotov Cocktails detonated at the Black Rock Dam, Connecticut, United States
properties.Description: Local police officials discover the remains of two devices that had detonated on an access road near the dam. Police identified the devices as Molotov Cocktails—IEDs made from gasoline-filled bottles. No injuries or damage to the dam are reported.

properties.Title: Gunmen kill two guards and detonate four explosives at the Baksanskaya Hydropower Plant, Russia, destroying two generators
properties.Description: Four gunmen attack a police station outside the Baksan Hydropower Plant, Russia, killing two facility guards and detonating four explosives, destroying two generators. A fifth explosive was later neutralized. Officials attribute the attack to the Baksan Dzhamaat group and two members are killed five days after the attack.

properties.Title: Solomon Islands women attacked while fetching water
properties.Description: Amnesty International reports that women in the slums of the Solomon Islands must walk over a kilometer to fetch clean water and are "continually harassed, attacked, and raped." A survey shows that 92 percent of households do not have a tap in their home, and that local water sources are often polluted, forcing women to walk through areas which are unsafe.

properties.Title: Unidentified militants blow up a tube-well in Pakistan
properties.Description: On Friday, in Doog village in South Waziristan, Federally Administered Tribal Areas, Pakistan, unidentified militants blow up a tube-well, resulting in no casualties and causing an unknown amount of property damage. No group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201104010003

properties.Title: Unidentified militants blow up a water pump in India
properties.Description: In Mogahataburu village in West Singhbhum, Jharkhand, India, unidentified militants blow up a water pump, causing an unknown amount of property damage but resulting in no casualties. The specific motive for the attack is unknown; however, it is believed the bombing is to protest the ongoing operation against militants in Saranda forest. No group claims responsibility, but it is suspected that Comm
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201103180002

properties.Title: Yemen damages citizens' water tanks during uprising
properties.Description: Violence in Yemen's capital Sana'a leads to "acute water and power shortages, forcing residents to rely on power generators and buy water extracted from wells and sold on a thriving black market." The violence arose during the Yemeni uprising that occurred during the Arab Spring protests across the Middle East. During the violence, government soldiers shelled neighborhoods and destroyed many rooft

properties.Title: Residential water tanks and water pipes in Dara'a, Syria are deliberately damaged by military and security forces
properties.Description: Residential water tanks and water pipes in Dara'a, Syria are deliberately damaged by military and security forces.

properties.Title: Drought contributes to tensions and the civil war in Syria
properties.Description: Severe political conflict in Syria has been aggravated by a multi-year drought gripping the region. More than 1.5 million people "mostly farmers and their families" have moved to cities and their outskirts. In 2008, US diplomats in Syria warn that the influx of rural people to cities "could act as a multiplier on social and economic pressures already at play and undermine stability in Syria." Poli
properties.Sources: Mohtadi 2012; Worth 2010; Femia and Werrell 2012; Gleick 2014

properties.Title: Four famers killed in Tanzania over water resource dispute
properties.Description: Conflicts over water are increasing in the Pangani River Basin in northeastern Tanzania between farmers and herders over dwindling water resources. In 2011 in Mbuguni village, four farmers are hacked to death by angry Maasai morans (warriors) as they tried to stop a group of cattle from trampling on maize seedlings.

properties.Title: Gaddafi cuts of water to Libya's capital
properties.Description: During the 2011 Libyan Civil War, forces loyal to dictator Muammar Gaddafi gain control of a water operations center and cut off water supply to the capital. The system controls Libya's Great Manmade River, which is a system of pumps, pipes, and canals that brings water from distant aquifers to Tripoli and other cities. Half the country is left without running water, prompting the UN and neighbori

properties.Title: Hacker targets water plant in South Houston, Texas
properties.Description: A hacker targets a water plant in South Houston following earlier news (later discounted) of an electronic attack on an Illinois water plant, reportedly to raise awareness of concerns over lax security for critical infrastructure. The breach occurs after the attackers hack into supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) software used by the utility, and uses IP addresses that originated in R

properties.Title: Iran reportedly attempts to enlist the Taliban to blow up a dam on the Helmand River in Afghanistan
properties.Description: In 2011, a Taliban commander reportedly claims that Iran offered him $50,000 to blow up the Kamal Khan dam in Afghanistan on the Helmand River, shared with Iran.

properties.Title: Israel destroys Palestinians' water pumps and wells
properties.Description: Israel's military destroys nine water tanks in the Bedouin village of Amniyr in the South Hebron Hills, in the West Bank, Palestine. Later, soldiers destroy pumps and wells in the Jordan Valley villages of Al-Nasriyah, Al-Aqrabiyah, and Beit Hassan.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers destroy wells in attack on West Bank village
properties.Description: Israelis from nearby settlements attack Qasra, a West Bank village of 6,000, destroying crops and a water well. Attackers previously burned down a mosque and damaged hundreds of olive trees.

properties.Title: Militants set fire to a water well and two water pumps in Somalia
properties.Description: Militants set fire to a water well and two water pumps on the outskirts of Garbaharey, Gedo, Somalia. No casualties are reported and the attack causes an unknown amount of damage. No group claims responsibility, but the militant group al-Shabaab is thought to be responsible for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201108270005

properties.Title: Multiple attacks on Libyan water networks disrupt supply.
properties.Description: NATO bombs a plant that builds pipelines for the Great Manmade River project that carries water from southern groundwater to coastal cities. Major water networks are disrupted in this conflict, which affects access to safe drinking water, sanitation, and hygiene throughout Libya. Armed groups attack water wells cutting off water to civilians.

properties.Title: NATO attacks water pipe factory in Libya
properties.Description: NATO forces attack a factory that produces pipes for the "Great Man-Made River" water-supply pipeline near Brega, Libya, reporting that it was used as a base for military operations and the launching of missiles.
properties.Sources: Center for Research on Globalization (CRG) 2013; Human Rights Investigations (HRI) 2011; Water Technology n.d.

properties.Title: Guards at the Machlagho Dam construction site in Afghanistan are attacked and killed
properties.Description: Armed militants attack and kill guards at the Machlagho Dam construction site in Afghanistan.

properties.Title: Rocket-propelled grenades are fired at the Thawt Yin Kha Dam in Myanmar
properties.Description: Unknown attackers fire rocket-propelled grenades at the Thawt Yin Kha dam construction site in Myanmar.

properties.Title: A water tank is damaged by a homemade bomb in Pakistan
properties.Description: Sometime during the night between December 16 and December 17, 2011, a water tank is damaged by a homemade bomb in the area of Mandeo in the district of Bannu, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.  No deaths or injuries occur.  No claim of responsibility is reported.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201112170024

properties.Title: A landmine explodes targeting a tanker providing water to Transitional Federal Government (TFG) soldiers, Somalia
properties.Description: A landmine explodes in Luuq town, Gedo Region, Somalia. The landmine is targeting a tanker providing water to Transitional Federal Government (TFG) soldiers. Three people are killed and an unknown number of people are injured in the attack. No group claims responsibility for the incident, although unnamed sources suspect Al-Shabaab.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201201150014

properties.Title: A water tanker is damaged when it is targeted by a roadside bomb
properties.Description: A water tanker is damaged when it is targeted by a roadside bomb in Khash Rod district, Nimruz province, Afghanistan. One person is killed and another wounded in the attack, for which no group claims responsibility.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201203170018

properties.Title: Afghani terrorists poison water at girls' school
properties.Description: Up to 150 schoolgirls are reported sickened by poison in a school water supply in an intentional attack thought to be carried out by religious conservatives opposed to the education of women.

properties.Title: An explosive device detonates and damages a tube well and surrounding area in Pakistan
properties.Description: An explosive device detonates and damages a tube well, a transmission line, and a mosque in Lakki Marwat city, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, Pakistan. There are no casualties reported, and no group claims responsibility for this incident. A school is also damaged in an explosion in the same location on this day.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201203150002

properties.Title: An explosive device detonates in a canal in the Philippines
properties.Description: An explosive device detonates in a canal in barangay Poblacion, M'lang town, North Cotabato province, Philippines. No casualties are reported in the blast. No group claims responsibility for this incident; however, police suspect the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Movement (BIFM).
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201208110008

properties.Title: An explosive device detonates near a water pipeline in Afghanistan
properties.Description: An explosive device detonates near a water pipeline in Pul-e-Alam district, Logar province, Afghanistan. Five people, including two Afghan police officers, are killed in the attack, and four people, including two Afghan police officers, are injured. No group claims responsibility; however, one person is arrested in connection with the incident and admits to remotely detonating the device.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201208010047

properties.Title: An explosive device planted in a water spring detonates as a group of children are grazing animals in Afghanistan
properties.Description: An explosive device planted in a water spring detonates as a group of children are grazing animals in Taywara district, Ghor province, Afghanistan. Seven children are killed in the blast. No group claims responsibility for the incident; however, sources attribute it to the Taliban.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201207240010; Shah 2012

properties.Title: An explosive device targeting a well in Pakistan detonates prematurely as the assailant is planting it
properties.Description: An explosive device detonates prematurely as the assailant is planting it in Wocha Khwara village, Federally Administered Tribal Areas, Pakistan. The device is targeting a fresh water well; the assailant is killed in the blast. No group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201205040033

properties.Title: Angry demonstrators protest over water supply in Tunisia
properties.Description: In June 18-19, demonstrators demanding a solution to a dried up water supply block the main road near Tozeur, Tunisia, leading to clashes with police.

properties.Title: Assailants bomb a government water tank in Pakistan
properties.Description: Assailants bomb a government water tank in the Aka Khel area of Bara tehsil, Federally Administered Tribal Areas, Pakistan. There are no reported casualties; however, the water tank is destroyed in the blast. No group claims responsibility for the incident; however, police arrest one individual in connection with the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201212060017

properties.Title: Assailants fire a mortar round, striking a water facility
properties.Description: Assailants fire a mortar round, striking a water facility in Mosul city, Nineveh governorate, Iraq. One security guard is killed in the attack, and the facility is damaged. This is one of 32 coordinated incidents across Iraq on the same day. Al-Qa'ida in Iraq claims responsibility for the attacks, stating that they are in retaliation for the torture and genocide that Sunni civilians face in Iraqi
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201202230016

properties.Title: Assailants open fire on security personnel escorting a water tanker convoy
properties.Description: Assailants open fire on security personnel escorting a Freeport Indonesia water tanker convoy in Mimika district, Papua province, Indonesia. There are no reported casualties, but the vehicles are damaged. This is one of two coordinated attacks on this convoy on this day, neither of which are claimed by any group.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201204140023

properties.Title: Attack on construction facilities at Turkish dams
properties.Description: 22 trucks are set on fire at dam sites in Turkey, in attacks attributed to Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK) militants.

properties.Title: Brazilian landowners poison tribe's water
properties.Description: Brazil's federal police respond to reports that water used by the indigenous Guarani-Kaiowa tribe was poisoned by nearby landowners. The dispute began in 2009 and has led to the deaths of three tribesmen, who say the water runs through sacred land.

properties.Title: Chinese protestors challenge Japanese factory for discharging contaminated wastewater
properties.Description: In July, thousands of protesters take to the streets in Qidong, Jiangsu Province to challenge a pipeline from a Japanese-owned paper factory that would discharge contaminated wastewater into the sea, potentially polluting a nearby fishery, and contaminating drinking water. Demonstrators clash with police and there are reports of damages to government buildings, cars, and other property. Fourteen p

properties.Title: Deaths in water conflicts in Tanzania
properties.Description: Violence between farmers and pastoralists expands in Tanzania's southeastern Rufiji valley, a region hit by drought. A farmer is killed in a conflict with a herdsman over access to water in the southern regions of Lindi and Mtwara. Five more people die and many more are injured in subsequent violence. According to local sources, violence has worsened during the prolonged drought.

properties.Title: Demonstrations turn violent over water and power shortages in Guinea
properties.Description: Demonstrations over water and power shortages in Conakry, Fria, and Dinguiraye, Guinea, turn violent, with dozens injured.

properties.Title: Disgruntled Egyptian irrigators kidnap tourists
properties.Description: Farmers from the Abu Simbel region in Egypt hold over 200 tourists hostage to protest inadequate irrigation water. The farmers capture the tourists after they visit nearby monuments, but release them after officials agree to a temporary release of water.

properties.Title: Egypt and Sudan allegedly target Ethiopia dam
properties.Description: Information is leaked about an alleged secret agreement that would allow Egypt to build an air base in Sudan to attack the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD). Egypt is concerned that the dam, under construction in Ethiopia just upstream of Sudan on the Blue Nile, would reduce flows into its territory. The news reports, strongly denied by Egypt, claim that Sudan would allow Egypt to launch atta

properties.Title: Escalating rhetoric and disputes over dams in central Asia
properties.Description: Tensions escalate over two proposed dams in central Asia: Kambarata-1 in Kyrgyzstan and the Rogun Dam in Tajikistan. These dams could affect water supplies in the downstream nations of Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan, and Kazakhstan. Uzbekistan's president, Islam Karimov, says the dams could cause "not just serious confrontation, but even wars."

properties.Title: Ethnic conflict in Kenya fueled by water
properties.Description: Extensive violence over water is reported in Kenya, with approximately 50 deaths in clashes between farmers and cattle herders. The conflict is part of a long-running dispute between Pokomo farmers and Orma, semi-nomadic cattle herders, over land and water. The current conflict is being exacerbated by Kenyan and foreign investment in vast tracts of land for food and biofuel cultivation, putting pr

properties.Title: Farmers clash with police over water transfers from Isfahan to Yazd, Iran. A water diversion pipe is destroyed.
properties.Description: Farmers  in Varzaneh clash with police and break a water pipe that transports 50 million cubic meters of water per year from Isfahan to the neighboring province of Yazd, Iran.

properties.Title: Husband and wife are murdered in Mexico for their defense of local water resources
properties.Description: Ismael Solorio Urrutia and his wife Manuela Solis Contreras are killed, likely for their defense of water near their community in Buenaventura, Chihuahua, Mexico, and their opposition to the El Cascabel mining company, a subsidiary of Canada's Mag Silver Corporation.

properties.Title: In Somalia, attackers shoot at and set fire to the town's water pump, destroying the only viable water source
properties.Description: Assailants attack the police station in El Adde town, Gedo region, Somalia. The assailants open fire on the station, causing the Transitional Federal Government (TFG) forces posted there to return fire. There are no reported casualties in the incident. The attackers also shoot at and set fire to the town's water pump, destroying the only viable water source. No group claims responsibility, but loc
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201206030027

properties.Title: Indonesian mobs battle over water source
properties.Description: Violence over access to a water source in Maluku, Indonesia. Rival mobs from two villages attack one another "with sharp weapons, guns and explosives" causing several deaths and injuries.

properties.Title: Injuries during protest over dam releases in India's Cauvery River
properties.Description: Thousands of farmers in Karnataka try to prevent the release of water from two dams (Krishna Raja Sagar and Kabini) on the Kaveri River. Injuries to protestors and police are reported. The water releases are ordered by the Indian Supreme Court, which requires Karnataka to deliver water downstream state of Tamil Nadu despite severe drought. The dispute continues later in the year when Karnataka aga

properties.Title: Iran is accused of efforts to prevent construction of Kamal Khan Dam, Afghanistan
properties.Description: Shakila Hakimi, a member of the Nimroz provincial council in Afghanistan, accuses Iran in 2012 of conducting an insurgency in order to prevent construction of the Kamal Khan Dam.
properties.Sources: Aman 2013; Etedaalier 2011; Muhammadi 2011; Radio Free Europe 2011

properties.Title: Islamist kill dam guards in Afghanistan
properties.Description: Islamist militants execute militia members defending the Machalgho Dam in eastern Afghanistan. The dam is being developed for irrigation and local power supply. This dispute is one of several surrounding the international waters of Afghanistan, Iran, and Pakistan, which share several rivers.

properties.Title: Killings in water conflicts in Brazil
properties.Description: Northeastern Brazil sees growing conflicts after severe drought reduces water availability. News agencies report that one person a day is being killed from "water wars," which involve locals fighting over scarce supplies.

properties.Title: Militants block work and attack water systems at the Wular Dam construction site in India
properties.Description: Militants block work at the Wular Conservation Project (Wular Dam) construction site in the Baramulla District of India and attack and sabotage water systems, and flood protection works in northern Kashmir. They attack engineers and workers and detonate explosives at the unfinished Tulbul Navigation Lock/Wular Dam - a project opposed by Pakistan. Indian officials suggest that these militants may h

properties.Title: Moroccan villagers cut off water supply to local mine fearing shortage and pollution
properties.Description: A group of Moroccan villagers, including many women and children try to stop the water supply to a local silver mine. They shut down some of the main wells that supply water for mining citing concerns about water allocation and pollution.
