The facility in Imiter, a Berber village nestled in the High Atlas mountains, has been accused of drawing more than its fair share of water and polluting what it

properties.Title: Police kill gold mine protestors in Peru
properties.Description: Several incidents of protests, injuries, and deaths are reported in regions of Peru where residents oppose large mines because of concerns over water quality and water rights. Police kill four protestors in clashes over the proposed Canadian-operated $5 billion dollar Minas Conga gold mine.

properties.Title: Protestors burn buildings at Belo Monte Dam site, Brazil
properties.Description: Work on the controversial $13 billion Belo Monte dam is halted after protesters burn buildings at three dam sites.

properties.Title: Protestors concerned about water quality and water rights are injured and 2 killed at Peru's Tintaya mine
properties.Description: Protests because of concerns over water quality and water rights around the Xstrata Tintaya copper mine lead to two deaths and 50 injuries.

properties.Title: Somali insurgents poison peacekeepers' well
properties.Description: Somali Al Shabaab insurgents poison a well and damage water infrastructure near the port city of Kismayo, Somalia. Insurgents are fighting against Kenyan peacekeeping troops participating in the African Union mission in Somalia.

properties.Title: South Africans riot over water and power
properties.Description: Protesters in poor communities of Cape Town, South Africa riot over inadequate water and power. Hundreds burn tires, destroy cars, and throw rocks at police in anger over the lack of basic services.

properties.Title: Syrian Civil War battle damages Aleppo pipeline
properties.Description: During the Syrian Civil War, the major pipeline delivering water to the city of Aleppo is badly damaged. The city of three million suffers severe shortages of drinking water.

properties.Title: Two explosive devices placed in a well are discovered and defused in Pakistan
properties.Description: Two explosive devices placed in a well are discovered and defused in Nar Raza Khan village, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, Pakistan. No group claims responsibility for the incident.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201208130026

properties.Title: Uzbekistan cuts gas over Tajik dam project
properties.Description: Uzbekistan cuts natural gas deliveries to Tajikistan in retaliation over a Tajik hydroelectric dam which Uzbeks say will disrupt water supplies. Gas flows resumed after a new contract is signed.

properties.Title: Villagers protest contaminated water in Egypt
properties.Description: Villagers from the Nile Delta province of Menoufia north of Cairo briefly lock the Egyptian health minister and a provincial governor inside a hospital room in protest over contaminated water caused hundreds of residents to fall ill. Egypt's state-run news agency MENA says the number of sick people reached more than 400. The two are released with police intervention.

properties.Title: Violence between camel herders and farmers over access to water and concerns about water contamination leave one dead in Kenya
properties.Description: Several farmers from Mwatate, Kenya are attacked, one killed, by camel herders after attempts to chase camels from agricultural land. The camel herders have moved to the area to find land and water for their stock. Farmers are also concerned about the potential spread of disease and pollution of the local water sources from camel feces and urine.

properties.Title: Violence over water in Nairobi, Kenya's slums
properties.Description: Violence, including several deaths, occurs during disputes over access to water in the poorest slums around Nairobi, Kenya.

properties.Title: Violent conflict among Kenyan and Ugandan herders
properties.Description: Tensions lead to violence between Uganda and Kenya after Kenyan Pokot herdsmen cross the border seeking water and pasture. In October, the Ugandan government sends 5,000 soldiers to control violence among pastoralists from the two countries.

properties.Title: Violent protests over food and water in Mauritania
properties.Description: Protests and violence over water shortages occur in the capital of Mauritania, Nouakchott. By July 2012, over 70,000 Malian refuges were seeking asylum in Mauritania, putting pressure on scarce food and water supplies.

properties.Title: Violent protests over water shortages in Egypt
properties.Description: Public protests over drinking and irrigation water shortages take place across Egypt. Several protests turn violent: in Beni Suef, one person is killed and many injured during a conflict over irrigation water; in Minya, villagers clash with officials over water shortages and water pollution; in Faiyum, hundreds of people protesting water shortages block a highway and set fires.

properties.Title: Violent protests over water shortages in New Delhi
properties.Description: Scuffles and protests break out around New Delhi during the summer of 2012 as residents surround water delivery trucks and fight over water. The summer is the hottest in 33 years, leading to extensive energy and water shortages.

properties.Title: Water-related violence in South Sudan refugee camp
properties.Description: Violence breaks out at water points in the Jamam refugee camp in South Sudan. Medicines Sans Frontieres reports that as many as 10 refugees die every day because of water shortages at refugee camps in South Sudan.

properties.Title: Women assaulted for attempting to take well water in India
properties.Description: Women from the village of Rasooh are reportedly assaulted for attempting to take water from a village well. The tensions over water are related to long-standing tensions between caste groups but have been worsened by drought and water shortage.

properties.Title: Four protesters against the Barro Blanco Dam are killed after blocking the Inter-American Highway in Panama
properties.Description: Three protesters, Mauricio Mendez, Frankline Javilla, and an unnamed person are killed by government forces as they are blocking the Inter-American Highway in Panama in protest of the Barro Blanco Dam.

properties.Title: One dead and two injured during a confrontation over a dam project in Guatemala, sparking protests
properties.Description: On May 1, 2012, two security guards hired by a dam-building company are suspected of shooting three community members from Santa Cruz Barillas, Guatemala. One of the community members,  Andres Pedro Miguel, dies, and two others are injured. The community members had been actively working to prevent the dam from being built. The death of Sr. Miguel sparks protests across the region. The Guatemalan

properties.Title: Organizer against dam projects in Honduras is killed in his home by police
properties.Description: Police shoot and kill Santos Alberto Dominguez Benitez, a community organizer against local dam projects in Intibuca, Honduras.

properties.Title: Man with known opposition to a dam in Bukidnon, Philippines, is shot and killed
properties.Description: A man who is known for his opposition to the Pulangi V dam project in Bukidnon, Phillippines is shot and killed by two people on a drive-by on a motorcycle. Mr. Margarito J. Cabal was a utlity worker and had reported being followed by police cars in the days leading up to his murder.

properties.Title: Syrian government attack on al-Haffe cuts water and electricity
properties.Description: Syrian government attack on al-Haffe cuts water and electricity.

properties.Title: Water clash on border of Mali and Burkina Faso
properties.Description: A clash along the border between Dogon villagers from Mali and nomadic Fulani herders from Burkina Faso kills at least 30 people, after an earlier agreement to share water and pasture land was revoked. Chaos following a military coup in March is partly responsible for the breakdown in law and order in Mali.

properties.Title: Syrian government attack on Tremseh starts with the cutting of electricity and water supply
properties.Description: Syrian government attack on Tremseh, Syria on 12 July 2012 starts with the cutting of electricity and water supply.

properties.Title: The Taliban detonate a bomb at the Kapisa Dam, Afghanistan, killing three
properties.Description: The Taliban detonate an explosive device at the Kapisa Dam construction site, Afghanistan, killing three and injuring four more.

properties.Title: Three killed in dispute over wells in Somalia
properties.Description: In August 2012, fighting between two clans in the Lower Juba region of south Somalia kills at least three people and wounds five. Reports from the village of Waraq (near the border with Kenya) indicate that the dispute began over the ownership of new water wells.

properties.Title: Workers at the Gomal Zam Hydroelectric Dam in Pakistan are abducted by Tehrik-i-Taliban
properties.Description: About 12 Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan fighters kidnap eight workers at the Gomal Zam Hydroelectric Dam, in the Murtaza Goth area of Pakistan.

properties.Title: Syrian rebels capture dam
properties.Description: In November, Syrian rebels fighting the government of President Bashar al-Assad overrun government forces and capture the Tishrin hydroelectric dam on the Euphrates River, after days of heavy clashes. The dam supplies electricity to part of Syria and is considered strategically important to the Syrian regime.

properties.Title: Small dam is cyber attacked
properties.Description: U.S. authorities discover that Iranian hackers have successfully infiltrated the control system of a small dam in New York State. Due to work being performed on the dam, the hackers are unable to alter dam operations, however the event exposes the risks of cyberattacks on U.S. infrastructure.

properties.Title: Sri Lankan archbishop accuses army of killing unarmed protestors
properties.Description: The Catholic Archbishop of Colombo, Sri Lanka accuses the Sri Lankan army of shooting unarmed protesters and desecrating a church during demonstrations against water contamination. In the incident, at least six people are reported killed and more than 50 others wounded. Thousands of residents were protesting against the contamination of groundwater supply to 15 local villages, allegedly by chemica

properties.Title: Tanzanian herders attempt to take control of water source
properties.Description: Conflicts over water continue in Tanzania between farmers and herders over dwindling water resources. In 2013, herders attempt to take over the central water source in the village of Makenya.

properties.Title: Village water well destroyed in Somalia
properties.Description: The water well for a village in the Bay region is destroyed by an attack attributed to Al-Shabaab.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201312020003

properties.Title: Violence escalates over water in Yemen
properties.Description: The dwindling water supply from shared mountain springs leads to growing violence between Marzouh and Qaradh, Yemen. At least six deaths occur and many more are wounded in clashes since 2000. "Wherever in Yemen you see aquifers depleting, you have the worst conflicts," said Abdul Rahman al-Eryani, former minister for water and environment, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water project employees kidnapped in Iraq
properties.Description: Four water project employees are kidnapped by assailants dressed as police officers. The outcome of the kidnapping is unknown and no group has claimed responsibility for the incident.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201311190066

properties.Title: Water shortage causes riots in Jordan
properties.Description: The Syrian civil war and the displacement of refugees into Jordan contribute to a growing water supply crisis. In summer 2013, riots associated with water shortages are reported by the director of water utilities in Jordan's Mafraq governorate. He notes, "Mafraq was on fire. People burned tires in the street and closed the roads. There was tension over water in the past, but the Syria crisis has m

properties.Title: Water shortage leads to local fighting in India
properties.Description: An acute water shortage in Ahmednagar district, Maharashtra, India leads to fights among locals and one death. The government expresses concern about large-scale public unrest due to a severe drought and major water shortages.

properties.Title: Water station in Egypt is attacked
properties.Description: A water station in Bir al-Abd, Egypt is attacked. No one is reported injured or killed. The attackers claim that the water station was built on their lands.

properties.Title: Water supply for the city of Kitchanga, Congo cut off resulting in deaths
properties.Description: The water supply for the city of Kitshanga, Congo is cut off in clashes between DRC armed forces and the Alliance des Patriotes pour un Congo Libre et Souverain (APCLS). The violence kills tens of civilians, including a humanitarian worker, wounds hundreds of people, and displaces thousands.

properties.Title: Water tanker in Myanmar is attacked
properties.Description: Employees of the private company Assam Rifles are escorting a water tanker near the Manipur-Myanmar border when they are attacked with a remotely-detonated bomb. No one is reported injured, however the water tanker is damaged.

properties.Title: Areas of Deir ez-Zor, Syria lose access to water when an armed group cuts supplies of food, potable water, and other essentials
properties.Description: Areas of Deir ez-Zor, Syria lose access to water when an armed group cuts supplies of food, potable water, electricity and fuel. The use of untreated water from the river resulted in health problems in the civilian population.

properties.Title: Basic services, including water and electricity, are cut to settlements in southern Dara’a, Syria while under siege
properties.Description: Basic services, including water and electricity, are cut to settlements in southern Dara’a, Syria while they are under siege in early 2013.

properties.Title: The water tower and electricity generator in Masharah, Syria are shelled by government forces who impose a siege on the village
properties.Description: The water tower and electricity generator in Masharah, Syria are shelled by government forces who impose a siege on the village.

properties.Title: Algerian protestors and police clash at protest over water shortages
properties.Description: Nineteen people are arrested in Algeria's Setif region when clashes erupt between protesters and police during a demonstration against water shortages. Around 300 protesters march on the town hall in Bouselam to demand improved water supplies and other local improvements. Clashes broke out when the security forces attempted to access the building by force, with 19 people arrested.

properties.Title: Armed gang sabotages main water pipeline in Yemen
properties.Description: An armed gang sabotages the main water pipeline from Sana'a to Ta'izz in al Hawjila, Ta'izz governorate on June 23.

properties.Title: Blockade causes shutdown of Gaza wastewater treatment plant
properties.Description: An Egyptian and Israel blockade of Gaza cut fuel supplies and causes a shutdown of Gaza's wastewater treatment plants, leading to massive overflow of sewage.

properties.Title: Car bomb targets water-supply systems in Colombia
properties.Description: A car bomb thought to be targeted at a community water supply detonates near an aqueduct. No group claims responsibility, however Colombian officials attributed it to the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC).
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201302060003

properties.Title: Chinese dam builders killed after attack by Uyghurs
properties.Description: Hundreds of Uyghurs are arrested following an attack on May 20th that led to the death of seven Han Chinese workers building a dam on the Karakash River in Hotan (in Chinese, Hetian) prefecture's Karakash (Moyu) county, China. Local officials ascribe the violence to land and water disputes and cultural differences between Han immigrants [to Xinjiang] and Uyghurs.

properties.Title: Clash between Kenyan herders and crop growers kills 56
properties.Description: Fighting between Borana herders and the crop-growing Burji community kills at least 56 people in 2013. The violence is rooted in historic clan rivalries as well as competition for water and pasture in the arid region. Clashes between the two communities date back to the 1960s and are related to disputes over the use of political power to take control of water and fertile land.

properties.Title: Colombian demonstration over water treatment plant turns violent
properties.Description: Violence breaks out in the town of Yopal, Colombia, which has been without water for nearly two years following the destruction of a water-treatment plant by a landslide. Demonstrations at government offices and offices of the water company lead to injuries and arrests.

properties.Title: Demonstrators killed during protests over water shortages in Somaliland
properties.Description: At least two demonstrators are killed and others wounded in Somaliland when protests against water shortages are broken up by police firing live bullets. The shortages were caused by a work stoppage by private water vendors protesting new taxes.

properties.Title: Egyptian politicians threaten sabotage of Ethiopian dam in a publicly broadcast meeting
properties.Description: During a publicly broadcast meeting hosted by Egyptian President Morsi, some meeting participants (Egyptian politicians) suggest sabotaging Ethiopia's dam on the Blue Nile.

properties.Title: Elsipotog Nation protests over fracking impact to water resources in Canada
properties.Description: Several injuries are reported and six police cars burned during a protest by members of the Elsipogtog First Nation in Canada over the impacts to local water resources from fracking operations.

properties.Title: Environmental activist Tomas Garcia is killed at peaceful protest of the Agua Zarca Dam project in Honduras
properties.Description: Environmental activist, Tomas Garcia, dies after being shot by the Honduran military during a peaceful protest of the Agua Zarca Dam project.

properties.Title: Farmers in Iran clash with police over water diversions
properties.Description: Hundreds of farmers in the town of Varzaneh, in Iran's Isfahan province, clash with police during a protest against the government's decision to divert water from the area to another province. Iranian media say farmers smash a pipeline carrying water from Zayanderud river to neighboring Yazd province in an effort to prevent the water transfer. Dozens are reported injured and more arrested.

properties.Title: Fight over control of water in Pakistan
properties.Description: In Karachi, rival sects of the Taliban fight for the control of water by controlling infrastructure and land.

properties.Title: Five killed at protest against lack of water at Indonesian prison
properties.Description: Protests against the lack of water and other basic resources at a prison in Medan, Indonesia leave five dead. Hundreds of convicts escape after the inmates set fire to the prison.

properties.Title: Islamic militants cut water supply to Timbuktu, Mali
properties.Description: Attacks by Islamic militants on utilities cut water supply to Timbuktu, Mali. There are also reports that Malian forces carried out executions in the conflict area near the towns of Sevare, Mopti, and Niono with bodies being dumped in water wells.

properties.Title: Kyrgyz villagers block canal at border
properties.Description: A water and land dispute between villagers living on the border between Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan escalates when Kyrgyz villages block a canal to prevent water flowing to farmers on the Kazakh side of the border.

properties.Title: Militants attack pumping station in Libya
properties.Description: Water for the capital of Libya, Tripoli is disrupted by an attack in southern Libya on a pumping station for the country's 'Great Man Made River' by militants loyal to former Libyan leader Muammar al-Gaddafi.

properties.Title: Militias cut off water supplies to Tripoli
properties.Description: Militias cut off water supplies to Tripoli for several days in continuing unrest in the country.

properties.Title: Pakistan and India clash over Siachen Glacier
properties.Description: Tensions continue between India and Pakistan over access to and control of the Siachen Glacier in Kashmir, with a demand by Pakistan that India withdraw troops stationed there.

properties.Title: Protester killed after attending a rally against a dam in Panama
properties.Description: After attending a protest against the Barro Blanco Dam in Panama, Onesimo Rodriguez is killed.

properties.Title: Guatemalan community activist against mining and hydroelectric projects is killed
properties.Description: In Santa Elalia, Huehuetenango, Guatemala, Daniel Pedro Mato disappears and is found dead several days later. He was an indigenous community activist helping to organize against mining and hydroelectric projects in the region.

properties.Title: Water supply to the besieged town Muadamiyah, Syria is “cut off for weeks at a time” between April and December 2013
properties.Description: Water supply to the besieged town Muadamiyah, Syria is “cut off for weeks at a time” between April and December 2013.

properties.Title: A drinking water facility in Georgia, U.S., is attacked and the treatment settings are changed risking quality of tap water
properties.Description: In April 2013, the drinking water facility of a small town in North Georgia is attacked. The attackers gain access to the monitoring system and change the fluorine and chlorine settings, leading the management company to advise the 400 residents not to use the tap water for a few days.

properties.Title: Syrian government forces and Hezbollah cut water to the town of Al-Quasayr during a seige
properties.Description: In May 2013, Syrian government forces and Hezbollah launch a major offensive on Al-Quasayr, controlling two water stations and cutting water to the town.

properties.Title: Man displaced by dam project killed in Colombia for his opposition to additional projects
properties.Description: A man who is displaced by a dam, Nelson Giraldo, is shot for his opposition to further dam projects in Colombia. The Hidroituango dam project had displaced his family and many others from their home for around six months when he was shot.
properties.Sources: Movimiento Rios Vivos 2013; International Rivers n.d.

properties.Title: Pakistani gunmen attack the Ghallanai Irrigation Dam site in Pakistan, damaging equipment and abducting civilians
properties.Description: About 12 Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan fighters attack the Ghallanai Irrigation Dam site, Pakistan and abduct at least four citizens, and set fire to construction equipment.

properties.Title: The Syrian government drops barrel bombs in Dawar Qadi Askar, Syria, severing the main water supply line
properties.Description: The Syrian government drops barrel bombs on 28 November 2013 in Dawar Qadi Askar, Syria, severing the main water-supply line.

properties.Title: Riots over erratic water supply in Kitwe, Zambia
properties.Description: Kitwe's Wusakile and Chamboli townships in Zambia experience riots over erratic water supply. Residents engage police in skirmishes and eight people are arrested.

properties.Title: Russian shelling damages the Siverskyi Donets-Donbas canal and kills nine
properties.Description: Russian shelling of the pumping station of the 1st lift of the Siverskyi Donets-Donbas canal and other structures of the canal kills two and injures three. Over the course of several days, a total of nine people are killed and 26 employees of KP "Donbas Water Company" are injured.
properties.Sources: Donetsk Regional State Administration 2021; Ulytsky et al. 2018

properties.Title: Three US citizens arrested for planning attack on water-treatment plants
properties.Description: Three men in the state of Georgia are arrested for planning to attack water-treatment plants, power grids, and other infrastructure in an effort to incite anti-government militias in the United States to fight the federal government. A criminal complaint charges them with conspiring to receive and possess firearms, specifically pipe bombs and thermite grenades. Thermite grenades are military-grade

properties.Title: U.S.-led coalition launches airstrikes to protect dams from ISIS
properties.Description: A U.S.-led coalition launches a series of air strikes against targets of the Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) in areas around Mosul Dam, Fallujah Dam, and Haditha Dam in Iraq. U.S. aircraft destroy an ISIL-built earthen berm near the Fallujah Dam. This berm was built by ISIL to control downstream water supplies and to increase the flow of the Euphrates River into Abu Ghraib canals, floo

properties.Title: Ukraine accused of cutting water supply to the North Crimea Canal
properties.Description: After Russia annexes Crimea from the Ukraine, they accuse Ukraine of cutting off the water supply in the North Crimea Canal, leading to a water shortage for Crimea's agricultural fields growing grapes, rice, maize, and soya. The canal delivers water from the Dnipro River in southern Ukraine and accounts for 80% of Crimea's water supply.

properties.Title: Upper caste women in India reportedly restrict access to water sources
properties.Description: Tensions revive in the area of Jammu and Kashmir in early 2014 when upper caste women reportedly restrict access to higher-quality water sources.

properties.Title: Violence over access to water between neighbors in Kyrgyzstan leaves one dead
properties.Description: In Kyrgyzstan an Uzbek farmer is killed by his Kyrgyz neighbor who reportedly attacks him over access to irrigation water.

properties.Title: Water construction equipment damaged in India
properties.Description: Maoists in Dharambandha, India claim responsibility for a bombing attack on a culvert and the excavation machine being used to build a canal.

properties.Title: Water pipeline destroyed in South Sudan
properties.Description: Fighting around Malakal, in South Sudan, displaces thousands and leaves many dead. A military checkpoint and water pipeline to the United Nations Mission in South Sudan (UNMISS) compound and nearby Protection of Civilians area are targeted and destroyed.

properties.Title: Water pipeline is damaged in Colombia
properties.Description: A water pipeline is damaged by an explosive device near the Pan-American Highway in Colombia. The attack is attributed to the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC).
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201407310004

properties.Title: Water purification plant in Fallujah attacked
properties.Description: At least two people die from a blast at a water purification plant in the city of Fallujah, in Al Anbar Province. More than 20 people are hospitalized from chlorine poisoning from the destruction of a water-purification chemical storage facility.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201406130089

properties.Title: Water system in Donetsk, Ukraine damaged during conflict with Russia
properties.Description: During the conflict between the Ukraine and Russia, water systems for the city of Donetsk are damaged. The Donbas Water Company stops three pumping station from bringing water to the Siversky Donets-Donbas channel due to damages from the conflict.

properties.Title: Water treatment facilities and a dam in the Ukraine are attacked
properties.Description: Gunmen fire at a water filtration center, causing an unknown number of casualties. Landmines are planted at a dam in Bakhmut (formerly Artemivsk district), Donetsk Oblast, Ukraine. The outcome of the attack is not reported and no group claims responsibility, although it is attributed to the Donetsk People's Republic.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201407140120

properties.Title: Water well attacked in Pakistan
properties.Description: Two schools and a solar-powered tube well are destroyed in a blast attributed to militants in the Mohmand Agency. No injuries are reported.

properties.Title: Water-supply reservoir is attacked in Ukraine
properties.Description: The regional water utility company in the Donetsk Oblast province of eastern Ukraine reports that the reservoir in rebel-controlled Horlivka has been hit by shells. Two injuries are reported.

properties.Title: Three people are killed in Mothutlung, South Africa in a protest over sabotage of local water pumps
properties.Description: Three people are killed during protests in Mothutlung, South Africa after the pumps supplying water are sabotaged, reportedly by private owners of water tankers who had paid kickbacks to municipal officials to cut the supply.

properties.Title: A bomb damages a water pipeline in the city of Minva Al-Qamh, Egypt
properties.Description: A person is injured when an explosive device placed under a water pipeline is detonated in the city of Minya Al-Qamh, Egypt. The pipeline is not damaged. No one claims responsibility for the explosion.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201409270026

properties.Title: Armed bandits force villages to pay a 'water tax'
properties.Description: Drought in northern India causes local bandits to threaten to kill nearby villagers unless they deliver water to their rural hideouts. Twenty-eight local villages obey the order and take turns paying what the bandits call a daily "water tax.'

properties.Title: At least 30 people reported killed in attack on Haditha Dam, Iraq
properties.Description: At least 30 people are reported killed during an attack on a dam in the Haditha area of Iraq. No group claims responsibility for the attack but it is thought to have been carried out by Islamic State.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201412290031

properties.Title: Attack on an aqueduct in Colombia cuts off water for 16,000
properties.Description: An unclaimed attack on an aqueduct leaves approximately 16,000 people without water. The  Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) is blamed for the attack and criticized by a Colombian military general for causing a war crime by breaking international law that prohibits attacks on water systems.

properties.Title: Attack on water construction project in Iraq
properties.Description: Shots are fired at a checkpoint for an Iranian company working on a water project south of Kirkuk, Iraq.

properties.Title: Bombing injures two at water project in Iraq
properties.Description: Two people are injured when a bomb explodes near the center of the city Tuz Khurmatu at the town's water project.

properties.Title: Christian militias reportedly kill Muslim civilians
properties.Description: Continued sectarian violence in the Central African Republic between Muslim and Christian militias includes reports of Muslim civilians being killed and their bodies thrown down water wells.

properties.Title: Civilians suffer violence, intentional water cuts along Georgia-Russia border
properties.Description: Despite continuing talks between Georgia and Russia following a 2008 war over South Ossetia and Abkhazia, violence continues in the region. Georgia says civilians along the line suffer violence and kidnappings, that school children are detained regularly by Russian troops, and that water and gas supplies are regularly cut off.

properties.Title: Free Syrian Army cuts off water supply to Wadi Barada
properties.Description: Syrian government forces are driven out of Wadi Barada's villages after the Free Syrian Army (FSA) cuts off water supply from Ain al-Fijah, a major spring serving the Damascus area.

properties.Title: Government reportedly bombs pumping stations in Aleppo, Syria
properties.Description: The water supply for the Syrian city of Aleppo is cut off in both regime and opposition-controlled areas. The city's water pumping stations are bombed, reportedly by the government, as are water distribution networks in opposition-controlled areas. In return, the Syrian Ministry of Foreign Affairs blames opposition forces for the water crisis.

properties.Title: Groups attack Venezuelan water treatment plant
properties.Description: Unidentified groups attack the Bourgin water-treatment plant in Merida, Venezuela and contaminate the local supply with diesel fuel. The contamination is discovered through water-quality testing and the water supply to Merida is shut off for a short period for cleaning.

properties.Title: Haditha Dam on the Euphrates River is attacked
properties.Description: Reports from a military spokesperson from Iraq say security forces fought off militant groups trying to seize Haditha and a nearby dam on the Euphrates River. 40 militants are killed by the security forces.
properties.Sources: Derechos Human Rights and Equipo Nizkor 2014

properties.Title: Hydroelectric power plant is attacked in Turkey
properties.Description: A hydroelectric power plant in Kars Province, Turkey is attacked by the Kurdistan Workers' Party. Three attackers are killed. The next day, three off-duty Turkish soldiers are killed in retribution.

properties.Title: In Texas, family feud over water well erupts in violence
properties.Description: A dispute over a water well leads to a violent assault with a sword and gun. Two men are arrested.

properties.Title: Insurgents seize Falluja Dam
properties.Description: Insurgents from Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) seize the Fallujah Dam in Iraq and close the floodgates to cause upstream flooding and to cut downstream water supply. The objective is to flood the area around the city of Fallujah to force government troops to retreat and lift a siege, while cutting water supplies and hydroelectricity generation for other parts of the country.

properties.Title: Islamic State blocks access to water
properties.Description: In Iraq, Islamic State blocks access to water in the predominantly Christian town of Qaraqosh (also known as Bakhdida), take over farms and agricultural land, and expel most of the 50,000 residents.

properties.Title: Islamic State contaminates drinking water supplies, Syria
properties.Description: Islamic State deliberately contaminates drinking water with crude oil in the Balad district of the Salahaddin Governorate. Poisoned water supplies are also reported from Aleppo, Deir ez Zor, Raqqa, and  Baghdad.

properties.Title: Islamic State destroys water wells, pumps, and pipes in agricultural regions in Iraq
properties.Description: Farmers returning to their land in northwestern Iraq after the Islamic State (ISIS/ISIL) find widespread devastation to agricultural land, livestock, and machinery, including water pumps, wells, pipes and generators. One assessment by the Iraq Food and Security Cluster Partnership notes that of the 450 deep wells, only 50 are still useable. Another assessment by Amnesty International details the d
properties.Sources: Joint Crisis Coordination Center 2015; Amnesty International 2018b

properties.Title: Islamic State fighters advance on Haditha Dam
properties.Description: Iraqi officials report that fighters with the Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) are advancing on the Haditha Dam, the second-largest in Iraq, raising the possibility of catastrophic damage and flooding. The ISIL militants are fighting for control of the Euphrates River dam, about 120 miles northwest of Baghdad and government forces were fighting to halt their advance. Army officers tell

properties.Title: Islamic State forces take control of Mosul Dam
properties.Description: Mosul Dam in Iraq is taken over by Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) forces in August 2014. Over the next several weeks, Kurdish and US forces attack ISIS/ISIL in the region and retake control of the dam.

properties.Title: Islamic State uses Falluja Dam as a weapon
properties.Description: Islamic State closes the Falluja Dam floodgates and diverts water over an irrigation channel into a side valley, inundating land up to 100km away and placing parts of the city of Abu Ghraib under up to four meters of water. More than 10,000 houses and 200 square kilometers of farmland are flooded and extensive livestock killed. Water for millions of people in the cities of Karbala, Najaf, and Babi

properties.Title: Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan security clash over border dispute
properties.Description: Security forces in Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan clash over a border dispute. Among the targets of the violence, which leave security forces wounded on both sides, are a small dam and electricity substation inside Kyrgyzstan. The local grievances include disputes over access to pasture and water resources.

properties.Title: Levees along the Shebelle River, Somalia are destroyed
properties.Description: Levees are destroyed along the Shebelle River in Somalia, in an attempt, sources claim, to make roads unusable by military forces. The attack is attributed to Al-Shabaab.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201409010003

properties.Title: Libyan tribal disputes include attacks on pumps and pipeline systems
properties.Description: Disputes between two tribes in Libya, the Zwai and Tebu, include attacks on power stations and power cut for the pumps that operate the 'Man Made River' pipeline system, leading to water shortages.

properties.Title: Militia burn and loot South Darfur refugee camp, destroying water wells
properties.Description: Armed Janjaweed militia burn and loot a South Darfur refugee camp, killing a local sheikh, injuring others, looting property and livestock, and destroying all water wells.

properties.Title: Multiple water projects are attacked in Iraq
properties.Description: In less than a week, several water projects are the targets of explosives, killing six employees of one of the water projects and damaging the infrastructure in Al-Shirqat District, Saladin Governorate, and Mushirfah, Nineveh Governorate, Iraq.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201404090018, 201404130034

properties.Title: Over 1000 killed in Nigeria over access to land and water
properties.Description: Violence between farmers and herders kills over 1,000 people in 2014 in Nigeria alone, according to Human Rights Watch. At the root of this violence is the issue of access to land and water resources.

properties.Title: Palestinian water supply and wastewater system hit by Israeli war planes
properties.Description: Israeli warplanes hit water supply wells, pipelines, and wastewater treatment plants servicing hundreds of thousands of Palestinians in the Gaza region. Palestinian officials state that the attacks intentionally targeted the infrastructure, mounting a  war crime under the Geneva Protocol.

properties.Title: Pojom II hydroelectric project facilities in Guatemala is set on fire
properties.Description: The Pojom II hydroelectric project facilities are set on fire. This is one of three proposed hydroelectric projects in the northern Huehuetenango region of Guatemala that has been opposed by many of the local community.

properties.Title: Protests over water shortages in South Africa turn violent
properties.Description: At least four people are killed in South Africa during protests over water shortages in the northern town of Brits.

properties.Title: Leader of opposition to the Tigre dam project in Honduras is found dead
properties.Description: A leader in the opposition to the Tigre Dam project, Justo Sorto, is found dead in Intibuca, Honduras. The dam is planned to be on a river the flows between Honduras and El Salvador.

properties.Title: Four people killed and others injured in clashes over a dam project in Pakistan
properties.Description: In February 2014, four people are killed and another seven are injured in clashes between Pakistani communities over the construction of the Diamer-Bhasha Dam. The Pakistani government has failed to intervene in the dispute which has erupted between regional communities over who will be responsible for the construction and other aspects of the dam's administration.

properties.Title: Indigenous organizer supporting efforts to oppose Las Mintas Dam in Honduras shot and killed
properties.Description: Indigenous organizer, Pedro Perez, working in opposition of Las Mintas Dam is shot and killed in Honduras.

properties.Title: Coordinator of Honduran indigenous organization opposed to the Agua Zarca Dam attacked
properties.Description: A woman, Maria Santos Dominguez, serving as the coordinator of COPINH (Consejo Civico de Organizaciones Populares e Indigenas de Honduras) in Rio Blanco, Honduras, is attacked in her home. COPINH is an indigenous organization that has been organizing people to oppose the Agua Zarca Dam in Honduras.

properties.Title: Farmers and herdsmen clash over land and water access in Nigeria
properties.Description: In Nigeria, Tiv/Agatu farmers and Fulani herdsmen clash over access to grazing land and water points. In April 2014, President Goodluck Jonathan orders military operations in Benue, Nasarawa, and Plateau states in an effort to reduce the violence.

properties.Title: Village clashes with police over spring
properties.Description: A confrontation over a water spring between 1,500 police and residents of a village on Mexico City's western outskirts leaves more than 100 police injured and five people arrested. It is the latest in a series of clashes over increasingly scarce water in the city, which draws much of its supply from surrounding states. Local governments say they are trying to extend municipal supply to the area; r

properties.Title: Impaired electrical grid and water supply in Aleppo, Syria lead to a lack of potable water and sanitation
properties.Description: The Al-Nusra Front “deliberately impairs” the electrical grid and water supply in Aleppo, Syria between May 5-14th, leading to a lack of potable water and sanitation.

properties.Title: Community activist organzing to block construction of the Agua Zarca Dam in Honduras murdered
properties.Description: Community activist, William Jacobo, in Rio Blanco, Honduras, is murdered. The Rio Blanco community was organizing to block the construction of the Agua Zarca Dam.

properties.Title: Organizer against corporate dam and mining projects killed in Mexico
properties.Description: A local leader opposing corporate hydroelectric and mining projects, Antonio Esteban Cruz, is killed in Mexico. Sr. Cruz was a leader of the Movimiento Independiente Obrero, Campesino, Urbano y Popular (MIOCUP) and had recently formed the Encuentro Nacional Indígena y Campesino (Ficam) to defend the land against corporate mining, gas pipelines, and dams.

properties.Title: Protests over spring diversion erupts in violence in Mexico
properties.Description: Protestors in San Bartolo Ameyalco on the outskirts of Mexico City battle anti-riot police during a protest to prevent the diversion of local spring water to a new development nearby. More than 100 people are injured, some seriously.

properties.Title: Extensive damage to Gaza's water systems during the Israeli attacks on Gaza
properties.Description: Water and sanitation facilities in Gaza are heavily affected by the escalation of violence in 2014 with 63 water facilities damaged and 23 completely destroyed. More than 80 percent of the water wells in Gaza are estimated to not function with about 1.2 million people lacking access to water and sanitation services. Key sewage facilities are partially destroyed, including 60 percent of the treatme

properties.Title: Israeli missile hits water tank in Gaza, killing several
properties.Description: Several days of attacks on houses in Gaza by Israeli missles in July destroy water tanks and kill several occupants.

properties.Title: Water tanks are destroyed in Khuza'a, Gaza by Israel military and a water treatment plant is bombed
properties.Description: Khuza’a town in Gaza is attacked by Israel Defense Forces. The electricity is cut and reports indicate that many water tanks on the rooftops of homes are attacked and destroyed by the IDF. The Israeli  ground operation in Khuza’a also results in extensive damage to the water tower that serves the civilian population and to a water treatment plant.

properties.Title: Six dead including two children as protests against a dam project turn violent in Guatemala
properties.Description: Indigenous Q'equchi community members at risk of displacement from the Santa Rita hydroelectric dam in Guatemala are met with an esitmated 1,500 police sent to evict residents to allow dam construction to begin. At least six people, including two children are killed in confrontations related to the dam.

properties.Title: ISIS cuts off water and electricity supply to Kobane, Syria ahead of their assault in September 2014
properties.Description: ISIS cuts off water and electricity supply to Kobane, Syria ahead of their assault in September 2014.

properties.Title: Fisherman with connetions to organization opposing the Hidrosogamoso dam in Colombia dies from stabbing
properties.Description: A fisherman with connections to an organization working to oppose a dam in Colombia, Armando Caballero Toscano, is stabbed while walking to a friend's house. Mr. Toscano was the son of a leader of the local organization opposing the construction of the Hidrosogamoso Dam on the Sogamoso River in Colombia.

properties.Title: Protests against the construction of the Sivens dam in France turn violent, one protestor is killed
properties.Description: In 2014 protestors camped out to prevent the construction of the Sivens Dam on the Tescou River in southern France have multiple confrontations with police. On the night of October 26, one protestor, Remi Fraisse, is hit by a grenade thrown by the police and dies. His death prompts widespread protests across the country and shortly afterward, the regional government decides to halt the project com

properties.Title: Honduran youth killed for supporting efforts opposing the Agua Zarca Dam
properties.Description: In October 2014, Maycol Ariel Rodriguez Garcia, a 15 year old indigenous youth from Honduras, was found dead. His death is thought to be connected to his involvement with anti-dam efforts in his community where indigenous Lenca community members have been working to stop the construction of the Agua Zarca Dam on Rio Blanco.

properties.Title: Revolutionary forces in Colombia blow up a water treatment facility
properties.Description: According to military officials, Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) "detonated a high power explosive device," destroying a water facility in the town of Algeciras, in southwestern Colombia, disrupting service to about 13,000 people.

properties.Title: Russian Federation forces bomb water-treatment facility in Aleppo, Syria
properties.Description: Russian Federation forces bomb the al-Khafsa water treatment facility in the city of Aleppo, reportedly mistaking it for an oil-production facility. The treatment plant draws water from the Euphrates River and is called "one of the most important in Syria, producing an average of 18 million liters of drinking water daily." According to UNICEF, "The bombing caused severe damage and cut off piped wa

properties.Title: Saudi Arabia destroys a water bottling plant in Yemen; kills dozens
properties.Description: A plant is destroyed and 14 workers are killed in Yemen in an aerial bombing by a Saudi-led coalition. The Yemeni Defense Ministry claims the facility was a bottled water plant, while the Saudi-led coalition claims the facility was used to make bombs and train African migrants. Human Rights Watch finds no evidence the facility was anything except a bottling plant.

properties.Title: Silvan Dam construction site attacked
properties.Description: A remotely detonated explosive on a road used for delivering supplies to the Silvan Dam construction site kills one civilian, a child. The bomb is thought to be planted by the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK) and targeted at military vehicles.

properties.Title: Small Peruvian town protests company's groundwater pumping
properties.Description: In a feud over water, farmers in the Peruvian town of Ocucaje challenge an effort by a company, Agricola La Venta, to start pumping from three wells. Water from the wells would flow through about eight miles of pipes to a farm that produces table grapes for export. Protesters set fire to plastic pipes and one protester is injured by company representatives during a dispute. The town is already str

properties.Title: Soldiers guarding Turkish dam are attacked
properties.Description: Turkish soldiers guarding a hydroelectric dam are attacked with rockets and long-range gunfire by the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK) in the Kulp district of Diyarbakir. One soldier is killed and three wounded.

properties.Title: State water corporation is damaged in Yemen
properties.Description: A state water corporation is damaged in the conflict in Yemen, in Shibam Kawkaban District, Al Mahwit, Yemen.

properties.Title: State water utility infrastructure is attacked in Yemen
properties.Description: A state run water utility is a casualty to the conflict in Yareem District, Ibb, Yemen.

properties.Title: Terrorists bomb main water pipe into Aleppo, Syria
properties.Description: Fighters from Jabhat al-Nusra, the Syrian branch of Al-Qaeda, bomb the main pipeline carrying water to the city of Aleppo from the Euphrates River. More than 100 people are sickened by contaminated water.

properties.Title: Ukrainian town loses water supply
properties.Description: Most of the villages and towns in the southwest portion of the Luhansk region in Ukraine lose access to their water supply after fighting damages a water main.

properties.Title: Water drilling rig is damaged during conflict in Yemen
properties.Description: A water drilling rig is damaged in Yemen, in the Haydan District, Saada.

properties.Title: Water factory is hit in multiple air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: The Adhban water factory is hit during three air raids that occurred in August, in Sirwah District, Marib, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water infrastructure is attacked in Yemen
properties.Description:
A water project in Al-Sharyah District, Al Bayda, Yemen is damaged. On the same day in Al-Mahabishah
District, Hajja, water wells are also damaged in an air raid.

properties.Title: Water pipe attacked in Egypt
properties.Description: A bomb explodes under a water pipe in al-Rahmaniya village, Abu Kebir, Sharqia governorate, Egypt.

properties.Title: Water pipeline damaged in Pakistan
properties.Description: A water pipeline is damaged in a blast from an explosive reportedly placed by the Baloch Republican Army in Nokani, Balochistan province, Pakistan. No injuries are reported.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201502220004

properties.Title: Water plant in Colombia severely damaged in attack
properties.Description: A water plant near Algeciras, Colombia is severely damaged from an explosive, leaving approximately 13,000 people without services. The Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) rebels are blamed for the attack. This attack on infrastructure comes after several other similar attacks on electrical plants and towers.

properties.Title: Water project is hit in air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: A water project in Majzar District, Marib, Yemen is hit during multiple evening air raids.

properties.Title: Water supply well is attacked in Yemen
properties.Description: A water supply well attacked in Al-Ashah District, Amran, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water tank is damaged in an air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank is damaged in air raids in Sa'ada District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water tank is hit in an air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank is damaged in an air raid in Al-Sab'ein District, Capital, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water truck is damaged in Yemen
properties.Description: A water truck owned by an oil & gas company is damaged in the conflict in Haydan District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water trucks are damaged in conflict in Yemen
properties.Description: Water trucks are damaged during the conflict in Az Zuhrah District, Al Hudaydah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water well hit in air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water well is damaged during an air raid in Mustaba District, Hajjah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Yemen's civil war leaves millions without access to drinking water
properties.Description: The Yemeni Interior Ministry claims up to 4,000 people die annually from water-related violence including raids on wells and other fights over water access involving armed groups. A report from Yemen's pro-government newspaper estimates that 70-80% of conflicts in rural areas are about water. The UNFAO estimates that about 20 million Yemenis do not have access to drinking water because of the ongo

properties.Title: A death is reported in ongoing farmer-herder conflicts in Tanzania
properties.Description: A group of Maasi cattle herders beat and kill a crop farmer in a conflict over water access. The farmer and his family had moved near the river because of changes in precipitation, making water unpredictable.

properties.Title: A local activist is injured during attempt to observe well digging operation by a private agricultural corporation in Peru
properties.Description: A local activist in Peru seeking to block corporate agricultural company, Agricola La Venta, from drilling wells is assaulted as she attempts to view a new well.

properties.Title: A water filtration plant in the Ukraine is damaged
properties.Description: During multiple attacks on the city of Shchastya, Ukraine, a water filtration station is hit. One person is killed and two others wounded during the attacks. While no group claims responsibility for the attacks, they are attributed to the Luhansk People's Republic.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201501140039

properties.Title: A water truck is damaged in Yemen
properties.Description: A water truck is damaged during the conflict in Amran District, Amran, Yemen.

properties.Title: Air raid damages water well in Yemen
properties.Description: A water well is damaged during an air raid in Razih District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Air raid hits water well in Yemen
properties.Description: An early morning air raid hits a water well in Al Mokha District, Taizz, Yemen.

properties.Title: Air raids damage water infrastructure in Yemen
properties.Description: Multiple air raids over the course of two days damage water wells and a water factory in Sihar District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Air raids damage water wells in Yemen
properties.Description: Multiple air raids over the course of a week damage water wells in Bakil Al-Mir and Hayran Districts, Hajjah, Yemen.

properties.Title: An air raid damages a water tank in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank is damaged in an air raid in Craiter District, Aden, Yemen.

properties.Title: Attack on water supply reservoir in Libya
properties.Description: A reservoir appears to have been the target of an attack by supposed Muslim fundamentalists in the city of Zintan, Jabal Al Gharbi district, Libya.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201503050029

properties.Title: Attacks interrupt construction of Ilisu and Silvan dams, Turkey
properties.Description: At the end of 2014, work on the Ilisu Dam stops for four months after the armed wing of the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK) kidnaps two of the project's subcontractors. When construction resumes, the largely non-Kurdish workforce is escorted to the site by military tanks. Militants also target Diyarbakir's Silvan Dam by placing explosives on the roads leading into the site, and security threats in

properties.Title: Bombing of an oil pipeline leads to contamination of water supplies in Colombia
properties.Description: Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) rebels bomb an oil pipeline, causing it to spill 10,000 barrels of oil into the Mira River, leaving an estimated 150,000 people without drinking water. Colombian President Juan Manuel Santos declares a state of emergency in the state of Tumaco due to the spill.

properties.Title: Clashes between pastoralists and farmers over water access.
properties.Description: Clashes continue between pastoralists and farmers over access to water and grazing lands in the face of severe drought. Water shortages have forced the herders to move to farmers' land for grazing, leading to clashes.

properties.Title: Cuts in water access trigger protests in Egypt
properties.Description: Residents of Giza block a road to protest ongoing water cuts. Police break up the protest with tear gas.

properties.Title: Explosive devices discovered near water-treatment plant in Canada
properties.Description: Two small explosive devices are discovered near a water-treatment plant in Barrie, Canada. No one is injured and no suspects are identified in a report on the incident.

properties.Title: Farmer kills herder's donkeys who compete for land and water in Tanzania
properties.Description: A Maasi herder loses their herd of donkeys when they are killed by a local farmer who sees the donkeys as competition for their land and water.

properties.Title: Government water storage infrastructure is damaged in Yemen
properties.Description: A government-owned water storage unit is damaged in night-time air raids in Dhamar City District, Dhamar, Yemen.

properties.Title: In Somalia, dozens are killed in local land and water disputes
properties.Description: Dozens are killed in clan-related violence in Somalia's Hiran region. Sources say the violence is related to tensions over access to water and pasture, as well as local politics.

properties.Title: Iranian border guards open fire on Afghan villagers accessing river water
properties.Description: A dispute over the allocation and rights to water from the Hari Rud River between Afghanistan and Iran leads to at least ten deaths when Iranian border guards allegedly open fire on Afghan villagers trying to collect river water. The river provides water for agricultural production in Herat province, Afghanistan, and to the downstream Iranian city of Mashhad, Iran's second largest city.

properties.Title: Irrigation canals are damaged in two attacks in Yemen
properties.Description: Two attacks spanning a week in Al-Marawi'ah District, Hudaydah, Yemen leave irrigation canals damaged.

properties.Title: Islamic State attacks Iraqi security forces around Euphrates River dam
properties.Description: The Islamic State attacks Iraqi security forces and affiliated militiamen stationed around Adhaim dam on the Al Uzym River, north of Baghdad. Accounts differ as to the total number of deaths, however both sides suffer losses of life and numerous fighters are injured.
properties.Sources: IANS 2015; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019. ID. 201501280049

properties.Title: Islamic State attacks Mosul Dam on the Tigris
properties.Description: An attack by Islamic State (IS) fighters on the Mosul Dam on the Tigris river leads to the death of 16 Kurdish Peshmerga soldiers. The dam provides electricity to part of the Anbar province, which was under IS's control at the time. In August 2014, the dam is seized by Kurdish and Iraqi government forces from IS, who had taken control of it weeks earlier. The 1,200 families who are said to have be

properties.Title: Islamic State captures Tharthar Dam on the Tigris River
properties.Description: The Islamic State attacks and successfully captures Tharthar Dam near Fallujah, on the Tigris River in Anbar province, Iraq. The attack leaves 127 Iraqi soldiers dead. After capturing the dam, the fighters open at least one floodgate, affecting downstream communities.

properties.Title: Islamic State cuts off irrigation water in Iraq
properties.Description: Following the capture of Ramadi Dam, the Islamic State cuts water for irrigation systems and treatment plants in the predominantly Shiite downstream provinces of Babil, Karbala, Najaf and Qadisiya, threatening Iraqi food production.

properties.Title: Islamic State shuts off water flow below Ramadi Dam
properties.Description: In June 2015, Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) militants shut off and redirect water flows below Ramadi Dam in order to facilitate military movements across the Euphrates River. As a result, communities downstream face water shortages.

properties.Title: Kosovo authorities shut off water to Pristina as preventative measure against a planned attack
properties.Description: A planned attack on the water supply in Pristina, Kosovo is prevented before its execution. The attack is intended to contaminate Badovac Lake, the largest water reservoir uphill of the city. As a preventative measure, Kosovo authorities temporarily shut off the water supply to tens of thousands of people in the capital after police arrest five suspects linked to Islamic State.

properties.Title: Local farmers burn a water pipe in attempt to stop groundwater pumping
properties.Description: Local farmers in Peru burn a plastic water pipe in an attempt to stop groundwater pumping by a corporate agricultural company Agricola La Venta.

properties.Title: Militants attack water-treatment facility in Philippines
properties.Description: Suspected New People's Army (NPA), Philippines militants hold up a water-treatment facility claiming that it had failed to pay "revolutionary taxes" to the NPA. No people are harmed, but armed guards are disarmed and additional items are taken from the personnel at the facility.

properties.Title: Night air raid damages water project in Yemen
properties.Description: An air raid damages a water project in Harad District, Hajjah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Philippine town loses access to water after pipeline is bombed
properties.Description: A predominantly Muslim town in southern Philippines loses access to water after a group known as Abu Sayyaf is suspected in an explosion that damaged local water pipes. This group is said to be linked to al-Qaeda and/or at least inspired by al-Qaeda's strategy to attack water infrastructure in Iraq. The affected town is without municipal water for at least several days. In a possibly related incid

properties.Title: Attack of a dam blocked in Iraq
properties.Description: In early January 2015 military troops in Iraq block an attack by IS militants that is attempting to destroy a dam and block a road connecting Diyala and the Shiite holy city of Sammara.

properties.Title: A member of the Gualinga indigenous community opposing a hydroelectric project is found dead in Honduras
properties.Description: A  member of the Gualinga indigenous community, Juan Francisco Martínez, in Honduras is found dead  in January 2015. He is thought to have been murdered for his opposition to a hydroelectric project on his community's territory.

properties.Title: Violent confrontation over a dam project leads to one being shot, eventually dying in Guatemala
properties.Description: A violent confrontation breaks out in Santa Eulalia, Guatemala, between citizens and municipal employees over a dam project. The municipal employees, which includes the mayor, shoot at the citizens who were part of a local radio station that broadcast in the Mayan Q'anjob'al language and allows people in opposition to the dam project to speak. Pascual Basilio Pascual Diego is shot and dies two mon

properties.Title: Baloch Republican Army fighters in Pakistan abduct six construction workers from the Shadi Kaur Dam construction site
properties.Description: Baloch Republican Army fighters in Balochistan Province, Pakistan abduct six construction workers from the Shadi Kaur Dam construction site.

properties.Title: Water and land rights advocate shot and killed in the Philippines
properties.Description: Water and land rights advocate, Teresito Mula Labastilla, is shot and killed by two men on a motorcycle in Maramag, Bukidnon, Philippines. Mr. Labastilla advocated for water and land rights for farmers and indigenous peoples.
properties.Sources: Task Force Detainees of the Philippines 2015

properties.Title: Andhra Pradesh and Telangana police clash over water releases from the Nagarjuna Sagar dam on the Krishna River
properties.Description: On February 13, 2015 in India, Andhra Pradesh police clash with Telangana police over the control of the Nagarjuna Sagar Dam in a water-sharing dispute over the releases of the Krishna River.

properties.Title: Suspected ISIL attackers bomb the Adhaim Dam, Iraq, with at least 18 deaths reported
properties.Description: Suspected Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) suicide bombers detonate explosives-laden vehicles while other attackers open fire on Adhaim Dam, Iraq. At least 18 people (14 ISIL fighters) are killed and 23 people are injured.

properties.Title: Human rights and environmental defender murdered over his opposition to over-exploitation of groundwater
properties.Description: Human rights and environmental defender, Alberto Almeida Fernandez, is murdered in a shopping center in Chihuahua, Mexico. Sr. Fernandez had opposed over exploitation of groundwater from the Flores Magon Ahumada Aquifer. Several of his legal actions to defend the aquifer had been successful in closing wells.

properties.Title: A Mayan community member known for protesting the K'anbalam River dam project is killed in Guatemala
properties.Description: A Mayan community member, Pascual Pablo Francisco, is found dead in March 2015 in the northern department of Huehuetenango, Guatemala. Sr. Francisco is known for opposing the construction of a dam on the K'anbalam River.

properties.Title: Two Mayan leaders accused of sabotaging a dam company are arrested in Guatemala
properties.Description: In March 2015, two Mayan leaders from Huehuetenango, Guatemala accused of sabotaging a dam company by damaging equipment and other property are arrested without a warrant. Their lawyer is allegedly beat by police after demanding a warrant.

properties.Title: Two Guatemalans fighting against the Hidro Santa Cruz dam project are killed
properties.Description: Two Guatemalan men, Cano Mateo Mateo and Alfredo Felipe Ramos, are killed in spring of 2015. They were members of a group that was actively involved in stopping the Hidro Santa Cruz Dam in Guatemala.

properties.Title: Baloch Liberation Front fighters kill 20 working on Sohrab Dam in retaliation for military operations conducted in the area
properties.Description: Baloch Liberation Front (BLF) fighters shoot at construction laborers working on the Sohrab Dam, Balochistan, Pakistan, killing 20. The BLF stated that the attack was retaliation for military operations conducted in the area.

properties.Title: Citizen activist working to protect community control over water wells is murdered in Mexico
properties.Description: A citizen activist, Jose Isabel Cervantes Angeles, organizing on behalf of maintaining community control over water wells, is found dead inside of one of the wells in Coyotepec, Mexico, on April 18, 2015.

properties.Title: Second member of the Gualinga indigenous community opposing a hydroelectric dam is found murdered in Honduras
properties.Description: A member of the Gualinga indigenous community in Honduras, Silvestre Manueles Gomez, is brutally murdered in May 2015 near the border of the department of Intaibuca and La Paz. In this region there is a conflict between the owners of a hydroelectric company and the Indigenous community who oppose the dam because they say the permitting process violated their rights by failing to consult them. He i

properties.Title: Two demonstrators protesting a dam in Brazil are killed by a car that is driven into the group of protestors
properties.Description: Anti-dam demonstrators, Daniel Vilanova Dias and Leidiane Drosdroski Machado, are struck and killed by a car driven at a group of protestors protesting the Belo Monte hydroelectric plant in Vitoria do Xingu, Brazil. Another person is also injured by the same car.
properties.Sources: Comissão Pastoral da Terra 2015; Global Witness 2024

properties.Title: Suspected Kurdistan Workers' Party fighters set fire to construction equipment at the Kocali Dam, Turkey, construction site
properties.Description: Suspected Kurdistan Workers' Party fighters set fire to construction equipment at the Kocali Dam, Turkey, construction site.

properties.Title: Suspected ISIL attackers bomb the Haditha Dam, Iraq, killing at least 33
properties.Description: Suspected Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) suicide bombers detonate explosives-laden vehicles at the Haditha Dam, Iraq. At least 10 security force members and 23 attackers are killed;  16 security force members are injured.

properties.Title: A leader of a Honduran indigenous organization opposed to the Agua Zarca Dam is killed; his son is also nearly killed
properties.Description: In July 2014, a leader of COPINH  (Consejo Civico de Organizaciones Populares e Indigenas de Honduras), Tomas Garcia, and his son are attacked, killing Sr. Garcia and nearly killing his son. COPINH is an Indigenous organization that has been organizing people to oppose the Agua Zarca Dam in Honduras.

properties.Title: Syrian rebel groups cut off water supply to Damascus
properties.Description: In August 2015, Syrian rebel groups cut off water from a spring in Ain al-Fijah, reducing water output to Damascus by 90 percent for three days and leading to water shortages and rationing. Rebels continue to periodically shut off the supply from the spring, resulting in water shortages in Ain al-Fijah and Damascus. Rebels threaten to blow up the spring if government forces enter the region.

properties.Title: Spiritual leader opposing construction of dams in Guatemala is murdered one day after a court victory for indigenous people
properties.Description: A 70 year old Mayan spiritual leader known for supporting efforts to legally suspend the license for the construction of the Vega I and Vega II dams in Ixil territory, Guatemala, is murdered one day after the courts rule in favor of the indigenous groups. The courts found that the company that was issued the permits for the project had failed to consult the indigenous communities, making the permi

properties.Title: Indigenous movement leader of the Gualinga community opposing a dam project in Honduras is murdered -- the third in 2015
properties.Description: The body of indigenous movement leader in Honduras, Javier Vasquez Benitez, is found near his home. He is the third member of the Gualinga community to be murdered in 2015. He was a member of the Movimiento Indígena Independiente Lenca de la Paz - Honduras (MILPAH) and had been organizing the communitiy in opposition to a dam in their territory.

properties.Title: A local environmental leader and mayor of Yagen, Peru is killed after protest over the Chadin II dam project
properties.Description: In Yagen, Peru, Hitler Rojas Gonzalez is shot and killed while walking home from a protest against the Chadin II Dam project. Rojas is the elected mayor of the town and local leader of environmental and social causes.

properties.Title: Community anti-dam activist shot and killed in Guatemala
properties.Description: In Jocotan, Guatemala, Dona Rosalinda Perez is shot and killed after five men enter her store. Dona Perez was known for community organizing against the Trest Marias Dam.

properties.Title: Public officials taken hostage after an oil spill polluted local waters
properties.Description: An indigenous community in the Peruvian Amazon takes at least eight public officials hostage after an oil spill pollutes local water and lands. The Wampis community of Mayuriaga seizes a military helicopter and holds crew members and public officials in concern over inadequate emergency response plans.

properties.Title: Pumping station is attacked in the Russia-Ukraine conflict, interrupting the water supply for 1.5 million people
properties.Description: A pumping station that is part of the Yuzhnodonbasskaya water pipeline is attacked. This pumping station provides water to 1.5 million people in the Donbas region, Ukraine.

properties.Title: Rioting and looting over energy and water cuts in Venezuela
properties.Description: Rioting and looting in Venezuela over energy and water cuts leaves fifteen people injured, as severe drought effects on hydropower generation worsen energy availability and economic disruptions. President Maduro blames the crisis on the fall in global oil prices, a drought that has affected hydroelectric power generation, and political factors.

properties.Title: Riots over water in Bangalore, India
properties.Description: At least two people die and others are injured in riots over water in Bangalore, India in the state of Karnataka. The unrest starts when the Indian Supreme Court orders Karnataka to release water from dams on the Kaveri river to neighboring Tamil Nadu. Over 400 people were arrested.

properties.Title: Scarce water supplies in drought regions of India spark violence
properties.Description: Scarce water supplies in drought-hit regions of India spark violence. As northern and central India suffer thorough severe drought and heat, police in Bundelkhand and several other regions including Tikamgarh are reporting a rise in violent, often deadly clashes over water. Indian police report that the fighting is getting more frequent and bloody, with numerous injuries and deaths reported.

properties.Title: Several water pumps are hit by air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: Several water pumps are hit during two air raids in Baqim District, Sa'ada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Soqam dam damaged during air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: The Soqam dam in Sirwah District, Marib, Yemen, is damaged during air raids.

properties.Title: Stand-off over water rights and use resulting in death
properties.Description: In a standoff over water rights and land use, armed ranchers occupy a federal wildlife refuge in a protest that ends with one death and several arrests.

properties.Title: Threats to cut off supply of water in northwestern Tunisia
properties.Description: Residents of Fernana in northwestern Tunisia threaten to cut off the supply of water delivered by a pumping station to the capital, Tunis. They are protesting economic conditions and repeated water cuts that have sparked anger and protests among the local population. At least one death is reported. Water shortages are experienced in Sousse, Nabeul, Sfax, Kef, Siliana, Beja, Sidi Bouzid, Ben Arous,

properties.Title: Three water trucks struck in air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: Three water trucks are struck in air raids in Al-Mahabishah District, Hajjah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Two dams are hit by air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: The Al-Hayathim dam and the Mahalli dam were both struck during air raids in Nihm District, Sanaa, Yemen in early December.

properties.Title: Two desalination plants are hit by air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: Air raids damage two desalination plants in Al Mokha and At Ta'iziyah Districts, Taizz, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water barrier is damaged by air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water barrier is damaged by an air raid in Al-Qubaytah District, Lahj, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water driller is damaged by air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: A water driller is damaged by ongoing air raids in Arhab District, Sanaa, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water driller is damaged in air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: An air raid causes damage to a water driller in Sirwah District, Marib, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water driller is damaged over the course of four air raids
properties.Description: Four air raids cause damage to a water driller in Al-Safra'a District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water factory is struck during air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: The Al-Qis water factory is struck during two air raids in, Al-Hawta District, Lahj, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water pipeline is bombed in Turkey
properties.Description: A water pipeline is the target of an explosive device in Mardin, Turkey.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201604170038

properties.Title: Water pipeline is damaged in Pakistan
properties.Description: A water pipeline is damaged from explosions when it is misidentified as a gas pipeline, disrupting the water supply to a nearby village.

properties.Title: Water project is attacked during air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: A water project is struck during an air raid in Kitaf Wa Al-Boqe'e District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water pump is damaged in Yemen
properties.Description: A water pump in Kitaf Wa Al-Boqe'e District, Sa'ada, Yemen, is damaged in the ongoing sub-national conflict.

properties.Title: Water pumping plant attacked in Syria
properties.Description: A water pumping station is attacked by the Islamic State (IS) in Deir ez-Zor, Syria as part of numerous attacks that leave hundreds of people dead, including Syrian soldiers, IS militia, and civilians.
properties.Sources: Latin American Herald Tribune (LAHT) 2016; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 201

properties.Title: Water shortage led to clashes between two groups in the parched Sehore district, India
properties.Description: Water shortage led to clashes between two groups in the parched Sehore district, India leaving 7 people injured and one critical.

properties.Title: Water stations are hit during air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: Water stations hit during air raids in Bila Al-Rus District, Sanaa, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water tank and pump are damaged by air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank and pump are damaged by air raids in Al-Safra'a and Haydan Districts, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water tank is damaged in air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank is damaged in an air raid in Abs District, Hajjah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water tank is damaged in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank in Bani Sa'ad District, Al Mahwit, Yemen is damaged by the ongoing conflict.

properties.Title: Water tank is hit during air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank is hit by air raids in Al-Sab'ein District, Capital, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water tank is hit during three air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank is hit during three air raids in Al-Mahwit District, Al Mahwit, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water tanks are hit in air raids in Yemen
