properties.Description: Water tanks are hit during air raids in Khadir and Al-Mawasit Districts, Taizz, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water well is damaged by air raid
properties.Description: In Nihm District, Sanaa, a water well is damaged by an air raid in the ongoing conflict in Yemen.

properties.Title: Water well is damaged by air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water well is damaged in Bilad Al-Rus District, Sanaa, Yemen by an air raid.

properties.Title: Water well is hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water well is hit during an air raid in Asilan District, Shabwa, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water wells attacked in Somalia
properties.Description: Two wells are attacked by militia thought to be part of Al-Shaabab.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201605140030

properties.Title: Well is damaged by air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water well is damaged in Harf Sufyan District, Amran, Yemen, by an air raid.

properties.Title: 18 killed and 200 injured after reopening of the Munak canal, India
properties.Description: At least 18 people are killed and 200 injured after the Indian Army intervenes to reopen the Munak canal, which supplies New Delhi with three-fifths of its freshwater supply. The canal is shut down by economic protests in Harayana state. Sabotage of the canal left more than 10 million people in India's capital, Delhi, without water.

properties.Title: A severe drought sparked "water riots" in Northern KwaZulu-Natal
properties.Description: A severe drought in Northern KwaZulu-Natal sparks 'water riots' in the town of Jozini over access to water and in opposition to the installation of water meters. Violence erupts after a community protest, leading to the arrest of 23 people.

properties.Title: A water tank project is  hit in air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank project and a water tank are hit during air raids in Harib and Sirwah Districts, Marib, Yemen.

properties.Title: Air raids damage water project in Yemen
properties.Description: An air raid causes damage to a water project in Razih District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Air raids damage wells in Yemen
properties.Description: Five air raids leave wells damaged in Sirwah District, Marib, Yemen.

properties.Title: Al-Awadh dam is damaged in air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: The Al-Awadh dam is damaged in an air raid in Radman District, Bayda, Yemen.

properties.Title: Armed guards clash with farmers over drought-struck region of Bundelkhand, India
properties.Description: Armed guards and farmers clash over access to water from Jamunia River at the Bari Ghat Dam in the drought-struck region of Bundelkhand, India

properties.Title: Attack by the Islamic State on Tishreen Dam on the Euphrates River
properties.Description: Islamic State (IS) militants attack the Tishrin Dam on the Euphrates River in Syria. The attack is repelled with airstrikes. The following day a similar attack occurs during which at least 14 IS militants are killed.

properties.Title: Attack on a local dam in India
properties.Description: A group of heavily armed militants fire blank rounds of bullets at a dam, creating panic among the locals. No casualties are reported.

properties.Title: Bombs detonated at hydroelectric power plant in East Java
properties.Description: Several explosive devices detonate at a hydroelectric power plant in Biltar, East Java, Indonesia.

properties.Title: Colleague of Berta Cáceres, Lesbia Janeth, is also murdered for opposing the same dam
properties.Description: Four months after internationally known Honduran environmental activist Berta Cáceres is murdered, a second woman and environmental activist from the same organization is also murdered in Honduras. The second murdered woman, Lesbia Janeth, was working to stop the construction of a dam in La Paz, Honduras.

properties.Title: Dam and water driller are damaged by air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: The Al-Aqran dam and a water driller are hit during ongoing air raids in Nihm District, Sanaa, Yemen.

properties.Title: Desalination company is hit by air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: An early morning air raid causes damage to a desalination company in Al Mokha District, Taizz, Yemen.

properties.Title: Desalination plant is hit by air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: A desalination plant is hit during multiple air raids in Al Mokha District, Taizz, Yemen.

properties.Title: Disruption of electrical system cuts power to water supply in Damascus, Syria
properties.Description: Attacks on Syrian electricity transmission stations cut power needed for water pumping to the eastern and western parts of Damascus. Authorities temporarily restore power five days later, but new fighting again cuts water to the city.

properties.Title: Environmental activist with international recognition killed in Honduras
properties.Description: In 2016, Berta Cáceres -- a prize winning activist working to stop development of the internationally funded Agua Zarca hydroelectric dam on the Gualcarque river -- is murdered after years of death threats and state persecution linked to her campaign. In recent years hundreds of environmental activists have been killed in Latin America, including two of Sra. Cáceres colleagues.

properties.Title: Up to 4 million in Damascus lose water after attacks on Wadi Barada and Ain al-Fija springs, which serve 70% of the city
properties.Description: Up to four million people in Damascus, Syria suffer a loss of water services after springs outside the city are attacked. Water from the Wadi Barada and Ain al-Fija springs, which serve 70 percent of the Syrian capital, is cut off when water infrastructure is deliberately targeted.

properties.Title: Guard at water well in the Sudan is attacked
properties.Description: A guard at water wells in the Um Burma area, Sudan is attacked and severely injured by paramilitaries.

properties.Title: Intentional attacks on water infrastructure in Syria
properties.Description: Control over power and water infrastructure and intentional attacks on that infrastructure are being used as  weapons in the civil war in Syria. Officials estimate there has been a 50 percent reduction in access to safe water in the country since the war began.

properties.Title: Islamic State fighters destroy pipeline that provides water for eastern Mosul, Iraq
properties.Description: Islamic State fighters cut electricity to pumping stations and destroy a pipeline that provides water for eastern Mosul, Iraq, leaving more than half a million people without water. The group has intentionally cut off water supplies to various neighborhoods, according to a Mosul City Council official responsible for supervising the city's water and energy services.

properties.Title: Islamic State militants raid Great Manmade River Project pumping station, Libya
properties.Description: Around 60 Islamic State militants raid the Great Manmade River (GMR) project station in Ash Shwayrif, southwest of Sirte. This system provides water to the Libyan Sahara and the northern cities of Benghazi, Sirte, and Tripoli.

properties.Title: Main water tank is damaged in Yemen
properties.Description: In Hayfan District, Taizz, a communities main water tank is damaged in the ongoing conflict in Yemen.

properties.Title: Massive opposition to project that threatens Indian heritage and water supplies
properties.Description: Hundreds of people are injured and arrested at protests over the construction of a major oil pipeline that threatens water quality on and near Native American lands. Massive opposition to the project developed in 2016 when the planned route reached the site of a Native American tribe called the Standing Rock Sioux, who say the plans threaten their heritage and local water supplies.

properties.Title: Millions of people in Yemen deprived of access to clean water
properties.Description: Attacks on water and power infrastructure throughout Yemen deprive nearly 20 million people of reliable access to clean water.

properties.Title: Multiple attacks damage water tanks in Yemen
properties.Description: Water tanks are damaged over the course of several days in Kamaran District, Hudaydah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Oil pipelines are destroyed and used to contaminate the Tigris River and water supplies for downstream communities
properties.Description: In 2016, explosives are placed around the Baiji oil refinery north of Baghdad. Armed groups break the main pipelines to the refinery and divert the outflow to the Tigris River flowing towards Baghdad. Six downstream areas, including Baghdad, are affected by the oil pollution, leading to a shutdown of water supplies in those regions.

properties.Title: Opposition leader killed in South Africa after opposing threats to land and water
properties.Description: Environmental activist and opposition leader Sikhosiphi Radebe in South Africa is murdered after opposing industrial mining development that threatened community water and land.

properties.Title: Protests against the building of dams on the Brahmaputra River in India
properties.Description: Protests against the building of dams on the Brahmaputra River in India's Himalayan state of Arunachal Pradesh turns violent, leading to two deaths and additional injuries. Both China and India have put forward plans for nearly 150 dams in the region, with major local opposition.

properties.Title: Municipal water infrastructure is attacked in South Africa in a corruption scheme to obtain contracts
properties.Description: In January 2016, Delangokubona "business forum" members, described as part of South Africa's "water mafia," lay siege to municipal infrastructure in KwaZulu-Natal threatening to kill stormwater teams and destroy infrastructure in an effort to get tenders without a bidding process.

properties.Title: Grassroots leader of efforts to stop hydroelectric projects in Brazil goes missing and is then found dead in a reservoir
properties.Description: A leader of the Movement of People Affected by Dams, Nilce de Souza Magalhaes, goes missing in January 2016 and is found dead six months later in the Jirau Dam reservoir. She had criticized Sustainable Energy Brazil, the organization leading a dam hydroelectric plant.

properties.Title: Baloch Republican or Liberation Army fighters abduct and kill two laborers working at the Shadi Kaur Dam, Pakistan
properties.Description: Baloch Republican or Liberation Army fighters abduct and kill two laborers working at the Shadi Kaur Dam, Pakistan.

properties.Title: Nelson Garcia, environmental activist in Honduras working to protect land and water rights, is assassinated
properties.Description: Nelson Garcia, an enviornmental activist working in Honduras to protect land and water was assassinated by unidentified gunmen. He was a member of Berta Caceres's indigenous group COPINH. She was murdered in 2015 as well for her opposition to the Agua Zarca Dam.

properties.Title: A community leader representing families displaced by dam project killed in Sinaloa, Mexico
properties.Description: In Sinaloa, Mexico, Atilano Roman Tirado is shot and killed while hosting a local radio show. Mr. Tirado was a leader of the approximately 800 families that were displaced by the Picachos dam and were asking the Mexican government for compensation.

properties.Title: Two people killed and many injured protesting arrest of anti-dam activist in India
properties.Description: On May 2, 2016 in India, two people, Tsering Tempa and Nyima Wangdi, are shot and killed by police while protesting the arrest of the anti-dam activist, Lama Lobsany Gyatso. Others at the protest are injured.

properties.Title: An unidentified group conducts an armed assault and attack on the Yasmak Dam construction site, Gumushane, Turkey
properties.Description: An unidentified group assaults the Yasmak Dam construction site in Gumushane, Turkey with rifles and shotguns.

properties.Title: Attacks on energy and water systems in Aleppo, Syria cut off water to two million residents
properties.Description: Two million people lack water supply in Aleppo, Syria after attacks on energy and water infrastructure.

properties.Title: Indigenous activist opposing a dam project is found dead in a suspicious suicide in Chile
properties.Description: An Indigenous activist, Macarena Valdes, is found dead in her home in Chile in an apparent suicide on August 2016. However, Valdes and her husband had been actively campaigning against the construction of a dam, including organizing blockades of the project. In 2018, prosecutors were foced to repoen the case when her family found a police report that implicated involvement of a man in her death.

properties.Title: Suspected Kuki National Front fighters fire blank bullets at the Lamlai Khullen-Maphou Dam, India
properties.Description: Suspected Kuki National Front fighters fired blank bullets at the Lamlai Khullen-Maphou Dam, India during an armed assault in an attempt to incite panic.

properties.Title: Residents and police clash over lack of access to water in South Africa
properties.Description: Residents of Bushbuckridge, Ehlanzeni, Mpumalanga, South Africa, who claim to have asked for water for three years, stage a protest, including burning tires. Police respond to the protest, firing rubber bullets.

properties.Title: Residents in a violent protest over water shortages in South Africa
properties.Description: Residents of Msinga, Umzinyathi, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa, protesting against water shortages, block roads, target vehicles with stones, loot shops, and shoot at police.

properties.Title: Residents of Babar, Algeria protest lack of water and sanitation infrastructure
properties.Description: Residents of Babar, Khenchela, Algeria demonstrate to demand improvement of sanitation and drinking water infrastructure. The demonstrations include blocking roads with burning tires.

properties.Title: Road is blocked during protests over lack of access to water in Algeria
properties.Description: Citizens of Grarem Gouga, Mila, Algeria block a road with barricades to demand more water supply, in protest of only receiving water every 15 days.

properties.Title: Suspected Taliban militants blow up irrigation dam in Afghanistan
properties.Description: Suspected Taliban militants blow up a dam storing water for irrigation in Shorabak, Kandahar, Afghanistan.

properties.Title: Syrian forces regain control from the Islamic State over water treatment and pumping plants
properties.Description: Syrian governmental forces and their allies regain control from the Islamic State of the water treatment and pumping plants for the strategic town of al Khafsa on the Euphrates River.

properties.Title: Three Jordanian water authority employees are attacked in conflict over water well
properties.Description: Three employees of a water authority agency in Ar Ramtha, Irbid, Jordan are shot at and assaulted during their attempt to prevent the drilling of a water well.

properties.Title: Two communities clash over water, leaving one dead
properties.Description: A fight over water resources erupts in Oaxaca, Mexico, on May 17 between neighboring community members, leaving one dead, several injured, and numerous houses destroyed. The conflict began due to a disagreement between two communities, Ayutla and Tamazulápam del Espíritu Santo, around which one has the right to a spring that provides potable water supplies.

properties.Title: Two people killed in fight over control of water well, Somalia
properties.Description: Two people are killed when local clans fight over control of a water point in Galkayo, Mudug, Somalia.

properties.Title: US coalition against the Islamic State damages main pipeline to Raqqa
properties.Description: The U.S.-led coalition against the Islamic State damages the main pipeline supplying water to Raqqa. The official Syrian Arab News Agency says the pipeline was damaged in an airstrike.

properties.Title: Violence between herdsmen and farmers in Sudan over access to water and pasture
properties.Description: Local militia allegedly supported by the Sudan People's Liberation Army attack Kenyan herdsmen who crossed the border in search of water and pasture for their cattle.

properties.Title: Violence erupts during protests over lack of clean water in South Africa
properties.Description: Residents protesting road conditions and lack of clean water are dispersed by police with teargas in Matatiele, Alfred Nzo, Eastern Cape, South Africa. The protest tactics were reported to include blocking roads with burning tires and large boulders.

properties.Title: Violent clashes between youth and police over water and power cuts in Guinea
properties.Description: Violent clashes break out between youth and police, causing injuries on both sides, including reports of two youths sustaining injuries from gunfire. The youth are protesting against power and water cuts in Sangarédi, Boke, Guinea.

properties.Title: Violent clashes in South Sudan lead to 10 dead, many wounded, and destruction of a water point
properties.Description: Violent clashes between government and the Sudan People's Liberation Movement-In Opposition (SPLA-IO) cause the destruction of a water point and leave a reported 10 people dead and 16 more wounded in Wichok, Rubkona, Unity, South Sudan.

properties.Title: Water and electrical systems in Yemen are attacked
properties.Description: Airstrikes by coalition forces hit water and electricity systems in Sahar, Sadah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water and electrical systems in Yemen are attacked by coalition forces, killing two
properties.Description: Two civilians in Maqbanah, Taiz, Yemen, are killed from airstrikes by coalition forces targeting water and electricity systems.

properties.Title: Water and land disputes between farmers and pastoralists leave one dead in Kenya
properties.Description: One person is shot and killed after violence erupts between the farmer and herder communities in Kenya where water and land disputes are a driver of conflict.

properties.Title: Water and sewage treatment plants are attacked during air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: Two water and sewage treatment plants in Hudaydah, Yemen are hit during air raids in Bajil and Al-Hawk Districts.

properties.Title: Water drill is hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water drill is hit during an air raid in Al-Safra'a District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water for the Bari community, Somalia is cut off
properties.Description: Water for the Bari community in Somalia is cut off during a violent confrontation between militants that reportedly support Islamic State and Puntland security forces.

properties.Title: Water project is bombed during air raid
properties.Description: A water project is bombed during an air raid in Sihar District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water project is hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water project is hit during an air raid in Hamdan District, Sanaa, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water pumps destroyed in conflict over access to water in Mali
properties.Description: People identified as militants destroy several water pumps and a number of fields and seize cattle in Kewa, Djenne, Mopti, Mali.

properties.Title: Water tankers are destroyed in Yemen
properties.Description: Al-Qaeda affiliated militants are reported to destroy multiple water tankers that belonged to Houthis militants and the Yemeni Army in Wald Rabi, Al Bayda,  and Lawdar, Abyan, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water truck is damaged by air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water truck is damaged by an air raid in Al-Khawkhah District, Hudaydah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water truck is hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water truck is hit during an air raid in Harf Sufyan District, Amran, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water truck near a market is hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water truck is hit during an air raid near Hayran market, Hayran District, Hajjah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water well and desalination plant are hit during air raids
properties.Description: A water well and a desalination plant in Al Mokha District, Taizz, Yemen are hit during air raids.

properties.Title: Water well attacked in Yemen during air raids
properties.Description: A water well is hit during air raids in Mawza District, Taizz, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water well is poisoned in Somalia, killing more than 30 people
properties.Description: Thirty-two people are reported killed and many others sickened from a poisoned water well in Masuubiye, Baidoa, Somalia. It is suspected that the well was poisoned by al Shabaab, a militant group affiliated with Al-Qaeda, in an attempt to prevent government soldiers from drinking the water.

properties.Title: Well is hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: The Al-Naysani well in Maqbanah District, Taizz, Yemen is hit during an air raid.

properties.Title: A locally organized blockade in protest of a dam project polluting a river is repeatedly attacked
properties.Description: Community members from Pajuiles, Honduras organize a blockade in protest of a dam project that is causing their local river to be polluted. People at the blockade are attacked several times over the course of a year with tear gas and other acts of physical violence. Some of the organizers are arrested and charged with usurpation and coercion.

properties.Title: Pro-government snipers are reported to shoot at people trying to reach a spring in the besieged town Madaya, Syria
properties.Description: Pro-government snipers are reported to shoot at people coming from the besieged town Madaya, Syria who were trying to reach the spring in Bqine.

properties.Title: 11 injured in clashes in Sudan between farmers and herdsmen over access to water
properties.Description: Eleven people are injured after clashes between herdsmen over a water point in South Kordofan, Sudan.

properties.Title: 38 killed; 30 injured in fighting over access to water and land in South Sudan
properties.Description: According to a report, 38 people are killed and another 30 are injured in fighting between two local clans over water points, grazing land, and border areas, in Gogrial West, Warrap, South Sudan.

properties.Title: A bomb planted at water, oil, and gas pipelines explodes in Iraq
properties.Description: One of four bombs planted near water, gas, and oil pipelines explodes, injuring a policeman in Dibis, Kirkuk, Iraq.

properties.Title: Air raid damages water well in Yemen
properties.Description: An air raid damages a water well in Nihm District, Sanaa, Yemen.

properties.Title: Airstrike by coalition forces hits water and electric systems in Hajjah, Yemen
properties.Description: An airstrike by coalition forces hits water and electricity stations in Abs, Hajjah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Airstrike by coalition forces hits water and electric systems in Sanaa, Yemen
properties.Description: An airstrike by coalition forces hits water and electricity stations in Al Kaniq, Hamdan, Sanaa, Yemen.

properties.Title: Airstrikes in Jahran, Dhamar, Yemen hit water systems
properties.Description: Airstrikes in Jahran, Dhamar, Yemen by coalition forces hit water and electricity systems.

properties.Title: Airstrikes in Yemen destroy water and electrical systems
properties.Description: Airstrikes by coalition forces over a few days hit water and electricity systems in Sanaa and Taiz, Yemen.

properties.Title: Airstrikes, shelling and sniper attacks damage water structures in Al Mohka, Yemen
properties.Description: Fighting in Yemen has damaged water infrastructure in several cities. Airstrikes, shelling, and sniper attacks in and around the town of Al Mokha have killed and injured scores of civilians and halted most services, including the main market and the water supply system.

properties.Title: Al-Aqran dam is hit during three air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: The Al-Aqran dam is hit during three air raids in Nihm District, Sanaa, Yemen.

properties.Title: Al-Shabaab militants destroy water well in Kenya
properties.Description: Al-Shabaab militants destroy an important water well that had been drilled by the Kenyan army in the town of Lafey, Mandera County, leaving hundreds of residents without water.

properties.Title: Attack on Islamic State forces controlling the Tabqa Dam
properties.Description: A combined U.S. and Syrian force launches an attack in Syria on Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) forces controlling the Tabqa Dam, a "choke point" on the Euphrates River and source of hydroelectric power for the region, which has been under ISIL control. The bombs cause sever damage to the dam, stopping the flow of the river through it, threatening the integrity of the dam itself. Tens
properties.Sources: Gibbons-Neff 2017; British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) 2017; Phillips, Khan, and Schmitt 2022

properties.Title: Bomb damages water truck in Egypt
properties.Description: Islamic State fighters claim responsibility for bombs placed in a road that detonates, damaging a water truck and a tank in Sheikh Zuweid, North Sinai, Egypt.

properties.Title: Citizens protesting water cuts shut down local government in Algeria
properties.Description: Citizens protesting against water cuts shut down the local government, including the city hall, for several days in two cities in northern Algeria.

properties.Title: Civilians protesting lack of access to water are reportedly beaten and tortured in Sudan
properties.Description: Five people from a citizen water committee and local police in El Fasher, North Darfur, Sudan, are reportedly beaten and tortured by military officers after asking the military to allow local residents to organize and take water from a local source. Residents stage protests against the violent reaction of the military.
properties.Sources: Sudanese Online 2017; Raleigh et al 2010 ID. 318554

properties.Title: Clashes in Darfur between farmers and herders over water access result in deaths
properties.Description: At least 70 people have been killed in the past few months in clashes between farmers and herders over access to water resources and land in the southern part of Darfur.

properties.Title: Clashes over the ecological impacts of a proposed coal plant on aquatic ecosystems and fisheries
properties.Description: Protesters take to the streets in the center of Dhaka and more than 50 are injured in clashes over the potential ecological impacts of a proposed coal plant on aquatic ecosystems and fisheries. These actions follow on protests from 2016.

properties.Title: Community demonstrator murdered while protesting local hydroelectric projects
properties.Description: Don Sebastian Alonzo Juan, 72, is shot and killed while participating in a peaceful community demonstration in Yichkisis, village of San Mateo Ixtatan, Huehuetenango, Guatemala, against three local hydroelectric projects. The shots are reportedly fired by the private security guards hired by the hydroelectric project company.

properties.Title: Conflict over water between two clans in Somalia kill at least 14 people
properties.Description: A conflict between two clans that started over the use of water and pastureland leaves at least 14 people dead and 50 others injured in Garowe, Nugaal, Somalia. Sources differ on the number of dead and the date of the violent clash.

properties.Title: Conflict over water quotas lead to two deaths in Iraq
properties.Description: Conflict arises between two tribes over water quotas, leading to two deaths and eight injuries in Suq Al-Shoyokh, Thi-Qar, Iraq.

properties.Title: Demonstration in South Africa over access to clean drinking water turns violent
properties.Description: A demonstration led by locals from the northwest village of Moeka, South Africa for clean drinking water turns violent. One protester is shot and killed and several homes are destroyed.

properties.Title: Demonstration over access to clean water turns violent
properties.Description: Demonstrations led by residents of Ketu South, Volta, Ghana, who are upset about the drying up of domestic groundwater wells, turn violent. Property is damaged and clashes between residents and police result in the death of one resident and the injury of two others.

properties.Title: Demonstration over access to water and concerns about salary payments turn violent
properties.Description: Citizens from Crater, Aden, Yemen, demonstrate to demand salary payments and access to water and electricity services by blocking roads and setting tires on fire.

properties.Title: Ethiopian troops reportedly attack a water source in Somalia
properties.Description: Troops from Ethiopia reportedly attack a water source used by al Shabaab, leading to further fighting, in Bulo Burto, Hilraan, Somalia.

properties.Title: Fight breaks out over access to water storage tank, Somalia
properties.Description: A fight breaks out between two Somalia militias over a dispute concerning an underground water storage tank.

properties.Title: Fighting between two clans in Sudan over ownership of water point leaves six dead
properties.Description: Fighting between two clans near Lagawa, West Kordofan, Sudan over ownership of a water point leaves six dead and several others wounded.

properties.Title: Fighting damages the Donetsk Filtration Station supplying water to 40,000 people in Ukraine
properties.Description: Fighting in the Avdiivka area of Ukraine damages and stops the Donetsk Filtration Station, which supplies around 40,000 people in Avdiivka and surrounding villages (March 2017).

properties.Title: Fighting over access to water between herdsmen and farmers
properties.Description: Fighting is reported between youth and herdsmen over the alleged killing of a farmer by the herdsmen and the destruction of water sources used by their cattle.

properties.Title: Five killed; 11 injured in airstrikes on water systems in Yemen
properties.Description: Airstrikes by coalition forces hits several water projects, killing five civilians and injuring 11 others in Saadah and Sahar, Sadah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Four people killed in clash over access to a water point in North Darfur, Sudan
properties.Description: Former Sudan Liberation Army-Abdul Wahid (SLA-AW) rebels and Arab nomads clash over access to a water point in Kebkabiya, North Darfur, Sudan, resulting in the death of a total of four people from both sides of the conflict.

properties.Title: Government forces in Yemen seize two major water wells; kill 12 Houthi
properties.Description: Government forces seize two major water wells and kill 12 Houthi fighters in Sirwah, Marib, Yemen.

properties.Title: Gunfire exchanged during a water dispute in Somalia
properties.Description: Gunfire is exchanged between a soldier and a local resident due to a water dispute in Qandala, Bari, Somalia. Both parties are wounded.

properties.Title: Illegal water connections contribute to fight between citizen groups, South Africa
properties.Description: Two citizen groups fight two days in a row over allegedly illegal water and electricity connections in the City of Tshwane, Gauteng, South Africa.

properties.Title: Irrigation water pipe destroyed in Kenya by residents concerned over local access to water
properties.Description: Residents of Nyeri Town, Nyeri, Kenya reportedly destroy an irrigation water pipe that is being run through their land for a neighboring community. The residents who caused the destruction are upset because they don't believe they would benefit from the project.

properties.Title: ISIS floods villages in the Deir Hafer Plain of east Aleppo
properties.Description: In response to the advance of the Syrian Arab Army, Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) floods villages they control in the Deir Hafer Plain of east Aleppo by pumping water from Lake Assad into the Al-Jar channel.

properties.Title: Islamic State is accused of cutting off water to a village by diverting a river
properties.Description: A group identified as the Islamic State is accused of cutting off the water flow to a village by diverting a river in Abu Karmah, Muqdadiyah, Diyala, Iraq.

properties.Title: Islamic State militants burn water purification plant in Iraq
properties.Description: Militants from the Islamic State burn a water purification plant in a town north of the city of Mosul.

properties.Title: Israeli forces destroy water pipes in the West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli forces destroy water pipes in a rural area, leading to clashes between Israeli forces and local Palestinians in Jenin, West Bank, Palestine.

properties.Title: Lack of water triggers violent protest in Guinea
properties.Description: People protesting the lack of water supply are dispersed by police after some items are set on fire and access to a local highway is blocked in Matam, Conakry, Guinea.

properties.Title: Multi-day airstrikes in Yemen destroy water and electrical systems
properties.Description: Airstrikes by coalition forces over a multi-day period hit water and electricity systems in Bajil and Al Hawak, Al Hudaydah, Yemen, killing one civilian.

properties.Title: Multiple airstrikes hit a water storage facility, desalination plant, and other water infrastructure
properties.Description: Multi-day airstrikes by coalition forces hit a water storage project, a desalination station, and other water infrastructure and electricity systems in Kamaran, Al Hudaydah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Multiple coalition airstrikes against water and water systems in Yemen
properties.Description: Coalition airstrikes target civilian infrastructure, including a water truck and a water pump, injuring at least one civilian in Al Azqul, Sahar, Sadah, and Al Irafaf, Damt, Ad Dali, Yemen.

properties.Title: Nearly 3 million people left without reliable access to water supplies in Ukraine
properties.Description: A long series of attacks on energy and water systems in the Ukraine has intermittently left nearly three million people without access to reliable water supplies. This has included repeated damage to the Donetsk Filtration Plant, the South Donbas water pipeline, energy plants that supply power to water treatment and distribution systems, and the Carbonit Water Pumping Station.
properties.Sources: ReliefWeb 2017b; UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (UNOCHA) 2017; International Committee for the Red Cross

properties.Title: One killed in dispute over access to a water well
properties.Description: Two men from within the same clan get in a violent dispute over a well and the water supply in the village of Buundo Weyn, Beledweyne, Hiiraan, Somalia. One of the men shoots the other, killing him.

properties.Title: Police kill five farmers during protests over water shortages in Madhya Pradesh, India
properties.Description: Water shortages due to drought and mismanagement contribute to violent unrest of farmers in Madhya Pradesh, India. During one protest, police shoot into a crowd, killing five protestors.

properties.Title: Power lines in Dhamar, Yemen are damaged, cutting water to over a million
properties.Description: The power lines connected to a main water supply system in the city of Dhamar, Yemen are damaged by military activity, affecting approximately one million residents who rely on the water source.

properties.Title: Protest over shortages of drinking water turn violent in Morocco
properties.Description: A protest in Zagora, Drâa-Tafilalet, Morocco, over shortages of drinking water turns violent, resulting in 21 arrests, damage to property, and injuries to members of the security force.

properties.Title: Protest over water outages, power cuts, and fees turns violent
properties.Description: Students lead a campus protest at a university over water outages, power cuts, and fees in Nkonkobe, Amathole, Eastern Cape, South Africa. The protest turns violent and the campus is shut down.

properties.Title: Protestors and police clash during march against water and electricity cutoffs
properties.Description: A march is led by members of Bundu dia Kongo who are protesting the cut off of water and electricity services to their leaders' residence. Protestors and police clash, leading to injuries in both parties.

properties.Title: Protests in Guinea over lack of water and electricity lead to violent clashes, killing three and injuring many
properties.Description: Protests in Boke, Guinea about lack of water and electricity lead to clashes between police and protestors, leading to property damage, three dead, and 41 wounded.

properties.Title: Protests over cuts in water and power services turn violent in Guinea
properties.Description: People protesting in Sangarédi, Boke, Guinea against cuts to water and power services are reported to have raided and attacked the gendarmerie, the police premises, and the residences of several individuals.

properties.Title: Protests over decreasing local drinking water supply
properties.Description: Protestors from the city of Chetma, Biskra, Algeria block roads to protest against the decreasing local drinking water supply.

properties.Title: Protests over inadequate water, food, and healthcare lead to 10 arrests in Nigeria
properties.Description: Ten people who had been uprooted from their homes by Boko Haram are arrested when protesting for better water, food, and healthcare at their temporary camps in Konduga, Borno, Nigeria.

properties.Title: Protests over water cuts turn violent in Tunisia
properties.Description: A long series of protests in July 2017 are reported associated with cuts in the distribution of drinking water. Buses are reportedly damaged by residents; citizens blockade a road; security forces and protesters are injured in confrontations.

properties.Title: Protests over water outages turn violent in Sudan
properties.Description: People protesting water outages are dispersed by police officers in Port Sudan, Red Sea, Sudan.

properties.Title: Protests over water shortages turn violent in South Africa
properties.Description: Residents in South Africa protesting against water shortages are reported to have burned water tanks meant to help alleviate the problem.

properties.Title: Baloch Liberation Front fighters assault and injure two workers at the Shadi Kaur Dam construction site in Pakistan
properties.Description: Baloch Liberation Front fighters assault and injure two workers at the Shadi Kaur Dam construction site in Pakistan.

properties.Title: Three workers from the Saichang-Maphou Dam site are abducted and two are killed by suspected Kuki National Front fighters, India
properties.Description: Suspected Kuki National Front fighters abduct three workers from the Saichang-Maphou Dam site, India and then kill two of them.

properties.Title: Fighters from an Ethiopian liberation movement attack the Grand Renaissance dam in Ethiopia
properties.Description: Suspected Benishangul Gumuz People's Liberation Movement fighters attempt a bombing/explosive attack on the Grand Renaissance Dam. At least 13 attackers are killed.

properties.Title: New People's Army fighters in the Phlippines attack the Lepanto Mine Tailings Dam injuring two and destroying equipment
properties.Description: New People's Army fighters in the Phlippines attack the Lepanto Mine Tailings Dam using explosive devices and firearms, injuring two and destroying construction equipment. The fighters state the attacks are meant to punish the mining company for environmental damages.

properties.Title: Suspected Taliban detonate explosive devices, destroying Shorabak Dam, Kandahar, Afghanistan
properties.Description: Suspected Taliban detonate explosive devices, destroying Shorabak Dam that supplies drinking water in Kandahar, Afghanistan.

properties.Title: Suspected ISIL fighters attack security forces at a small dam near Kifri, Diyala Province, Iraq
properties.Description: Suspected Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) fighters attack security forces at a small dam near Kifri, Diyala Province, Iraq.

properties.Title: Environmental defender killed for activisim against Provenir II dam in Colombia
properties.Description: An environmental defender, Luis Edilson Arango Gallego, is killed in August 2017 near his home in Puerto Nare, Colombia. He had been recently working to defend the Samana Norte River and block the Provenir II hydroelectric plant from being built.
properties.Sources: Global Witness 2024; Universidad Nacional de Colombia n.d.

properties.Title: Suspected Taliban fighters fire rockets at the Kajaki Dam, Afghanistan, injuring one
properties.Description: Suspected Taliban fighters conduct an attack by firing rockets at the Kajaki Dam, Afghanistan injuring a security guard.

properties.Title: Community leader fighting for Afro Rights and protection of water resources killed in Honduras
properties.Description: A 73 year-old community leader in Honduras, Silvino Zapata Martinez, who fought for the rights of the Garifuna (Afro-descendant) community and campaigned to protect local water and land resources, such as the Masca River, is shot by gunmen and left to die.

properties.Title: The water tanks on a hospital in Kafr Batna, Syria are damaged by pro-government artillery
properties.Description: The water tanks on a hospital in Kafr Batna, Syria are damaged by pro-government artillery.

properties.Title: Sanitation facility damaged in conflict in Yemen
properties.Description: A sanitation facility in Zabid District, Hudaydah is damaged in the ongoing conflict in Yemen.

properties.Title: Tank used to store water is by an airstrike
properties.Description: A tank used to store potable water is hit by a Saudi coalition airstrike in Kamaran district, Yemen.

properties.Title: Tens of thousands admitted to hospitals from contaminated drinking water, inciting violence that leads to deaths and injuries
properties.Description: Tens of thousands fall ill from contaminated drinking water in Basra, Iraq. In response, citizen unrest escalates and protests turn violent, leaving at least 15 dead and hundreds injured.

properties.Title: The Donetsk Filter Station is attacked, affecting water supply to 350,000
properties.Description: Damage to the Donetsk Filter Station from shelling as well as gunfire directed at water station staff affects the reliability of water supply to approximately 350,000 people. This event is related to previous disruptions in water services from similar attacks in 2017.
properties.Sources: Dymkovskyy 2018; UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (UNOCHA) 2018b

properties.Title: Two airstrikes hit a water dam
properties.Description: The Saudi-led coalition launches two airstrikes at Houthi forces in the Al Wahabiyah area in Al Sawadiyah district, Al Bayda Governorate, Yemen, hitting a water dam and killing a number of Houthi fighters.

properties.Title: Two killed while protecting water rights in Mexico
properties.Description: A father and son, Ramon Hernandez Nevarez and Anselmo Hernandez Andujo, are murdered while traveling together in Namiquipa, Chihuaua, Mexico. The two were farmers and known locally as water defenders, working to protect the Carmen River from over exploitation. The murderers have not been identified, however, it is thought that they were killed by people from an armed cartel called "La Linea."

properties.Title: Two men receive death threats for fighting against water wells in Chihuahua, Mexico
properties.Description: Two men, Martin Solis Bustamente and Eraclio Rodriguez, receive death threats for fighting what they see as the illegal construction and operation of water wells in the Carmen River Basin, Chihuahua, Mexico.

properties.Title: Two people are killed because of a dispute over a watercourse
properties.Description: Two people are killed and another is injured as a result of a tribal dispute over a watercourse in Bait Al-Hadi Village, Al-Qiblah, Ar Rujum District, Al-Mahwit Governorate, Yemen.

properties.Title: Violence breaks out at a line waiting for water at a spring in Cape Town
properties.Description: Violence breaks out at a line waiting for water at a spring in a neighborhood in Cape Town after severe drought leaves the city with dwindling water supplies. After restrictions were imposed in early 2018, citizens have taken to collecting water from local springs, which are not counted in their water restrictions. In response to this violence, guards are posted at the spring and restrictions put

properties.Title: Violence in western Ethiopia, in part driven by shortages of water and land resources, displaces one million people
properties.Description: Violence in western Ethiopia displaces one million people. The violence is in part driven by shortages of water and land resources.

properties.Title: Violent clashes between farmers and police over access to water leave one dead in Kazeroon, Iran
properties.Description: One person is killed when police are sent to halt "illegal" water pumps Kazeroon, Iran, used by farmers and other villagers to access water.

properties.Title: War and soaring temperatures leave women and children water collectors vulnerable in South Sudan
properties.Description: War and soaring temperatures leave many rural communities in South Sudan with little or no local water sources. Women and children are subjected to violent attacks, rape, and murder when traveling to regional sources to collect water. Leaders report conflicts sparked by water shortages occurring between the many of the 60 different ethnic groups across the country.

properties.Title: Water company truck hits landmine on the way to repair water pipes in Ukraine, injuring two
properties.Description: A water company truck driven by two employees strikes a land mine on its way to an emergency repair of damages to a water pipe due to attacks. Two water utility employees are injured. The water pipe services approximately 45,000 people with water.
properties.Sources: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (UNOCHA) 2018a

properties.Title: Water dam hit during air raids
properties.Description: A water dam is hit during multiple air raids in Al-Sawadiyah District, Bayda, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water driller damaged in conflict in Yemen
properties.Description: A water driller in Baqim District, Saada is damaged in the ongoing conflict in Yemen.

properties.Title: Water driller hit during air raid
properties.Description: A water driller is hit during an air raid in Al-Dhuha district, Hudaydah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water driller hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water driller is hit during an air raid in Majz District, Sa'ada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water driller hit during air raids
properties.Description: A water driller is hit during multiple air raids in Sihar District, Sa'ada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water driller is hit during air raids
properties.Description: A water driller is hit during multiple air raids in Sihar District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water fountain damaged in air raid
properties.Description: A water fountain in Haydan District, Sa'ada, Yemen is damaged in an air raid.

properties.Title: Water fountain is damaged in air raid
properties.Description: A water fountain in Haydan District, Saada, Yemen is damaged in an air raid.

properties.Title: Water infrastructure attacked in Syria
properties.Description: Water infrastructure are attacked, along with health centers and hospitals, in an offensive by Turkey in the Afrin and Tal Rifaat districts in Syria.

properties.Title: Water project hit during air raid
properties.Description: A water project is hit during an air raid in Sihar District, Sa'ada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water project hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: A water project is hit during an air raid in Al-Safra'a District, Sa'ada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water pump hit during air raid
properties.Description: A water pump is hit during an air raid in Sihar District, Sa'ada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water shortages in India lead to deadly fights between civilians and harassment of water company employees
properties.Description: Lack of water in cities across India -- due to drought, growing populations, and inadequate infrastructure -- leads to high tensions, resulting in at least two deaths when a fight breaks out between residents waiting in line at a water truck. Those working for the water companies with the responsibility of turning different water lines on and off, called "key men," are increasingly harassed by res

properties.Title: Water tank hit during air raid
properties.Description: A water tank is hit during an air raid in Haydan District, Sa'ada, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water tank is damaged in conflict in Yemen
properties.Description: A water tank in Al-Durayhimi District, Hudaydah is damaged in the ongoing conflict in Yemen.

properties.Title: Water well digging machine is destroyed by airstrikes
properties.Description: In Sadah, Yemen, a civilian is injured and a water well digging machine is destroyed by 5 Saudi-led coalition airstrikes that hit in Farwah and Mahather areas of Sahar district.

properties.Title: Water wells hit during air raids
properties.Description: Water wells are hit during multiple air raids in Kamaran, Hudaydah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Water wells project hit during air raids
properties.Description: A water wells project is hit during multiple air raids in Al-Hudaydah city, Al-Hudaydah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Well damaged in Al-Silw District, Taizz, Yemen
properties.Description: A water well in Al-Silw District, Taizz is damaged in the ongoing conflict in Yemen.

properties.Title: Well damaged in conflict in Hays District, Hudaydah, Yemen
properties.Description: A water well in Hays District, Hudaydah is damaged in the ongoing conflict in Yemen.

properties.Title: 11 deaths attributed to ongoing conflict between herdsmen and farmers
properties.Description: Eleven are killed and 11 more injured during an attack by Fulani herdsmen on a farming community in Central Nigeria, ignited by the continuing conflict over land and water resources.

properties.Title: A fight over a water point in Syria between government militias and Islamic State fighters results in multiple deaths
properties.Description: Regime and loyalist militia forces including Hezbollah clash with Islamic State fighters in the Tlul al Safa area of Syria over an important water point. Regime air and missile strikes kill at least four Islamic State fighters and an estimated 10 others.

properties.Title: A large number of deaths are reported in attack on farmers by armed herdsmen in Nigeria over water and land resources
properties.Description: On a single day in Nigeria's Plateau state, an attack by armed herdsmen leaves dozens in farming communities dead. This attack is part of ongoing violence that is in part triggered by access to water and land resources. Reports vary in the number dead; one cites 200 and another, 86.
properties.Sources: Agence France-Presse (AFP) 2018b; Reuters 2018; McNeill and Akwagyiram 2018

properties.Title: A water tank and other farm items are damaged in an attack on a cowshed construction site in India
properties.Description: On August 15, a water tank, a tractor, and motorbike are damaged and several cows are injured in an attack on a cowshed construction site in Bara, Bihar, India. The Communist Part of India - Maoist claim responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201808150052

properties.Title: A water well and other structures are damaged by airstrike
properties.Description: Five farmers are killed in an airstrike while getting water from a pump in Al-Jah in Bayt Al-Faqih, Yemen. The water well, a mosque, and houses are damaged in the airstrike.

properties.Title: Air raid targets a water tanker
properties.Description: A Saudi-led coalition air raid is reported on Al Ghawl area of Nihm district, northeast of Sanaa city, allegedly targeting a water tanker. No casualties are reported.

properties.Title: Air raids are reported on a water rig
properties.Description: Two Saudi-led coalition air raids are reported on a water rig in Al Ammar area of Sadah border Governorate in northwestern Yemen. No casualties are reported.

properties.Title: Air raids are reported on water wells and reservoirs in Yemen
properties.Description: Five Saudi-led coalition air raids are reported on water wells and reservoirs across the Kamaran island in the north of Hudaydah Governorate, western Yemen. No casualties are reported but a large number of wells and reservoirs are destroyed.

properties.Title: Airstrike hits a water project in Yemen
properties.Description: A Saudi-led coalition airstrike reportedly hits a water project in the Nushur area of Safra district, threatening to affect up to 10,500 people who rely on the water supplied by the project in Yemen. No injuries or fatalities are reported. This particular project has been hit three times by airstrikes.

properties.Title: Airstrike targets a water project in Yemen
properties.Description: A Saudi-led coalition airstrike targets a water project in the al-Hamzat area of Sahar district, Yemen.

properties.Title: Airstrike targets a water-transport truck in Yemen
properties.Description: A young girl is seriously injured by a Saudi-led coalition airstrike that targeted a water-transport truck in Ad Dhahi area of Hudayda, Yemen.

properties.Title: Airstrikes damage water and sanitation infrastructure in Yemen
properties.Description: Airstrikes over several days in July in the city of Al-Hudaydah, Yemen, damage water and sanitation infrastructure, including many drinking water wells.

properties.Title: Al Shabaab diverts a river, forcing opposition to undefendable higher ground in Somalia
properties.Description: Al Shabaab, a group fighting to create an Islamic state in Somalia, diverts water from the Jubba River, causing a flood that forced Somali, Kenyan, and U.S. military forces to move to higher ground where they were later ambushed. One soldier from the U.S. was killed in the ambush.

properties.Title: At least 8 more killed in ongoing conflict over access to water between farmers and herders, Mali
properties.Description: At least eight more people die in an ongoing conflict between the Fulani and Dogon communities in Mali near the town of Koro, sparked by access to water and grazing disputes. Since March 2018, the total death toll from this ongoing conflict is reported to be 25.

properties.Title: Attacks in Farah, Afghanistan are reportedly related to efforts by Iran to disrupt water projects in Afghanistan
properties.Description: Attacks in Farah in the western region of Afghanistan in early 2018 by the Taliban may, in part, be supported by Iran in an attempt to reduce Afghanistan's ability to pursue water dam infrastructure projects.
properties.Sources: Hussein and Jedinia 2018; Jerusalem Post, The 2018

properties.Title: Attacks on water infrastructure in the ongoing conflict in eastern Ukraine cause water shortages for millions
properties.Description: Water shortages are reported by residents living in at least 20 communities near the line of conflict in the Donbas region of Ukraine. In spite of a ceasefire negotiated in 2014, the ongoing violence has damaged public utilities, leaving millions of residents without water for days and weeks at a time.
properties.Sources: REACH 2018; Protection Cluster 2018; Strategic Foresight Group 2019

properties.Title: Brothers murdered for opposing hydropower project in Guatemala
properties.Description: Neri and Domingo Esteban Pedro were found murdered near the San Andres hydroelectric project on the Yal Witz River in western Guatemala. The brothers were vocal opponents of the project.

properties.Title: Clashes between tribesmen and pro-Houthi force over a water project
properties.Description: For the second consecutive day, clashes between Al 'Ukad tribesmen and pro-Houthi forces are reported in the Ridmah district of Ibb Governorate, Yemen. Clashes are reportedly initiated on September 24 as the tribesmen seize the weapons of a group of Houthi fighters to show their opposition to a water project. Injuries are reported on both sides but no fatalities.

properties.Title: Clashes expand between Tajik and Kyrgyz farmers over irrigation water
properties.Description: Clashes between farmers over irrigation water across the Tajikistan-Kyrgyzstan border escalate, with at least two instances involving armed guards to defuse the fights. One fight started over the installation of a water pump.

properties.Title: Clashes over a water project in Yemen result in injuries
properties.Description: Clashes between Al 'Ukad tribesmen and pro-Houthi forces are reported in the Ridmah district of Ibb governorate, Yemen, over opposition to a water project. Injuries are reported on both sides.

properties.Title: Conflicts over pasture land and water resources lead to violence
properties.Description: 14 people are killed in Northern Kenya, near Marsabit, after ongoing conflicts between two ethnic groups over land and water resources turns violent.

properties.Title: Deaths are reported in protests over a mine that would contaminate local water sources in Honduras
properties.Description: Beginning in 2011, members of the community of El Guapinol, Honduras, protest the development of an open-pit mine that would contaminate their water source, the Guapinol River. In 2018, the group protesting set up an encampment that after several months is violently broken up by security guards. One protestor is shot and killed and others are arrested and put in jail. In October, 2020, another mem

properties.Title: Desalination plant hit during air raids in Yemen
properties.Description: A desalination plant in Hays District, Hudayah, Yemen is hit during air raids.

properties.Title: Farmer injured in a clash with police in Varzaneh, Iran during a strike over access to water
properties.Description: A farmer reports losing sight in one eye and wounds from a pellet gun from a clash with police ignited by a strike over lack of water in Varzaneh, Iran.

properties.Title: Father and son are beaten to death in water dispute in Delhi, India
properties.Description: In Delhi during severe drought in 2018 a father and son are beaten to death in a water dispute.

properties.Title: Haryana, India villages clash over water and 12 are injured
properties.Description: Residents of one village in Haryana install a motor pump to draw water from a local water source, but residents of a nearby village object to it, leading to violent clashes, twelve injuries, and the destruction of the pump.

properties.Title: Iranian security forces shoot at protesters, injuring one, at demonstration in Khorramshahr over lack of water
properties.Description: Protests over lack of drinking water supplies in Khorramshahr, Iran begin in front of government buildings. Social media posts of these protests include sounds of shots and cries and show security forces shooting at protesters. Only one protester is reported injured.

properties.Title: Iraqi female activist assassinated
properties.Description: An Iraqi activist, Su?ad al-?Ali, is assassinated while getting into her vehicle in Basra, Iraq. Mrs. Al-Ali had worked to bring better government services to her community, including safe and reliable drinking water.

properties.Title: Main water tank is targeted by military shelling
properties.Description: The main water tank of Durayhimi district, Yemen, which supplies most of the district in drinking water, is reportedly targeted by military shelling. Conflicting reports from local sources attribute the bombing to either pro-Houthi forces or Saudi-led coalition Apache helicopters that are targeting Houthi snipers near the tank. No casualties are reported.

properties.Title: Major dam and water source captured in Syria
properties.Description: The Afrin Dam, a major dam and water source on the Afrin River in Syria, is captured by Turkish forces and their Syrian militia allies, causing concern for the nearby communities that depend on the dam for water. These same forces are also in control of nearby Maydanki dam. Interruptions to water and electricity in communities dependent on these dams have occurred, along with reports of attacks on

properties.Title: Man shot over access to water in Delhi, India
properties.Description: A man is shot in Sangam Vihar -- one of the poorest parts of Delhi -- during a dispute over water. The summer of 2018 sees one of the worst droughts in Dehli, India.

properties.Title: Mexican farmers and white landowners clash over water
properties.Description: An alliance of local Mexican farmers storm the property of a white family living in Chihuahua state, Mexico, in frustration over what the farmers believe is an excessive use of water from the local river and aquifer. The clash results in one farmer being shot and other injuries to individuals on both sides of the conflict.

properties.Title: More than 70 water systems damaged in Ethiopia related to violent conflicts
properties.Description: At least 76 water systems have been damaged by the ongoing conflict in the Oromia region of Ethiopia. The larger conflict leading to these casualties is between different ethnic groups and has displaced hundreds of thousands of people.

properties.Title: One killed and several injured in fighting between farmers and herders in Tanzania over access to water
properties.Description: One person is killed and several police officers are injured in a fight over access to water resources between herders and farmers near the village of Dihida, Morogoro, Tanzania.

properties.Title: Over 230 people are sickened by poisoned drinking water in the conflict between Ukraine and Russia
properties.Description: Over 230 people are poisoned through contaminated drinking water in the town of Makiivka in the Donetsk region Ukraine. That water is thought to have been intentionally contaminated.

properties.Title: Pro-Hadi forces manage to take a water project
properties.Description: Pro-Hadi forces manage to take a number of sites after clashing with Houthi militias in the Alb area of Sadah, Yemen. According to the source, pro-Hadi troops take the areas of Mimna, Dhaher al-Burkan, Al Masirah, Water project, Al-Faridh, Qiraa'a, and the sniper outpost, all in the Alb area. No information on casualties.

properties.Title: Pro-Houthi forces reportedly blow up a water tank
properties.Description: Pro-Houthi forces reportedly blow up the main water tank of Tuhayta as anti-Houthi forces are closing in on the city.

properties.Title: Pro-Houthi forces reportedly destroy water collection tanks
properties.Description: Property destruction: Pro-Houthi forces reportedly destroy water collection tanks in Bani Zuhayr village west of Hays city in the south of Al Hudaydah Governorate. The tanks are reportedly the only source of water for the citizens of Hays district.

properties.Title: Protest to prevent oil pipeline that threatens water resources is met with violent arrests
properties.Description: Dozens of protestors are arrested and restrained, sometimes with violent tactics, as they physically resist the construction of a major oil pipeline in the United States. The protestors argue that the pipeline is proceeding illegally and that it threatens their water supplies as well as other resources that are vital to their way of life.
properties.Sources: L'eau Est La Vie Camp 2018; Zanolli 2018; Jamail 2018

properties.Title: Protests by farmers over transfers of water from Isfahan to Yazd, Iran turn violent and cause damage to water transfer systems
properties.Description: Water systems used to transfer water from Isfahan to the neighboring province of Yazd are damaged multiple times by farmers protesting the loss of the water supply. Farmers blame the Iranian government for the lack of water in their region.  Reports from social media show police officers attacking farmers associated with the destroyed pipes.

properties.Title: Protests in Iran over lack of water, mismanagement, and corruption around water lead to deaths of dozens
properties.Description: Ongoing protests against lack of water, mismanagement, and corruption around water are led by residents in small towns in western Iran. These protests gain more media attention as tensions escalate over time and may be linked to demonstrations from late 2017 and early 2018 that lead to the death of dozens of people. In 2018, at least nine environmental leaders are arrested, and one jailed environm

properties.Title: Protests in Iran over water shortages and poor quality lead to injuries. Deaths also reported by some outlets
properties.Description: In Iran, protests over the shortage and poor quality of drinking water turn violent as police open fire at rioters who throw stones and attack public buildings and banks. There are conflicting reports over the number of casualties; while Iranian officials deny any fatalities, they acknowledge that 11 people, including police officers, were wounded. Opposition websites report up to 4 fatalities amo

properties.Title: Protests over water shortages turn violent in Iran
properties.Description: Water shortages in the southern part of Iran have caused protests, which turned violent with attacks on property and other infrastructure. Police action to break up the protests lead to several injuries and deaths.

properties.Title: A truck carrying water tanks is used by suicide bombers in a failed attack in Syria
properties.Description: On January 18, a group of suicide bombers using a truck carrying water tanks in Aleppo, Syria is stopped by the Free Syrian Army. Members of the FSA kill three of the assailants and a fourth dies when he detonates his explosive device.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201801180025

properties.Title: Police shoot and kill a man protesting the Bonyunyu Dam project in Kenya
properties.Description: On January 9, 2018, Jomo Nyanguti is shot by police who are guarding surveyors mapping a dam site for the Bonyunyu Dam project in Kenya. It is reported that Nyanguti confronted the police officers and that three other people were arrested. Protestors are concerned that the dam will displace thousands of people from their ancestral lands and have stated that the project has moved forward without pu

properties.Title: Organizer of an encampment in protest of a dam project is killed by security forces in Honduras
properties.Description: An organizer of an encampment in protest of a dam project on the Jilamito River, Ramon Fiallos, is shot and killed by security forces in Arizona, Atlantida, Honduras. The community names a bridge in commemoration of him.

properties.Title: Police kill farmer and another community organizer who helped protect a local river in Honduras
properties.Description: A farmer who helped environmental organizers defending a local river, Geovanny Diaz Carcamo, is taken from his home in Pajuiles, Honduras, and killed by police in the middle of the night. His murder occurs one day after the murder of Ramon Fiallos, another community organizer who also worked in support of protecting a local river.

properties.Title: Grenades are thrown and rounds are fired at a water pump house outside an Air Force post in India
properties.Description: On February 20, grenades are thrown and rounds are fired at a water pump house outside an Air Force post in Malangpora, Jammu and Kashmir, India. No injuries are reported and no group claims the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201802200026

properties.Title: The Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist detonate an explosive device at the dam site of the Upper Karnali Hydropower Project, Nepal
properties.Description: The Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist (CPN-Maoist-Chand) detonate an explosive device at the dam site of the Upper Karnali Hydropower Project, Nepal, demanding the Nepalese government use Nepali companies and labor during hydropower construction.

properties.Title: The Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist detonate an explosive at the powerhouse of the Arun-III Hydropower Project, Nepal
properties.Description: The Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist (CPN-Maoist-Chand) detonate an explosive-laden pressure cooker at the powerhouse of the Arun-III Hydropower Project demanding the Nepalese government use Nepali companies and labor during hydropower construction.

properties.Title: Extensive violence reported over water scarcity in southern Iraq
properties.Description: In the province of Dhi Qar, Iraq, widespread clan clashes erupt due to water scarcity.

properties.Title: A water tanker is damaged in an attack on a construction site in India
properties.Description: On March 2, a water tanker is damaged during an attack on a construction site near Kadambaguda, Odisha, India. One construction worker is kidnapped before being released a short time later.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201803020025

properties.Title: Environmental activist found dead near Kyiv, Ukraine
properties.Description: An environmental activist, Nikolay Yarema, is found dead near Kyiv, Ukraine. Yarema was known for his opposition to the seizure of the Svyatoshinsky Lake area, a well-forested northwestern part of Kyiv bordering the Nyvka River.

properties.Title: A leader of local opposition to the Quadriver Mini Hydro Dam in the Philippines is murdered by assassins
properties.Description: A local leader of opposition to a dam project, Ricardo Pugong Manymi, is shot and killed by assassins in Tinoc town, Ifugao, Philippines. He is a known leader of the opposition to the Quadriver Mini Hydro Dam and of the Ifugao Peasant Movement, which seeks to defend human rights of the indigenous people of the Ifugao province.

properties.Title: Suspected Muslim extremists attack the Djenne Dam construction site in Mali
properties.Description: Suspected Muslim extremists attack the Djenne Dam construction site, Mali, buring cranes, vehicles, generators, and construction materials.

properties.Title: A water filtration vehicle is attacked in Ukraine, five employees are injured
properties.Description: On April 17, a water filtration vehicle is attacked in Donetsk, Ukraine. At least five employees are injured.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201804170026

properties.Title: Employees of Voda Donbassa are wounded during attacks on water systems in Ukraine
properties.Description: In April 2018, five Voda Donbassa (Donbas Water) employees are attacked and injured, resulting in the suspension of operations and evacuation of water sector employees and water shortages for nearly 380,000 civilians, particularly in Avdiivka, Yasynuvata, and Betmanove cities.

properties.Title: A Honduran lawyer who represents people protesting a hydroelectric dam is murdered
properties.Description: Honduran lawyer Carlos Hernandez is found murdered in Tela de Altantida, Honduras after committing to defend the town's mayor that was facing criminal charges for peaceful opposition to a hydroelectric dam project planned on the Jilamito River.

properties.Title: Indigenous leader in Mexico murdered for defending territory from mining and transmission lines from hydroelectric projects
properties.Description: In May 2018 , indigenous leader, Manuel Gaspar Rodriguez, is murdered in the middle of the night in Sierra Norte de Puebla, Mexico. Rodriguez was one of eight facing charges brought by the national electric utility company for participating in a blockade of transmission lines that were to be built in connection with several hydroelectric projects in his home territory.

properties.Title: A water tanker is a casualty in one of seven attacks in Jharkhand, India on the same day
properties.Description: On May 29, seven related attacks occur across Jharkhand, India. A water tanker is a casualty in one of the attacks. The Communist Party of India - Maoist is suspected as the group responsible for the attacks.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201805290033

properties.Title: Of 18 related violent attacks in one day in Thailand, one occurs at a waterworks authority compound
properties.Description: On May 20, 18 related violent attacks occur across Thailand. One of the 18 occurs at a Provincial Waterworks authority compound in Bacho, Narathiwat. No group claimed responsibility for the attack, however, the Barisan Revolusi Nasional are thought to be responsible.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201805200020

properties.Title: A Guatemalan indigenous man involved in opposing dam construction projects is killed
properties.Description: Luis Armando Maldonado Marin, a known member of the Social Pastoral of the Earth, a local group actively opposing the construction of a dam, is shot and killed in Quetzaltenango, Guatemala. This is a region in Guatemala where many indigenous defenders are opposing hydroelectric projects.
properties.Sources: Global Witness 2024; Business & Human Rights Resource Centre n.d.-a

properties.Title: Seven people injured during dispute over irrigation water in Pakistan
properties.Description: Two groups in Jaffarabad District of Balochistan, Pakistan clash over an irrigation water dispute on June 25. Seven people are injured.

properties.Title: Xiangyang, China residents protest water and air pollution and clashes with police lead to injuries and arrests
properties.Description: On June 30, 2018, more than 1000 residents protest untreated wastewater discharge and groundwater contamination at a waste treatment plant in Xiangyang, China. Forty residents are arrested during clashes with local police; some residents are also injured and hospitalized.

properties.Title: Second environmental activist opposed to Gaya dam in the state of Puebla, Mexico, is murdered
properties.Description: A well known leader of local opposition to the Gaya hydroelectric dam in the Mexican state of Puebla, Adrian Tihuilit, is found dead in his car one day after he was reported missing. He is the second defender of human and environmental rights to be killed in the state of Puebla in two weeks.

properties.Title: Suspicious circumstances around death of a young activist fighting water pollution in Ukraine
properties.Description: A young activist known for fighting industrial pollution of local reservoirs and for human rights, Mykola Bychkho, is found dead in the woods near his home in the township of Eskhar (Kharkiv oblast), Ukraine. Although local officals rule his death a suicide, the community disputes this claim and protests authorities to pressure them into further examination of his death. Bychkho had been successfu

properties.Title: A dispute over severe water shortage in the Hansi district of India leads to a clash causing a dozen injuries
properties.Description: On June 26, a violent clash occurs between two villages, Puthi Mangal Khan and Dhani Peeranwali, in Hansi district, India over acute water shortages. At least 12 people are reported injured.

properties.Title: A protest against a bill threatening water privatization in El Salvador is suppressed by government security forces
properties.Description: On June 14, 2018, students from El Salvador University and affiliates to the National Administration for Water Supply and Sewerage march to the Legislative Assembly in San Salvador City to protest a water bill that they claim aims to privatize water. Security forces use teargas to disperse the protesters. One injury is reported.

properties.Title: Clans in Pakistan clash over water supply, killing one and injuring four
properties.Description: Machi and Wadhayo clans clash over water supply and agricultural lands on June 23 in Pakistan. A youth is killed and 4 others are injured.

properties.Title: Protests over water and electric services in South Africa turn violent and leave one police officer injured
properties.Description: On June 30, Dube Village residents block a highway, burn tires, and throw stones at the police demanding service delivery of water and electricity from eThekwini Municipality in Inanda. A police officer is injured during the protest.

properties.Title: Suspected Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist fighters detonate bombs targeting the office of the Arun-III Hydropower Project, Nepal
properties.Description: Suspected Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist (CPN-Maoist-Chand) fighters detonate three bombs targeting the office of the Arun-III Hydropower project, Nepal.

properties.Title: Residents of Tall-e Kushk, Iran clash with police after water pumps are confiscated from well; one person is killed
properties.Description: In July, residents of Tall-e Kushk, Iran clash with police, objecting to the confiscation of water pumps from the wells in the village. One person was killed after the police fired a warning shot to disperse the rioters.

properties.Title: Water project is attacked and workers are killed and kidnapped by suspected militant group in Libya
properties.Description: In July 2018, Daesh militants are suspected of two attacks to workers of the Great Man-made River, a large water supply project that transports water from the Sahara desert to communities and agricultural fields in northern Libya. During the first attack four technicians from the Al-Hassouna plant are kidnapped, but later released. During the second attack two workers are killed and two guards are
properties.Sources: OOSKANews Correspondent 2018; Raleigh et al 2010 ID. 6743748

properties.Title: A dispute over water distribution in Shikarpur, Pakistan leads to more than two dozen injuries
properties.Description: On July 15, 26 men sustain critical injuries in a clash between two groups of Sethar community in Batro area, Shikarpur, Pakistan over water distribution.

properties.Title: A dispute over water used for local mining in Chile leads to a demonstration, clashes with police, and injuries
properties.Description: On July 19, in Salamanca, Chile near Illapel (Coquimbo), around 60 residents of Cuncumen hold a demonstration calling for the end of water usage at the Los Pelambres mine. Police clash with demonstrators; eight injuries are reported.

properties.Title: A hydroelectric plant in Guatemala is destroyed in a dispute over local water control
properties.Description: On July 30, in Purulha, Baja Verapaz, Guatemala, community members occupy a hydroelectric plant, disarm local security, and destroy the infrastructure. The protestors claim that the hydroelectric plant is occupying lands of the community.

properties.Title: A well is damaged in an attack on Kenya Defense Forces in Somalia
properties.Description: On July 27, in an attack on Kenya Defense Forces soldiers in Dhamaso, Gedo, Somalia, a well and telecommunications equipment are damaged. No group claims responsibility for the incident; however, sources attribute the attack to Al-Shabaab.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201807270003

properties.Title: Lack of access to potable water in the Dominican Republic leads to violent demonstrations
