properties.Description: On July 9, 2018, shots are fired at the police station in Navarrete, Villa Bisono, Dominican Republic during a violent demonstration to demand improved access to potable water.

properties.Title: One person is killed during a protest to demand potable water and electricity in Bolivar state, Venezuela
properties.Description: On July 16, one person is killed in San Felix, Ciudad Guayana, Bolivar state, Venezuela during a protest to demand potable water and electric power in the city.

properties.Title: Protests against poor water quality and services in Iraq lead to one death and several injuries
properties.Description: On July 10, hundreds of citizens in Al-Haritha, north of Basra, Iraq block a road in protest against poor services and the salinity of the water. One demonstrator is killed and several wounded.

properties.Title: Protests in Algeria for improved water supply and electricity leads to clashes with police and injuries
properties.Description: On July 22, residents of Ouargla, Algeria protest high electricity prices due to the heat wave and demand better access to tap water. Protesters clashed with police leading to several injuries. On September 15, protests expand and between 5,000 and 25,000 people demonstrate against inadequate water and sanitation infrastructure and a lack of jobs. This clash with police also leads to injuries.

properties.Title: Jaw Prison detainees routinely punished with water cuts in temperatures of up to 122°F, protesters attacked and sent to solitary confinement
properties.Description: In 2018, authorities at Jaw Prison in Bahrain reportedly impose water cuts lasting several days at a time during extreme summer heat, providing prisoners with only one cup of water a day, forcing them to drink toilet water. The prison is said to routinely use water deprivation as a form of punishment. In 2019 a prisoner is assaulted and placed into solitary confinement for protesting the outages,
properties.Sources: Americans for Democracy & Human Rights in Bahrain 2018; Bahrain Today 2019

properties.Title: Environmental activist in India who worked to stop a dam on the Kali River is killed
properties.Description: An environmental activist, Ajit Maneshwar Naik, is killed by a masked assailant in Dandeli, in the state of Karnataka, India, where he worked. Naik had spent years fighting against the construction of what whould have become the seventh dam on the Kali River.

properties.Title: Seven people are hospitalized after a fight over sewage water in Pakistan
properties.Description: Neighbors clash over the issue of sewage water in the street in Gujranwala City, Pakistan on August 16. Seven people are hospitalized.

properties.Title: Two are injured during protests over water services in Miranda, Venezuela
properties.Description: On August 16, in Los Teques, Miranda, Venezuela a group of people build barricades using burning tires and garbage bags to protest problems with water services. The riots turn more violent when a group of people clash with the municipal police with firearms. Two police officers are injured. The police arrest a group of rioters.

properties.Title: Two groups clash at the water plant in Muranga, Kenya, with damage to the water supply system
properties.Description: On August 27th, two groups clash at the water plant in Muranga, Kenya, with one group damaging the water supply at the plant.

properties.Title: Indingenous leader in Mexico known for opposition to hydroelectric projects in his region disappears
properties.Description: An indigenous leader in the Movimiento Agrario Indigena Zapatista (MAIZ) organization, Sergio Rivera Hernandez, goes missing on August 23, 2018. He had been active in working to protect the rights of indigenous communities in the state of Puebla, Mexico, where two dam projects were underway. As of September 2020, a judge ruled to acquit the three individuals who had been charged with his disappear

properties.Title: A fight over the distribution of irrigation water in Pakistan injures seven
properties.Description: On August 18, two groups of the Bozdar tribe clash over distribution of irrigation water in Pir Barhi village, Sindh, Pakistan using sticks, rocks, and guns. Seven people are injured and hospitalized and one person arrested.

properties.Title: A police station water tank is damaged in an attack on the station in India
properties.Description: On June 27, assailants throw a grenade at a police station in Pulwama district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. No people are injured, however the station's water tank is damaged.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201806270018

properties.Title: An attempted theft of a water pump leads to an injury and arrest
properties.Description: On August 21 in Campo Grande, Mato Grosso do Sul, Brazil a man is beaten after he tried to steal a water pump. The man was arrested.

properties.Title: Islamic State militants attack the Sarir water station in Libya and clash with government forces
properties.Description: In August, Libyan government forces clash with Islamic State militants near Haruj following an attack on the Sarir water station.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al 2010 ID. 6738502; National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 20180

properties.Title: One injury reported in protests in the Dominican Republic over access to potable water
properties.Description: On August 1, a woman is injured by gunfire during a protest in Navarrete, Bisono, Dominican Republic to demand access to potable water.

properties.Title: A dispute over access to water leads to one injury in India
properties.Description: On September 14, two families clash in Daria area of Chandigarh over getting water from a tap. A 17 year old girl was injured with a sickle in the fight.

properties.Title: A fight over irrigation water in Pakistan kills two villagers and injures three more
properties.Description: On September 23, the Lanjar and Chandio tribes clash over irrigation water near Moro city (Naushahro Feroze, Sindh). Two villagers are killed and three are injured.

properties.Title: A fight over water drainage in Pakistan kills one person
properties.Description: On September 23, two groups of the Kalhoro community clash over water drainage issues in Kholar, Sindh, Pakistan, killing one man.

properties.Title: A local water system is damaged in a shootout between drug trafficking gangs in Brazil
properties.Description: On September 30, in Sao Goncalo, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil a shoot-out between drug trafficking gangs in the Minha Casa, Minda Vida complex of Joquei neighborhood damages the local water system, leaving residents without water.

properties.Title: A UN mission collecting water for their base in South Sudan is attacked, injuring one Nepalese peacekeeper
properties.Description: On September 15th, an officer with the National Security Service of South Sudan fires at an UNMISS convoy collecting water for the UNMISS base in Yei, wounding a Nepalese peacekeeper.

properties.Title: Indigenous anti-dam activist killed in the state of Nayarit, Mexico
properties.Description: Indigenous anti-dam activist, Margarito Diaz Gonzalez is shot and killed in the state of Nayarit, Mexico. He opposed a dam that threatened the historic territories of the Huichol Indians, also known as the Wixarika.

properties.Title: Water well hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: On October 10, a water well is hit during an air raid in Bait Al-Faqih District, Hudaydah, Yemen.

properties.Title: A demonstration against the lack of water supply in Talara, Peru results in two injuries
properties.Description: On October 1, in Talara, Piura, Peru, hundreds of local inhabitants demonstrate against the lack of water supply in the area. Police clash with demonstrators by shooting rubber bullets and throwing tear gas, injuring two rioters.

properties.Title: A water project is attacked with explosive devices in Iraq
properties.Description: On October 4, a water project in Gharib, Kirkuk, Iraq is targeted and attacked with explosive devices. No group claims responsibility for the attack; however, sources attribute the attack to the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL).
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201810040002

properties.Title: Explosives detonate at a water plant in Iraq
properties.Description: On October 6, explosives detonate at a water plant in Abbasi, Kirkuk, Iraq. No group claims responsibility for the attack; however, sources attribute the attack to the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL).
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201810060046

properties.Title: Government employees repairing a water pipeline are attacked in Iraq
properties.Description: On October 5, government employees from the Ministry of Transportation who are repairing a water pipeline are attacked in Shatt al-Arab, Basra, Iraq. No group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201810060039

properties.Title: One death and several injuries are reported in a clash over wastewater drainage in India
properties.Description: On October 29, a woman is killed and 7 others wounded in a clash over a waste water drainage in Sheharmalpur village (Panipat, Haryana), India.

properties.Title: One injury is reported when Israeli forces destroy a water pipeline in Kafr Qaddum, Palestine
properties.Description: On October 13, Israeli forces destroy a water pipeline in Kafr Qaddum sparking clashes with locals. An elderly woman is reportedly injured when she is hit with a tear gas canister.

properties.Title: Prison inmates in Campeche, Mexico riot to protest lack of drinking water; two injuries are reported
properties.Description: On October 24, inmates of the San Francisco Koben Social Reintegration Center, Campeche, Mexico demonstrate against the lack of drinking water supply. They clash with the Penitentiary Police for about two hours until the Campeche State Undersecretary of Public Security mediates the riot. Two injuries are reported.

properties.Title: Member of an Indigenous rights organization in Mexico working to defend land and rivers is killed
properties.Description: In October 2018 a member of Comite de Defensa de Los Pueblos Indigenas (CODEDI), Noel Castillo Aguilar, is killed in Mexico. Aguilar was an active member in campaigns defending land and rivers in his home territory in the state of Oaxaca.

properties.Title: Violence over irrigation water leads to at least one injury in India
properties.Description: On November 16, in Singanapalle village in Banaganapalle tehsil (Kurnool, AP), India, local political parties clash with each other over water pipes. One person is injured and hospitalized.

properties.Title: An explosive device detonates at an oil pipeline, causing oil to pollute a local waterway in Colombia
properties.Description: On November 21, an oil pipeline is attacked in Boyaca, Colombia, polluting a local waterway with oil. No group claims responsibility; however, sources attribute the attack to the National Liberation Army (ELN).
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201811210002

properties.Title: An oil pipeline is attacked multiple times, causing oil to flow into a local waterway in Colombia
properties.Description: On November 12, an oil pipeline is attacked four times in Arauca, Colombia, causing oil to flow into and pollute a nearby waterway. No group claims responsibility; however, sources attribute the attack to the National Liberation Army (ELN).
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201811110002, 201811110030, 201811120

properties.Title: Clashes near a hospital in Hodeidah, Yemen damage building, destroy water tanks, injure patients; 149 killed in 24 hours
properties.Description: On November 11, fierce clashes between pro-Houthi and anti-Houthi National Resistance forces are reported in the direct vicinity of Al-Thawrah hospital in the south of Hodeidah city, resulting in damages to the building, including the destruction of its water tanks, and the injuring of many patients as they evacuated the hospital. On November 12, medics in hospitals across the city reported the de

properties.Title: Farmers in Qarneh, Iran destroy facilities diverting water from the city of Isfahan to neighboring Yazd province
properties.Description: In mid-November, angry farmers in Qarneh demonstrate against farming water shortages and destroy facilities diverting water from the city of Isfahan to the neighboring Yazd Province, Iran. The security forces clash with the rioters.

properties.Title: Injuries are reported when hundreds of students protest increases in water prices in Sulawesi, Indonesia
properties.Description: On November 30, hundreds of students protest increases in water prices in Watampone district, South Sulawesi province, Indonesia. Two students are injured by unknown attackers.

properties.Title: Suspected Taliban fighters assault and attack the Kamal Khan Dam, Afghanistan, 13 deaths and 7 injuries
properties.Description: Suspected Taliban fighters assault and attack the Kamal Khan Dam, Afghanistan, killing twelve guards. One attacker is killed and seven are wounded.

properties.Title: Water coolers at a mosque are poisoned in Iraq
properties.Description: On December 5, an unknown assailant poisons water coolers at a mosque in Nasiriyah, Dhi Qar, Iraq.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201812050004

properties.Title: Water tank hit during air raid in Yemen
properties.Description: On December 10, a water tank is hit during an air raid in Kamaran District, Hudaydah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Workers at a pumping station in Libya are threatened by attackers with firearms
properties.Description: On December 18, attackers with firearms enter the Hasawneh pumping station and threaten workers in Jafara, Libya. No casualties are reported but valuables are stolen during the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201812180008

properties.Title: Four water wells are attacked in Libya
properties.Description: On December 9, four water wells are attacked in Libya. No group claims responsibility for the attacks.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201812090005, 201812090006, 201812090

properties.Title: Injuries are reported during protests to demand potable water, food, and energy in Lara state, Venezuela
properties.Description: On December 17, in the Palavecino municipality in Lara state, Venezuela residents from several communities protest to demand the distribution of food, domestic gas, and potable water. State police officers use force against protesters by shooting pellets and tear gas to disperse the protest. Pregnant women and the elderly are reportedly beaten by police officers.

properties.Title: 37 killed in herder-farmer conflict over water and land resources in Mali
properties.Description: Thirty-seven civilians are killed in the ongoing conflict between herders and farmers who are fighting over access to water and land resources in central Mali.

properties.Title: 50,000 people flee their homes in central Mali as conflict escalates over land and water
properties.Description: A series of massacres in central Mali, fueled by conflict over land and water resources, causes 50,000 people to flee their homes.

properties.Title: A fight over the price of water leads to deadly clashes in Sudan
properties.Description: A woman was killed in a dispute over the price of water in El Gedaref, Sudan. This violent clash triggers more fighting between familial groups, leading to at least eight more deaths and dozens of injuries.

properties.Title: A grandmother and her grandson are beaten when seeking water from a private well
properties.Description: A grandmother and her grandson in Badhauli village, Sultanpur, UP, India insist on being allowed to use a private well to get water. The well owner refuses and sends men to beat the grandmother and grandson with sticks, injuring them both. The owner is concerned that his well cannot provide water to the entire village and therefore, he does not want to provide water to anyone.

properties.Title: A man is stabbed by his neighbor over a water sharing dispute in India
properties.Description: A man is stabbed by his neighbor over a water sharing dispute in Chennai, India. Current drought and water supply management problems have led to lack of water for millions living in the city.

properties.Title: A violent demonstration over access to water in Panquehue, Chile ends in the destruction of a local water system
properties.Description: A group of citizens demonstrated violently in Panquehue, Chile in the Valparaiso Region. The group destroyed water facilities used for farming claiming that drought and industrial use is leading to water shortages for local residents.

properties.Title: At least 11 people are killed in a conflict over access to water between pastoralists and farmers near the Kenya-Ethiopia border
properties.Description: A deadly flight between farmers and pastoralists over a watering point near the Kenyan-Ethiopian border leaves eleven people dead, two injured, and four missing.

properties.Title: Caste violence in India continues, with access to water and water resources being regular triggers or casualties of conflict
properties.Description: Marginalized socioeconomic groups, known as "Dalits" in the Hindu-based caste system, experience threats, torture, and violence when seeking access to water in Maharashtra, India. Dalit women are targets of violence in particular. Additionally, water sources used by Dalits are at times purposely contaminated with human excrement.

properties.Title: Chilean social unrest tied to cost of living -- including water and power -- and inequality turn violent
properties.Description: Protests in Chile include general civil unrest and reflect dissatisfaction in social and economic inequality, including the price of water and other utilities. Protests grow and turn violent with thousands injured and several dying during clashes with security forces.

properties.Title: Demonstrators protesting over water, electricity, and jobs attacked by Iraqi security forces, with over 100 deaths
properties.Description: Iraqi citizens publicly demonstrating to protest ongoing water, electricity, and job challenges are met with violent repression by Iraqi security forces. Over 100 people are killed within the first seven days of protests.

properties.Title: Disputes between herders and farmers over access to water lead to growing violence in Chad
properties.Description: Herders and farmers who used to coexist peacefully are now inflicting deadly wounds on each other over fights driven by water shortages in the Sahel region of Chad.

properties.Title: Eco-vandalism disrupts water diversions
properties.Description: A water diversion system in the Colorado Rocky Mountains near Berthoud Pass is vandalized, disrupting water deliveries for several days. The act is suspected to be linked to ongoing tensions over water diversions from the Colorado River basin to Denver and other Front Range communities.

properties.Title: India responds to deadly attack by threatening to curtal water deliveries to Pakistan
properties.Description: An attack by a Pakistan-based group that killed at least 40 Indian soldiers has worsened tensions between the two countries, prompting Indian leaders to threaten to curtail water deliveries from rivers flowing into Pakistan. Curtailment of water deliveries could undermine the 1960 Indus Water Treaty, which stipulates the sharing of water resources between the two countries.

properties.Title: Indigenous activist killed at home for opposing Proyecto Integral Morelos, which threatened local water resources
properties.Description: Samir Flores Soberanes, an indigenous environmental activist from Mexico, is killed in his home for his opposition to the Proyecto Integral Morelos, a thermoelectric plant and pipeline that threatened local water and land resources. The Moreles state prosecutor claims that the murder has nothing to do with the thermoelectric plant project. The People's Front in Defense of the Land and Water for th

properties.Title: Israel reportedly cuts off water supplies to approximately 1500 Palestinians
properties.Description: Palestine News Network reports a water shut off to 17 Palestinian communities in Hebron, West Bank. The report suggests that the shut off was a purposeful act by Israel to force the movement of local Palestinians out of their homes to make room for more Israeli settlements.

properties.Title: Kenyan school water tank poisoned
properties.Description: A water tank at a primary school in Kirinyaga County, Kenya, is vandalized and poisoned. No one was harmed by the poisoned water.

properties.Title: More than 130 killed in Mali in conflict between hunters and herders over land and water
properties.Description: More than 130 people are killed in the conflict between hunter and herder communities in Mali. In this instance, Dogon hunters are accused of attacking Fulani herders.

properties.Title: Mortar shells are fired severely damaging a water tanker
properties.Description: Pro-Houthi forces reportedly fire mortar shells near the city of Hays in the south of Hodeidah Governorate, western Yemen. Several houses and a water tanker are severely damaged by shrapnel. At least one civilian is injured in the shelling.

properties.Title: Numerous fights, including attacks on water tankers, break out over access to water in Madhya Pradesh, India during severe heat
properties.Description: Water shortages in Madhya Pradesh, India intensify during a heat wave, leading to numerous violent conflicts among Indian civilians. In one instance in Khajrana a water truck driver is beaten. In another instance in Panna, eight people are injured during a fight. The local government responds to the violence by ordering police to escort water trucks.

properties.Title: One death reported in a fight over water in southern India during a severe heat wave
properties.Description: In Tamil Nadu, India, a 33 year old man dies as the result of a fight that broke out over water. India is experiencing a heat wave with temperatures reaching higher than 50 degrees C/120 degrees F.

properties.Title: One Israeli killed and two others injured at a spring in the West Bank
properties.Description: A homemade bomb explodes at a spring, killing an Israeli teenager and injuring her father and brother. Springs in the occupied West Bank area have become hotspots for conflict between Israeli and Palestinian citizens who both visit during the summer while seeking to cool off from the heat.

properties.Title: One man stabs six others in a fight over water shortages in Ranchi, India during severe heat wave
properties.Description: In Ranchi, India, one man stabs six other people due to water shortages. India is experiencing a heat wave with temperatures reaching higher than 50 degrees C/120 degrees F.

properties.Title: Pakistan claims India's unexpected release of water from a dam is an act of war
properties.Description: India releases water from a dam on the River Sutlej, causing moderate flooding downstream in Pakistan. India claims that the release is a "routine exercise" during monsoon season. The release and resultant flooding are seen as an act of war by Pakistan. This incident adds to the already strained relations between the two countries which have been at odds since February 2019 when 40 Indian parliame

properties.Title: Protests over water, electricity, and university fees in the Democratic Republic of Congo turn violent; four deaths reported
properties.Description: Students from the University of Lubumbashi demonstrate over water, electricity, and fees. The demonstrators barricade roads, block access to the university, and burn tires and vehicles. Police forces opened fire on the demonstrators, killing three. A police officer also dies after being hit by a police car.

properties.Title: Shrapnel damages a water pipeline, leaving more than three million people without water
properties.Description: Shrapnel from an exploding shells damages a pipeline in the Siverski Donetsk-Donbass channel near Horlivka, Ukraine. The damages leave 3 million civilians on both sides of the conflict with either unreliable or no water supplies.

properties.Title: The water supply to Tripoli, Libya is cut off during war
properties.Description: The water supply serving Tripoli, Libya's capital city, is cut off by gunmen that force employees managing the system to turn off the flow of water. The war is between Libya's National Army and UN-backed forces and has been ongoing since 2014.

properties.Title: Turkey forces destroy Syrian power lines, leaving a city without water
properties.Description: The Syrian city Al Hasakeh is left without water after Turkish military forces destroy power lines in the area. The attack also leaves at least 11 civilians dead and causes damage to other infrastructure.
properties.Sources: Save the Children 2019; British Broadcasting Corporation 2019

properties.Title: Two men are seriously injured in clash over access to water in India
properties.Description: Two men are seriously injured in a clash over water in Krishna Nagar Colony, India.

properties.Title: Two people shot while protesting water and electricity shortages in Caracas
properties.Description: Two people are shot by police and paramilitary groups responding to the protests of citizens who have been without electricity or water for several weeks as a result of ongoing political fighting at that national level in Venezuela.

properties.Title: Violence erupts in rural Ghana between Chokosis and Bimobas communities as groups fight over land and access to water resources
properties.Description: In Chereponi district, Ghana, houses are burned to the ground, animals are killed, and many flee as violence erupts along ethnic lines between Chokosis and Bimobas peoples who are fighting over land and water resources.

properties.Title: Water pipeline for Amman Jordan sabotaged
properties.Description: The Disi Water Conveyance Project, a pipeline sending over half of the water supply to Amman, Jordan, and the surrounding area, is sabotaged over 60 times in 2019, at times disrupting supply for five days. Rural communities that are experiencing extremely high rates of unemployment are thought to be behind the sabotage.

properties.Title: Water resources engineers from the Iraq Ministry of Water Resources are attacked while opening waterways to flood a marsh
properties.Description: An team of engineers working on flood prevention from the Ministry of Water Resources in Missan Province, Iraq is attacked by an unidentified armed group while opening waterways to turn a flood into the areas of the central marsh. One death and one injury are reported.

properties.Title: Water shortages linked to child kidnappings in South Sudan
properties.Description: Local authorities cite water shortages as a growing influence on the number child kidnappings in the Boma region of South Sudan. When there is a water shortage, shepherding communities spend more time on the move seeking water for their livestock, but, as a side business, they will also abduct children who can then be resold for profit.

properties.Title: Water station serving 80,000 people damaged in Syria
properties.Description: A water station in Basida, Idlib Governorate, Syria, serving 80,000 people is damaged by ongoing violence.
properties.Sources: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (UNOCHA) 2019

properties.Title: Violence in northwest Syria damages civilian infrastructure including 29 individual water systems
properties.Description: From January through July 2019 violence in northwest Syria leaves civilian infrastructure damaged and destroyed including at least 29 water stations serving approximately 610,000 people.

properties.Title: 9 water workers killed, 26 injured, in ongoing conflict in eastern Ukraine
properties.Description: Nine water workers are killed and 26 are injured since the start of 2019 through July in the ongoing conflict in the eastern region of the Ukraine. Additionally 58 sanitation facilities are damaged or destroyed.

properties.Title: A water well is shelled causing material damages in Yemen
properties.Description: On January 5th, pro-Houthi forces shell a mosque and a water well in the Al Jabaliyah area of At Tuhayat district, Yemen, causing material damages. No injuries nor fatalities are reported.

properties.Title: Armed clash erupts over water and grazing land near Afmadow, Somalia
properties.Description: On January 16 an armed clash erupts between Bartire and Mohamed Zuber clan militias over water and grazing land near Afmadow town. There are several casualties on both sides during the clashes.

properties.Title: Attempt to plant an explosive device in a water channel in Yemen results in one death
properties.Description: On January 11, assailants attempt to plant an explosive device in the Qamt water channel and open fire on security forces in Taizz, Yemen. One assailant is killed. No group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201901110035

properties.Title: Fatalities and injuries are reported in a clan fight over access to water wells and land
properties.Description: On January 15, clan militias in Somalia fight over water wells and land in the area. There are reports of fatalities and injuries.

properties.Title: Five people are injured in a clash over sharing irrigation water in India
properties.Description: On February 22, five persons are injured in a clash between two groups of farmers over sharing irrigation water at Singhi village near Bolpur (Birbhum, West Bengal), India. They use bamboo sticks and crude bombs in the clash.

properties.Title: One person is killed during a protest over the lack of water in KwaZulu Natal, South Africa
properties.Description: On February 27, protesters blocked a road in Umzimkhulu, KwaZulu Natal, South Africa protesting the lack of water. Two protesters are shot and one killed.

properties.Title: The Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist (CPN-Maoist-Chand) detonate three explosive devices at the Arun III Dam’s powerhouse, Nepal
properties.Description: The Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist (CPN-Maoist-Chand) detonate three explosive-laden pressure cookers at the Arun III Dam’s powerhouse, Nepal, damaging a generator.

properties.Title: Suspected New People’s Army fighters set fire to construction equipment at the Kaliwa Dam construction site, Philippines
properties.Description: On February 8, assailants set construction equipment at a dam construction site on fire in Maysaysay, Quezon, Philippines. No group claims responsibility; however, sources attribute the attack to the New People's Army (NPA).
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201902080001; U.S. Department of Home

properties.Title: A dispute over sewage water discharge leads to one death in India
properties.Description: On March 18, in Kalakkad (Tiruppur, Tamil Nadu), India, a dispute over sewage water discharge leads to one death.

properties.Title: A dispute over sewage water discharge leads to two deaths and four injuries in India
properties.Description: On March 30, two groups clash over the drainage of dirty water in Lisana village (Rewari, Haryana), India. Two persons are killed and four others injured in the clash.

properties.Title: Air raid hits water truck and causes four civilian fatalities in Yemen
properties.Description: On March 4, a water truck is hit in an air raid in Al-Sawadiyah District, Bayda, Yemen. Four civilians are also killed in the raid.

properties.Title: Assailants attack schools, houses, trucks transporting drinking water and other infrastructure in Yemen
properties.Description: In early March, assailants use guns to attack schools, houses, trucks transporting drinking water, and other infrastructure in Al-Abaysa, Hajjah, Yemen. No group claims responsibility for the incident; however, sources attribute the attack to Houthi extremists.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201903080004, 201903090040

properties.Title: Injuries are reported during demonstrations against the lack of water and electricity in Caracas, Venezuela
properties.Description: On March 31, a protest is held in San Jose de Cotiza, in Caracas, Venezuela to protest blackouts and the interruption of water services. The protest is met by officials of the Faes, the National Guard, and armed militias of the ruling party. Two people are reportedly wounded by bullets.

properties.Title: Injuries are reported during demonstrations against the lack of water and electricity in Maracay, Venezuela
properties.Description: On March 31, residents of Maracay, Aragua state, Venezuela protest blackouts and the lack of water. Five are wounded amid demonstrations.

properties.Title: One farmer is killed and three others injured in armed clash over access to water in Bangladesh
properties.Description: On March 16, a farmer is stabbed and killed and three others injured in an armed clash between two groups of villagers at Dhaki village, Mithamain, Bangladesh over a water body dispute.

properties.Title: Protestors trying to stop the diversion of floodwaters in Iran clash with Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps; one protestor dies
properties.Description: On March 4, clashes break out between Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) forces and protestors trying to stop the diversion of floodwaters in Dashte Azadegan, Iran. One protestor reportedly dies after being shot by IRGC forces.

properties.Title: Two herders are killed at water points in Kenya
properties.Description: On March 24th, two herders are killed at water points at the border of Isiolo and Garissa counties, Kenya.

properties.Title: Workers clash over a water connection in Pakistan, injuring one
properties.Description: On March 3, workers of PPP and PTI clash in Liaquatabad town, Karachi, Pakistan over water connections. At least one individual is reported injured.

properties.Title: Man threatened and killed for working land claimed to be owned by a hydropower company in Honduras
properties.Description: A man is threatened and killed in March 2019 for working on the land that was allegedly owned by DESA, the hydropower company in Honduras responsible for the Agua Zarca Dam project. The man, Olban Adonay Gómez García, was the son of a leader of the community resistance to the Agua Zarca hydroelectric project that is associated with the murder of Berta Caceras and several of ther local activists.
properties.Sources: Global Witness 2024; Business & Human Rights Resource Centre n.d.-b

properties.Title: National leader Dilma Ferreira Silva, working for policy reform to support people affected by dams, is murdered in Brazil
properties.Description: The leader of an effort to establish national policy in Brazil that would guarantee the rights of people affected by dams, Dilma Ferreira Silva, was murdered, along with her husband and their friend, in March 2019. Sra. Silva had been personally displaced by a dam in her childhood and had spent her life advocating for rights for her family and the river.

properties.Title: A former employee takes control of a Kansas-based water company's computers to remotely to alter the treatment of drinking water
properties.Description: In March 2019, the public water utility in Ellsworth County, Kansas, is the target of malicious action by a former employee who takes control of the company’s systems remotely to alter the treatment of drinking water intended for the population.

properties.Title: Shelling and airstrikes destroy water supply systems in Tripoli during ongoing war in Libya
properties.Description: Heavy shelling and airstrikes between April and June of 2019 destroy water infrastructure in Tripoli, Libya.
properties.Sources: International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) 2019

properties.Title: Violence over access to local water leads to gunfire and injuries in India
properties.Description: On April 17, two rival groups, including a local official, clash at Kotla Doom village (Amritsar, Punjab) in a dispute over a village pond water/land. Both groups accused the other of gunfire, with two persons injured.

properties.Title: At least 12 people are injured in a clash over rainwater drainage pipes in Haryana, India
properties.Description: On April 19, two groups of residents clash over the laying of rainwater drainage pipes in Mollewali village in Pinjore sub-division (Panchkula, Haryana), India. At least 12 injuries are reported.

properties.Title: Five are hurt when a car hits them during a demonstration over lack of drinking water in Miranda state, Venezuela
properties.Description: On April 10, five are hurt when a car tries to run them down during a demonstration demanding water in San Diego de los Altos, Los Teques, Miranda state, Venezuela.

properties.Title: In Chittoor, India, five people are injured in a dispute over a panchayat (village council) water connection
properties.Description: On April 27, in Gudupalle Mandal area (Chittoor, Andhra Pradesh), two groups clash over a panchayat water connection at Alugumanupalle. Five persons are injured.

properties.Title: One death and eight injuries reported in a dispute over grazing and water access in South Sudan
properties.Description: On April 15th, clashes break out between pastoralists in the area of Manya , South Sudan in an apparent dispute over grazing and water access in the area. One person dies and eight are injured in the violence.

properties.Title: One person is killed in a clash over the distribution of irrigation water in India
properties.Description: On April 8, a youth was killed during a clash between groups from two villages in Shopian district (Jammu and Kashmir), India over the distribution of irrigation water.

properties.Title: One reported killed during protests over the lack of water and power services in Zulia state, Venezuela
properties.Description: On April 5, in Santa Rita, Zulia state, Venezuela a man is shot dead by police and military officers during a protest demanding basic services such as electric power and potable water.

properties.Title: A water system is destroyed in an attack in the Philippines
properties.Description: On May 4, Cambalidio, Camarines Sur, Philippines is attacked; while no casualties are reported, a water system is destroyed in the incident. No group claims responsibility; however, sources attribute the attack to the New People's Army (NPA).
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201905040041

properties.Title: A woman is killed in a clash over the sharing of drinking water in Kurnool, India
properties.Description: On May 10, in Kurnool district (Andhra Pradesh), India a woman is killed during a clash between two families over sharing of drinking water.

properties.Title: Conflict over local drinking water leads to two injuries in Nepal
properties.Description: On May 19, a group of people beat up a couple in Khamari locality of Siranchowk Rural Municipality near Gorkha, Nepal for blocking the registration of the drinking water source of the village.

properties.Title: During one of four attacks in the area, water tunnels and bridges are blown up in Yemen
properties.Description: On May 29, water tunnels and bridges are blown up in one of four attacks in Ad Dali, Yemen. No group claims responsibility for the incident; however, sources attribute the attack to Houthi extremists.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201905290035

properties.Title: On two days in May, assailants set fire to water tankers and  other vehicles at construction sites in India
properties.Description: On May 8 and again on May 12, assailants set fire to water tankers and other construction vehicles and a building in Maharashtra, India. Maoists are suspected to be responsible for the attacks.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201905080014, 201905120043

properties.Title: Six Dalits are injured in a clash over access to drinking water in Mainpuri, India
properties.Description: On May 28, six Dalits, a marginalized socioeconomic group, were injured in an attack in Mainpuri (Uttar Pradesh), India over drinking water from a tube well.

properties.Title: Two injuries are reported during protests over the lack of access to water in Pietermaritzburg, South Africa
properties.Description: On May 20, residents from Henley Village, Pietermaritzburg, South Africa demonstrate over lack of access to water. Police use rubber bullets and teargas to disperse the crowds. Two demonstrators are injured and one is hospitalized.

properties.Title: Suspected Boko Haram fighters assault the Alou Dam, Nigeria killing 20
properties.Description: Suspected Boko Haram fighters attack the Alou Dam in Nigeria, killing at least 20 civilians.

properties.Title: Seven people injured during dispute over water supply in Rajasthan, India
properties.Description: On June 6, residents of two villages near Kishangarh Bas (Alwar district, Rajasthan), India clash with each other following a dispute over water supply in the neighborhood. Seven people were injured in the fight.

properties.Title: Several people are injured in a fight over a water tanker in Delhi, India
properties.Description: On June 10, two groups of residents clash over a water tanker in Delhi-Mehrauli (South Delhi, National Capital Territory of Delhi) injuring several people. The state has been facing water shortages.

properties.Title: Six injuries are reported in a dispute over water in Mundera, Madhya Pradesh, India
properties.Description: On June 8, locals residents attack each other following a dispute over water at Mundera village of Lavkush Nagar Tehsil (Chhatarpur district, Madhya Pradesh). Six people are injured in the clash.

properties.Title: Two are injured in a fight over irrigation water scheduling in Pakistan
properties.Description: On June 22, unidentified men attack and injure two people over irrigation water rotation scheduling, in Hamo Pota village, Pakistan.

properties.Title: Two people accused on stealing water pumps are beaten in Guatemala
properties.Description: On June 27, in Zona 8, Mixco Town, Guatemala, two people who allegedly stole water pumps are beaten by a group of local inhabitants.

properties.Title: Two people are injured in a water dispute in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India
properties.Description: In early June, a clash over water occurs between two groups in Indore (Madhya Pradesh), India. Two people are injured in the encounter.

properties.Title: A dispute over the use of water resources turns violent in Kyrgyzstan
properties.Description: On June 9, about 30 residents of Ak-Suu, Kyrgyzstan reportedly attack a couple because of conflicts over water.

properties.Title: A projectile is launched at a desalination plant in Saudi Arabia
properties.Description: On June 19, a projectile is launched at a desalination plant in Shuqaiq, Jazan, Saudi Arabia. Houthi extremists claim responsibility for the incident.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201906190010

properties.Title: A water dispute in Pakistan results in one death and other injuries
properties.Description: On June 26, two tribal sub-clans attack each other with weapons and knives over a water dispute in Jangal Pir Alizai, Balochistan, Pakistan resulting in one fatality and injuries to two others.

properties.Title: A water tank is targeted in an attack on the Somali National Army in Somalia
properties.Description: On June 9, a water tank is targeted in an attack on the Somali National Army by Al-Shabaab, near Wanlaweyne, Lower Shebelle, Somalia.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201906090020

properties.Title: Eight people are injured in a fight over water in Pawai, Madhya Pradesh, India
properties.Description: On June 5, residents of Pawai village (Panna district, Madhya Pradesh), India attack each other following a dispute over water. Eight people were injured.

properties.Title: In an attack on a convoy escorting food and water supplies, at least three are killed and one injured in Burkina Faso
properties.Description: On June 24, in an attack on a convoy escorting food and water supplies in Burkina Faso, at least three people are killed and one is injured. No one claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201906240011

properties.Title: One person is severely injured in a water dispute in Gwalior, Madhya Pradesh, India
properties.Description: Around June 8, a youth is attacked with baseball bats by unidentified people in Gwalior (Madhya Pradesh) over a water dispute. The victim sustains severe injuries.

properties.Title: The Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist detonate an explosive device at the Upper Karnali Hydropower Project dam in Nepal
properties.Description: he Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist (CPN-Maoist-Chand) detonate an explosive device at the Upper Karnali Hydropower Project dam construction site in Nepal.

properties.Title: An unidentified group sets fire to machinery and leaves an explosive device at a hydropower project in Nepal
properties.Description: An unidentified group of attackers set fire to machinery and leave an explosive device at the Solukhola Dudhkoshi Hydropower Project in Bhadaure, Nepal.

properties.Title: Ten are injured in a dispute over irrigation water in Pakistan
properties.Description: On July 6, two groups of Janwri community attack each other with sticks and metal rods over the rotation of water in Gharo village, Pakistan. Ten men are injured during the incident.

properties.Title: Three people injured in a dispute over access to drinking water in Kyrgyzstan
properties.Description: In July, violence over access to drinking water is reported in the Iskrinsk district, Chui region of Kyrgyzstan. Several people are reported hospitalized.

properties.Title: Two are killed and one injured when an explosive device detonates targeting police transporting water in Afghanistan
properties.Description: On July 13, a explosive device targeting a police truck transporting water detonates, killing two and injuring one in Miya Nishin district, Kandahar, Afghanistan. No group claim responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201907130028

properties.Title: Violence between Hindu and Muslim farmers over irrigation water in Uttar Pradesh, India leads to one injury and two arrests
properties.Description: Around July 15, Hindus and Muslims clash in Semra village (Agra district, Uttar Pradesh), India over a farm field water channel. Two people are arrested and one injured.

properties.Title: A dispute related to access to clean water in Haiti leads to multiple deaths and arrests
properties.Description: On July 1, shots are fired at protestors demanding clean drinking water, killing two near Petite-Riviere de Nippes and Anse-a-Veau, Haiti. Unidentified people fire back, killing four more. Some of the initial attackers are arrested by police.

properties.Title: A gunfight over the sharing of irrigation water in the Mangal area of Pakistan kills six and injures three others
properties.Description: On July 7, members of two Pashtun tribes Pakha Khel and Maduzai exchange gunfire over a 40-year-old dispute regarding sharing of irrigation water in Mangal area of Pakistan. Six people are killed and three others sustain injuries.

properties.Title: Clash over water shortage in Clifton, Karachi injures 7, including 2 police officers; 40+ demonstrators arrested
properties.Description: On July 28, at least five people are injured in a clash between local activists, government representatives, and local political parties in Clifton town, Karachi, Pakistan over shortage of water and failure to maintain city's sewerage. Police intervene to disperse the demonstrators and arrest more than 40 demonstrators. Two police officers are also injured in the incident.

properties.Title: Airstrike hits water tank in Saada, Yemen
properties.Description: On July 9, an airstrike by Coalition forces hits a water tank in Razih District, Saada, Yemen.

properties.Title: An irrigation water dispute kills one in Pakistan
properties.Description: On July 23, the family of members of a Junejo community individual who was killed in a irrigation water dispute burn tires, block Sukkur-Larkana highway, and hold a sit-in along with the victim's body in Shikarpur district, Pakistan.

properties.Title: Demonstrators in Tunisia wreck water pipes to a neighboring town, protesting water service interruptions
properties.Description: Around July 3 in Jelma, Tunisia, demonstrators wreck water pipes delivering water to neighboring towns, demonstrating against water service interruptions.

properties.Title: Explosive device detonates near a water facility in Iraq, killing one civilian
properties.Description: On July 9, 2019, an explosive device detonates near a water facility in Shirqat, Saladin, Iraq, killing one civilian. No group claims responsibility for the incident.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201907090008

properties.Title: Farmers in the Punjab, India fight over irrigation water. One injury is reported
properties.Description: On July 21, farmers fight in Lubanianwali village (Muktsar, Punjab) after a farmer stops rainwater flow from a pipe. One person is injured.

properties.Title: Farmers stop the main water pumping station in El Hamma, Tunisia, disrupting local drinking water supply
properties.Description: On July 22, farmers hold a demonstration and stop the main water pumping station in El Hamma, causing disruptions of drinking water in the area.

properties.Title: One death and several injuries are reported during a protest over lack of water and other services in Sao Paulo, Brazil
properties.Description: On July 18 in Valinhos, Sao Paulo, Brazil approximately 400 people protest the lack of water and inadequate school and health assistance in the Marielle Vive Camp. A supporter of Jair Bolsonaro drives a truck through the crowd, killing an elderly man and injuring five people, including a journalist.

properties.Title: One death is reported in dispute over water at a public tap in Andhra Pradesh, India
properties.Description: On July 15, a woman is killed during a clash over water between two groups at a public tap in the Srikakulam district (Andhra Pradesh), India.

properties.Title: One injury is reported in a fight over drainage water in Devsar town, India
properties.Description: On July 5, two families fight over a water drainage issue in Devsar town (Kulgam, Jammu and Kashmir), India. One injury is reported.

properties.Title: A dispute over water rotation in Pakistan leads to one death
properties.Description: On August 29, two groups of the Marri community attack each other with axes over a water rotation dispute in Dilshad village near Golarchi town, Pakistan, resulting in one fatality.

properties.Title: A protest over delays to repair of water and sanitation infrastructure in Pakistan leads to injuries
properties.Description: On August 16, locals reportedly attack and injure four Water and Sanitation Authority staff at Gulberg Colony in Multan city, Punjab, Pakistan over delays in repair work.

properties.Title: A water tank and a vehicle are damaged in an attack on a House Assembly member in Zimbabwe
properties.Description: On August 21, House Assembly member Caston Matewu is fired upon, in Nyameni, Mashonaland East, Zimbabwe; a water tank and a vehicle are damaged but no casualties are reported. No group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201908210017

properties.Title: Afghan police attack Taliban militants trying to destroy a water well; seven deaths are reported
properties.Description: As reported on August 9, Afghan police forces attack Taliban militants trying to destroy a water well in Almar district, Faryab. Seven militants are killed and nine are wounded.

properties.Title: At least 50 people are injured in Bangladesh in a dispute over a local water body
properties.Description: On August 6, at least 50 people are injured in a clash between two groups of villagers at Modhurapur village, Bangladesh over a local water body. Police fire tear gas and rubber bullets to disperse the crowd.

properties.Title: 600 residents in Honduras destroy mining equipment, clash with police over threats to water and forest; injuries reported
properties.Description: On August 16 in Yorito, Yoro, Honduras around 600 inhabitants destroy heavy machinery of the mining company Comercializadora de Agregados de Honduras S.A. (CEASA). During the conflict with police, multiple injuries are reported. The demonstrators oppose the operation of the mining company, which they consider threatens water sources and the forest. The territory is inhabited by Tolupan indigenous

properties.Title: One death is reported in an armed clash over control of water in Bangladesh
properties.Description: On August 21, an eight-year old boy is killed in an armed clash between two groups over the control of water body in Kulaura Upazila, Maulvibazar, Bangladesh.

properties.Title: Two are injured in a dispute over access to water in Uttar Pradesh, India
properties.Description: On August 7, two persons are injured in a clash between two families in Bhadri village (Pratapgarh, Uttar Pradesh), India over taking water from a tap.

properties.Title: Two deaths and multiple injuries reported in violence over irrigation water in two villages of India
properties.Description: On August 4, two villages clash over sharing irrigation water for their crops in the villages of Sagar and Panchagochhia, India. Two people are killed and 15 injured.

properties.Title: Two groups exchange gunfire, killing two, over irrigation water in Pakistan
properties.Description: On September 22, two groups exchange fire over a dispute about irrigation water use in Maidan Khwar area of Upper Dir district (KP), Pakistan resulting in two fatalities.

properties.Title: Vehicles and machines at a water pipeline construction site are burned in India
properties.Description: On September 28, vehicles and machines at a water pipeline construction site are burned in Masenar, Chhattisgarh, India. Maoists are suspected to be responsible for the attacks.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201909280055

properties.Title: Indigenous women murdered for their resistence to hydropower projects and land grabs in Omoa, Honduras
properties.Description: In September and October 2019 at least six women from the indigenous Garifuna communities in coastal Honduras are murdered for their resistance to government-backed land decisions and hydropower projects that affected their territory. One of the six, Mirna Teresa Suazo Martinez, was the president of the board of trustees in the Masca community of Omoa.

properties.Title: A clash over access to a drinking water fountain leads to injuries and a demonstration in Pakistan
properties.Description: On September 10, police and Rangers resort to using batons and tear gas after supporters of three men who were injured during a clash over use of a water fountain blocked the road and demonstrated in Darya Khan Mari village, Sindh, Pakistan.

properties.Title: A clash over water sharing leads to at least eight injuries in Pakistan
properties.Description: As reported on September 19, two groups clash after dispute about water sharing in the Multan district (Punjab), Pakistan resulting in injuries to at least eight persons.

properties.Title: A dispute over water drainage in Pakistan results in seven injured
properties.Description: On September 9, two groups from Tamma village attack each other with batons, sticks, and stones over a dispute about water drainage in their village near Islamabad city, Pakistan. Seven people are injured when one of the groups rammed their SUV against the other group's car.

properties.Title: A fight between farmers and pastoralists at a water source in Kenya results in serious injuries
properties.Description: On September 20th, in Mkocheni village, Kenya, a fight between farmers and herders over access to a water source results in serious injuries.

properties.Title: A man is beaten to death for allegedly stealing a water pump in Rajasthan, India
properties.Description: On September 21, a Dalit man is beaten to death by locals for allegedly stealing a water pump at Ghatoli village (Jhalawar, Rajasthan), India.

properties.Title: A water tank and windows are damaged during an attack on a polling center in Afghanistan
properties.Description: On September 28, a water tank and several windows are damaged during an attack on a polling center in a female high school in Fayzabad, Jawzjan, Afghanistan. The Taliban claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201909280054

properties.Title: A demonstration over water privatization and indigenous rights in Chile leads to a violent confrontation with police
properties.Description: On October 12, in Santiago, Chile a Mapuche demonstration over the defense of indigenous territories and the privatization of water leads to a violent confrontation with police and the destruction of public facilities.

properties.Title: A fight over restoration of a drinking water tap in AP, India injures eight
properties.Description: On October 28, Telugu Desam Party (TDP) and Yuvajana Shramika Rythu Congress Party activists clash over the restoration of a drinking water tap in Santakaviti village (Srikakulam, Andhra Pradesh), India, injuring eight.

properties.Title: Al Shabaab militants flood a highway and ambush Somali soldiers who arrive to cut off the water
properties.Description: On October 11, Al Shabaab militants ambush Somali military soldiers after they arrive to close the water gate of Hawaadley water storage opened by the militants to flood the highway between Bal'ad and Jowhar.

properties.Title: Farmers protesting the diversion of the Zayandehrud River damage diversion water pipes
properties.Description: In October, a group of farmers in Isfahan, Iran demonstrate against the diversion of Zayandehrud River to other provinces; some demonstrators damage the water pipes of the diversion infrastructure.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers reportedly flood Palestinian-owned olive groves with sewage
properties.Description: On October 27, Israeli settlers flood Palestinian-owned olive groves in the village of Deri al Hatab east of Nablus, Palestine with sewage water.

properties.Title: Local residents in Mat, Albania clash with police over the construction of a hydroelectric plant
properties.Description: On October 26, residents of Zall Gjocaj village in Mat, Albania clash with police while demonstrating against the construction of a hydroelectric power station. The residents are worried about local water supply.

properties.Title: One man is killed and his son injured in a dispute over drawing water in Maharastra, India
properties.Description: On October 6, two men attack a Muslim father and son over drawing water from a nearby tap in Ghatkopar (Mumbai Suburban, Maharashtra), India. The father dies of his injuries at a hospital.

properties.Title: Six people are injured and one killed in a dispute over irrigation water in Uttar Pradesh, India
properties.Description: On October 15, two groups of villagers from Kataura and Pradhan Paksha, Uttar Pradesh, India clash over irrigation water. Six persons are injured. The dispute continues in late October and a death is reported.

properties.Title: Six police officers are injured during demonstrations demanding the restoration of water services in South Africa
properties.Description: Around October 16, 2019 residents from Tshikota, South Africa demonstrate, demanding the restoration of water service in their area. Six police officers are injured and 12 demonstrators are arrested.

properties.Title: Turkey takes control of Syrian water system in Allouk and interrupts water supply
properties.Description: Turkey and Turkish-backed forces take control of the Allouk water station in northeastern Syria in October 2019. The water station, near the town of Ras al-Ain (Serekaniye), serves 460,000 people in al-Hasakeh governorate. Water pumping has been interrupted several times since the start of the year, with the latest interruption on March 29, 2020. Officials say the interruptions are a tactic to for

properties.Title: Suspicious circumstances surround the death of a legal advocate who opposed a dam in Indonesia
properties.Description: In October 2019, Golfrid Siregar is found dead. Environmental activists argue that Siregar had been targeted for his work as a legal advocate and that suspicious circumstances of his death indicate that he was in fact murdered. Siregar's most recent work included the filing of a lawsuit accussing the North Sumatra government of forging a signature to allow for the permitting of a dam in the region

properties.Title: The destruction of a water plant leads to clashes in Yemen, killing and injuring many
properties.Description: On November 26, clashes are reported between 'Abidah Al-Qaz'ah tribesmen and Islah-affiliated pro-Hadi forces after tribesmen established a roadblock to demand compensation for a water factory that was damaged in an airstrike and killed the son of the factory's owner. On November 28, reports alleged that 20 people were killed, most of them civilians, and 85 others injured in the clashes that laste

properties.Title: Four are injured in a fight over water supply in Kurnool, AP, India
properties.Description: On November 16, four persons are injured in a clash between villagers and local leaders belonging to the Yuvajana Shramika Rythu Congress Party over drinking water at Kalvabugga (Kurnool, Andhra Pradesh), India.

properties.Title: Pastoralists in Morocco fight with local residents and destroy water supply infrastructure
properties.Description: On November 11, in Toumliline, Morocco, pastoralist nomads attack residents of the area with rocks and sticks, resulting in the destruction of water supply infrastructure.

properties.Title: A water tanker is damaged in an attack on a convoy in Somalia
properties.Description: On December 10, an explosive device detonates targeting a convoy in Isbartiba, Mogadishu, Somalia, causing an unknown number of casualties and also damaging a water tanker. No group claims responsibility for the attack.
properties.Sources: National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism (START) 2019 ID. 201912100031

properties.Title: A fight in Kenya between two clans over a watering point leaves one dead, 18 injured
properties.Description: A fight erupts between two clans in the town of Arbeqarmso, Kenya, over a watering point. The incident leaves one person dead and 18 others injured.

properties.Title: An attack on a water pumping station in Libya cuts of the water supply to 2 million people in the capital of Tripoli
properties.Description: An armed group attacked a control station at Shwerif, Libya, cutting off the water supply to more than 2 million people in the capital Tripoli. The UN condemns the attack as "reprehensible."

properties.Title: Attacks on water and sanitation systems in the Donbas region of the Ukraine cut off water services for civilians
properties.Description: A resumption of aerial attacks between the Ukraine and Russia in the Donbas region again target water and sanitation systems, leaving civilians without running water in the midst of the Covid-19 pandemic.

properties.Title: Clashes over shared water point kills at least 10 in northern Uganda
properties.Description: Clashes between South Sudanese refugees and local community members in Tika village near the Rhino refugee settlement in northern Uganda over access to a shared water source leave at least 10 dead, 19 injured, and several missing.

properties.Title: Fighting in Libya continues to include attacks on water supply infrastructure
properties.Description: Fighting between the Libyan National Army (LNA) and the internationally recognized Government of National Accord (GNA) leads to cuts in water and power to Tripoli when gunmen storm a control room, cutting water flows from the Great Manmade River Project.

properties.Title: Israel detects and thwarts a cyberattack on water systems
properties.Description: Israeli authorities link Iran to an attempted cyberattack aimed at disrupting water supplies in at least two locations in Israel in late April 2020.

properties.Title: Mexican military forces and farmers clash over water releases from a dam
properties.Description: Farmers protest at the Las Virgines dam in northern Mexico where water is being held to repay a water debt to the U.S. Several hundred overtake control of the dam, causing property damage estimated at $4.6 million.  The farmers say that without the water, they will no longer be able to farm.

properties.Title: Multiple cyber-attacks on Israel water infrastructure
properties.Description: Several cyberattacks on Israeli water infrastructure are reported by the Israeli Water Authority. The attacks are aimed at agricultural water pumps in the Upper Galilee and on water and wastewater treatment systems in the central part of the country. No mention is given of who carried out the attacks, but previous similar attacks were attributed to Iranian hackers.

properties.Title: Protestors injured in Tripoli, Libya, while protesting water shortages and other deplorable living conditions
properties.Description: Several hundred people hold a protest of deplorable living conditions, including from water shortages, in Tripoli, Libya. Some protestors are injured from shots by security forces working to disperse the demonstrations.

properties.Title: Several killed in an attack on a dam in Afghanistan
properties.Description: Several military personnel are killed and others wounded during an attack on a dam, Machalugu village, Paktia province, Afghanistan.
properties.Sources: Pettersson et al. 2021; Sundberg and Melander 2013

properties.Title: Two killed during widespread protests at La Boquilla dam that diverts water to the United States from Mexico
properties.Description: Two are killed a gunfight with Mexico’s military police near several days of protest in La Boquilla, Chihuahua, at a dam that diverts water to the United States. Mexicans in the northern state of Chihuahua are angry at water being diverted across the border hurl Molotov cocktails and rocks at security troops, and occupy the La Boquilla dam, closing the sluice gates.

properties.Title: White supremacists in the United States plot to poison water supplies
properties.Description: In secret surveillance, the US Federal Bureau of Investigation records neo-Nazis recruiting members and plotting terrorist acts including poisoning water supplies in an effort to ignite violence. Seven arrests were made.

properties.Title: Six deaths reported in clashes over the use of water wells in Chad
properties.Description: In January, members of the Arab and Boulala communities clash in the area of Djokhana (Dababa, Hadjer-Lamis), Chad over the usage of water wells. Six people were reportedly killed.

properties.Title: Three injuries and two deaths are reported in a dispute over drainage water in Madhya Pradesh, India
properties.Description: On January 12, two unidentified groups fire at each other in Gwalior (Gwalior, Madhya Pradesh) India over the flow of drainage water, leading to two deaths and three injuries.

properties.Title: Clashes in Lebanon over water, electricity and energy shortages lead to injuries and arrests
properties.Description: On January 13, demonstrators in Sidon city, Lebanon protest the crises in the country, including lack of water, electricity, gasoline, diesel, and gas. Clashes between rioters and military forces are reported, leaving five people injured and several arrested.

properties.Title: Three Afar militiamen shot by Somali forces in Garbo Ciise, Ethiopia amid ongoing clashes over water and grazing rights
properties.Description: In late January, three Afar communal militiamen are shot and wounded by Somali ethnic forces in Garbo Ciise (Adaytu), Ethiopia. Clashes over grazing areas, water, and other resources are common in the area.

properties.Title: Protests against the water crisis in Pernik, Bulgaria turn violent, injuring at least 32 people
properties.Description: On January 27, at least 300 residents in Sofia protest the water crisis in Pernik and clash with police. Police use tear gas and at least 32 people, including two police officers, are injured.

properties.Title: Farmers break into La Boquilla dam in Mexico in opposition to water releases to the United States
properties.Description: The National Guard and the army are deployed to La Boquilla Dam in October after protesters occupy it in protest of proposed water releases to the United States.

properties.Title: Suspected Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist fighters detonate an explosive device at the Arun III Hydropower Dam, Nepal
properties.Description: Suspected Communist Party of Nepal–Maoist (CPN-Maoist-Chand) fighters detonate an explosive device at the Arun III Hydropower Dam construction site, Nepal, damaging a generator.

properties.Title: Seventeen people reported injured in a dispute over irrigation water scheduling in Pakistan
properties.Description: On February 6, a clash erupts between two groups of the Gaho community, Kumb town, Pakistan, over water rotation. Both groups attack each other with sticks and axes injuring 17 people.

properties.Title: Two farmers kill their neighbor in a dispute over sharing water from a well in Madhya Pradesh, India
properties.Description: On February 26, two farmers beat to death their neighbor in Majhgawan village (Chhatarpur, Madhya Pradesh), India during a dispute on sharing water from a well.

properties.Title: One death and several injuries are reported during a clash between farmers and pastoralists in South Sudan
properties.Description: On February 22, farmers from Kuajiena county, South Sudan attack armed pastoralists from Tonj at a water point, leading to a firefight which kills one and wounds between three and five people.

properties.Title: Protests in Algeria for improved water supply and energy leads to clashes with police and injuries
properties.Description: On February 26, 2020, police forces and rioters clashed in Draa El Caid, Algeria over gas and water services, resulting in the injury of two police officers and several dozen rioters.

properties.Title: Afghan security forces transferring water targeted by assailants
properties.Description: In Kandahar, Afghanistan Afghan border security forces are attacked by assailants while transferring water. No casualties are reported.

properties.Title: Two deaths are reported when Syrian militias fight with smugglers during a raid on water tankers
properties.Description: On March 29, QSD forces clash with smugglers during a raid on water tankers in the town of Thiban in Deir-ez-Zor, Syria. Clashes result in at least two deaths and additional wounded.

properties.Title: Afar and Somali forces clash over water and pasture land, killing at least 11
properties.Description: Around March 12, at least 11 people are killed during armed confrontations between Oromo and Afar pastoralists in the Bordeda (Mieso) area, Ethiopia over water and pasture.

properties.Title: Four are injured in a land and water dispute in Thanamandi, India
properties.Description: On March 1, four people attack a family in Thanamandi (Rajouri district, Jammu and Kashmir), India over a land and water dispute. Four members of the family are injured.

properties.Title: At least one injured in explosion en route to repair water pipe in Yebyu, Myanmar
properties.Description: At least one person on their way to fix a water pipe is injured when an explosive device is detonated in Yebyu, Myanmar. A related attack occurs in Yebyu on the same day. Sources suspect the Karen National Union is involved.

properties.Title: A dispute over drinking water stalls in Maharashtra, India leads to two deaths and one injury
properties.Description: On April 27, two people die and one is seriously injured during a clash between two Muslim groups in the Shivaji Nagar slums of Govandi town (Mumbai city, Maharashtra), India over drinking stalls.

properties.Title: Two police officers killed and one injured while carrying water from a well in Mirza Ali, Afghanistan
properties.Description: An assailant opens fire on police officers carrying water from a well in Mirza Ali, Afghanistan, killing at least two and injuring another. No group claims responsibility.

properties.Title: The curtailment of water and electricity after cyclone Amhan leads to protests in Budhal, India. One injury is reported
properties.Description: On May 26, a mob attacks police and Trinamool legislators in Kolkata city (Kolkata district, West Bengal), India while calling for restoration of electricity and water supply affected by cyclone Amphan. One injury is reported.

properties.Title: The Sudanese Armed Forces clash with Amhara militants after the Ethiopians try to make use of river water in Sudan
properties.Description: On May 28, Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) clash with Amhara militia in the area of Barakat Norein, Sudan, after the SAF refused to allow the Ethiopians to make use of river water in the area. The area is claimed by Ethiopia. One Ethiopian militiaman is wounded.

properties.Title: Two groups clash over irrigation water in Pakistan, with severe injuries to two people
properties.Description: On May 8, two groups of Solangi community clash over water rotation in Mirpurkhas city, Pakistan. They attack each other with weapons, resulting in severe injuries to two men.

properties.Title: A dispute over water distribution in Budhal city, India leads to three injuries
properties.Description: On May 19, a dispute over water distribution in Budhal city (Rajouri district, Jammu and Kashmir), India leads to three injuries.

properties.Title: A fight over access to water in the Al-Qatri camp for displaced persons in Syria leads to one death
properties.Description: On May 15, a civilian is killed during clashes between displaced people in Al-Qatri camp near Idlib, Syria. Clashes erupted after residents of the camp lost access to water.

properties.Title: A major riot breaks out between Kyrgyz and Uzbek nationals over access to water; more than 200 people are hospitalized
properties.Description: On May 31, a riot breaks out between dozens of Kyrgyz and Uzbek nationals over access to water sources near the village of Chechme on the border with Uzbekistan. Rioters hurl rocks at each other while setting properties on fire on both sides of the border. 200 Uzbek citizens are hospitalized in Sokh enclave including 13 with gunshot wounds; 25 Kyrgyz villagers are taken to the hospital in Kadamjai

properties.Title: A protest in Mexico against the cost of water services turns violent; at least 16 people are injured
properties.Description: On May 14, in San Miguel de Allende, Guanajuato, about 80 people block a street to demonstrate against the cost of water service. About 100 municipal and state police intervene and 16 people are reported injured.

properties.Title: A protest over the lack of water services in Senegal turns violent and demonstrators and police are injured
properties.Description: On May 30, residents demonstrate in Cap Skirring in Oussouye, Ziguinchor, Senegal against the lack of running water. They burn tires on the roads. The gendarmerie forces fire tear gas and live ammunition to disperse them; demonstrators reply with stones. Three demonstrators and three gendarmes are severely injured. 16 people are also arrested.

properties.Title: A water dispute between farmers in Kazakhstan turns into a riot, injuring 12
properties.Description: On May 29, a water dispute between farmers near the town of Zhetisay of Turkistan region of Kazakhstan turns into a riot resulting in a dozen injuries. Police arrest two rioters.

properties.Title: Clashes over water resources in Yemen result in eight deaths and additional injuries
properties.Description: On May 1, clashes between Al Tilan and Al Dahshal Ba al Harith tribal militias over water resources in Al Ghiwayl valley of Usaylan district (Shabwah), result in eight deaths and additional injuries.

properties.Title: Demonstrators in Gheyzaniyeh, Iran are injured while protesting water outages
properties.Description: On May 25, police shoot tear gas and rubber bullets at demonstrators in Gheyzaniyeh, Iran demonstrating against frequent outages of water. Several injuries are reported.

properties.Title: Israeli forces damage drinking water tanks while trying to disperse Palestinian protestors
properties.Description: On May 15, Israeli forces use live fire, rubber bullets, and tear gas to disperse Palestinians protesting settlement policies in Kafr Qaddum. Two drinking water tanks are damaged during the event.

properties.Title: One injured during protests demanding water in Trujillo, Venezuela
properties.Description: On May 11, in Carvajal, Trujillo, Venezuela, residents protest to demand water and are met with force by Special Action Forces (FAES). One person is injured.

properties.Title: A clash over water and land kills one and injures three in Somalia
properties.Description: On June 21, militias from the Leelkayse and Majeerten clans clash in the Deeralay area, Mudug, Somalia over water and land. One person is killed and three others injured in the clash.

properties.Title: A demonstration against the lack of water services in Senegal leads to injuries and arrests
properties.Description: On June 22, 2020, residents demonstrate in Koumpentoum town (Koumpentoum, Tambacounda) against a lack of running water. One gendarme is severely injured and several demonstrators are arrested.

properties.Title: A dozen injuries are reported in violence over access to water for cattle in Chad
properties.Description: Around June 1, youth militias from Kouri Bougoudi and Sakine, Chad clash over water for their cattle at Hadjar Hadid (Ouaddai). A dozen people are reported injured.
