properties.Title: Armed clash between Husen and Reer Garaad militias in Sanaag, Somalia over pasture and water disputes injures six
properties.Description: On June 27, an armed clash is reported between a Husen Sub-Clan militia and Reer Garaad Sub-Clan militia, both from the Warsengeli clan, Sanaag, Somalia. The motive of the clash is believed to be disputes over pasturelands and water resources. At least six people are injured in the clan clash.

properties.Title: Egyptian hackers claim responsibility for a cyberattack on dam operations at the Ethiopian Grand Renaissance Dam
properties.Description: Egyptian hackers launch a cyber attack on Ethiopian water systems in June 2020. On a Facebook page maintained by the hackers, they allude to Egyptian opposition to the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam on the Blue Nile. The attack is carried out as the reservoir behind the dam is being filled despite the lack of an agreement between Egypt and Ethiopia.

properties.Title: Several killed in a fight over a watering point in Marsabit, Kenya
properties.Description: In mid-June, herders clash over a watering point in Marsabit, Kenya. Several fatalities are reported.

properties.Title: Shelling damages Siverskyi Donets-Donbas water pipeline, Ukraine
properties.Description: Shelling reported at the Siverskyi Donets-Donbas water pipeline in contested areas of eastern Ukraine (June 2020). The pipeline delivers water to people in the region from the Siverskyi Donets River.

properties.Title: Suspected New People's Army fighters conduct an armed assault on the Igbaras Hydropower Plant, Philippines
properties.Description: Suspected New People's Army fighters conduct an armed assault on the Igbaras Hydropower Plant, Iloilo, Philippines, with automatic/semi-automatic rifles. An unknown number of attackers are injured.

properties.Title: Explosion outside water station near Naanaah, Iraq, injures at least one
properties.Description: An explosive device is detonated in front of the central water station near Naanaah, Iraq, injuring at least one station employee. A related bombing occurs in this area on the same date. No group claims responsibility.

properties.Title: Gunmen attack water and sewage workers in Cukurca, Turkey
properties.Description: Assailants carrying firearms and rockets attack water and sewage employees in Cukurca, Turkey, injuring two. No group claims responsibility, but sources believe the Kurdistan Workers’ Party to be behind the assault.

properties.Title: Three deaths and four injuries are reported in a gunfight over irrigation water rotation and money in Pakistan
properties.Description: On July 7, a clash takes place between two groups of the Bhangwar community near Kandhkot city, Pakistan over water rotation and money. Both groups fire at each other, killing three men and injuring four others.

properties.Title: Deaths and injuries reported in a clash over the use of a water pump in Burkina Faso
properties.Description: On July 11, members of Lobi and Dogosse communities clash over the use of a water pump for a Lobi customary rite in Tako-Yoyora (Loropeni, Poni), Burkina Faso. Three people are killed, two injured.

properties.Title: Four injuries reported in a dispute over irrigation water rotations in Pakistan
properties.Description: On July 3, a clash takes place between two groups from the Janwri and Dhalo communities over rotation of water in Abdul Hakeem Dhalo village in Radhan, Pakistan. Four men are injured.

properties.Title: Injuries are reported in a demonstration over access to improved water and electricity in Guinea
properties.Description: On July 14, residents demonstrated in Siguiri town (Siguiri, Kankan), Guinea to demand better electricity and water supply. Security forces fire tear gas to disperse them, injuring several people.

properties.Title: Officials inspecting the flow of water in Gulkani, Haryana, India are attacked. Seven injuries and 50 arrests are reported
properties.Description: On July 1, around 50 people attack officials inspecting the flow of water into a pond in Gulkani village, Jind (Haryana), India. Seven policemen and officials are injured and 50 arrested.

properties.Title: Violent protest at Las Virgenes dam, Chihuahua, Mexico over water releases
properties.Description: Demonstrators at the Las Vírgenes Dam in Chihuahua, Mexico burn several government vehicles, block railway tracks and set fire to a government office and highway tollbooths to protest the release of water from local dams to meet treaty obligations with the United States.

properties.Title: A dispute over irrigation water leads to a clash between conflicting political parties in Nepal
properties.Description: On August 28, a clash related to watering paddy fields leads to dozens of injuries between cadres of Nepali Congress and JSPN at Kachanari village in Bariyarpatti, Nepal.

properties.Title: In a caste dispute, over two dozen Dalit are attacked with acid for using a handpump for water in Jhansi, India
properties.Description: On August 25, a mob attacks and injures over two dozen Dalit people with acid in Basaar village in Jhansi (UP), India over using a handpump for water.

properties.Title: One injury reported in a fight in Garhi Khairo, Pakistan over irrigation rotations
properties.Description: On August 5, a clash takes place between two groups of the Buledi community in Jnai Wandh village in Jacobabad, Sindh, Pakistan over rotation of water, causing injuries to one.

properties.Title: One man is shot during protests in Miranda, Venezuela to demand potable water services
properties.Description: On August 26 in Cupira, Miranda, Venezuela, residents protest to demand basic services including potable water and are met with force by municipal police.  One man is severely injured with a bullet.

properties.Title: One person reported killed in clash over water in Kupwara, India
properties.Description: On August 3, two families clash at Kacchama village in Kupwara city (Kupwara district, Jammu and Kashmir), India over a water dispute. One person is killed in the incident.

properties.Title: Protests in Caxito, Angola for improved water supply leads to clashes with police and injuries
properties.Description: On August 5, 2020, people stage a demonstration in Caxito (Dande, Bengo, Angola) in support of employees who had not received their wages and against the lack of water supply in the region. Police used tear gas and live fire to disperse the demonstrators. Injuries and arrests are reported.

properties.Title: Sniper shoots and wounds civilian fetching water in al-Rawdah, Yemen
properties.Description: A civilian is injured after being shot by a sniper while attempting to fetch water. Sources attribute the attack to Houthi extremists.

properties.Title: A fight over the rotation of irrigation water in Pakistan injures eight
properties.Description: On September 12, a clash takes place between the Chandio and Solangi communities over the rotation of water in Zahra Machhi village in Radhan, Pakistan. Both groups attack each other with sticks, injuring eight men.

properties.Title: Demonstrators storm a desalination plant in Tunisia, disrupting local potable water supply
properties.Description: On September 23, demonstrators storm a desalination plant in Nagga, Kebili, Tunisia and interrupt the provision of potable water in the region to demand the pavement of a public road in their village.

properties.Title: Four are injured during protests in Cojedes, Venezuela over lack of public water services, including water
properties.Description: On September 29 in San Carlos, Cojedes, Venezuela, residents protest the lack of basic public services such as water, electricity, and gas. GNB and CPNB officers arrest 12 individuals. Four people are injured by state security forces.

properties.Title: Injuries are reported during a protest in Yaracuy, Venezuela demanding better potable water services
properties.Description: On September 26 in Chivacoa, Yaracuy, Venezuela, around 60 people demonstrate to demand better basic services including potable water. National Guard and police fire tear gas after rioters destroy the headquarters of the Mayor's office. An unknown number of people are injured and arrested.

properties.Title: Nine people are reported injured in a clash over wastewater in Fazilka district, Punjab, India
properties.Description: Around September 2, a clash in Ramnagar Urf Jatwali village of Fazilka district (Punjab), India over waste water injures nine.

properties.Title: One death and multiple injuries reported during protests over lack of potable water and other services in Sucre, Venezuela
properties.Description: On September 30 in Cariaco, Sucre, Venezuela, residents block a road to demand basic services including potable water, gasoline, gas, food, and electric power. State police officers arrive to disperse the group of people but are received with stones. One person is killed during the demonstration and an unknown number of people are injured.

properties.Title: One death is reported in dispute over control of water in a refugee camp in Bangladesh
properties.Description: On September 3, a dispute over access to water between Rohingya refugees and residents of the Chakmarkul Rohingya Camp (Teknaf, Cox's Bazar) in Bangladesh leads to a death.

properties.Title: Water distribution workers abducted in the Democratic Republic of the Congo
properties.Description: Employees of a Ngongo water distribution organization are abducted and later released unharmed. Sources attribute the incident to the Patriotic and Integrationist Forces of the Congo.

properties.Title: Soldiers wounded, water pumps and transformers destroyed in attack on Popular Mobilization Forces checkpoints in Al-Wehda, Iraq
properties.Description: An attack on Popular Mobilization Forces checkpoints leaves at least two soldiers injured and destroys several water pumps and transformers. Sources believe the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant to be behind the incident.

properties.Title: Engineers and customary chief kidnapped on way to repair water piping system destroyed by attack
properties.Description: Three engineers working for the water distribution network of Ngongo and a customary chief are kidnapped in Mwanga, Ituri, Democratic Republic of the Congo. The abduction occurs en route to repair a water piping system that was destroyed by assailants in an earlier attack. Their fate is not known. Sources attribute the attack to the Patriotic Resistance Front in Ituri.

properties.Title: Environmental defender killed along with two of his family members in conflict over a dam in Patuca, Honduras
properties.Description: Gunman kill Jose Antonio Teruel, an environmental defender in the Patuca community in Honduras, in part due to his connection to a conflict between a power company and the community over a dam. During the murder, which occurs in his home, his sister and her husband are killed as well.
properties.Sources: Global Witness 2024; Business & Human Rights Resource Centre. n.d.-c

properties.Title: Attempted arson at irrigation construction site in Baleguian, Philippines
properties.Description: An attempted arson occurs at an irrigation construction site. A subsequent clash with security forces leaves an unknown number of assailants injured. Sources attribute the attack to the New People’s Army.

properties.Title: In Guyana, security guards protecting a water pump used to flood land as weapon against squatters are attacked
properties.Description: On October 15 in Success, Demerara-Mahaica, Guyana, two armed men attack a security guard protecting a water pump controlled by the Guyana Sugar Company. The company is accused of using the water to flood land to drive squatters out.

properties.Title: Well-known businessman and environmental activist kidnapped and killed in Veracruz, Mexico
properties.Description: In October 2020 a local businessman and environmental defender, Miguel Vazquez Martinez, is kidnapped in Tlapacoyan, Veracruz, Mexico. His body is not found until 2021. He was known for over 15 years of organizing to prevent hydroelectric projects on local rivers and he founded a local environmental defense organization that had filed injunctions to try to prevent some dam projects from progressin

properties.Title: A clash over the installation of water tanks at a reserve in Chiapas, Mexico results in at least 10 injuries
properties.Description: On October 25, in the municipality of San Cristobal de las Casas, Chiapas, Mexico in the Gertrude Duby reserve, a clash is reported over the installation of water tanks in the reserve. At least 40 people participate, shots are fired, and at least 10 people are injured.

properties.Title: A fight over the laying of water pipes in Puri district, India kills two and injures more than ten
properties.Description: On October 11, a fight over the laying of water pipes in Dimirisena village in Brahmagiri, Puri district (Odisha), India kills two and injures more than ten.

properties.Title: An environmental activist in Honduras who protested an open-pit mine that would contaminate the Guapinol River is assassinated
properties.Description: In October 2020, a member of the El Guapinol community in Honduras who participates in the protests over the development of an open-pit mine that would contaminate community water sources of the Guapinol River is shot and killed in his own home. (See also events of 2018.)

properties.Title: Six police officers killed and two wounded in attack at irrigation dam in Qala-e Fath, Afghanistan
properties.Description: An attack on a police outpost guarding an irrigation dam leaves six officers dead and two injured. Sources suspect the Taliban are responsible, but they deny the claim.

properties.Title: Seven killed in clan dispute over land and water in Mandera, Kenya
properties.Description: Around November 20, Garre and Murle clans clash over pasture land and water in Yado village, Mandera, Kenya. At least seven people are killed.

properties.Title: Three youth collecting water are injured when attacked in South Sudan
properties.Description: On November 14, three youth collecting water from the river Horse are wounded by armed militia in Torit county, South Sudan.

properties.Title: Demonstrations against a proposed privatization of the drinking water system in Mexico leads to injuries and arrests
properties.Description: On November 30, in Chignautla, Puebla, Mexico, hundreds of residents demonstrate against the proposed privatization of the drinking water system. The demonstration turns violent. The municipal police intervene with pepper spray;  the demonstrators react by looting and burning the municipal presidency. Arrests and injuries are reported.

properties.Title: Demonstrations in Chile over land and water rights lead to conflicts with the police, injuries, and arrests
properties.Description: In November 2020, demonstrations in the Los Rios region of Chile over land and water rights and other water concerns lead to conflicts with local police and arrests.

properties.Title: A demonstration over the shutdown of drinking water facilities in Manipur, India results in multiple injuries
properties.Description: On December 7, demonstrations over the shutdown of drinking water facilities in Manipur, India results in multiple injuries of police and demonstrators.

properties.Title: A dispute over the control of local water resources turns violent in Bangladesh Two deaths and over 50 injuries are reported
properties.Description: In late December, a dispute over control of local water resources between Shantipur village and Prajarkanda village in Itna Upazila (Kishoreganj), Bangladesh turns violent during local arbitration. Two people are killed and over fifty injured in the clash.

properties.Title: A Palestinian water network is damaged by Israeli settlers
properties.Description: On December 13, a group of Israeli settlers from the Maaoun settlement attack a Palestinian's house and damage a water network in Sarura area of Yuta village.

properties.Title: A police officer kills one in a dispute over water in Brown's Town, Jamaica
properties.Description: On December 14, near Brown's Town, Saint Ann, Jamaica, residents set tires on fire after an off-duty police officer shoots and kills someone in a dispute over water.

properties.Title: Over 100 injuries reported in a clash over access to water in local villages in Bangladesh
properties.Description: On December 18, two groups of villagers clash at Baniachong Upazila (Habiganj), Bangladesh over control of a water body. Over 100 people are injured in the clash.

properties.Title: Water supply network damaged in attack on Palestinian-owned land in Khallet Ad-Dabi, West Bank
properties.Description: Assailants damage a water supply network, attack a home and set fire to more than 400 olive trees during the incident. Sources attribute the attack to Israeli Settlers.

properties.Title: Nine indigenous leaders in the Philippines opposed to dam projects are killed during a police raid
properties.Description: Philippine National Police (PNP) and the Criminal Investigation and Detetion Group (CIDG) raid what they claim to be homes of armed members of the new People's Army (NPA) on the island of Panay in the Philippines. During the raid, the police claim that they were shot at and were acting in self defense when they shot and killed nine people. The police also made 16 arrests and claimed to find firear

properties.Title: Taliban attack a dam in Afghanistan
properties.Description: The Salma hydroelectric dam is attacked by Taliban rockets. The dam, in western Heart province, is one of the largest in Afghanistan and provides irrigation water and electricity. The dam was completed in 2016 with the assistance of India.

properties.Title: Two killed in protests over water shortages in Iran
properties.Description: Two men are killed during growing public protests over water shortages in Khuzestan province in southwest Iran. The shortages are occurring during the worst drought in 50 years, with protests in several cities and towns over residential and agricultural water cuts and power blackouts.

properties.Title: Sabotage of water infrastructure by the "water mafia" is reported in Kwa-Zulu Natal, South Africa
properties.Description: Acts of sabotage and vandalism on water infrastructure are reported in 2021 in Kwa-Zulu Natal, South Africa so that water tanker contracts can be signed in growing corruption in South Africa's water system.

properties.Title: Attacks on water systems in Ethiopia have cut off water supply to thousands of people
properties.Description: Throughout the conflict in Tigray, Ethiopia, water infrastructure has been attacked, causing communities to lose access to safe water access. Water pumps have been destroyed, and equipment has been looted.

properties.Title: Conflicts over water in Cameroon between ranchers and fishermen kill at least 40 people
properties.Description: Tens of thousands of citizens of Cameroon flee bloody conflicts over water between cattle ranchers and fishermen in the region bordering Chad and Nigeria. The conflict kills at least 40 people.

properties.Title: Honduran environmental activist protesting construction of a new dam is shot and killed
properties.Description: An environmental activist from the town of Nueva Granada, Honduras is shot and killed. He and his fellow community members had been protesting the construction of a new dam on the Ulua River in Honduras.

properties.Title: Major clash over water kills more than 30 in Kyrgyzstan-Tajikistan dispute
properties.Description: At least 31 people are killed, scores injured, and 10,000 evacuated after a dispute over access to water and water facilities leads to major clashes on a disputed Kyrgyzstan-Tajikistan border.

properties.Title: A fight over a water well in Wadi al Barkani, Yemen injures six
properties.Description: On January 8, unknown militias clashed in Wadi al Barkani, Yemen over a water well, injuring six people.

properties.Title: A fight over access to a water well escalates into extensive violence in the Upper Nile state of South Sudan, killing at least 1
properties.Description: On January 3rd and 4th, a fight over water access at a well escalates in the disputed border town of Meganis, Upper Nile, South Sudan.  17 are killed across two days of fighting and more than 20 are injured.

properties.Title: At least six killed in clan disputes over land and water in Garbatulla, Kenya
properties.Description: On January 9, militias from the Ajuran and Borana clans fight over land and water in the vicinity of Eldera village, Garbatulla, Kenya. At least six people are killed.

properties.Title: One killed and others injured in clan clashes over grazing land and water points in Marsabit, Kenya
properties.Description: On January 13, armed gunmen from Dasenaach ethnic militia clash with a Gabra ethnic militia over a grazing zone and watering point in Sibiloi National Park in Madho Dimtu village, Marsabit, Kenya. One person is killed and two others injured.

properties.Title: Six are injured during a protest in Peru demanding improved potable water and sewage services
properties.Description: On January 11, in Tumbes, Peru, citizens of Cerro Blanco block a highway to demand improvement of the potable water service and sewage system. Police forces intervene and six citizens are injured.

properties.Title: Villagers  in Mexico fight over access to water supplies, with injuries and arrests
properties.Description: On January 13, in Huejutla de Reyes municipality, Hidalgo, a clash between the communities of Coxhuaco II and Xiquila over access to water leads to injuries and arrests.

properties.Title: Violent clash over access to water in Lebanon leaves at least one injured
properties.Description: On January 18, Msheik and Hjula clans clashed over access to water using guns and RPGs in Hai el Lailaki, Baabda, Lebanon. At least one injury is reported.

properties.Title: A worker at a wastewater treatment plant in California, U.S., remotely uninstalls software needed for safe operation
properties.Description: In January 2021, a worker at a company providing services to the Discovery Bay, California, wastewater treatment plant remotely accesses the plant’s computers and uninstalls software critical to operating the facility’s network and protecting its water treatment systems, including water filtration, water pressure, and chemical levels maintenance. He is arrested, tried, and convicted.

properties.Title: Anti-dam activist receives death threats before his murder in Oaxaca, Mexico
properties.Description: An anti-dam activist, Fidel Heras Cruz, receives a death threat before being murdered in January 2021 in the state of Oaxaca, Mexico. He had also been opposed to commercial sand and gravel extraction that damanged the river bed. Follwing this murder, the Mexican government confirmed that the hydroelectric project would continue.

properties.Title: Kiowa and Cheyenne demonstrators rally in Taos, New Mexico against water pipeline; one injured during protest
properties.Description: On February 24, demonstrators from Kiowa and Cheyenne Tribes rally at a construction site in Taos, New Mexico against the construction of a water pipeline in protest of how the Abeyta Water Rights Settlement is being implemented. A demonstrator is injured

properties.Title: Eight are killed and many injured in a fight over a water well in Al Yatmah, Yemen
properties.Description: On February 13, 2021, militias affiliated with the Juayd Tribe and the Al Hussein tribe, and soldiers from the pro-Hadi 7th brigade in Al Yatmah (Khabb wa ash Shaaf), Yemen clash over a water well. The clashes result in eight fatalities and dozens of injuries.

properties.Title: Hacker successfully manipulates chemical treatment systems at a Florida water treatment plant adding dangerous levels of sodium
properties.Description: A hacker gains control of a small water treatment plant's chemical dosing computer program, causing the addition of dangerous amounts of sodium hydroxide to the community drinking water system. Staff spot the cyberattack and prevent contaminated water from being released to the community.

properties.Title: A fight over the rotation of irrigation water in Pakistan injures four
properties.Description: On March 4, a clash takes place between two groups of the Bohra community over the rotation of water injuring four men in Pir Chandam village, Shikarpur, Sindh, Pakistan.

properties.Title: 'Stop Line 3' protest related to water in US turns violent; demonstrators light fires, smash windows; arrests made
properties.Description: On March 11, about 100 people gather for a 'Stop Line 3' demonstration in Portland, Oregon, United States in support of abolishing ICE, protecting water rights and quality, and against the proposed Line 3 oil pipeline expansion. The demonstration turns violent when around 50 to 60 demonstrators light fires, smash windows, and pull protective plywood off a federal courthouse. Federal officers fire

properties.Title: Injuries are reported during a protest in Peru demanding the resumption of potable water and sewage services
properties.Description: On March 2, in Las Lomas, Peru, protestors block the Sullana highway that leads towards the International Bridge with Ecuador to demand the resumption of a potable water and sewage works that have been paused for eight years. Police intervene with tear gas and the demonstrators fight back rocks. An unknown number of injured citizens are reported.

properties.Title: Multiple deaths are reported in violence over access to water between pastoralists and local militia in South Darfur, Sudan
properties.Description: On March 1, Fellata pastoralists clash with Masalit militia in Gereida, South Darfur, Sudan after a member of the Fellata was killed at or near a water source. 11 more deaths are reported with another 36 wounded across two days of violence.

properties.Title: Residents in Senegal protesting bad water quality refuse to pay their bills and beat up water company representatives sent to cu
properties.Description: On March 22, residents beat up representatives of the Sen'Eau national water distribution company in Mbacke, Diourbel, Senegal as they try to suspend the distribution of water to residents who had not paid their bills in protest of bad water quality.

properties.Title: Four river defenders from Paso de la Reina in Oaxaca, Mexico, are murdered within the span of a month
properties.Description: Four river defenders from the 500 person community of Paso de la Reina in Oaxaca, Mexico are murdered within the span of a single month, March 2021. The four include Raymundo Robles Riano, Noe Robles Cruz, Gerardo Mendoz Reyes, and Jaime Jimenez Ruiz. This is the same community where a pervious murder of an anti-dam activist occurred in January of the same year.

properties.Title: A Nevada-based wastewater plant is targeted with malware that affects the SCADA system, USA
properties.Description: In March 2021, a Nevada-based wastewater plant is targeted with an unknown malware strain that affects the SCADA system.

properties.Title: Another nine indigenous leaders, also opposing a dam project, are killed in the Philippines by police
properties.Description: Three months after the killing of nine Indigenous leaders on Panay Island—deaths that human rights advocates link to opposition against two dam projects—a similar tragedy unfolded in the Philippines. On what became known as “Bloody Sunday,” joint raids by the Philippine National Police (PNP) and Philippine Army (PA) targeted Indigenous people accused of being communist insurgents. The raids result
properties.Sources: Global Witness 2024; International Federation for Human Rights 2021; Business & Human Rights Resource Centre n.d.-d

properties.Title: Community organizer and river defender murdered in Guerrero, Mexico
properties.Description: A community organizer and river defender, Isaías Elacio Palma, is murdered in March 2021, in Cacahuatepec in the state of Guerrero, Mexico. His son had been murdered in 2018 for defending the environment as well.
properties.Sources: Global Witness 2024; González Chacón et al. 2022

properties.Title: Two men are killed, three injured in a clash over irrigation water rotation in Pakistan
properties.Description: On April 29, a clash takes place between the Mangrio and Nohani communities over the rotation of water. Shots are fired, killing two men and injuring three others in Tando Allahyar city, Pakistan.

properties.Title: A fight over a drinking water well in Xudun, Somalia kills at least ten people
properties.Description: On April 16, a fight over a drinking water well between militias in the Dhabar-Dalool village near Xudun, Somalia kills at least ten people.

properties.Title: Border guards from Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan exchange fire over a water source, killing dozens and wounding hundreds
properties.Description: Between April 28 and 30, 2021, border guards and rioters from Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan exchange fire over the control of a water canal and pumping site along the Isfara River. Several dozen casualties and hundreds of hospitalizations were reported.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al 2010 ID. 7931294, 7931294, 7971544; Auyezov et al 2021

properties.Title: Multiple injuries are reported during demonstrations over cutoffs of water and electricity in North Darfur, Sudan
properties.Description: On April 11, students in El Fasher, North Darfur demonstrate against poor living conditions including the interruption of water and electricity. Rioters block main roads and set fire to tires and at least six members of the armed forces and several students are injured.

properties.Title: One death is reported in a fight over access to a water point in the village of Malual Deng Ngor, South Sudan
properties.Description: On April 16, two groups of women armed with sticks fight over access to a water point in the village of Malual Deng Ngor in Yargot payam, South Sudan. One woman dies in the incident and five are arrested.

properties.Title: 11 people are reported shot in a fire fight over access to water in Guinea
properties.Description: On May 17, villagers of Dankakoro and Banfelebougou villages, Guinea engage in fire fights around Dankakoro village over access to a disputed water point. 11 people are shot and injured.

properties.Title: A fight over the use of irrigation water in the Punjab, Pakistan injures at least eight
properties.Description: On May 6, at least eight people are injured in a clash between two groups of villagers in Bahawalnagar district (Punjab), Pakistan over the usage of irrigation water in the village.

properties.Title: Farmers in Isfahan, Iran clash with police over water shortages and diversions of water from the region
properties.Description: On May 25, farmers demonstrate in Isfahan, Iran over issues stemming from shortages and lack of access to Zayandehrud River water. A conflict with police result in several injuries.

properties.Title: Residents in Lumas, Albania protest against the use of a local water source, leading to at least one severe injury
properties.Description: On May 21, locals protest in Lumas, Albania against the use of a water source for municipal purposes. The police use force when residents block the road severely injuring one protester.

properties.Title: Violence over access to water leads to one death and two injuries in Cameroon
properties.Description: On May 18, villagers fight in Waza village (Logone-et-Chari, Extreme-Nord), Cameroon over access to a water point. One person is killed, and two others injured.

properties.Title: Extensive attacks on Gaza disrupt water supplies for 800,000 people
properties.Description: Around 800,000 people in Gaza lack regular access to piped water in the wake of the 11-day fighting in 2021 that damages around half of the water network or cuts off electricity required for the water system, says the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA). Gaza municipality authorities say in a statement that 290 water-supply facilities, including the only desalination plan

properties.Title: Kajaki Dam in Afghanistan is attacked by the Taliban
properties.Description: Kajaki Dam on the Helmand River in Afghanistan, which provides power to more than three million people including the cities of Kandahar and Lashkar Gah, is attacked by the Taliban.

properties.Title: A dispute over the diversion of flood waters for irrigation in Nepal leads to 15 injuries
properties.Description: On June 26, a group of locals, including a ward chairperson, clash with others in Maulapur municipality, Nepal over diverting flood water into local fields. 15 people are injured.

properties.Title: A fight over a water reservoir near Baxdo, Somalia kills two and injures three others
properties.Description: On June 5, two clan militias fight over a water reservoir near Baxdo village, Galgaduud, Somalia. Two people are killed and three others are injured.

properties.Title: A protest in Afghanistan over lack of clean drinking water and other services results in three deaths and more than 40 injuries
properties.Description: On June 8, 200 demonstrators in the city of Fayzabad, Badakhshan, Afghanistan protest inadequate security and a lack of electricity and clean drinking water. The demonstrators clash with police, throwing stones and wood at security forces and setting fire to a tax collection office building. Three demonstrators are killed and 43 others were wounded after police responded by shooting.

properties.Title: A water dispute in the Chui region of Kyrgyzstan results in at least one hospitalization
properties.Description: On June 23, a resident of Iskra village near Tokmok city in the Chui region of Kyrgyzstan is assaulted by the villagers from a neighboring town following a water dispute. One hospitalization is reported.

properties.Title: A water project in Al Hudaydah, Yemen is attacked
properties.Description: On June 16, Houthi attack the Al Khamiri water project in Hays city (Al Hudaydah), Yemen. According to the source, the work on the water project is interrupted by the attack.

properties.Title: Injuries are reported in a dispute over water wells and land near Qorilow village, Somalia
properties.Description: On June 25, Hassan Gaab clan militia clash with Cosoble Ali militia in Qorilow village, Somalia over water wells and land in the area. The injured are rushed to Warshikh town for medical attention.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers destroy Palestinian olive trees and irrigation systems in Wadi Qana, West Bank
properties.Description: On June 23, Israeli settlers uproot 10 olive trees and ruin water containers and an irrigation network in a Palestinian-owned farm in Wadi Qana, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers destroy Palestinian olive trees and steal a water pump in Nablus
properties.Description: On June 28, 2021, Israeli settlers from the Ahya settlement uproot Palestinian-owned olive trees and steal a water pump and electric wires in the village of Jalud, Nablus.

properties.Title: Local villagers and farmers tear down an earthen dam on the Dez River, Khuzestan, Iran in opposition to water diversions
properties.Description: On June 14, local villagers and farmers tear down an earthen dam on the Dez River near the Dehkhoda Sugarcane Development Company, Seffak (Khuzestan), Iran in opposition to the diversion of water from the river.

properties.Title: A Brahmin group assaults Dalit community members over drawing water from a shared well in Malarh, India
properties.Description: On July 9, a Brahmin caste group assaults Dalit community members at Malarh village in Udhampur district (J&K), India, over drawing water from a shared well. Several Dalit community members are injured in the attack.

properties.Title: A dispute over the construction of an irrigation water canal in Pakistan leads to two deaths and one injury
properties.Description: On July 9, farmers shoot and killed two other farmers and injure one other in Faisalabad, Pakistan, over the construction of an irrigation water canal.

properties.Title: A dispute over the rotation of water results in injuries in Pakistan
properties.Description: On July 25, two groups of the Khuhawar community attack each other with sticks, injuring one man in Sarang Khuhawar village, Pakistan. The dispute is over rotation of water.

properties.Title: A farmer is killed in a dispute over irrigation water in Punjab, India
properties.Description: On July 25, a farmer is attacked and killed by his neighbors in Kamal Wala village (Fazilka district, Punjab), India, over a water canal dispute.

properties.Title: A Water Resources Department staff member was assaulted by farmers in Punjab, India over the alleged theft of irrigation water
properties.Description: Around July 9, a staff member of the Water Resources Department, Punjab, India is attacked and injured by farmers in Lualaba village (Nabha, Patiala district), when stopped from allegedly stealing water from canals.

properties.Title: A water station is destroyed near Ar-Raqqa, Syria
properties.Description: On July 17, an IED planted by an unidentified armed group in Beir Al-Hashim in Ar-Raqqa detonates inside the well project and renders the water station inoperable.

properties.Title: An unidentified group destroys a water plant providing water in Libya
properties.Description: On July 29, an unidentified armed group blows up water station 353 on the Man-made River near Ash Shwayrif (Al Jabal Al Gharbi), Libya, disrupting water supply to Bani Walid, Misrata, Khoms, and Zliten.

properties.Title: Armed assailants attack police in Shadegan, Iran amid water shortage protests, killing one officer and injuring three others
properties.Description: On July 24, unidentified armed assailants open fire on and injure four policemen in Shadegan city (Khuzestan), Iran. The attack occurs in the context of continued tension and protests over water shortages. One police officer dies.

properties.Title: At least three are killed in a clash over the distribution of water in Pakistan
properties.Description: On July 8, at least three people are killed in a clash including exchange of gunfire over water distribution in Dera Ghazi Khan city, Punjab, Pakistan.

properties.Title: Deaths and injuries are reported in a fight over water supply in Pakistan
properties.Description: On July 27, two groups exchange fire killing two people and injuring seven in Tangoo Basi, Pakistan, over a water supply line.

properties.Title: Deaths are reported in demonstrations in Khuzestan, Iran over electrical outages, water shortages, and the transfer of water out
properties.Description: In mid- to late-July, protestors clash with police and other security forces in Bandar e Mahshahr, Susangerd, Ahvaz, Izeh, and Aligudarz (Khuzestan), Iran over repeated and prolonged electrical outages, water shortages, and the transfer of water from Khuzestan to other provinces. Explosions and gunfire are heard and several demonstrators and at least one policeman is reported killed.

properties.Title: Farmers protest lack of access to water and droughts in Isfahan, Iran. Several protesters are injured
properties.Description: On July 4, farmers protest in front of the Isfahan provincial governorate building in Iran over lack of access to water and droughts affecting the Zayandehrud River. Police reportedly intervene, beating and injuring several protesters.

properties.Title: Islamic State militants destroy a water tanker in Egypt
properties.Description: On July 17, 2021, Islamic State militants target a water tanker with an IED in Sheikh Zuwayid (Al Shiekh Zuwaid, North Sinai), Egypt, destroying it.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces destroy a Palestinian irrigation water tank in Nablus, West Bank
properties.Description: On July 15, Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian-owned water container of 500 cubic meters in Bayt Dajan, Nablus, West Bank, which was used for irrigation.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces destroy irrigation water pipes in the West Bank
properties.Description: On July 6, Israeli military forces raid the village of Al Jiwaya, Hebron, West Bank and uproot water pipes used for irrigation.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers destroy Palestinian orchards and irrigation systems in Nablus, West Bank
properties.Description: On July 2, Israeli settlers uproot 84 Palestinian-owned fruit trees and destroy irrigation pipes and water containers near the settlements of Shilo and Shvut Rachel in the lands of the village of Qaryut, Nablus.

properties.Title: Mauritanian protests over water shortages and electricity cuts result in injuries and arrests
properties.Description: On July 2, the Mauritanian police use tear gas to disperse hundreds of residents of Kobenni, Mauritania protesting regular water shortages and electricity cuts affecting the city. One policemen is injured and several people are arrested.

properties.Title: Palestinians destroy water pipes that provide water to Israeli settlements near Nablus in the West Bank
properties.Description: On July 5, Palestinians destroy water pipes that provide water to Esh Kodesh and Migdalim settlements near Qusra, Nablus, West Bank.

properties.Title: Protesters demanding improvements in water, sewage, and electrical services in Najaf, Iraq are attacked, injuring at least one
properties.Description: On July 31, dozens of people protest to demand improvements in basic services such as water, sewage, and electricity in Najaf city, Iraq. Unknown militants attacked the protesters with knives, injuring at least one protester.

properties.Title: Russian warplanes destroy an agricultural water pumping station near Idlib, Syria
properties.Description: On July 3, Russian warplanes launch airstrikes on the water pumping station in al Rouj area in Idlib, destroying the station that served over 3500 hectares of farmland. No fatalities or injuries are reported.

properties.Title: Sabotage to water infrastructure occurs in Hasawna, Libya
properties.Description: Around July 13, an unidentified armed group carries out acts of sabotage to damage water infrastructure in Hasawna (Wadi Ashshati, South), Libya.

properties.Title: The main water pipeline diverting water from Isfahan to Yazd, Iran is destroyed by farmers and residents
properties.Description: Around July 31, farmers and residents destroy the main water pipeline from Isfahan to Yazd around Isfahan, Iran.

properties.Title: Two communities in Ciudad de Mexico clash over water infrastructure, with several injuries reported
properties.Description: On July 20, in Ciudad de Mexico - Xochimilco, residents from two mountain communities clash with each other over a water project in San Mateo Xalpa. The confrontation was between supporters and opponents of the water work in the Cola de Conejo tank. Several people are injured.

properties.Title: While in search of pasture and water, militias clash with police at Laikipia Nature Conservancy, Kenya, killing at least one
properties.Description: On July 31, militias clash with police at the Laikipia Nature Conservancy in Wangwaci village in Ol-Moran town, Laikipia, Kenya. One officer is killed. The militias invade several ranches in the conservancy in search of pasture and water.

properties.Title: In Maine, U.S., a wastewater facility’s computer is attacked by ransomware, forcing the system to be shut down
properties.Description: In July 2021, a ransomware attack is launched against a Maine-based wastewater facility’s SCADA computer forcing the automated system to be shut down and run in manual mode.

properties.Title: Shelling by rebel forces in Donetsk, Ukraine cause blackouts and one village to loose water supply
properties.Description: On August 11, the NAF rebel forces fire mortars at the governmental positions near Novomykhaylivka, Donetsk, Ukraine. As a result of the shelling, one Ukrainian soldier is injured, and the territory of the farm and the power transmission line are damaged, causing blackouts of settlements and leaving Novomykhaylivka village without water supply.

properties.Title: A clash over shortage of drinking water, illegal fishing, and lack of electricity leads to two injuries in Pakistan
properties.Description: On August 17, a demonstration in Gwadar city, Pakistan against water shortages, illegal fishing, and electricity cutoffs leads to a clash injuring two people.

properties.Title: A dispute between local residents and farmers in Tarata, Bolivia over the use of water lead to at least two injuries
properties.Description: On August 18 in Tarata, Cochabamba, Bolivia locals and irrigation farmers clash near La Angostura dam after a blockade was implemented by the workers. The blockade was set up to demand the use of the water from the dam to irrigate land in the region. Local residents argue the water should be used for tourism and local fish farming activities. At least two injuries are reported.

properties.Title: Demonstrators in Kiev, Ukraine rally against renewal of water supplies to Crimea, resulting in violent clashes with police
properties.Description: On August 14, activists demonstrate in Kiev against the implementation of the Steinmeier formula on Donbas conflict settlement and the renewal of water supplies to Russia-occupied Crimea. The demonstrators provoke clashes with police using tear gas, tires, and other items. Eight officers are reportedly injured.

properties.Title: Farmer/pastoralist violence over land and water lead to at least one death in Baringo region of Kenya
properties.Description: On August 5, an unidentified communal militia shoot and kill a pastoralist in the Embosos area in Arabal village, Marigat, Baringo, Kenya. At least 38 cattle are stolen from pastoralists in search of pasture and water.

properties.Title: Four are killed and eight injured in a fight over the distribution of irrigation water in Pakistan
properties.Description: On August 1, two groups from the communities of Chandio and Bohar, Pakistan exchanged fire, killing four people and injuring eight, over the distribution of irrigation water.

properties.Title: Israeli forces demolish a medical unit, water well, and under-construction mosque in Hebron, West Bank
properties.Description: On August 25, Israeli military forces demolish a medical unit, water well, and an under-construction mosque west of Yuta town, Hebron, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces demolish agricultural facilities including a water well in the West Bank
properties.Description: On August 11, 2021, Israeli military forces demolish agricultural facilities, including a water well, caravans, and agricultural rooms in Mosafer Yuta, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces destroy a Palestinian agricultural water tank near Jenin, West Bank
properties.Description: On August 9, Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian-owned agricultural water tank used for farming near Yabad, Jenin, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces destroy an agricultural structure and water well in Bethlehem, West Bank
properties.Description: On August 4, 2021, Israeli military forces demolish five Palestinian-owned agricultural store rooms and a water well in Wadi Rahhal, Bethlehem, West Bank, on the pretext of not having building permits.

properties.Title: Landmines intended to disrupt the Al Hudaydah city water-supply pipeline in Yemen are defused
properties.Description: On August 13, the National Resistance forces engineering team defuse Houthi landmines planted around a water pipe near the Red Sea Mills company in Al Hudaydah, Yemen. According to the source, the mines were going to disrupt city water supply.

properties.Title: Clashes between fishers and ranchers over water displace thousands between Cameroon and Chad
properties.Description: Clashes between Mousgoum fishermen and Arab Choua cattle ranchers over water usage displace at least 11,000 civilians between Cameroon and Chad in August 2021.

properties.Title: Spokesperson for organization opposed to La Parota Dam in Mexico is kidnapped and never found
properties.Description: An activist and spokesperson for the Consejo de Ejidos y Comunidades Opositoras a la Presa La Parota (The Council of Ejidos and Community Opposing the Parota Dam), Vicente Iván Suástegui Muñoz, is kidnapped while driving his taxi cab in Acapulco, Mexico in 2021. Sr. Muñoz had been defending land and water since 2003. His brother, Marco Antonio Suástegui Muñoz, is murdered in 2025 after a multi-yea

properties.Title: Clashes in Missiska, Cameroon over water and land kill 45, injure 74; 19 villages burned, 40 abandoned after cattle deaths
properties.Description: In the village of Missiska in the Logone Birni division, northern Cameroon, violent clashes over water and land use erupt when a basin dug by Musgum fisherfolk trapped and killed cattle belonging to Arab Choa herders. The conflict kills at least 45 people and injures 74 others. At least 19 villages are burned down and 40 others abandoned by the inhabitants.

properties.Title: A clash over a proposed water project in Al Ashah, Yemen results in three deaths and one injury
properties.Description: On September 5, clashes erupt between Sufyan and Al Usaymat tribesmen in Al Ashah, Yemen over a water project that Houthi forces are planning in a contested area between the two tribes. The clashes result in three fatalities and one injury.

properties.Title: A pastoralist is killed and four others injured in a dispute over land and water near Kismayo, Somalia
properties.Description: On September 17, suspected Awramale clan militia shoot and kill a pastoralist from Majeerteen clan in the vicinity of Kismayo town, Somalia, in a dispute over water and land. Four other injuries are reported.

properties.Title: A pipeline carrying water to a joint Myanmar-China mine is bombed by a militia group opposed to the Myanmar military regime
properties.Description: On September 9, in Salingyi township (Yinmarbin district, Sagaing region), Myanmar a water pipeline transporting water from the Chindwin River to the Myanmar Wanbao and Myanmar Yangtze copper mines (a joint venture between China and a Myanmar military-owned holding company) is bombed by a local militia group. The group stated that the bombing was carried out because China had been ignoring the 'de

properties.Title: A social activist protesting inadequate water service is shot and killed in Mexico
properties.Description: On September 7, in Cuernavaca, Morelos, a social activist is shot and killed by two armed men in his house in Colonia Antonio Barona, Mexico. The victim led several protests to demand water service and created groups to prevent the Federal Commission of Electricity from suspending electricity to the wells.

properties.Title: Airstrike hits rural water and electricity authority in Sana, Yemen
properties.Description: On September 12, an airstrike hits a rural water and electricity authority in Al-Thawrah District, Sana, Yemen.

properties.Title: An attempt to steal water from a government-owned water channel leads to gunfire and injuries in Pakistan
properties.Description: On September 9, officials of an irrigation department are attacked in Faisalabad, Pakistan after refusing to let people steal water from a government-owned channel.

properties.Title: Farmers protesting water shortages and drought are beaten and arrested in Shaban village, Iran
properties.Description: On September 1, farmers protest water shortages and drought in Shaban village near Nahavand city (Hamadan), Iran. They are confronted by police who beat them and make several arrests.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces destroy a water-distribution system near Bayta in the West Bank
properties.Description: On September 17, Israeli military bulldozers raze all roads and a water distribution system leading to Jabal Subeih in Bayta town (Nablus) to prevent access to the area.

properties.Title: One farmer is killed in a dispute over canal water irrigation in Punjab, India
properties.Description: On September 23, a group of men allegedly attack a farmer and his son in Amarpura village (Abohar, Fazilka district, Punjab), India, over a canal water irrigation dispute. The farmer died.

properties.Title: Protest over lack of irrigation water in Azerbaijan leads to at least one injury
properties.Description: On September 28, 2021, residents of Nabatkend, Ortamugan and Kolpu villages of Saatly region of Azerbaijan protest the lack of irrigation water and control of water by "Baku oligarchs." Police intervene and injure a protester who was transported to the hospital.

properties.Title: Protests in Cangandala, Angola for improved water and electricity supply leads to clashes with police and injuries
properties.Description: On September 25, 2021, protests in Cangandala (Cangandala, Malanje, Angola) to demand water and electricity are met with violence. Three injuries and eight arrests are reported.

properties.Title: Hackers use a ransomware variant against a New Jersey-based wastewater treatment facility, USA
properties.Description: In September 2021, threat actors use a ransomware variant against a New Jersey-based wastewater treatment facility.

properties.Title: Five people are injured in a clash over the waters of the Longone River, Cameroon.
properties.Description: Arab Choa people from Malia and Toukoumaya villages clash in a land and water dispute around Waza, a town in Logone-et-Chari. Five people are injured.

properties.Title: A conflict over control of and access to water results in one death to a child collecting water in Yemen
properties.Description: On October 24, 2021, clashes between Abu Shusa tribesmen and Sabara tribesmen supported by Houthi forces result in the death of a child in Wadi Mawr (Al Maghrabah, Hajjah), Yemen as the child was getting water from the area. According to one source, Houthi forces prevent citizens from other tribes from collecting water in the area claiming that it is within 'Hashemite property'.

properties.Title: A dispute over canal water in Okara, Pakistan kills two and injures three
properties.Description: On October 9, two people are killed and three are injured when armed men fire bullets at a family in Okara, Pakistan, allegedly over a canal water dispute.

properties.Title: A man is killed in a clash over irrigation water in the Punjab, Pakistan
properties.Description: On October 8, a man was shot dead during a clash between two groups over irrigation water at Gagar village, Punjab, Pakistan.

properties.Title: Civilians fetching water are killed by Islamic militants in Mozambique
properties.Description: On October 22, Islamist militia attack and kill several civilians fetching water in Lumumua, Mozambique.

properties.Title: Injuries are reported in a clash between farmers and miners over the Arque River in Bolivia
properties.Description: On October 23, in Capinota, Cochabamba, Bolivia farmers and mining workers from Sicaya clash for control of Arque river and a watershed. Drought and a dispute over water for mining are the main issues. Three people were injured in the clash.

properties.Title: Israeli forces destroy a waterdistribution network near Hebron in the West Bank
properties.Description: On October 25, Israeli military forces bulldoze a water distribution network and a road that links Palestinian villages with Musafer Yuta, Hebron, West Bank.

properties.Title: One death is reported in a dispute over the control of a local water body in Bangladesh
properties.Description: On October 3, armed assailant(s) shoot and kill a person at Digdair village in Tarail Upazila (Kishoreganj), over the control of a local water body.

properties.Title: Saudi-led air raids target the Rural Electricity and Water Authority in Aththaorah district, Yemen
properties.Description: On October 21, Saudi-led coalition air raids targeted Aththaorah district, Yemen, striking the Rural Electricity and Water Authority. The Saudi-led coalition claims to have struck Houthi sites used for launching drones and ballistic missiles in Sanaa.

properties.Title: Report that Ethiopian National Defense Forces conducted an airstrike against the Tekeze Dam
properties.Description: On November 30, the Ethiopian National Defense Force conduct an airstrike in the Tekeze area targeting Tigray People's Liberation Front forces, in Asgede, Tigray, Ethiopia. The TPLF claim the government attacked Tekeze dam; the government deny the accusation and warn members of TPLF not to release the water from the dam. Casualties due to the airstrike are unknown.

properties.Title: A demonstration in France over the construction of irrigation tanks leads to the destruction of water infrastructure and a clash
properties.Description: On November 6, demonstrators gathered in Cramchaban, France to protest the construction of 19 irrigation tanks in the area. They damage water pumps, burning equipment, and empty a water reservoir. Police respond with tear gas.

properties.Title: A Palestinian-owned water spring near Hebron is sealed by Israeli military forces
properties.Description: On November 4, Israeli military forces seal a Palestinian-owned water spring east of Bayt Ummar, Hebron, West Bank, to prevent farmers from using it.

properties.Title: A water well is damaged after Houthi forces target civilian farms in Al Hudaydah, Yemen
properties.Description: On November 4, Houthi forces target civilian farms in At Tuhayat district (Al Hudaydah), Yemen, with mortars damaging a water well. Casualties are unknown.

properties.Title: Farmers rally in Isfahan, Iran over water shortages; police use tear gas and rubber bullets, dozens injured, 60+ arrested
properties.Description: On November 26, farmers and supporters rally at the dried Zayandehrud riverbed and throughout the streets of Isfahan, Iran demanding the government address water shortages. Police attacked the demonstrators with tear gas, rubber bullets, and batons, and make between 60-214 arrests; the internet is shut down, and the city is put under security lockdown. Dozens of demonstrators are injured.

properties.Title: Protests in Araucania and Biobio, Chile over water rights and hydro plant spark clashes, equipment destruction, and arrests
properties.Description: In November 2021, demonstrations in the Araucania and Biobio regions of Chile over water rights, a local hydroelectric power plant, and other water concerns lead to conflicts with local police, destruction of equipment from the Rural Drinking Water Project, shootings, and arrests.

properties.Title: Israeli forces destroy a water tank in Al Khadir, the West Bank
properties.Description: On November 1, 2021, Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian water tank in Al Khadir (Bethlehem, West Bank) for not having a building permit.

properties.Title: Israeli forces destroy Palestinian agricultural facilities, including water tanks, near the Maskiyot settlement, West Bank
properties.Description: On November 1, 2021, Israeli military forces demolish Palestinian-owned agricultural facilities  including sheds, water tanks, and solar cells in Khirbat Al Maytah west of Maskiyot Israeli settlement, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian water well in Nilin; a protest results in multiple injuries
properties.Description: On November 29, 2021, Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian-owned water well in Nilin (Ramallah and Al Bireh), West Bank used for agricultural purposes. A protest against the destruction results in multiple injuries from rubber bullets and tear gas.

properties.Title: Israeli forces dismantle and destroy residential tents and water tanks of Palestinian farmers in Umm al Jamal, West Bank
properties.Description: On November 7, Israeli military forces dismantle and destroy residential tents and sheds, including three water tanks, belonging to Palestinian farmers in Umm al Jamal, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces raze a Palestinian irrigation system near Khirbat Atuf, West Bank
properties.Description: On November 28, 2021, Israeli military forces raze a Palestinian water network (four kilometers long) used for agricultural purposes in Khirbat Atuf, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers destroy agricultural equipment, including water tanks in Shufah, West Bank
properties.Description: On November 21, settlers from Avnei Hefetz break into a Palestinian-owned agricultural warehouse in Shufah village, West Bank, and destroy agricultural materials and water tanks.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers from the Yitzhar settlement damage a Palestinian-owned water well
properties.Description: On November 6, Israeli settlers from the Yitzhar settlement damage a Palestinian-owned water well in the Nabeaa Sufli area, West Bank.

properties.Title: A clash over access to water between different ethnic groups in Cameroon leads to at least 22 deaths and hundreds of refugees
properties.Description: On December 9, armed men from the Musgum and Massa ethnic groups clashed with Arab Choas in Kousseri town (Logone-et-Chari, Extreme-Nord), Cameroon over the ownership of a water spot. At least 22 people were killed over three days; hundreds of people are displaced to N'Djamena in Chad.

properties.Title: A man who tries to rob a water pump in Mexico is beaten by local residents
properties.Description: On December 10, in Oaxaca de Juarez, Mexico a man is undressed, beaten, and tied up after he tries to rob a water pump.

properties.Title: A protest in Trelew, Argentina over environmental and water concerns of a mining project turns violent
properties.Description: In December 2021 in Trelew (Chubut), Argentina, a group protests the approval of the mining project in Chubut's province, demand the law's repeal and improved protection of water. Multiple government buildings are set on fire; police respond with tear gas and rubber bullets; more than 30 injuries are reported.

properties.Title: An attack on a water tanker delivering water to pastoralists affected by drought in Somalia kills four
properties.Description: On December 2, an unidentified group attacks a water tanker delivering water to pastoralists affected by drought near Buulo Xaaji village, Lower Juba, Somalia. Four people are killed.

properties.Title: Azerbaijan and Armenian forces clash over water
properties.Description: On December 8, 2021, the Ministry of Defense of Armenia report that military forces of Azerbaijan open fire on a car belonging to the military of Armenia transporting water in the South-Western part of the Armenia-Azerbaijan border.

properties.Title: Israeli military cut down Palestinian olive trees and destroy a water well in Birin, West Bank
properties.Description: On December 14, 2021, Israeli military forces raze 120 olive trees belonging to Palestinians in Birin, West Bank and destroy a water well.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces confiscate West Bank Palestinian agricultural equipment, including five water tanks
properties.Description: On 1 December 2021, Israeli military forces confiscate Palestinian-owned agricultural equipment in Khirbet ar Ras al Ahmar, West Bank, including tractors, vehicles, and five water tanks.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian-owned house and a water well near Hebron
properties.Description: On December 7, 2021, Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian-owned house and a water well in Abu Khashabeh, Hebron.

properties.Title: Militants sabotage electric lines near Ouorou, Burkina Faso, disrupting water access
properties.Description: In December militants sabotage the electric lines and destroy a water pump near Ouorou (Toeni, Sourou), Burkina Faso cutting off the water supply to the area.

properties.Title: Militias in Somalia kill two in a dispute over water and grazing land
properties.Description: On December 20, militias fighting over water and grazing land kill two from Hawadle clan near Banyaaley village, Hiraan, Somalia.

properties.Title: Multiple deaths and population displacements reported in clash between clans in Basuke, Marsabit, Kenya
properties.Description: Around December 25, Degodia and Borana ethnic militias engage in an armed clash over scarce water and pasture at Basuke, in Marsabit county, Kenya. 11 people are killed and over 300 families are displaced by the conflict into Basir town in Wajir.

properties.Title: Residents in Kerman, Iran clash with police over wells drilled on their lands to transfer water to other regions
properties.Description: On December 25, residents demonstrate in Shamsabad village (Kerman), Iran in protest over wells being drilled on their lands to transfer water elsewhere. Police clash with the protesters.

properties.Title: Saudi-led air raids target the Water and Rural Corporation in Yemen
properties.Description: On December 9, Saudi-led coalition air raids again target the Water and Rural Corporation in Aththaorah district, Yemen, damaging civilian property. Casualties are unknown.

properties.Title: The water supply for a factory is destroyed by opponents of the government regime in Myanmar
properties.Description: On December 11, in Myaing township, Magway, Myanmar, members of the People's Revolutionary Army burn down and destroy water supply for a local factory. Locals said the military controlled the factory and soldiers were working there.

properties.Title: Unknown assailants attack an Iraqi security force during release of irrigation water from the Sabla River
properties.Description: On December 6, unknown militants attack a security force in Rifai district of Thi Qar province, Iraq. The attack takes place while the Iraqi force is providing security during the release of irrigation water from the Sabla river.

properties.Title: Clashes occur among herders, farmers, and fishers, northern Cameroon
properties.Description: Clashes among herders, farmers, and fishers break out over water and land in the village of Ouloumsa, Logone-Birni town, Far North, Cameroon.

properties.Title: Violence breaks out again between Choa Arab ranchers and Musgum fishers and farmers over access to water in northern Cameroon
properties.Description: Clashes break out in northern Cameroon between Choa Arab and Musgum groups following a dispute over dwindling water resources in the Logone River, a tributary to Lake Chad. The violence then spread to neighboring villages, leaving 10 villages burned to the ground.

properties.Title: Violent protest in Argentina in a demonstration against a mining project and threats to water resources
properties.Description: In a violent protest in Andalgala, Catamarca, Argentina, against the MARA mining project and threats to water resources, the Agua Rica company offices are set on fire.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al 2010 ID. ND  ARG9974

properties.Title: Residents of Zabol, Iran attack the trucks of Afghan traders during a demonstration to demand more water from the Helmand River
properties.Description: In growing tensions over the shared Helmand River, residents of Zabol, Iran attack the trucks of Afghan traders near the border during a demonstration to demand more water from the Helmand River.
properties.Sources: Faizee and Schmeier 2023; Radio Free Europe 2023

properties.Title: Conflict over mining and water pollution in Guyana has led to threats and violence against local villagers
properties.Description: Threats and violence are reported in conflicts between villagers and miners over illegal gold mining in indigenous communities in Guyana that is contaminating local waters.

properties.Title: Injuries are reported in a demonstration in South Africa over water shortages
properties.Description: Residents of several farms and townships in KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa, set tires on fire and block the entrances to Umzinto in a demonstration against water shortages in the region. During the demonstration, a private security officer opens fire and injures three people.

properties.Title: One person is killed in India in fight about access to water from a community tap
properties.Description: A 45-year-old woman in south Dehli, India is stabbed to death following a fight about filling water from a community tap.

properties.Title: A water tanker is attacked by Al Shabaab militants in Somalia killing ten and injuring 15
properties.Description: On January 1, Al Shabaab militants detonate a remote-controlled IED, targeting an Ethiopia forces water tanker near Feerfeer village, Somalia. Al Shabaab claims ten soldiers are killed and 15 others injured.

properties.Title: Israeli military demolish Palestinian agricultural facilities in the West Bank, Palestine, including a water tank
properties.Description: On January 4, 2022, Israeli military forces demolish Palestinian-owned agricultural facilities in Ibziq, West Bank, including a water tank.

properties.Title: Militants attack villages in Mali, killing four and destroying water infrastructure
properties.Description: On January 3, ISWAP (Greater Sahara) militants attack the villages of Labodji, Doreye, and Oussadia, in Gao, Mali. Four people are killed, houses burned, and a water tank is destroyed.

properties.Title: Russian warplanes attack the water station near Idlib, Syria damaging the water system and injuring at least one
properties.Description: On January 2, Russian warplanes conduct airstrikes on the vicinity of Sijer - Bqesemtoh town water station near Idlib, Syria, damaging the pipes and rendering the station inoperable. At least one worker at the water station is injured.

properties.Title: In January 2022, the Taliban cut flows of water to Iran and the Hamoun wetlands from the Helmand River
properties.Description: in January 2022, the Taliban divert the Helmand River south, cutting the flow of water to Iran and the Hamoun wetlands.

properties.Title: A Colombian water and sewerage company is bombed
properties.Description: In Saravena, Arauca, Colombia, members of the FARC group bomb the local water, sewage, and sanitation communal company of the municipality.

properties.Title: Palestinian property, including a water well, is destroyed by the Israeli military to build a road
properties.Description: Israeli military forces destroy Palestinian-owned properties in Battir, Bethlehem, West Bank, uprooting 370 olive trees, demolishing a retaining wall and water well, and razing Palestinian lands to build a road.

properties.Title: Saudi-led forces attack water reservoirs in Sadah, Yemen
properties.Description: The Saudi-led coalition air force attacks water reservoirs in Saadah district, Sadah, Yemen.

properties.Title: Violence over water in Nigeria between Fulani pastoralists and Aworo farmers lead to three deaths
properties.Description: Aworo community members kill three Fulani pastoralists in Ogun, Nigeria. The pastoralists were accused of destroying farms and water resources.

properties.Title: Palestinian property, including water wells, are destroyed by the Israeli military
properties.Description: Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian-owned house and two water wells in Mosafer Yuta, Hebron, West Bank, for the benefit of settlement projects.

properties.Title: Cameroon separatists ambush a convoy of water trucks, killing one soldier
properties.Description: Ambazonian separatists ambush a military convoy with explosive and engage in a firefight with the soldiers in Cameroon, killing one soldier and destroying two trucks from the national water production company, Source du Pays.

properties.Title: Saudi-led forces attack water infrastructure in Yemen
properties.Description: Saudi-led coalition air forces attack water infrastructure in Tamrah, Yemen, destroying it and interrupting water delivery services.

properties.Title: A violent demonstration over the lack of access to drinking water in Beni Ayat, Morocco leads to injuries
properties.Description: A group of demonstrators attack and injure journalists covering a protest in Morocco over access to drinking water.

properties.Title: Israeli military destroy water supply line in the West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli military forces raze Palestinian-owned lands and destroy a water supply line in Kafr Qaddum on the West Bank.

properties.Title: Violence over access to water in a canal in Bangladesh leads to one death and several injuries
properties.Description: One person is killed and at least 7 injured in a clash between two groups at the Madhusudanpur canal, Bangladesh, in a dispute over water sharing.

properties.Title: Water station in Syria is attacked
properties.Description: An unidentified armed group targets the water station of Thiban with heavy machine guns and rockets in Deir ez Zor governorate, Syria, causing extensive damage.

properties.Title: A water-supply facility is destroyed during a protest in India over access to healthcare for prisoners
properties.Description: CPI-Maoist cadres attack construction workers and destroy a water supply transformer in Giridih city, India during a call for healthcare facilities for prisoners.

properties.Title: A water-supply station in Syria is attacked by Turkey
properties.Description: Turkish forces shell the water station of Al Hishah in Ar Raqqa governorate, Syria, rendering it inoperable.

properties.Title: Turkish forces fire rockets at the water station in Fatsa, Syria
properties.Description: Turkish forces fire rockets at the water station in Fatsa, Ar Raqqa, Syria.

properties.Title: Armed rebel group in Indonesia damages water-supply system for a military post
properties.Description: The TPNPB, the armed wing of the Free Papua Organization, damage the water pipes of the Indonesian military forces post in Jenggernok, Papua province, Indonesia.

properties.Title: Herder-pastoralist violence over access to water in Kenya kills one and injures others
properties.Description: Suspected Pokot pastoralist militias attack herders watering animals at the Arabal river, Baringo county, Kenya. One person is killed; two others injured, and close to 40 cattle stolen.

properties.Title: Russian troops destroy Ukrainian dam that blocked water to Crimea
properties.Description: In late February, Russian troops invading Ukraine destroy a concrete dam built by Ukraine in 2014 to cut off water to Crimea after Russia annexed that region. The canal was originally built to provide water from the Dnipro River to Ukraine's Kherson region and Crimea.

properties.Title: Egyptian hackers attack the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam in an effort to trigger downstream flooding and power failures
properties.Description: The Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam on the Blue Nile is attacked in February 2022 by Egyptian hackers with the motto “Black Pyramid War” in an effort to trigger downstream flooding and power failures.

properties.Title: Mexican farmer threatened and then murdered for his connection to a hydroelectric project development
properties.Description: A farmer, Francisco Vázaquz Domínguez, is threatened and then murdered in Ayala, Morelos, Mexico, for his role as a land owner and connection to Asociación de Usarios del Río Cuautla (ASURCO). ASURCO is a water user association that had been involved in decisions related to hydroelectric power and other water projects. He was threatened by the president of the association before being murdered.

properties.Title: The Pechenihy Dam on the Siverskyi Donets River is damaged during the Russia-Ukraine war
properties.Description: Two gates and the roadway over the Pechenihy Dam in Pechenihy on the Siverskyi Donets River (the main tributary of the Don River) in the Kharkiv region are blown up by the Armed Forces of Ukraine in order to widen the Siverskyi Donets River, and make it difficult for the Russians to encircle the city of Kharkiv (February 2022).

properties.Title: Israeli military forces and settlers destroy Palestinian water wells near Hebron and Nablus in the West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli military forces demolish and raze four Palestinian-owned water wells near in Khallet ed Dabaa, Hebron, West Bank on February 2nd, in order to construct settlement projects in the area. Two days later, Israeli settlers destroy a water well in Qaryut village, Nablus, West Bank, and pollute the well with chemicals.

properties.Title: An attempt to cut off the power supply of a water well in Morelos, Mexico, is met by violence
properties.Description: Residents in Chamilpa, Morelos, Mexico, seize a van from the Federal Electricity Commission after employees try to cut off the power supply of their water well. The residents also held the workers from the state-owned company and threatened to set the unit on fire.

properties.Title: Militants in Mali attack a village, damaging the water tower
properties.Description: Militants in Mali attack the village of Tadjalalt, Ansongo, Gao. The militants loot and burned the medical dispensary and damage the water tower. The village is part of the Tuareg Kel Essouk community.

properties.Title: Two families in Syria clash over water for agricultural lands
properties.Description: Clashes take place between two armed families over the watering of agricultural lands in Al-Hessan village in Deir ez Zor, Syria. One injury is reported.

properties.Title: Women protesting against water prices in Comoros clash with police
properties.Description: Women demonstrating against the payment of water bills from the National Company for the Exploitation and Distribution of Water in Comoros clash with police, who respond with tear gas. At least 6 women and two children are injured.

properties.Title: The Israeli military demolish a Palestinian water tank used for irrigation in the West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian-owned water tank used for agricultural purposes in Furush Bayt Dajan, Nablus, West Bank, to maintain the Israeli control over water resources in the area.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces destroy Palestinian water systems and land near Salfit and Tubas in the West Bank
properties.Description: On 10 February 2022, Israeli military forces raze Palestinian-owned lands in Sarta village, Salfit, West Bank, to construct a water supply pipeline for a nearby settlement. They also destroy retaining walls and uproot more than 160 trees. That same day Israeli military vehicles and tanks destroy a Palestinian-owned water network, agricultural roads, and crops during their military exercise in Jord

properties.Title: Militants in Burkina Faso destroy water systems
properties.Description: Presumed Jama'at Nusrat al Islam wal Muslimin militants set off explosives and destroy a water pump and two water towers in the village of Hamkane, Arbinda, Soum, Burkina Faso.

properties.Title: Israeli forces demolish a Palestinian-owned water well and olive trees near Hebron in the West Bank
properties.Description: On 13 February 2022, Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian-owned water well, and uproot olive trees in Bayt Ummar, Hebron, West Bank, under the pretext that they were located near Israeli settlements.

properties.Title: A protest over bottled water company exploitation of groundwater is broken up by Mexican police and national guard
properties.Description: State police forces and national guard disperse protesters in Cholula de Rivadavia, Puebla, Mexico, who had been occupying the facilities of the Bonafont water bottling company since March 22, 2021. The protesters denounced the overexploitation of the aqifers, leaving neighboring towns without water.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces demolish a water well and house in Khallat al Mayyah in the West Bank to forcibly evict Palestinians
