properties.Description: In late February 2023, water supplies in the Druzhkivka, Mykolaiv, and Bashtanka districts are only available irregularly and are regularly cut off by direct damage, attacks on electricity systems, and inability to repair infrastructure damaged by the war.

properties.Title: Iranian schoolgirls targeted with poison at school water systems in multiple incidents
properties.Description: Iranian schoolgirls are targeted in spate of poisoning attacks on school water systems. In Qom on February 14, more than 100 students from 13 schools were taken to hospitals after what the state-affiliated Tasnim news agency described as “serial poisonings.” Poisonings in other cities were also reported.

properties.Title: Protest over water supply issues in Hubballi, India, turns violent as councillors of two parties clash
properties.Description: Indian National Congress council members protest inside Hubballi Dharwad Municipal Corporation in Hubballi city, Hubli, Dharwad, Karnataka, India, against water supply issues, but violence ensues as members of the Bharatiya Janata Party physically attack them.

properties.Title: In Colombia, residents block highway over water service outages, and a confrontation with police results injuries
properties.Description: In San Juan Nepomuceno, Bolivar, Colombia, residents block the Western Highway in response to a ten-day water service outage. Colombian National Police riot control forces use tear gas to disperse protesters, and police officers fire their guns, resulting in injuries to four demonstrators.

properties.Title: Unidentified militia invade and vandalize Kaakong Primary School, including water tank, in Kibish, Turkana, Kenya
properties.Description: A group of unidentified militia carry out acts of vandalism on buildings and equipment, including the water tank, at Kaakong Primary School in Kibish, Turkana, Kenya.

properties.Title: Military forces set fire to betel nut farm, destroy water pumps, and property in Ka De Village, Myanmar
properties.Description: Military forces burn a betel nut farm, two tents, two water pumps, a tractor, and other property in Ka De village, Palaw township, Myeik district, Tanintharyi region, Myanmar.

properties.Title: Drone attack in Myanmar kills one soldier and injures another during water tank transport
properties.Description: A drone attack is carried out by the Myaing People's Defense Force and an unknown local defense force on military forces transporting a water tank in Hpa Lan Kaing village, Pauk township, Magway region, Myanmar. The attack results in the death of one soldier and injuries to another.

properties.Title: Protesters in Liben, Amhara, Ethiopia stage road blockade demanding water and electricity access. Security forces fire live bull
properties.Description: For several consecutive days, young individuals in Liben, West Gojam, Amhara, Ethiopia, block roads with burning tires, stones, and wood, halting vehicles to protest the lack of drinking water supplies and electricity for more than six months. Security forces fire live bullets, killing one and injuring three others.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al 2010 ID. ETH9836, ETH9837, ETH9838

properties.Title: Deadly three-day attack by ethnic militia over access to water in Mekenisa Kebele, Oromia, Ethiopia
properties.Description: Ethnic Somali militias in Mekenisa kebele, East Hararge, Oromia, Ethiopia, attacks ethnic Oromo civilians for three days blocking their access to drinking water, resulting in four deaths and nine injuries.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al 2010 ID. ETH9828, ETH9829

properties.Title: Israeli settlers attempt to seize the Ayn Bliebel spring from Palestinian farmers, Tubas, West Bank
properties.Description: In an attempt to seize the spring that is used by Palestinian farmers, Israeli settlers carry out construction activities at the water spring of Ayn Bliebel east of Ayn al Bayda, Tubas, West Bank.

properties.Title: The Balochistan Liberation Army attacks a water transport tractor at Hazara dam construction area in Harnai district, Balochista
properties.Description: The Balochistan Liberation Army orchestrates an improvised explosive device attack on a tractor transporting water to workers at the Hazara dam construction site in Harnai district, Balochistan, Pakistan. The driver is fatally wounded, and three more individuals suffer injuries as a result of the incident.

properties.Title: Water drainage conflict sparks clash between families, leaving five injured in Jammu and Kashmir, India
properties.Description: A confrontation erupts between two families in the Khalis Kullian area of Miran Sahib block, Jammu district, Jammu and Kashmir, India, due to a dispute over water drainage. The clash leaves five individuals injured.

properties.Title: Violent ethnic clashes claim seven lives in Tulu Guled, Ethiopia, over grazing land and access to water
properties.Description: Violent ethnic clashes erupt in Tulu Guled, Ethiopia, resulting in seven fatalities and numerous injuries. The conflict is rooted in disputes over grazing land and water resources. In response, the Oromia regional government initiates a restructuring of the contested land, dividing it into two administrative district to accommodate the Oromo and Somali pastoralist communities.

properties.Title: Water collection dispute triggers clash in Baglung Municipality, Nepal, and residents attempt to destroy water tank
properties.Description: A water collection dispute sparks a clash between hundreds of locals from two districts in Baglung municipality, Baglung district, Nepal. Residents of Ratnechaur, from Beni municipality of Myagdi district, try to destroy Baglung's water tank.

properties.Title: Coordinated attacks target water supply company in Natal, Brazil, with Sindicato do Crime suspected
properties.Description: Unidentified individuals in Natal, Rio Grande do Norte, Brazil target a local water supply company, setting fire to its facilities and a vehicle in the Cidade da Esperanca neighborhood. These coordinated attacks occur across the state for ten days, with the Sindicato do Crime organization believed to be responsible.

properties.Title: Protesters in Azerbaijan block Baku highway demanding irrigation water, resulting in injuries and arrests
properties.Description: Villagers from Nabatkend and Orta Mugan, Azerbaijan, stage a protest, blocking the highway to Baku, demanding irrigation water to address the significant crop damage. Police use rubber bullets, causing two protesters to be hospitalized, and arrest around 10 demonstrators.

properties.Title: Houthi forces assault workers and shut down a water factory in Yemen
properties.Description: Houthi forces assault workers at the Shamlan Mineral Water Factory in Shamlan, Hamdan, Sanaa, Yemen. They demolish parts of the factory, close it, and block the entry and exit for employees and trucks. The attack is allegedly due to the factory owners' refusal to share revenues with a Houthi figure.

properties.Title: Assault on Water Resources Department staff in Diyala Province, Iraq
properties.Description: Two individuals attack Water Resources Department staff in Diyala province, Iraq, while they are dismantling unauthorized structures along waterways. Law enforcement apprehends the attackers, and casualties remain uncertain.

properties.Title: Rioters clash with police in Al Islah district, Thi Qar, Iraq, over water scarcity
properties.Description: Clashes erupt between rioters and police in Al Islah district, Thi Qar, Iraq, triggered by water scarcity. Police reinforcements are deployed and use live ammunition to disperse the riot. Tires are set on fire by the rioters. Casualties unknown.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al 2010 ID. IRQ45205; Crisis 24 2023

properties.Title: Violent clash over water resources in Muzabd, North Kordofan, Sudan claims two lives and injures seven
properties.Description: Two rival ethnic groups engage in a violent confrontation in Muzabd, Gharb Bara, North Kordofan, Sudan, due to a conflict over access to water resources. The clash results in two deaths, including an off-duty police officer, and leaves seven individuals injured.

properties.Title: Tiquipaya residents protest against water-treatment plant relocation in Bolivia
properties.Description: Police clash with residents of Tiquipaya, Cochabamba, Bolivia, protesting the relocation of a water-treatment plant.

properties.Title: A building of the water-supply company in Donetsk, Ukraine is attacked
properties.Description: A Russian report says Ukrainian Armed Forces strike the building of the water-supply company in the Petrovsky district of Donetsk (March 22, 2023).

properties.Title: Fatal confrontation over the distribution of water resources claims life near New Manik Wah in Tando Bago, Pakistan
properties.Description: Confrontation over the fair distribution of water resources between Athela and Kaloi factions near New Manik Wah in Tando Bago, Sindh, Pakistan results in the death of an individual.

properties.Title: Clashes erupt on World Water Day march demanding improved water services in Merida, Yucatan, Mexico
properties.Description: On World Water Day, March 23, 2023, around 100 people march in Merida, Yucatan, to demand improved water services. The demonstrators clash with state police, resulting in one police officer being injured. Authorities arrest four individuals.

properties.Title: Water pipelines in Port Sudan are attacked
properties.Description: A water crisis worsens in Port Sudan, Sudan after attacks on the city's water pipelines by unknown persons. The neighborhoods of Shargi, Hadal, Salbouna, and Abu Hashish are worst affected.

properties.Title: Many injuries reported at massive protests in Sainte-Soline, France to stop construction of water reservoirs
properties.Description: In late March 2023, as many as 200 protesters and 50 police officers sustain injuries in a battle in Sainte-Soline, France to stop the construction of giant water "basins" to irrigate crops. Protesters throw projectiles including improvised explosives; police respond with tear gas, water cannons, and rubber bullets.
properties.Sources: France 24 2023a; Gounden and Byers 2023; Raleigh et al 2010 ID. FRA24671

properties.Title: Migrants' protest for water in Ciudad Juarez, Mexico, detention center ends in at least 39 fatalities
properties.Description: Migrants detained in Ciudad Juarez, Chihuahua, Mexico, set fire to mattresses in protest of their deportation, poor living conditions, and lack of drinking water. The fire escalates, resulting in the deaths of at least 39 migrants and injuring 29 others.

properties.Title: Clash over water pollution in India, leaves over 30 injured as villagers confront each other and police intervene with tear gas
properties.Description: Villagers from Konsakhul and Leilon, India, clash over water pollution concerns. The clash escalates into a confrontation, with both sides throwing stones. State police intervene, using tear gas to restore order. Over 30 people sustain injuries in the incident.

properties.Title: Suspected militants from Islamic State Sahel vandalize water pump station, causing water scarcity in Dori, Burkina Faso
properties.Description: Suspected militants from Islamic State Sahel vandalize the water pump station located at the dam in Yakouta, Dori, Burkina Faso, resulting in a water scarcity affecting the town of Dori.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers contaminate Palestinian water resources in Hebron City, West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli settlers pollute water resources in Hebron City, West Bank, by spraying chlorine into the water used by Palestinians.

properties.Title: Israeli systems that monitor irrigation and wastewater treatment are hit by a cyberattack, affecting water for farms
properties.Description: In April 2023, Israeli systems that monitor irrigation and wastewater treatment in the Hula Valley, the Jordan Valley, and the Galil Sewage Corporation’s are hit by a cyberattack, affecting water for at least ten farms.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers destroy agricultural resources, pollute water well, and damage trees on Palestinian land in Shufah, West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli settlers from Avnei Hefetz settlement destroy agricultural resources and pollute a water well on a Palestinian-owned land in Shufah, Tulkarm, West Bank. Settlers also destroy fig, grape, and olive trees.

properties.Title: Farmers stealing water from a canal assault Irrigation Department Team in Hansi City, Haryana, India
properties.Description: Farmers assault an Irrigation Department Team at Mujadpur village in Hansi City, Hisar, Haryana, India, when the team discovers that the farmers are stealing water from the canal and try to stop them.

properties.Title: Clash over water rotation and access injures one person in Sindh, Pakistan
properties.Description: A clash takes place between two groups of the Chachar community, injuring one person in Muhammad Hashim Chhachhar village, Sindh, Pakistan, over the rotation of water.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers, attack homes in Kifil Haris, West Bank, disrupting water supply; clashes injure one, arrest made
properties.Description: Israeli settlers, protected by Israeli forces, attack Palestinian homes and properties in Kifil Haris, Salfit, West Bank, causing property damage and disrupting the water supply. Palestinian residents retaliate with stone-throwing, and Israeli forces respond with stun grenades and tear gas, injuring a woman. A Palestinian is also arrested.

properties.Title: Residents in Taizz, Yemen, protest against military over a water well controversy, resulting in four injuries from an armed atta
properties.Description: Residents protest in Al Nashmah, Al Maafer, Taizz, Yemen, against former military police for digging water wells near drinking water sources. Armed men fire at protesters, injuring four.

properties.Title: Ongoing major armed clashes in Sudan damages water plant and other water infrastructure and kills more than 220 people
properties.Description: Armed clashes between Sudan Armed Forces and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces with heavy and medium artillery and anti-aircraft shells in Bahri, Khartoum, Sudan, damages the Bahri water plant and other water infrastructure. At least 220 people, including civilians and soldiers, are killed.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al 2010 ID. SUD19739, SUD19712; Human Rights Watch 2023

properties.Title: In Myanmar, military claims Kachin Independence Army and People's Defence Force destroyed vehicles, water tanks, and windows
properties.Description: In Mabein township, Mongmit district, Myanmar, the military alleges that the combined force of the Kachin Independence Army and local People's Defence Force destroy building windows, a motorcycle, a fire truck, a water truck, doors, and water tanks.

properties.Title: Ede minority protest in Cu Kuin, Vietnam over contaminated water; police response leaves seven injured, twelve arrested
properties.Description: Ede ethnic minority citizens stage a protest in Ea Bhok, Cu Kuin, Vietnam, against failures of the water-sewage system, alleging contamination of natural water sources in Dak Lak's Cu Kuin district. Police beat the protesters, resulting in at least seven injuries, and twelve protesters are arrested.

properties.Title: Israeli forces clash with Palestinian protesters in Qarawat Bani Hassan, Salfit, West Bank, during a raid to seize a water tank
properties.Description: Israeli forces clash with Palestinian protesters in Qarawat Bani Hassan, Salfit, West Bank, during a raid to seize a water tank vehicle. Rubber-coated bullets and tear gas canisters are used by the Israeli forces. Three protesters sustain bullet injuries.

properties.Title: Residents in Dominican Republic hold a demonstration demanding water supply, higher wages, and a reduction in the cost of living
properties.Description: Residents in San Francisco de Macoris, Duarte, Dominican Republic, demand water supply, higher wages, and lower living costs in a demonstration with burning tires and road blockades. Police intervene with firearms, injuring one protester.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers in Bethlehem, West Bank, destroy water-supply network and vandalize crops in Husan village
properties.Description: Israeli settlers in Husan village, Bethlehem, West Bank, destroy a water-supply network belonging to Palestinian farmers and uproot trees and vandalize crops.

properties.Title: Local resident fatally shoots Houthi supervisor in Al Arim village over ownership of a disputed water well
properties.Description: A local resident shoots and kills a Houthi supervisor in Al Arim village in Uzlat Bani Mahalhal region, Sanaa, Yemen, after repeated attempts to seize a water well.

properties.Title: An unidentified drone attacks a water tower in Vyazovoye, Russia
properties.Description: An unidentified drone attacks a water tower in Vyazovoye, Russia. No casualties occur.

properties.Title: Residents clash in Sullana, Peru, over access to water amid prolonged service disruption
properties.Description: Residents in Sullana, Piura, Peru, clash outside the EPS Grau water company facilities trying to access potable water due to prolonged service disruption.

properties.Title: Armed bandits attack Olng'arua primary school in Isiolo, Kenya, setting fires, vandalizing property, and stealing a water pump
properties.Description: Armed bandits from neighboring villages attack Olng'arua primary school in Leparua, Isiolo, Kenya, setting vehicles on fire, vandalizing classes and a residential building, and damaging water tanks and a biogas digester. They also steal a motorcycle and a water pump.

properties.Title: Al-Qaeda militants in Abyan target military vehicles with IEDs, including a water truck, in Imbaqirah, Mudiyah
properties.Description: Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula militants target military vehicles of 'mercenaries' in Imbaqirah, Mudiyah, Abyan, with three improvised explosive devices, including a water truck.

properties.Title: Darfur militia burns 17 villages, seizes water sources, and displaces civilians in Bielel, South Darfur, Sudan
properties.Description: Militants, identified as the Darfur communal militia, burn 17 villages in Bielel, South Darfur, Sudan, and seize the local water resource. Civilians are displaced from these 17 villages and an additional 12 neighboring villages to refugee camps.

properties.Title: Clash over water supply in South Africa, leads to shootings and injuries
properties.Description: A group of protestors demanding water supply clash with local authorities in Charlestown, Newcastle, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa. Three people are shot and hospitalized.

properties.Title: Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf activists clash with police during demonstrations in Karachi, Pakistan, setting fire to water tankers
properties.Description: During demonstrations in Karachi - Shah Faisal, Pakistan, a large number of Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf activists throw stones at police personnel and set fire to two water tankers. Police respond with batons and tear gas shells to disperse the demonstrators.

properties.Title: Russian forces shell Kherson region, inflicting damage on civilian infrastructure, water pipelines, and industrial facilities
properties.Description: Russian forces shell Kherson region, damaging civilian infrastructure, water pipelines, and an industrial enterprise in 10 separate attacks. One civilian is killed and one is injured.

properties.Title: Israeli forces damage and destroy several water systems in an agricultural area in the West Bank
properties.Description: In a week-long series of attacks in May 2023, Israeli military forces damage and destroy water pipes, water tanks, water-distribution systems, and wells along with many crops and other agricultural equipment at multiple locations in the West Bank.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al 2010 ID. PSE33608, PSE33586, PSE33585, PSE33643, PSE33891, PSE33912, PSE33262

properties.Title: Militants in Burkina Faso attack a village causing multiple fatalities and destroying a water tower at medical center
properties.Description: Militants from Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin attack volunteer fighters and civilians in Bilguim-Doure village near Ouargaye, Burkina Faso resulting in multiple fatalities and the destruction of a water tower at a medical center.

properties.Title: Residents resist Houthi forces seizing dam for military use; 72 civilians, including 15 children, abducted, villages blockaded
properties.Description: Local residents attempt to resist Houthi forces who are trying to seize a nearby dam to divert water to their military sites. In response, the Houthi forces abduct 72 civilians, including 15 children, and impose a blockade on villages in Shib al Maahirah, Jabal al Mukaydam, and Al Kharabah.

properties.Title: Russia destroys the dam of the Karliv reservoir in Donetsk region of Ukraine
properties.Description: Russia destroys the Karlivka dam of the Karlov reservoir in Donetsk region of Ukraine, northwest of Pervomaisky, May 25, 2023.

properties.Title: One death and one injury reported in water dispute in Sabih village, Yemen
properties.Description: A dispute over a natural water pool that is filled with rainwater leads to one man shooting and killing another man and injuring his brother in Sabih village in Al Hada district, Yemen.

properties.Title: Multiple clashes on the Iran-Afghanistan border over water rights and access; deaths, injuries reported
properties.Description: In late May 2023, violent encounters between Iranian and Afghan forces along the border occur related to a water rights dispute on the Helmand River leading to multiple deaths and injuries. In Sasuli, Sistan, and Baluchestan, Taliban forces enter Iranian territory and seize military equipment. Fighting leaves two Iranian police officers and one Taliban militant dead and several other civilians inj
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al 2010 ID.IRN23337, AFG66609; Gambrell 2023

properties.Title: Two civilians are injured in groundwater pumping dispute in Iraq
properties.Description: An intra-tribal clash over groundwater injures two women in the Shatra district, Thi Qar, Iraq.

properties.Title: Explosive device hits civilians at a water pump in Tin Rhassane, Burkina Faso, killing 21, likely targeting IS Sahel militants
properties.Description: Civilians who regularly fetch water from a water pump in the village of Tin Rhassane, Tin-Akoff, Burkina Faso, are hit by an explosive device, likely targeting IS Sahel militants who regularly come to refuel at this fountain, resulting in the death of 21 men, women, and children.

properties.Title: Russian forces shell Uhroyidy in the Sumy region, Ukraine, damaging a water tower
properties.Description: Shelling by Russian forces in Uhroyidy in the Sumy region, Ukraine, damages a water tower and other civilian structures.

properties.Title: Attacks on water systems in Western Oromia, Ethiopia
properties.Description: In Western Oromia, Ethiopia, an attack and looting in June 2023 damages health posts, a hospital, and other facilities, including a water reservoir, causing a water-supply shortage.

properties.Title: Local terrorist attack on hydroelectric facilities in the United States
properties.Description: A man in Idaho, United States is charged with two counts of destruction of an energy facility after federal prosecutors said he shot a rifle at Hells Canyon Dam and Brownlee Dam electrical systems on the Snake River, damaging both. The dams produce, transmit, store and distribute electricity to Washington, Idaho, and Oregon and the shooting caused over $100,000 in damage.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers vandalize water supply networks in Palestinian Village
properties.Description: Israeli settlers vandalize water-supply networks and steal property belonging to Palestinian farmers in the Al Sahel area of Turmus Ayya village in the West Bank (June 3).

properties.Title: Israeli settlers vandalize water supply networks of Palestinian farmers in the Al Sahel area of the West Bank
properties.Description: A group of Israeli settlers vandalizes water supply networks belonging to Palestinian farmers in the Al Sahel area of Turmus Ayya village, Ramallah and Al Bireh, West Bank.

properties.Title: Prison inmates clash over water in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh
properties.Description: Prison inmates clash over water in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, resulting in two inmates being injured.

properties.Title: Workers at a water station in Sudan are abducted resulting in a water shortage
properties.Description: A group called Rapid Support Forces abducts water station workers from Soba Water Station in Khartoum - Soba West, Sudan, resulting in a water shortage.

properties.Title: Russian forces shell Kharkiv, damaging water pipe
properties.Description: Russian forces shell Kharkiv on June 5, damaging water pipe and buildings.

properties.Title: Russian forces shell Kharkiv, damaging infrastructure and a water pipe
properties.Description: Russian forces again shell Kharkiv, damaging residential buildings, a gas station, water pipe, and cars, with no reported casualties.

properties.Title: The massive Kakhovka Dam on the Dnipro River is destroyed, causing massive upstream and downstream consequences
properties.Description: The massive Kakhovka Dam on the Dnipro River is destroyed on June 6, 2023, presumably by Russian occupying forces, causing massive flooding, more than 50 deaths, and ecological devastation downstream, and cutting off water supply for cities, power plants, and irrigation systems.

properties.Title: Dozens hospitalized after consuming poisoned water in Farah religious school
properties.Description: Nearly 40 female students from a religious school in the Naw Abad area of Qala-i-kah district, Farah, are hospitalized after consuming water contaminated with poison, with the reason and perpetrators of the incident remaining unknown.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers seize water well in Mosafer Yuta, Hebron, and prevent Palestinian access
properties.Description: Israeli settlers seize a water well in Mosafer Yuta, east of Yuta in Hebron, West Bank in early June, preventing Palestinian shepherds from reaching it. Settlers also destroy Palestinian-owned crops.

properties.Title: Baloch separatists set machinery on fire and abduct workers laying down a water pipeline in Pakistan
properties.Description: On 7 June 2023, Baloch separatists set fire to machinery and abduct five workers laying a water pipeline for a private company in Gaza area of Kalat district, Balochistan.

properties.Title: Community in Santa Cruz Pueblo Nuevo blocks highway demanding water solutions to water shortages
properties.Description: In Santa Cruz Pueblo Nuevo, Mexico, residents block the Tenango-Ixtapan de la Sal highway, protesting water shortages caused by alleged theft by a group from San Pedro Tlanixco, prompting state police intervention with tear gas.

properties.Title: Police repress LUCHA protest over water connection fees in Tshikapa, Kasai
properties.Description: Police in Tshikapa, Kasai, repress a LUCHA protest, beat and arrest protesters demanding a reduction in the water connection fee, which increased from 220 to 320 USD.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces demolish a water well in Hizma Village, West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli military forces raze a road and demolish a water well in Hizma village, Al Quds, West Bank, claiming they were constructed in areas controlled by Israel.

properties.Title: Reports that Russia destroys a dam on the Mokri Yali River
properties.Description: A dam on the Mokri Yaly River is blown up by Russian forces in mid-June 2023, causing flooding on both banks. Reports are the dam was destroyed to slow down Ukraine’s counteroffensive.
properties.Sources: Borger and Sabbagh 2023; Brennan 2023; BBC News 2023

properties.Title: Russian missile strike hits bottled water warehouse, killing seven civilians
properties.Description: Russian forces fire cruise missiles at Kryvyi Rih, Dnipropetrovsk, hitting a bottled water warehouse and killing seven civilians.

properties.Title: Workers protest Arazati drinking water plant project in Montevideo
properties.Description: Unionized workers at the State Sanitary Works (OSE) company in Montevideo demonstrate against the Arazati drinking water plant project, alleging it doesn't ensure water supply, is unconstitutional, and privatizes the water service. Demonstrators clash with police, vandalize the OSE building, and throw smoke bombs.

properties.Title: An environmental activist and  defender of water and agroecology is fatally attacked in Mexico
properties.Description: An environmental activist and researcher defending water and agroecology, Alvaro Arvizu Aguiniga, is attacked with an axe at the Center for Sustainability of the Sierra Nevada Incalli Ixcahuicopa (CENTLI) in Tlalmanalco de Velazquez, Mexico, and dies. Assailants also intimidate his wife and collaborators, beating and tying them up. Aquiniga has advocated for a new General Water Law for over a deca
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al. 2010 ID. MEX75608; Scholars at Risk Network 2023

properties.Title: AQAP militants target water tanker with IED in Mudiyah district, Abyan
properties.Description: Al Qaeda in the Arab Peninsula (AQAP) militants target a water tanker belonging to Southern Transitional Council (STC) 13th Saiqa Brigade forces in Mudiyah district, Abyan, with an IED, injuring one STC fighter and damaging the truck.

properties.Title: At least 50 people are injured in a clash about the control and management of water and flooding in Sylhet, Bangladesh
properties.Description: On 14 June, locals from several villages burn tires and stage a demonstration against flooding in the area of Sylhet Sadar upazila (Sylhet), Bangladesh. They clash with locals from another village using indigenous weapons, when the latter opposed the decision to cut a raw canal on the side of the road to allow temporary water drainage. At least 50 people are injured, including police personnel, du

properties.Title: Demonstrators in Johannesburg block roads and loot trucks, demanding water and sanitation
properties.Description: Demonstrators in Johannesburg, Diepkloof, barricade roads with burning tires and loot trucks, demanding water and sanitation, prompting police to fire rubber bullets to disperse the crowd.

properties.Title: Water defender shot and killed by armed men in Guapinol, Colon
properties.Description: In Guapinol, Colon, unidentified armed men shoot and kill a water defender, the brother of another environmental activist killed earlier in the year.

properties.Title: Water dispute in Chupki village leads to violent clash
properties.Description: In Chupki village, Ludhiana, Punjab, three people clash with two others using lathis, crude weapons, and a gun after being confronted about diverting water supply to their fields, resulting in all five individuals sustaining injuries, including a bullet wound.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers loot water tank in a village in the West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli settlers loot water tank in Umm Safa village, Ramallah and Al Bireh, West Bank

properties.Title: Tribesmen clash over control of Marbun water dam in Al Said City
properties.Description: Al Yaslim and Al Bazaal tribesmen clash in Al Said city's market, Shabwah, over control of the Marbun water dam.

properties.Title: Residents in Acahuizotla, Guerrero, close water supply valves to demand infrastructure works
properties.Description: Residents in Acahuizotla, Guerrero close the pumping valves of the water system supplying the capital, demanding authorities start infrastructure works, and shut down the water system until the construction of the second stage of their water system begins.

properties.Title: The Iraqi army destroys Kurdish irrigation equipment and supplies and orders villagers not to cultivate crops
properties.Description: Iraqi army soldiers destroy irrigation equipment belonging to Kurdish farmers in the Balkanah village of Kirkuk. The farmers are not allowed to irrigate the fields and only do so at night to avoid their equipment being confiscated by the army.

properties.Title: Ukraine intercepts a Russian missile attack on Kryvyi Rih dam
properties.Description: On 22 June 2023, Ukrainian military forces intercept Russian air-launched 'Kinzhal' missiles over the Dnipropetrovsk region aimed at the dam near Kryvyi Rih.

properties.Title: VASKI staff and police assault residents and headman over water issue in Ipekyolu, Turkey
properties.Description: Van Metropolitan Municipality Water and Sewage Administration Directorate staff and police officers physically assault the headman and residents of Kecanis neighborhood during a visit to Ipekyolu, Van, to address a water problem.

properties.Title: Clash over water sharing in Kashmore leaves one dead and 14 injured
properties.Description: Two groups of the Jakhrani tribe in Malgazari village, Kashmore district, Kashmor, Sindh, clash over water sharing for agricultural lands, attacking each other with axes, clubs, and bricks, resulting in one death and 14 injuries.

properties.Title: Former police officer fires at villagers protesting water pipe construction in Walasmulla, injuring three
properties.Description: Former police officer fires at villagers protesting against the digging of a well near his house for the construction of a water pipe in Walasmulla, Hambantota, Southern, injuring three.

properties.Title: Violence in Sudan cuts water supplies and electricity to Khartoum
properties.Description: Violence between the regular Sudanese army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) cuts electricity and water supplies to large portions of the city of Khartoum (June 2023).

properties.Title: Presumed JNIM militants kill village chief and destroy water tower in Diabatou, Piela, Gnagna
properties.Description: Presumed JNIM militants kill the Diabatou village chief, demolish a water tower, and order residents to evacuate within 48 hours.

properties.Title: A Peruvian congress member is attacked in a dispute over the construction of a potable water and sewage project
properties.Description: On 26 June in Arequipa, Peru, a congress member is attacked with rocks, bottles, and dirt by residents of Apipa in a dispute over the construction of a potable water and sewage project in the Cerro Colorado district. Police intervene with tear gas.

properties.Title: RSF militants loot generator from water well station in Omdurman
properties.Description: RSF militants loot an electricity generator from the Jaden al-Takamol water well station in Omdurman, Khartoum, and fire shots in the air to disperse residents confronting the looting. No injuries are reported.

properties.Title: Russian forces shell Druzhkivka, damaging residential area and water supply networks
properties.Description: Russian forces shell Druzhkivka, Donetsk, damaging water supply networks along with residential areas, two multi-story buildings, two private houses, and road surfaces; no casualties are reported.

properties.Title: Suspected Kuki militants fire at Meitei farmers collecting irrigation water in Bishnupur, Manipur, India
properties.Description: Suspected Kuki militants fire at Meitei farmers heading to Phubala Maning High Canal in Bishnupur, Manipur, India to collect irrigation water amid ongoing ethnic violence, firing from bunkers between Haotak Khullen and Chingfe Ching villages, with no casualties reported. Water-supply systems are also sabotaged.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al. 2010 ID. IND134427; IND132818

properties.Title: Armed man kills one and injures another over water tank dispute in Adinah Village, Yemen
properties.Description: An armed man kills one man and injures his son in Adinah village, Ad Dali, Yemen over a water tank dispute.

properties.Title: Clashes over land and water disputes at Qurnat as Sawda peak, Lebanon leave three dead
properties.Description: Two armed clashes between Tawk clansmen and Bqaa Safrine militiamen at Qurnat as Sawda, Al Danniyeh, Lebanon result in two deaths. The army intervenes, killing one militiaman and arresting 19 individuals while seizing weapons and ammunition. Clashes in the area have been over land and water disputes.

properties.Title: Two families clash over water distribution in Mahmood Raqi, Afghanistan, wounding six
properties.Description: Two families clash over drought-affected water distribution in Mahmood Raqi, Kapisa, Afghanistan wounding six.

properties.Title: Israeli military operation in Jenin damages main water-supply network
properties.Description: Israeli military forces damage the main water-supply network during an operation in Jenin, including Mukhayyam Jenin refugee camp and Jenin city. Israel's stated intent of the operation is the defusing of explosives and IEDs.

properties.Title: Clashes over water dispute in Ar Raqqa, Syria injure six
properties.Description: Clashes among Fakheth Al 'lyat tribal militiamen in Karama, Ar Raqqa province, Syria over watering agricultural lands injure six people, with no fatalities.

properties.Title: Israeli police demolish Palestinian dwelling and confiscate water tanks in Al Quds
properties.Description: Israeli police demolish a Palestinian-owned dwelling in Wadi Abu Hindi east of Al Quds, West Bank and confiscate four water tanks.

properties.Title: Farmers protest electricity cuts blocking water supply in Viransehir
properties.Description: Around 300 farmers gather on the Sanliurfa-Viransehir road in Viransehir district to protest electricity cuts that prevent them from watering their lands, blocking traffic and burning tires. Gendarmerie and police intervene with tear gas and water cannons, detaining 20 people.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers trespass on Palestinian agricultural lands and contaminate water tank in Jericho
properties.Description: Israeli settlers move their sheep onto Palestinian agricultural lands in Al Muaarajat al Fawqa, west of Jericho city, and contaminate a water tank with an unknown material.

properties.Title: Tribesmen clash over water source in Amran, Yemen leaving two dead and eight injured
properties.Description: Tribesmen from Al Abraq and Qaynah villages clash over a water source in Jabal Salil, Amran, Yemen following disputes over water supply, resulting in two dead and eight injured.

properties.Title: Israeli military demolishes Palestinian-owned water well in Hebron
properties.Description: Israeli military forces demolish a Palestinian-owned water well in Al Baqaa, Hebron, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli military uproots Palestinian olive trees to build water network for settlements
properties.Description: Israeli military forces uproot numerous Palestinian-owned olive trees in Kifil Haris village, Salfit, West Bank, to build a water network for the Barkan and Ariel settlements.

properties.Title: Police beat and arrest La LUCHA protesters in Tshikapa, DR Congo over water cost increase
properties.Description: Police beat and arrest 10 La LUCHA protesters in Tshikapa (Kasai) Democratic Republic of Congo demonstrating against the public water cost increase by REGIDESO.

properties.Title: Demonstrators torch truck and assault driver in Gqeberha, South Africa over water shortage
properties.Description: Demonstrators torch a truck in Gqeberha, Nelson Mandela Bay, South Africa to protest water and electricity shortages, forcibly removing the driver and three passengers, and assaulting one passenger.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers pump sewage water onto Palestinian farm near Nahhalin
properties.Description: Israeli settlers from Baytar Illit pump sewage water onto a Palestinian-owned farm in Ayan Fares near Nahhalin village, Bethlehem, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli military demolishes Palestinian-owned facilities and water tanks in As Sawahira ash Sharqiya
properties.Description: Israeli military forces demolish Palestinian-owned facilities, including water tanks belonging to Bedouins in As Sawahira ash Sharqiya, Al Quds, West Bank.

properties.Title: Villagers clash over water drainage and river diversion in Sirsa, Haryana, India
properties.Description: Villagers from Farwai and Vaidwala clash with those from Panihari and Burj Karamgarh near Panihari village, Haryana, India over opening an underpass to drain water and divert the Ghaggar river, resulting in four injuries. Police deploy to control the situation.

properties.Title: Protest in Fatehabad, India over water barrier construction, gunshots reported
properties.Description: Some people protest in Fatehabad district (Haryana), India over the construction of a barrier to stop water from flooding into the village, with gunshots fired during the protest.

properties.Title: Clashes between Baggara tribal militias over water canal dispute injure woman in Deir ez Zor, Syria
properties.Description: Clashes between two Baggara tribal militia families in Jneineh, Deir ez Zor, Syria over a water canal injure a woman with a stray bullet, with no fatalities.

properties.Title: Local women and INC members protest waterlogging in Joshiyara, India and clash with police
properties.Description: Local women and INC members protest flooding in Joshiyara village, Uttarakhand, India raising slogans and black flags. They stop the convoy of a cabinet minister and clash with the police.

properties.Title: Groups clash over water extraction in Pharaya Malwala, India resulting in one death
properties.Description: Two groups clash with sticks and guns in Pharaya Malwala, Punjab, India over water extraction from fields, resulting in one death and three injuries.

properties.Title: Kilgoris boys high school students set fire to buildings in protest over water shortage in Kenya
properties.Description: Kilgoris boys high school students in Transmara West, Narok, Kenya set fire to a dormitory, dining hall, and classroom to protest a water shortage, leading to the arrest of 10 students and the school's indefinite closure.

properties.Title: Demonstrators in Inchanga, South Africa protest electricity and water shortages, resulting in five injuries from shooting
properties.Description: Demonstrators block roads in Inchanga (eThekwini, KwaZulu-Natal), South Africa to protest electricity and water shortages when unknown assailants open fire, injuring five people. Victims suffer leg gunshot wounds, with one also sustaining stomach and head injuries.

properties.Title: Israeli military destroys Palestinian lands and water wells near Hebron
properties.Description: Israeli military forces raze Palestinian-owned lands and destroy water wells in al-Hejrah, south of Hebron near Dura town, Hebron, West Bank.

properties.Title: KwaMaqoma, South Africa residents barricade road demanding safe drinking water, leading police to use teargas and rubber bullets
properties.Description: About 1,000 KwaMaqoma residents in Raymond Mhlaba, Amathole, Eastern Cape, including students and school staff, barricade Mbewu Road with burning tires and rocks demanding safe drinking water, leading police to use teargas and rubber bullets as demonstrators retaliate with stones.

properties.Title: Farming community leader attacked near Bopele Tank, Sri Lanka for addressing water shortage
properties.Description: An unidentified group attacks a farming community leader near Bopele Tank in Mayurapura, Hambantota city, Sri Lanka for helping farmers address a water shortage problem.

properties.Title: Israeli forces pour concrete into Palestinian well in Hebron, West Bank to stop irrigation
properties.Description: Israeli forces raid Palestinian farmland in occupied Hebron, West Bank, and pour concrete into the water sources to stop agricultural irrigation in an area that serves 25 families.

properties.Title: Jamali community protests in Tando Bago, Pakistan after youth dies in water rights clash
properties.Description: Family members and relatives of the Jamali community protest in Tando Bago town, Sindh, Pakistan after a youth dies in a clash over water rights rotation.

properties.Title: Tribal families clash over water dispute in Al-Hasakeh, Syria injuring two
properties.Description: Clashes over a water dispute between two tribal families who are cousins erupt in Khashman neighborhood in Al-Hasakeh city, Syria involving roadblocks with burning tires, resulting in two injuries and no fatalities.

properties.Title: Protestors block road in Chaco, Argentina demanding water access and police respond with rubber bullets
properties.Description: In Chaco province, Resistencia, San Fernando, Argentina protestors from the October 27 Social Movement cut off National Route 11 to demand water, electricity, and housing access, and police intervene with rubber bullets to disperse them.

properties.Title: Residents in Divandarreh, Iran protest drinking water outage, and clash with police
properties.Description: Residents in Divandarreh city, Kurdistan, Iran rally in front of the water and sewage department over a three-day drinking water outage, and police use tear gas and arrest some protesters to disperse them.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers assault Palestinian shepherds and block water access in Mosafer Yuta
properties.Description: Israeli settlers, protected by soldiers, assault Palestinian shepherds in Tuba, Mosafer Yuta, and set up a tent near a water well to prevent Palestinians from using the water.

properties.Title: A political clash turns violent in India over the failure to complete an irrigation project in Andhra Pradesh
properties.Description: On August 4, members of the YRS Congress Party and the Telugu Desam Party (TDP) clash in Beemaganipalle village on the outskirts of Punganur town, Andhra Pradesh, India in protests over unfinished irrigation projects. Police resort to lathi-charge and teargas to disperse the mobs. Police vehicles are set on fire and several TDP members and officials are injured.

properties.Title: Kurdish militia (QSD) members prevent water tanks from entering Al Tirwaziya village, Syria
properties.Description: Kurdish militia (QSD) members prevent water tanks from entering Al Tirwaziya village in ar Raqqa province, Syria.

properties.Title: Townspeople in Rachaya al Foukhar, Lebanon protest attacks on water station facilities
properties.Description: Townspeople in Rachaya al Foukhar (Hasbeiya, Lebanon) demonstrate outside the water station, condemning repeated attacks on its facilities, after unknown persons destroy a building and the electricity generator, stealing its contents and disrupting water pumping to the town and agricultural sector.

properties.Title: Villagers protest in Gaobeidian, China over flood response, clash with police, with at least one injury reported
properties.Description: Villagers in Gaobeidian city (Hebei, China) protest outside the local government's office over the flood response. Police beat one resident and arrest at least one demonstrator. The protests follow typhoon-induced floods that destroyed homes and killed at least 29. Villagers are angry at the media's downplaying of the disaster and the central government's discharge of excess water into their villa

properties.Title: Israeli settlers destroy water supply network in Khallet al-Homur, West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli settlers from Maale Amos destroy a water supply network in Khallet al-Homur, near Bethlehem, West Bank.

properties.Title: Clash between Karnataka and Tamil Nadu in Thanjavur, India over Cauvery river water release with 197 arrests
properties.Description: CPI members protest in front of central government offices in Thanjavur (Tamil Nadu, India) against Karnataka for not releasing Cauvery river water to Tamil Nadu and demand the  government solve the issue. Police clash with protestors and arrest 197 people, including 152 men and 45 women.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers destroy water networks in the West Bank village of Khirbat Tana
properties.Description: Israeli settlers destroy two kilometers of water networks in Khirbat Tana village, Nablus, West Bank, Palestine.

properties.Title: Farming community leader attacked in Hambantota, Sri Lanka for addressing water shortage, the second such attack in a month
properties.Description: An unidentified group attacks a farming community leader in Mayurapura, Hambantota city, Sri Lanka, for helping farmers solve a water shortage problem, the second such attack in a month.

properties.Title: Protesters in Swellendam, South Africa barricade roads and torch municipal office over water and electricity rate hikes
properties.Description: Demonstrators in Swellendam, Overberg, Western Cape, South Africa barricade roads with burning tires and torch the municipal head office, protesting increases in municipal rates for water and electricity. Rioters loot and damage shops, clashing with police while armed with sticks and improvised weapons. Police arrest more than 100 suspects on charges of public violence, malicious damage to propert

properties.Title: Vandalism of Aguas del Altiplano pipe causes water cut for 7,000 in Iquique, Chile
properties.Description: Unidentified individuals vandalize an Aguas del Altiplano pipe in the Tadeo Haenke area of Iquique, Tarapaca, Chile cutting water to over 7,000 people for several hours.

properties.Title: Environmental activists vandalize Beaumont-Saint-Cyr golf course and irrigation systems over water use during drought
properties.Description: During a lunch break of the SLT-organized 'Water Convoy' bicycle parade in France, 15 to 40 masked environmental activists cut through a fence and vandalize the Beaumont-Saint-Cyr golf course near Jaunay-Marigny, Nouvelle-Aquitaine. They tear up the lawn, damage the irrigation system, and paint slogans and the SLT logo, protesting water use for golf courses during droughts.

properties.Title: Taliban militants kill water suppliers to security check posts in North Waziristan, Pakistan
properties.Description: On August 21, Taliban Pakistan (TTP) assailants shoot dead two water suppliers at security check posts in Hamzoni village, North Waziristan, Pakistan.

properties.Title: Russian airstrikes in Idleb, Syria, target HTS positions and water station, killing two
properties.Description: On 22 August, Russian warplanes target Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham (HTS) militants and an old water station used by displaced families near Array village in Idleb countryside, Syria, killing two and injuring three others.

properties.Title: Supporters clash in Kerugoya, Kenya over county water project vandalism
properties.Description: On August 22, supporters of the Kirinyaga governor clash with supporters of the Kirinyaga women's representative and a Baragwi member of the county assembly (MCA) in Kerugoya, Kenya, pelting stones as the representative goes to answer police summons over county water project vandalism, resulting in injuries to the representative, the MCA, and several supporters.

properties.Title: Villagers in Bazhou, China, clash with police over flood response and compensation
properties.Description: On 22 August, villagers in Bazhou city (Langfang, Hebei, China) break windows and barge into a government office after learning that police beat women from their village who were protesting on August 8, prompting police to attack the demonstrators, hospitalizing two and causing injuries to others. The demonstration with around 3,000 participants protested government responses to typhoon-induced fl
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al. 2010 ID. CHN13600; CHN13651

properties.Title: Pastoralists and Douar Azaghar residents clash over water access in Toumliline, Morocco
properties.Description: Pastoralists and Douar Azaghar residents clash in Toumliline, Sidi Ifni, Guelmim-Oued Noun, resulting in two injuries after locals prevent access to water tanks in late August.

properties.Title: Pro-Kannada activists protest water release at KRS Dam in Karnataka, India
properties.Description: Over 100 pro-Kannada activists from Bengaluru protest and attempt to lay siege to the Krishna Raja Sagara Dam reservoir in Srirangapatna, Mandya, Karnataka, India (August 26), against the state government's decision to release Cauvery river water to Tamil Nadu. Police use force to stop protesters from pushing through barricades and detain them.

properties.Title: JNIM militants destroy water dam with explosives in Boni, Mali
properties.Description: On 28 August, JNIM militants set off explosives to destroy a water dam in Boni, Douentza, Mopti, Mali.

properties.Title: Police arrest gang leader for killing environmentalist opposing hydroelectric project in Tela, Atlantida, Honduras
properties.Description: An environmentalist who advocated for water rights and opposed a hydroelectric project on the Mezapa River that would affect water access for around 30,000 residents is killed in Tela, Altanida, Honduras. Police arrest a Los Conrados gang leader in late August.

properties.Title: The Iraqi army damages Kurdish irrigation pipes and supplies and orders villagers not to cultivate crops in a repeat of an event
properties.Description: In a repeat of an incident from June 2023, the Iraqi army raids Balkanah village in Kirkuk damaging irrigation supplies and pipes in August. The army detains two workers and their guards and the Iraqi commander in charge instructs villagers not to cultivate their crops.

properties.Title: Groups clash over water tank construction in Khempur, India, resulting in one death
properties.Description: Around 30 August, two groups led by a Pradhan and a former Pradhan clash with stones and gunfire in Khempur village, Rampur, Uttar Pradesh, India, over cutting trees to construct a water tank, resulting in one death and six injuries.

properties.Title: Azerbaijani forces wound civilian attacking water truck in Martakert region
properties.Description: On September 3, Azerbaijani forces fire small arms and grenade at a water truck near Chankatagh in the Martakert region, wounding one civilian.

properties.Title: Kenya security forces conduct airstrike in Saakow, Somalia, injuring two civilians and destroying water facility
properties.Description: On September 3, Kenya security forces conduct an airstrike in Saakow, Middle Juba, injuring two civilians and destroying a water company facility and buildings.

properties.Title: Mob attacks Qhara Qhara indigenous community members in Sucre, Chuquisaca, Bolivia, over water dispute
properties.Description: On September 3, in the rural area of Sucre municipality (Chuquisaca), Bolivia 11 to 15 local farming syndicate members attack members of the Qhara Qhara indigenous community with sticks, knives, and stones in Estancia Chullchuta, La Paz municipality. The syndicate members, backed by the local MAS political party, have a long-standing water dispute with the indigenous community.

properties.Title: Dominican Republic partially closes Haiti border to halt canal construction on shared Massacre River; military deployed
properties.Description: On 6 September 2023, in Dajabon, the Dominican government announces the partial closure of the border with Haiti in order to pressure Haiti to stop construction of a canal to take water from the Massacre River, shared by both countries. Soldiers, machinery, and armored vehicles are deployed in the area and borders are temporarily closed.

properties.Title: Dominican Republic closes border with Haiti to pressure halt on canal construction
properties.Description: On September 15, all borders between Haiti and Dominican Republic are closed, and military are deployed, to pressure Haiti to stop canal construction on the Massacre River, with visa suspensions and entry refusals for labeled provocateurs.

properties.Title: Armed group sabotages sewage network in Al Khums, Libya, causing contamination
properties.Description: An unidentified armed group sabotages a sewage network in Al Khums, Al Marqab, Libya, resulting in a water leak and area contamination in early September.

properties.Title: Teacher assaults Dalit student over drinking water from teacher's camper in Bayana, Rajasthan, India
properties.Description: In caste-based violence in Bayana town, Rajasthan, India, a Gujjar caste teacher assaults and injures a Dalit student for drinking water from a teachers' camper. This prompts another protest by family members, villagers, and Bhim Army members and the teacher is assaulted.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al. 2010 ID. IND141112; IND141193; IND140082

properties.Title: Israeli forces seize wastewater pumping truck in Nablus, West Bank
properties.Description: On September 10, Israeli forces seize a wastewater pumping truck in Burqah, Nablus, West Bank, citing the reason of it operating in Area C -- an area under Israeli control.

properties.Title: Boko Haram militants clash with gendarmerie forces and overturn a water tower in Extreme-Nord, Cameroon
properties.Description: On September 11, Boko Haram militants, JAS faction, clash with gendarmerie forces at the military outpost in Nguetchewe, Extreme-Nord, Cameroon, resulting in three deaths. The militants overturn a water tower during the conflict.

properties.Title: Indigenous women block National Route 86 in Tartagal, Salta, demanding water supply restoration
properties.Description: Indigenous women and children from the Wichi, Guarani, and Toba communities block National Route 86 in Tartagal, Salta, Argentina (September 11), demanding the restoration of their water supply. Protestors are attacked by an unidentified group at night.

properties.Title: Al Shabaab detonates IED at water borehole in Ceel Qoxle village, Galgaduud, Somalia
properties.Description: On September 13, Al Shabaab detonates an IED at a water source borehole in Ceel Qoxle village, Ceel Buur, Galgaduud, Somalia, destroying a water truck. The same day Al Shabaab detonates an IED at a water source borehole and destroys houses and water wells in Ceel Garas village, Dhuusamarreeb, Galgaduud, Somalia, targeting civilians from the Habar Gedir clan. The militants claim the US destroyed th
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al. 2010 ID. SOM42259; SOM42258

properties.Title: Residents protest water price hike by taking over El Ceibal purification plant in Rocafuerte, Manabi, Ecuador
properties.Description: On September 13, residents in Rocafuerte, Manabi, Ecuador, take over the El Ceibal water purification plant to protest a proposed water price hike, causing nearly 400,000 Manabi residents to lose drinking water service until pumping could resume.

properties.Title: Ukrainian forces shell Gordeevka, Kursk, damaging power lines and water main
properties.Description: Ukrainian forces shell Gordeevka, Kursk, September 14, damaging power lines and a water main.

properties.Title: Ukrainian drone strike damages water tower in Dolgoe, Belgorod, Russia
properties.Description: Ukrainian forces use a drone to strike the village of Dolgoe, Valuyki I Valuyskiy, Belgorod, Russia, damaging a water tower on September 16.

properties.Title: Israeli military fires warning shots at Palestinian wastewater vehicle in Gaza Strip
properties.Description: On September 16, Israeli military forces on the security fence fire warning shots at a Palestinian wastewater vehicle east of Khan Yunis, Gaza Strip, to keep Palestinians away, with no reported casualties.

properties.Title: Demonstrators barricade roads in Tsakane, South Africa, protesting water shortage
properties.Description: On September 18, hundreds of demonstrators in Tsakane, Ekurhuleni, Gauteng, South Africa, barricade roads with burning tires and rocks to protest a water shortage, stoning and damaging a Home Affairs office and police vehicles.

properties.Title: Residents in Guinea clash with law enforcement over demands for 24-hour electricity and water supplies, resulting in injuries
properties.Description: Residents in Boffa, Boffa-Centre, Boffa, Boke, Guinea, block a road with trunks and stones and clash with law enforcement, demanding 24-hour electricity and water supplies while denouncing corruption among the elite and mining companies; three demonstrators are injured by live ammunition fired by special forces.

properties.Title: RSF cuts off water and electricity supply in Ombada, Khartoum, Sudan
properties.Description: The Rapid Support Forces (RSF), a paramilitary group in Sudan, is accused of cutting off the water and electricity supply in Ombada, Um Bada, Khartoum, Sudan, September 18.

properties.Title: Residents in Naranjos, Veracruz, Mexico, block highway to demand water
properties.Description: On September 19, around Naranjos, Veracruz, Mexico, more than 300 residents protest for the second day, demanding water and blocking the Tuxpan-Tampico highway near Buena Vista. State police intervene, forcibly evicting residents with pushes, insults, and tear gas. Protesters also report warning shots being fired.

properties.Title: Residents in Yurimaguas, Peru, demand potable water outside the provincial government building and clash with police
properties.Description: On September 19, in Yurimaguas, Alto Amazonas, Peru, residents and members of the local Highway Defense Front gather at the provincial government building to demand potable water. Conflicting reports suggest possible clashes between rioters and police and the use of tear gas.

properties.Title: The Syrian military shells the Jeb ad Dam near Aleppo
properties.Description: Syrian military shells the Jeb ad Dam in rural western Aleppo with artillery.

properties.Title: Three fishers disrupt desalination plant construction in Valparaiso, injuring one worker
properties.Description: Three fishers disrupt construction of Aconcagua desalination plant between Puchuncavi and Quintero communes in Valparaiso, throwing stones at machinery. This follows eviction of Bahia Narau Quintero-Puchuncavi Fishermen's Federation members on August 3, who block access to area for 86 days over environmental concerns. One worker sustains injury; no fatalities occur.

properties.Title: Man poisons drinking water supplies for soldiers in Barnaul, Altai, Russia
properties.Description: Around 20 September 2023, a man in Barnaul, Altai, Russia, poisons the drinking water supplies intended for conscripted soldiers. The Federal Security Services allege that he does this to prove his desire to help Ukrainian war efforts.

properties.Title: Missile attack damages water supply in Kharkiv, Ukraine, cutting water to hospital and homes; 2 injured
properties.Description: A water-supply system in Kharkiv, Ukraine is damaged in an enemy missile attack, cutting water to a hospital and private homes, injuring two, in late September.

properties.Title: Residents in Ciudad de Mexico protest outside National Palace over killings and water access denial
properties.Description: On September 21, residents of Xacaxomulco in Ciudad de Mexico - Cuauhtemoc protest outside the National Palace to demand justice for the killing of two men by local leaders. Protesters claim that these leaders have denied them access to water unless they participate in political campaign events.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers pump wastewater on Palestinian agricultural lands, damaging olive trees and crops
properties.Description: Israeli settlers from Shaarei Tikva pump wastewater onto Palestinian agricultural lands east of Qalqilya, near Atmat Bayt Imrin and Azzun village (September 24), causing damage to olive trees and crops.

properties.Title: Clashes in Al Quds, West Bank: Palestinian rioters throw Molotov cocktails, Israeli police respond with tear gas and wastewater
properties.Description: On September 25, Palestinian rioters throw Molotov cocktails and clash with Israeli police who raided Al Quds - Isawiya, Al Quds, West Bank, Palestine. Police fire stun grenades, tear gas, and spray wastewater at the rioters.

properties.Title: Residents block road in San Antonio, Valparaiso, Chile, demanding municipal drinking water
properties.Description: On September 25, in San Antonio, Valparaiso, Chile, residents from the Maranata neighborhood block Road G-82 to demand drinking water deliveries from the municipality. This follows a halt in deliveries after drivers were attacked and injured on September 5th. Demonstrations continue at least until September 28th.

properties.Title: Shelling in Idleb Province, Syria, injures civilians and damages water truck
properties.Description: Syrian regime members shell residential neighborhoods and a market in Jisr-Ash-Shugur city, Idleb province, Syria, injuring seven to ten civilians, including two children and two women, and causing material damage, including to a water truck. No fatalities are reported.

properties.Title: Ukrainian forces shell Novaya Tavolzhanka, Belgorod, Russia, damaging water tower and electricity supply lines
properties.Description: Ukrainian forces shell Novaya Tavolzhanka, Shebekinskiy, Belgorod, Russia (September 26), with seven artillery rounds, damaging electricity supply lines, a water tower, and a house. No casualties are reported.

properties.Title: Protests erupt in Bengaluru, India, against release of Cauvery River water to Tamil Nadu; one farmer attempts suicide
properties.Description: On September 26, pro-Kannada activists, Kannada Rakshana Vedike members, and farmers protest in Bengaluru, Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka, India, against the state government's decision to release Cauvery river water to Tamil Nadu. One farmer attempts suicide at Freedom Park, and police detain the protesters.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces demolish foundations of Palestinian water pond in Bethlehem, West Bank, Palestine
properties.Description: On September 27, Israeli military forces demolish the foundations of a Palestinian-owned water pond in Artas village, Bethlehem, West Bank, Palestine, claiming it lacks a building permit.

properties.Title: Unidentified armed individuals attack water tanks in Bzal, Akkar, Lebanon, over clan feuds
properties.Description: Unidentified armed individuals attack a house and vehicle with grenades and fire at water tanks and solar panels in Bzal, Akkar, Lebanon, likely due to old clan feuds (September 27). One grenade explodes causing material damage, but there are no casualties.

properties.Title: Water pipe dispute escalates to armed clash in Nassriya, Iraq, resulting in one fatality
properties.Description: On September 27, a dispute over a water pipe escalates into an armed clash between cousins in Nassriya city, Iraq, resulting in the death of a Thi Qar Oil Company employee. Police arrest one person involved in the clash.

properties.Title: Israeli military forces destroy Palestinian water supply network in Hebron, West Bank, Palestine
properties.Description: On September 28, Israeli military forces destroy a Palestinian water supply network between Samu and Khirbat Susiyah village in Hebron, West Bank.

properties.Title: Ukrainian forces shell village in Kursk, Russia, damaging water tower and infrastructure
properties.Description: On 29 September 2023, Ukrainian forces shell the village of Gogolevka, Sudzhanskiy, Kursk, Russia, causing damage to houses, a former school, a maternity ward, electricity lines, and a water tower. No casualties are reported.

properties.Title: Flood-stricken citizens of Volos, Greece, protest waterway damming, mayor confronts protesters
properties.Description: On 30 September 2023, flood-stricken citizens in Volos, Greece, protest in Ali Meria against damming a waterway without required studies. The mayor confronts protesters and hits one who insults him, while police do not intervene.

properties.Title: Israel threatens full water cutoff to Gaza; water supply halts on October 30 amid blockade on electricity, food, and fuel
properties.Description: On October 9 2023, Israel threatens a complete cutoff of water to Gaza. Israeli Defense Minister Yoav Gallant says that “no electricity, no food, no water, no fuel” would be allowed into Gaza. Water supply from Israel to southern Gaza comes to a halt on 30 October and for some time no water is provided to Gaza from Israel.

properties.Title: FAMa and Wagner forces in Mali kill civilians and destroy water towers and blow up wells in the village of Ersane
properties.Description: Malian Armed Forces (FAMa) and Wagner forces in Mali kill 17 Tuareg civilians including elderly men and children in the village of Ersane (Bourem, Gao). They also destroy water towers and blow up wells in the village.

properties.Title: Groups clash over watering fields in Bannu, Pakistan, resulting in one death and three injuries
properties.Description: On 5 October 2023, two groups exchange fire over a dispute about watering fields in Miryakhel police limits in Bannu town, Pakistan, killing one person and injuring three others.

properties.Title: Turkish drone attacks on Syrian power stations cut water supplies to a million Syrians
properties.Description: Electricity systems in northeastern Syria are attacked by Turkey, cutting power and water to a million Syrians. The attacks extend from October 2023 to January 2024.
properties.Sources: Khoshnaw et al., 2024; Human Rights Watch 2023

properties.Title: Farmers and herders clash over water and land in northern Cameroon, with two deaths and many injuries reported
properties.Description: Between 6 and 7 October 2023, Kotoko farmers clash with Arab Choa herders over water and land in Makary, Goulfey, Amdagalgui and Ngouma (Logone-et-Chari, Far North, Cameroon) leading to at least two deaths and 20 injuries.

properties.Title: Turkish fighters shell the Khan Eljabal water station in rural Al Hasakeh, Syria
properties.Description: On October 7, Turkish fighters shell the Khan Eljabal (Khan Siri) water station in rural Al Hasakeh, Syria.

properties.Title: RSF shelling in Shambat, Khartoum, Sudan, cuts off electricity and water supply
properties.Description: The Rapid Support Forces (RSF), a paramilitary group in Sudan,  targets civilians with artillery in Shambat, Khartoum North - Shambat, Bahri, Khartoum, Sudan (October 8) cutting off electricity and water supply. Casualties are unknown.

properties.Title: Wagner mercenaries kill three pastoralists watering livestock at a well in the village of Inbogitane, Mali
properties.Description: Wagner mercenaries kill three pastoralists at a well in the village of Inbogitane (Kidal), Mali. The pastoralists were watering their livestock when they were killed.

properties.Title: Local indigenous opposition to a dam in Brazil that could flood a reserve results in two injuries
properties.Description: Indigenous people block access to a dam in Jose Boiteux (Santa Catarina), Brazil by setting obstacles on fire to demonstrate against the closing of the dam over concerns it would flood part of their Indigenous reserve. Military police use tear gas bombs and rubber bullets to disperse the demonstrators, injuring two.

properties.Title: Armed men occupy Municipal Water Company in Mixco, Guatemala, abduct employees and open fire on police
properties.Description: In Mixco, Guatemala, unidentified armed men occupy the Municipal Water Company of Guatemala City (Empagua) and abduct employees. As police forces arrive to reestablish water services, the armed men opened fire on them and flee the company's facilities.

properties.Title: Maya group seizes aqueduct and dam near Zaragoza, Guatemala; water cut off, clashes reported with police
properties.Description: Near Zaragoza, Chimaltenango, for the second consecutive day, members of the 48 Cantones de Totonicapan (Maya indigenous group) take over the Xaya Pixcaya Aqueduct and the El Tesoro dam and cut off the water supply to demand the resignation of the Solicitor General and the head of the Special Prosecutor Against Impunity (FECI). Clashes between participants and officers are reported. Police regain
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al. 2010 ID. GTM7723; GTM7469

properties.Title: Armed Israeli settlers from Avigail settlement attack Palestinian home in Al Mefqara, damaging water containers and solar panels
properties.Description: Armed Israeli settlers from the Avigail settlement attack a Palestinian home in Al Mefqara village near Massafer Yuta (Hebron, West Bank) breaking solar energy panels, damaging water storage containers, and chopping down fruit bearing trees.

properties.Title: Israeli military shells the main water tank in Lebanon
properties.Description: Israeli military forces shell the main water tank of Yarine (Tyr), Lebanon (October 11).

properties.Title: Israeli settlers from the Maale Amos settlement destroy water pipelines supplying Arab ar Rashaydah, Bethlehem, West Bank
properties.Description: On October 11,  Israeli settlers from the Maale Amos settlement destroy water pipelines in Arab ar Rashaydah, Bethlehem, West Bank, Palestine, disrupting the town's water supply.

properties.Title: Sudanese militia (RSF) work to prevent Chadian refugees from accessing a nearby water source in Central Darfur, Sudan
properties.Description: Sudanese militia (RSF) work to prevent Chadian refugees in Um Shalaiya IDP camp from accessing a nearby water source in Central Darfur, Sudan after clashes between RSF and the area's residents.

properties.Title: Tribal and clan forces attack QSD positions at Al-Hawayij water station in Deir ez Zor
properties.Description: On October 11, 2023, tribal and clan forces attack QSD positions at a water station in Al-Hawayij town, Deir ez Zor countryside, with rocket shells and machine guns. Casualties remain unknown.

properties.Title: Man assaulted and killed near Ghazipur, Uttar Pradesh, India over water pipe dispute
properties.Description: On October 12, Patidars (landowners) assault and kill a man near the Foxganj area in Ghazipur city (Uttar Pradesh), India over an issue related to drawing a water pipe through the fields.

properties.Title: Destruction of water and water infrastructure in Gaza cuts off water services for over a million Palestinians
properties.Description: As of October 12, 2023 following the Israeli invasion of Gaza at least six water wells, three pumping plants, one reservoir, and one desalination plant, serving over 1.1 million people, have been damaged.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers and forces storm villages, demolish water reservoirs in Hebron, West Bank, Palestine
properties.Description: On October 14, Israeli settlers, supported by Israeli forces, storm At-Tuba, Isfey at-Tahta, and Maghayir al Abeed villages in Hebron, West Bank, Palestine. They demolish water reservoirs, throw stones at houses, and loot solar panels and batteries.

properties.Title: The cutoff of fuel and destruction of energy facilities in Gaza forces the closure of the last remaining ocean desalination plan
properties.Description: The last remaining seawater desalination plant, out of the four servicing central and southern Gaza, shuts down on October 15 due to lack of fuel. Gaza’s only remaining operational wastewater treatment plant (out of five), which relies on solar energy, is also forced to shut down. Untreated sewage is being discharged to the sea and most of the 65 sewage pumping stations are not operational.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers and soldiers destroy water systems and an outhouse in Khirbat Susiyah, West Bank
properties.Description: On 16 October 2023, Israeli settlers and soldiers block all entrances to the village of Khirbat Susiyah (Hebron, West Bank), destroying three main water cisterns, cutting irrigation pipes, and causing a water shortage affecting four dunams of farmland. The settlers also raze olive groves, demolish a stone wall, cut a home's power cable, seal a livestock feed cave, and demolish an outhouse.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers cut down 70 olive seedlings, demolish water wells, and damage Palestinian lands in West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli settlers cut down 70 olive seedlings, cut barbed wires, and demolish three water wells on Palestinian owned lands in Khirbat Susiyah (Hebron, West Bank). Soldiers and settlers in military uniform are present at the scene.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers physically assault a Palestinian in Khirbat Ar Ratheem, West Bank and damage water tanks and pipes.
properties.Description: Five masked Israeli settlers in military uniforms physically assault a Palestinian in Khirbat Ar Ratheem (Hebron, West Bank) and damage water tanks and water pipes.
properties.Sources: Raleigh et al. 2010 ID. PSE51294; PSE50987

properties.Title: Indigenous Rights Group marches to Zocalo in Mexico City, Mexico, denounces water and land dispossession
properties.Description: In Ciudad de Mexico - Cuauhtemoc, Mexico, members of the Front for the Defense of the Rights of the Indigenous Peoples and Neighborhoods of the Anahuac Basin (FDDPBOCA) demonstrate and march to the Zocalo against the dispossession of water and land and the registration system established by the Political Constitution of Mexico City. Demonstrators attack police officers with sticks and strip them o

properties.Title: Presumed Islamic State Sahel militants kill civilians at a water well in Burkina Faso.
properties.Description: Presumed Islamic State Sahel militants kill four civilians in Lipsi and 13 civilians at a water well in Mira, Sampelga, Burkina Faso.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers raid and assault Palestinians in West Bank, injuring a man and two children, and damaging water pipes and tanks
properties.Description: Israeli settlers raid and assault Palestinians in Sudat Al Thula area east of Yuta, West Bank, injuring a Palestinian man and two children, and causing material damage to water pipes and tanks in the area.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers raid Palestinian homes in West Bank damaging water tanks
properties.Description: Israeli settlers raid Palestinian-owned homes in Isfy (Isfey at-Tahta), Hebron, West Bank, damaging water tanks.

properties.Title: Police at an environment protest in Panama over the impacts of a copper mine kill four and injure hundreds
properties.Description: A protest over the environmental impacts of a major copper mine in Panama, including severe impacts on rivers, is met with violence by local police. Hundreds are injured and four killed over a month of protests.
properties.Sources: Con las manos en la data 2024

properties.Title: Israeli settlers set fire to a Palestinian-owned house and destroy 800 meters of water pipelines in West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli settlers set fire to a Palestinian-owned house and destroy 800 meters of water pipelines in Al Awja (Jericho, West Bank) that feed thousands of dunams of agricultural lands.

properties.Title: A suspected Al Shabaab gunman fires on a water tanker in Kenya
properties.Description: A suspected Al Shabaab gunman fires on a water tanker near Nguni market, Kenya (Mwingi Central Sub- County, Kitui). The driver managed to escape unhurt and one of the tanker's front tires was punctured.

properties.Title: Armed individuals in Haiti open fire on a convoy of vehicles related to water and sanitation. No one is injured but vehicles are
properties.Description: In Croix-des-Bouquets, Ouest, Haiti, armed individuals open fire on a convoy of vehicles belonging to the National Directorate of Drinking Water and Sanitation, in the Beudet area. No one is injured but vehicles are damaged.

properties.Title: Israeli forces fire at a water transportation truck in Qab al Janib area in Hebron, West Bank
properties.Description: Israeli forces open fire at a water transportation truck as it was approaching the checkpoint in Qab al Janib area in Hebron, West Bank.

properties.Title: Israeli settlers destroy Palestinian property, including water containers and contaminate water wells in West Bank
